Other than recording set up, MediVoice also. adds convenient features such as drug reminders, an inbuilt help giving medical questions, and safe. doctor-patient communication channels. The system will save the company the time wasted on manual data entry. enhances the quality of documentation, simplifies administration. burdens, and helps to establish a continuous healthcare. patient and provider experience.

title: Two-layer Hybrid XGBoost based on Sample Nearest Neighbor Enveloping Transposition Projection Mechanism
authors: Zhixuan Fan, Jie Ma, Tao Zhou, Xiaoping Zeng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: XGBoost is a widely recognized and efficient ensemble learning algorithm. However, existing methods commonly train classifiers directly on the original samples without effectively considering the correlation information between them, leading to limited accuracy in cases of low sample separability. To address this, we propose a novel ’two-layer hybrid XG-Boost’ model based on the Sample Nearest Neighbor Enveloping Transposition Projection Mechanism (SNNEPM). This model constructs a two-layer sample space to train and fuse two separate XGBoost models. First, the nearest neighbor samples of each current sample are calculated based on manifold distance. Second, a sample envelope is constructed and reconstructed into a new envelope sample via transposition projection. Finally, two XGBoost models are trained on the original and envelope samples, respectively, and their predictions are fused. Experiments on 10 public datasets show that our method improves ACC by an average of 2% compared to using only the original sample space, and by an average of 1.54% compared to state-of-the-art XGBoost variants, demonstrating its effectiveness.

title: The Prospects of the Modal Control Method in Special Technological Equipment Automation
authors: Anastasiya V. Los, Alexey A. Gelashvili
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: The article considers the problem of modal control. The theoretical survey of the modal control method in the technological processes automaton tasks is carried out. The perspectives of applying the given method in the tasks of special technological equipment automaton are studied and the type of engineering tasks for which the modal control method is more effective is defined. The mathematical model of modal regulator is developed and the modelling of modal control by means of climate camera is presented. The efficiency and expediency of the method under study for the given tasks in terns of ensuring the quality indicators is shown by practical examples. The problems of synthesis and realization procedures are investigated and the ways of solving the problems are proposed.

title: Methods of Spectrograms' Processing for Deep Neural Networks for the Real-Time Recognition of Radio Signal Modulation
authors: Maksim O. Zhegulin, Sergey V. Karamov
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Automatic recognition of radio signal modulation is a key factor in processing radio signals in various technical applications, such as radio communications, radar, direction finding, and others. In this study, methods for automatic modulation recognition applicable to embedded and edge hardware platforms with limited performance and power constraints are considered. The foundation of the proposed approach is the application of a conventional short-time Fourier transform to obtain a two-dimensional time-frequency spectrogram, subsequently, various combinations of spectrogram output parameters and their transformation functions are studied to generate the neural network's input features. Our initial assumption has been that traditional modulation recognition, which relies solely on the magnitude of the spectrogram, results in an inherent and undefined reduction of the essential information present in the original complex samples. The initial output complex parameters are introduced into the processing pipeline; these include the real and imaginary parts, as well as derived parameters such as magnitude and phase. An optimised CNN class neural network, tailored as an optimal architecture for processing two-dimensional spectrogram inputs, is constructed. By adjusting the neural network's parameters, the dataset is processed to obtain output quality metrics. A comparison is presented, detailing the accuracy and efficiency of radio signal modulation processing across all proposed spectrogram output parameter representation methods. Our research shows that introducing a combination of the real, imaginary, amplitude, and phase spectrogram parameters significantly improves neural network recognition, achieving 96 % accuracy, which is 25 % higher than conventional magnitude-only input. The observed result is invariant to the type of neural network utilised, introducing only a minor increase in network complexity and computational burden.

title: Pocket Engineered Vertical TFET for Enhanced Drive Current
authors: Khushboo Singh, Ram Awadh Mishra, Kumari Nibha Priyadarshani
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This paper provides a detailed investigation of pocket area engineering in a vertical TFET structure, with an emphasis on the effects of shifting the pocket towards the source region. The study shows that moving the pocket towards the source increases the band-to-band tunneling probability. This leads in improved ON-state current, steeper subthreshold slope (SS), and a drop in threshold voltage. Further improvement in transconductance and other analog parameters, implying applicability for low-power analog/RF systems. The proposed structure achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{O N}/I_{O F F}=1.73 \times 10^{13}$</tex> and sub-threshold slope (SS) as 13.46 mV/dec along with the fully supressed ambipolarity. Achieved ON current is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$344 \mu \mathrm{A}/\mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> which is better as compare to reported Ge pocket VTFET. These results show that source-side pocket is an important design parameter for high-performance vertical TFETs.

title: Logistic Regression Optimization for Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: Anant Sahoo, Deep Patel, Sulaiman Mohyuddin, Sruthi Balammagary, Abubakar Mohammed, Kavitha Janamolla
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Credit card fraud remains a major concern in today’s digital financial systems. This study presents a Logistic Regression (LR) model for detecting fraudulent credit card transactions using a publicly available dataset from Kaggle. The dataset, containing over 200,000 records, was preprocessed by handling missing values, encoding categorical features, and scaling numerical variables to ensure consistency. The baseline model, trained with ten-fold cross-validation, achieved an accuracy of 92.4% and a false positive rate near 1%. To further improve performance, enhancements such as feature scaling, Principal Component Analysis (PCA), dataset stratification, and Lasso (L1) regularization were implemented. The refined model achieved an accuracy of 94.7%, precision of 91.4%, recall of 64.1%, and an F1-score of 75.4%. These results demonstrate that an optimized Logistic Regression model can effectively balance accuracy, interpretability, and computational efficiency, offering a reliable foundation for large-scale financial fraud detection and future work involving hybrid or ensemble methods.

title: A Framework for Stroke Prediction Using CDA-ResNet with Adaptive Feature Selection and Evaluation of Ensemble ML Methods
authors: Talekar Rohini Rustum, P Praveen, Mohammed Ali Shaik
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: This study provides a stroke prediction framework with CDA-ResNet with adaptive feature selection and a comparison of the performance of different ensemble machine learning (ML) approaches. The CDA-ResNet model is the second model that can improve the accuracy of the prediction process by using the dense convolutional blocks and attention to extract meaningful features out of the brain MRI images. Adaptive feature selection is the best approach to optimization of the model as it picks the most relevant features and minimizes overfitting and efficiency. Ensemble such as Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM are compared and evaluated on the framework to determine its performance. Based on the results obtained, the proposed CDA-ResNet model, with adaptive feature selection, has an accuracy of 94.5 % and F1-score of 0.93 and AUC of 0.97, which compares with the traditional models. The ensemble techniques also enhance the performance and XGBoost has the best precision and recall. This framework has a great potential of detecting early strokes which is a reliable instrument of clinical decision making and intervention planning.

title: A Multi-Scale D-ImgNet Learning Approach for Detecting Diabetic Retinal Abnormalities
authors: Pontagani Suresh, Ratnababu Pilli
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Nowadays, many people are suffering from retinal diseases, and it becomes more complex for all age groups. There are various reasons people can develop retinal diseases. Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is one of the most common retinal diseases that can be caused by diabetes. It is very important to detect DR at an early stage. In this paper, Multi-Scale D-ImgNet (MSDI) is introduced to detect DR at early stages, thereby helping prevent permanent vision loss. The proposed MSDI mainly captures multi-scale patterns that refine high-definition features from the input data. The preprocessing technique is a coarse-level denoiser that removes high-frequency distortions but ensures the global structural configuration of the retina map. The other filter is a fine-image enhancer that eliminates residual noise and enhances lesion edges without losing clinical information. To refine the final output, the D-Filter Model was introduced, which contains dual filters that show a refined output image. Finally, based on these refined output images, the multi-class classification layer is used to classify the multiple DR stages, such as DR (Healthy, Mild Non-Proliferative DR (NPDR), Moderate Non-Proliferative DR, and Severe Non-Proliferative DR). The experiments are conducted using the EyePACS dataset, with results showing high classification performance.

title: Greedy Analytics for Card Rewards: A Data-Driven Portfolio Optimizer With Simulation-Backed ROI Insights
authors: Dhanush Gopal Battina
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: This paper develops and deploys a business analytics pipeline to optimize consumer credit-card portfolios by maximizing net rewards subject to annual fees, category caps, and redemption regimes. A greedy selection algorithm with dynamic programming elements operationalizes an empirically specified objective over category spend, card multipliers, redemption valuations, static benefits, and costs, producing interpretable, incremental marginal value at each portfolio expansion. External data integration aligns household spend with income-stratified budgets, and large-scale Monte Carlo sampling quantifies expected return on spend, dispersion, and sensitivity to user preferences and benefit utilization. The implemented decision app (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex> Shiny) translates model outputs into actionable recommendations and category-level card assignments for end users. Results reveal diminishing marginal benefits beyond a small portfolio size and heterogeneous ROI driven by spend mix and benefit realization, illustrating how simulation and optimization inform product selection decisions in consumer finance analytics.

title: Bidirectional Distillation Network for Unsupervised Anomaly Detection and Localization
authors: Jianping Huang, Yong Tang, Bangshu Xiong, Zhidong Zhu, Zhibo Rao, Jiujiu Chen
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Unsupervised anomaly detection and localization are fundamental tasks in industrial inspection and related applications. However, existing distillation-based methods often suffer from weak anomaly filtering, insufficient global perception, and noisy anomaly scoring, leading to inaccurate localization. To address these issues, we present a BD (Bidirectional Distillation) framework that integrates both reverse and forward distillation branches. Specifically, we design a Progressive Gated Feature Fusion (PGFF) module to suppress irrelevant anomaly information, and a Multi-Perception Anomaly Discriminator (MPAD) that adaptively fuses multi-scale anomaly maps to reduce noise contamination. Furthermore, a Dynamic Residual Anomaly Fusion (DRAF) strategy is proposed during inference, which refines localization by preserving residual anomaly cues. Extensive experiments on the MVTec AD benchmark demonstrate that BD achieves state-of-the-art performance in anomaly localization.

title: Attention-Enhanced YOLO Framework for Real-Time Human Detection and Counting
authors: Sudha Rani V, Katkuri Thrisha, Meghana Reddy Pingili, Pravalika Reddy Veligeti, Rajesh Bandari
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This paper presents Real-Time Human Detection and Counting, an innovative system developed using machine learning and deep learning techniques, with a focus on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). Beyond the core functionality of detecting and counting humans in real-time from video streams, the system integrates advanced features such as facial detection and dynamic adaptability to varying environments. These features enhance its accuracy and scalability for applications such as crowd management, security surveillance, and resource planning. The facial detection module extends functionality by enabling identity-based tracking or enhanced analytical insights. Leveraging frameworks like OpenCV and TensorFlow, the system ensures efficient processing, while optimized algorithms enable robust performance in diverse settings. By addressing challenges of real-time processing and scalability, this system offers a reliable, efficient, and accessible solution for human detection and counting in dynamic scenarios.

title: Compact and Flexible Antenna Design for Discrete Body-Worn Communication Devices
authors: M Ramesh, Ch V S Pavan Kumar, Suresh Chinnathampy Manickam, Jeyalakshmi Vijayarajan, T Srikanth, P V Ranthi Dev Varma
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: This paper presents designing and simulation of a wearable antenna that operates in the 5.8 GHz ISM band for healthcare communication devices. Despite the challenges in designing the wearable antenna and approaching miniaturization, mechanical flexibility, and low SAR while maintaining near to the human body. The design makes use of its low profile and simplicity of integration. our findings indicate the resonant frequency matched to 5.8GHz with a return loss of −16.85dB in flat configuration, 34MHz of an approximate bandwidth With a VSWR of 1.3 below the limit of 2.0, it shows the peak directivity of antenna at 7.07dBi providing directivity radiation away from the body and analysis of SAR shows only 0.50W/kg well below the 2.0W/kg ICNIRP Safety Guidelines. In addition to comprehensive bending analysis demonstrates mechanical robustness with antenna maintaining stable performance under deformation achieving return loss at −14.45dB and analysis of SAR shows 0.50W/kg even at 90mm bend radius. These results are shows that successfully combines compactness, flexibility and safety for practical wearable for health care monitoring applications. This work bridge gap between simulation based antenna designing and requirements for body worn system.

title: The Impact of Cryptocurrency Market on Stock Returns: Evidence from Bahrain
authors: Bayan Alkhabbaz, Daouia Chebab, Hussain Al Araibi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: This study explores the relationship between cryptocurrency returns, specifically Ethereum, and stock market performance in Bahrain, with an emphasis on the effects of macroeconomic variables such as interest rates, inflation, and oil prices. The research aims to: understand and examine the interaction between cryptocurrencies and stock returns in Bahrain, assess the impact of cryptocurrency trends on stock market behaviour, evaluate the effects of volatility and risk transmission from the crypto market to traditional financial markets, and identify any time-lag or lead-lag dynamics between the two. Monthly data were analysed using the Autoregressive Distributed Lag (ARDL) model, allowing for both short- and long-run relationships to be captured. The results indicate a statistically significant long-run impact of Ethereum returns and oil prices on stock returns, suggesting growing market integration. Short-run dynamics also reveal evidence of volatility spillover and delayed effects, confirming a lead-lag relationship. These findings underscore the increasing influence of digital assets on traditional markets and have practical implications for investors, policymakers, and regulators in emerging financial systems like Bahrain.

title: Semantic Integrity Preserving Recommendation Framework with Large Language Models
authors: Rongbing Niu, Yunfeng Fan, Haibo Ye
abstract: Recommendation systems based on deep learning and graph neural networks have effectively captured complex user-item relationships but struggle to fully leverage textual data for personalized recommendations. This is primarily due to three key challenges: (1) shallow semantic integration in language model representations, (2) semantic degradation during the fusion of large language model (LLM) embeddings with collaborative filtering signals, and (3) scalability bottlenecks in sparse data and large-scale interaction scenarios. To address these challenges, we propose SIP-Rec, a Semantic Integrity Preserving Recommendation Framework based on LLM and contrastive learning. First, SIP-Rec utilizes LLM to generate semantically rich profiles, accurately capturing fine-grained differences in user preferences and item characteristics. Second, it introduces an innovative cross-modal fusion mechanism that employs a dynamic alignment strategy to bridge the representation space gap between high-dimensional LLM embeddings and collaborative filtering signals, effectively mitigating contextual information loss. Finally, by integrating knowledge distillation with contrastive learning objectives, SIP-Rec optimizes the consistency between semantically rich representations and interaction patterns.

title: An AI-Enabled Non-Invasive Framework for Early Detection of Oral Cancer
authors: T. Fasiha Khanam, T. Baba
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The oral cancer is a significant health concern in the world bearing in mind that morbidity and mortality of the late diagnosis occurrence is indeed high. Alternative intervention is noteworthy in order to achieve patient and treatment survival. The assumption made by the researcher of the given paper is the possibility to create the system of oral cancer diagnosis on AI-basis and reproduce the deep learning models, e.g., Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), DenseNet or MobileNet. To enhance the model, a dataset of images of an oral lesion was trained in the assistance of the traditional preprocessing techniques (resizing, normalization, and data augmentation). The evaluation was confirmed by the 10-fold cross-validation and average accuracy of 97 per cent, the precision of 0.97/F1-score per-fold, and the F1-score of 0.97/ F1-score per-fold. It was also found out that denseNet was useful in sharing features in subtle lesion patterns, CNN learnt the hierarchical features and MobileNet was a fast network and could be implemented in real-time. The success of the provided system is represented in the fact that it allowed the comparative analysis of the past works. The framework offers a resource, that is efficient, fast and devoid of a particular invasive procedure and has the potential to help the clinicians to identify the presence of oral cancer at an early stage and the capacity to reduce the human factor and improve the performance of the patients. The next generation implementation of work will also be based on multimodal clinical information and mobility development to provide increased accessibility.

title: Integrating XAI and Blockchain to Enhance Security and Resilience in Industrial Operations
authors: Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Hamza Ibrahim, Cosmas Ifeanyi Nwakanma, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Industrial systems are increasingly exposed to cy-ber-physical threats as sensing, automation, and machine learning (ML)-driven control expand the operational attack surface. This work introduces an integrated XAI-Blockchain framework that combines an interpretable one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) and long short-term memory (LSTM) intrusion detection models with PureChain's tamper-resistant logging to secure end-to-end industrial data flows. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves real-time detection with low-latency explanation generation, maintaining a high explanation stability of 0.98. It supports reliable access control by blocking 100 % of unauthorized attempts and provides deterministic, near-linear blockchain logging suitable for industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) operations. The framework improves security transparency, operational continuity, and auditability, demonstrating its practical viability for resilient industrial environments despite the limitations, notably elevated false-positive rates and low tamper-detection performance. These are key areas for enhancement. Future work will refine the ML models and explanation mechanisms to reduce misclassification, improve interpretability fidelity, and strengthen security through enhanced authentication and encryption.

title: Mamba Flow Head: Polar Constraint Module for Stereo Matching
authors: Xiaoshi Lin, Shuiping Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Iterative optimization-based stereo matching methods have demonstrated significant value in industrial applications, with the number of iterations providing a flexible trade-off between accuracy and computational cost. However, current research primarily focuses on improving the cost volume module, context feature extraction, or the iterative update mechanism within different network architectures, often neglecting a fundamental principle of stereo matching: the epipolar constraint. In this paper, we propose an enhanced module in iterative optimized model’s flow head by adding Mamba block. Our approach aims to constrain the module in theoretical level of expressive power rather than a complex loss function. The proposed module can be independently integrated into existing iterative models such as RAFT-Stereo, IGEV-Stereo, and Selective-Stereo. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of our method. The corresponding evaluation is on Scene Flow and KITTI 2015. Code is available at https://github.com/xslynin/stereo-mamba-flowhead.

title: Backtracking-based Error Correction for Ultra-short-term Power Forecasting of Wind Farm Cluster
authors: Jingwen Liu, Qingyi Liang, Yanxu Chen, Yangfan Xia, Yongning Zhao
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Existing methods for correcting wind power forecasting errors ignore the underlying mechanism that generate these errors, which is not conducive to achieving precise correction and model optimization. Especially in wind farm cluster forecasting, the difficulty of error correction is further exacerbated. Therefore, this paper uses the regression tree algorithm to trace the generation mechanism of the errors. By extracting the maximum error path through backtracking analysis, this path is introduced as a new structural feature into the prediction model for multi-round iterative optimization. Experiments show that this method improves the accuracy of power prediction in cluster fields by backtracking errors and enhancing the model’s ability to perceive areas with high errors.

title: Posit Arithmetic Multiplier for High-Speed and Low-Energy Applications
authors: Ramanamma Parepalli, Sudhakiran Ponnuru, Rahul, Yashas L, Shashank M Gundad, Rohit Varale
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This work presents a high-performance, low-energy posit arithmetic multiplier tailored for visual contrast enhancement and image processing applications, effectively balancing accuracy and energy efficiency. Conventional IEEE 754 floating-point multipliers are often inefficient for such workloads, as their fixed precision results in higher power consumption and limited adaptability to the irregular data patterns commonly encountered in vision applications. The posit number system offers a compelling alternative due to its flexible regime–exponent–mantissa representation, which adjusts precision based on input data and delivers greater accuracy with a wider dynamic range at the same bit width. To further improve energy efficiency, particularly when the mantissa width is small, we propose a modular multiplier architecture. In this design, mantissa multiplication is divided into smaller units that are selectively activated according to the regime width, ensuring that only the necessary hardware is engaged at any moment. Key optimizations include an adder-based leading-one detector to reduce delay and area, modified Booth encoding for efficient partial product generation, and refined control logic to lower dynamic power consumption. Experimental synthesis results indicate energy savings of 28–30% and delay reductions of up to 18% for Posit (8,0), Posit (16,1), and Posit (32,2). These gains highlight the suitability of the proposed multiplier for real-time embedded image processing in wearable and edge AI systems, where energy efficiency and computational precision must be carefully balanced.

title: Decision Making System for Landslide Monitoring
authors: K. Vanitha, Deepak S, Mathan J
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Landslides represent a critical natural hazard in mountainous and hilly terrains, frequently resulting in loss of life, damage to infrastructure, and disruption of essential services. Timely identification of unstable ground conditions is therefore essential for effective disaster mitigation. This work presents a low-cost and scalable IoT-enabled landslide monitoring and early warning framework designed for continuous real-time observation of environmental factors influencing slope stability. The proposed system integrates an Arduino-based sensing unit with soil moisture, vibration, rainfall, and gyroscopic sensors to capture dynamic variations in terrain conditions across landslide-prone zones. Sensor data are transmitted to a cloud platform, where machine learning models analyze temporal patterns and detect abnormal behaviors indicative of potential slope failure. Upon identifying risk conditions, the system immediately triggers local alerts through an on-site buzzer mechanism, enabling rapid awareness among nearby communities and authorities. Experimental deployment demonstrates the system's capability to track critical environmental changes and issue timely warnings under varying conditions. Owing to its modular architecture, affordability, and ease of deployment, the proposed solution can be adapted to different geographical regions, supporting proactive disaster preparedness and enhancing communitylevel safety.

title: ContrastFormer-NPI: A Sequence-Based Framework for NPIs Prediction via Representation-Based Feature Interaction
authors: Yifan Li, Wei Li, Peng Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Interactions between non-coding RNAs and proteins (NPIs) are pivotal in diverse biological processes and disease mechanisms. While computational methods utilizing molecular structure and graph topology are widely used for NPI prediction, they often struggle to achieve higher accuracy due to their dependence on prior knowledge and their inability to capture correlation information between NPI sequences. In this paper, we introduce a sequence-based NPI prediction model that integrates Longformer with contrastive learning. Our model utilizes Longformer to extract features from long biological sequences and employs the representation-based method for feature interaction. Furthermore, the InfoNCE loss is applied during training to enhance the quality of feature embeddings. Our model achieved accuracies of 88.7%, 98.0%, and 95.1% on the RPI488, RPI1807, and NPInter2.0 datasets, respectively. Experiments demonstrate that our model is comparable to or better than several state-of-the-art NPI prediction methods on both large-scale and small-scale datasets. This superior performance validates the effectiveness of our proposed model.

title: Sound From the Air by Optical Tracking in Real Time
authors: Mian Xing, Alexei Sourin
abstract: This paper presents a hand-tracking-based digital Theremin that integrates Unity3D real-time audio synthesis with Leap Motion Controller 2 optical tracking. The system maps 3D hand positions to core musical parameters, pitch and volume, while allowing real-time control of tone type, waveform blending, reverb, and octave through additional spatial dimensions. A modular architecture combines continuous hand-tracking input, visual feedback, and procedural audio processing within a responsive user interface. Visual guidance panels support intuitive learning by displaying real-time feedback for pitch and volume control. A user study with both musicians and non-musicians demonstrated that the system was learnable, expressive, and responsive, though limitations in tracking precision and sound smoothing were observed. A user evaluation indicates that the system provides responsive and expressive control, and is learnable for both musicians and non-musicians.

title: A Novel 1-Bit Adder Cell and Filters for Oversampling Superconducting ADCs
authors: Patricia Gonzalez-Guerrero, Panagiotis Zarkos, Carl Grace, George Michelogiannakis
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: One of the major engineering challenges that applications that rely on cryogenic refrigeration, e.g., superconducting sensors or quantum computing, face are complex readout schemes. Advances in superconducting classical computing attempt to address these challenges by enabling us to place all the services provided by room temperature electronics into the cryogenic environment. Superconducting ADCs are one of the key elements to make this possible. In this work, we propose a novel 1-bit adder cell and evaluate the building blocks for the digital signal processing necessary for superconducting ADCs. We find that our DSP blocks consume 50% less power and area than state-of-the-art.

title: Closed Loop Bridge Type DC-DC Converter with High Voltage Gain and Lower Switching Stress
authors: Abhishek Raj Aman, Sagun Kumar, Awadhesh Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This work present, a closed-loop bridge-type DCDC converter that achieve a high voltage gain and also minimizing voltage stress on power switches. For high-demand applications such as electric vehicle charging, renewable energy systems (solar power) and high-voltage DC systems (HVDC) this converter is designed. To ensure reliable and consistent operation, a feedback loop based on a proportional-integral (PI) controller is used to provide steady and reliable output performance. This controller dynamically readjusts the switching patterns in response to changes in input voltage. The result is that this system remains within the operating range and can balance out sudden changes in either input voltage or load demand with a stable output. The closed-loop control improves transient performance, reduces ripple, and alleviates voltage overshoot and current spikes. MATLAB Simulations confirm the converter ability to maintains a constant output voltage under sudden changes in load and input voltage across a range of test scenarios. The feedback mechanism maintains high efficiency, permits smooth regulation, and reduces current spikes. Thereby improving operational reliability and reducing stress on switches. Overall, the converter offers a robust, efficient, and safe solution for high-performance DC-DC power conversion.

title: Research on Photovoltaic Power Generation Prediction Model Based on Deep Learning
authors: Yuan Kai, Yi Le'an, Rao Shaozhou, Tang Lei, Yu Ying, Jin Jianguang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Under the background of rapid development of renewable energy, achieving high precision PV power forecast is key to ensure stable operation of power system. A new PV power forecast algorithm is proposed, which integrates improved transformer and spatiotemporal attention mechanism. By using adaptive location coding, it improves the multi-head attention mechanism so as to capture long sequence dependency. Combining temporal and temporal attention fusion module, the paper uses multiscale feature fusion layer to enrich feature expression. Experiments were carried out using real PV data sets. The results show that RMSE, mean absolute error (MAE), determination coefficient (R2) were used as evaluation indicators in comparison with ARIMA, LSTM, GRU and traditional transformer algorithm. In short term forecast, this algorithm has a RMSE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{0. 0 8 2}$</tex>, lower by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 8. 8 \%}$</tex> compared with best comparison algorithm; MAE is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.065,21.7 \%$</tex>; R2 reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.921,8.3 \%$</tex> higher. RMSE of 0.053, MAE of 0.041, R2 of 0.956 in ultra-short term prediction scenario are significantly better than that of comparing algorithms. In addition, under different weather conditions, this algorithm can maintain good prediction stability. It is proved that this algorithm is practical and innovative in PV power generation prediction.

title: A Closed-Loop Framework for Autonomous Vehicle Safety: Root Cause Analysis, Enhanced Sensor Fusion, and Simulation Validation
authors: Zhiyuan Wen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Ensuring the reliability of the perception-decision-making chain is crucial for autonomous vehicle safety. Current research often focuses on technical failures, lacking a systematic framework covering root cause analysis, solution design, and validation. To address this, we propose a closedloop framework integrating a three-layer (Technical-ProcessManagement) root cause model with an Enhanced Multisensor Redundancy System (EMSRS). EMSRS features a dynamic triggering mechanism that activates millimeter-wave radar under uncertainty, using a 2 -out-of-3 voting fusion logic and a dynamic safe distance model. Extensive simulations on CARLA show our approach outperforms baselines, with significant reductions in Risk Priority Number (from 180 to 65), pedestrian miss detection rate (33% decrease), and emergency braking events (58% reduction), while achieving 100% path planning success. Ablation studies confirm each component's contribution. This work offers a holistic solution for AV safety from risk identification to mitigation.

title: Performance Evaluation of Hybrid Machine Learning Models for Heart Disease Prediction in U.S. Clinical Decision Support Systems
authors: Ekramul Hasan, Mustafizur Rahaman, Dipta Paul, SK Rakib Ul Islam Rahat, Nurtaz Begum Asha, Md Al Amin
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Heart-disease continues to be a major contributor to illness and death across the United States. This is highlighting the importance of reliable early-prediction tools. That can aid clinicians in making informed decisions. In this work, researchers analyze the effectiveness of multiple hybrid machine learning approaches for predicting heart disease using a set of clinically meaningful attributes. The evaluated models include Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), Gradient Boosting Machine (GBM), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and k-Nearest Neighbor (KNN). To enhance model relevance, univariate feature selection was used to determine the most influential predictors. Model performance was measured using accuracy (ACC), precision (PRE), and F1-score. This experimental analysis indicates that the Random Forest algorithm consistently delivered the strongest performance. This is reaching an accuracy of 98.31% on the test set. In addition, limiting the model to the eight highest-ranking features increased interpretability while maintaining high predictive accuracy. Overall, the results suggest that Random Forest both alone and in combination with feature selection provides a robust and dependable framework for heart disease prediction in U.S. healthcare environments. And provide also supporting improvement in clinical decision-making and patient care.

title: Advancing Fake News Detection using Hybrid Deep Learning with FastText and Explainable AI
authors: L. Sri Lakshmi, B. Shynitha, A. Vaishnavi, N. Venkata Sireesha
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The dissemination of fake news across digital platforms has become a critical challenge, affecting public opinion, social stability, and decision-making processes. Various machine learning and deep learning approaches have been proposed in the detection of fake news, but most suffer from limited contextual understanding and interpretability, hindering their applicability in real-world applications. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning framework, CNN-LSTM, for effective capturing of both local linguistic patterns and longrange contextual dependencies in news articles. In addition, FastText word embeddings are used to enhance semantic robustness so that the model is capable of handling misspellings and rare word variations popularly encountered in deceptive content. Explainable AI is further implemented using LIME to obtain transparent and human-understandable justifications for model predictions. The proposed approach is evaluated on the WELFake dataset using standard metrics for classification. Experimental results depicted the hybrid model achieved a superior performance of 94.8 %, thus outperforming traditional and separate models of deep learning with a balanced precision and recall. The integration of explainability further ensures trustworthiness, making the proposed system suitable for deployment in high-stakes misinformation detection scenarios.

title: LFRNet: A Large Language Model-guided Feature Reweighting Network for Lightweight Intrusion Detection
authors: Dalong Zhang, Wenjie Tian, Zhiqing Tang, Yuncong Lu, Wenbo Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Intrusion detection systems in complex network traffic scenarios face critical limitations: conventional statistical methods fail to capture non-linear inter-feature dependencies of system-level features like session duration and TCP handshake delay, while existing learning-based approaches either lack interpretability for security auditing or incur high computational overhead, leading to an "explainability-adaptability trade-off" that undermines detection accuracy, generalization to emerging attacks, and decision traceability. To address these issues, this study proposes Large Language Model-guided Feature Reweighting Network (LFRNet), a lightweight IDS framework integrating data-driven statistical relevance estimation and Large Language Models (LLMs)-derived semantic knowledge. Its core design includes a statistical module quantifying feature-attack relevance via Mutual Information and Kullback–Leibler divergence to filter key features, a knowledge-guided encoder distilling LLM knowledge to assign interpretable semantic weights by correlating feature descriptions with attack-stage templates, and an adaptive fusion module synthesizing dual scores into ordered representations for sequential classification using compact GRU or Tiny-Transformer. Extensive experiments on the UNSW-NB15 dataset—with over 2.5 million traffic records and nine attack categories—show LFRNet outperforms state-of-the-art methods like NSGA2-LR, GA, and MI-Boruta in accuracy, F1-score, and false positive rate, while maintaining robust interpretability and efficiency under resource constraints, validating that LLM-guided system-level feature reweighting effectively enhances the resilience and explainability of cyber defense systems.

title: Prediction of Human Animal Conflict Through Machine Learning Based HAR Algorithm
authors: Preethi D, Valarmathi R S, Bhuvaneshwari V, Prithivirajan V, Gnanaprakash V, Vaishnavi P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Conflicts and interactions that arise when human wants and activities directly or indirectly collide with those of wild animals are referred to as human-animal conflicts. This conflict can occur in a variety of locations, including rural, urban, and natural settings, and it can take many different forms. Crop raiding, livestock predation, human attacks, and disease transfer are a few frequent interactions with animals. Therefore, it is crucial to keep an eye on animal behaviour and movement patterns, undertake study on it, and be aware of better management techniques and early conflict detection systems. Machine learning algorithms are used to observe and prevent the hunting of endangered species for purposes other than self-defence because it has been seen that human attacks on these animals are growing. The human action recognition (HAR) refers to the comprehension of actions made by a human in a video. Actions modelling is mapping an action to a label that clearly defines a particular occurrence of that activity from various angles and at various speeds. In addition, there are a number of difficulties with newline completely automated HAR systems, including background clutter, newline occlusion, scale, and look. Cost-effective equipment makes it simpler to collect 3D data. As a result, numerous objects and people in a scene can provide depth information in the form of depth pictures. An updated learning method called Self-newline adaptable Differential Evolution-Extreme Machine Learning (SaD-EELM) for action classification is used to overcome problems and challenges of HAR, such as background clutter. Single Layer Feed- forward Network (SLFN) with Depth Motion Maps-Local Newline Binary Pattern (DMM-LBP) is used to improve action classification utilizing depth-based input. Furthermore, newline misclassification is decreased by using the Kernel-based Extreme Learning Machine (KELM) classifier.

title: Calibration of an Omnidirectional Vision System and Obstacle Distance Measurements
authors: Ivan Yu. Kholodilin, Yuilya S. Kuznetsova, Kristina M. Saranceva, Ksenia A. Egorova, Tao Zhou, Zihan Zhang
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: This paper introduces an open-source, Python-native framework for fisheye camera calibration and single-image obstacle distance estimation using camera and laser emitter. The pipeline performs intrinsic calibration of a fisheye camera, combines robust laser-stripe detection with sub-pixel centerline extraction, and computes geometrically metric ranges from a single omnidirectional capture. Our primary contribution is replacing a prior MATLAB pipeline with a transparent, reproducible Python implementation that supports widely adopted open-source libraries. This shift improves accessibility and portability across platforms. We evaluate the system on both synthetic and real datasets under diverse scenes and illumination conditions, demonstrating accurate calibration and reliable single-shot ranging. The full codebase, experiment scripts, and comprehensive documentation are publicly available. Together, these contributions provide a flexible, reproducible, and readily deployable platform for omnidirectional perception with structured laser illumination. The paper code and supporting materials are available online: https://github.com/kholodilinivan/CVSystemPython.

title: Image-Based Anomaly Detection for Industrial Applications
authors: C Muruganantham, Narasimhan K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Ensuring consistent product quality in industrial manufacturing requires precise detection of visual anomalies. Traditional manual inspection is time consuming, error-prone and unsuitable for large-scale operations, while existing automated methods often struggle with robustness, generalization, and computational efficiency. This study systematically evaluates four state-of-the-art deep learning model Patch Distribution Modeling (PaDiM), PatchCore, Reverse Distillation, and Reverse Distillation++, for image-based anomaly detection using the MVTec Anomaly Detection (MVTec-AD) dataset comprising 5,354 high-resolution industrial images. To simulate practical deployment scenarios, models were trained under limited computational resources using transfer learning with pretrained convolutional backbones. Performance was assessed using the Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (AUROC) metric, achieving average scores between 0.93 and 0.99, with Reverse Distillation++ consistently outperforming others in both detection accuracy and localization precision. These findings highlight the effectiveness of advanced deep learning-based anomaly detection frameworks in enhancing industrial quality control, enabling predictive maintenance, and minimizing production costs. The study further establishes a foundation for future research in multimodal fusion, real-time inspection, and lightweight model optimization for edge deployment.

title: Evolutionary AI-Driven Android Malware Detection Using Genetic Algorithm-Optimized Feature Selection and Hybrid Machine Learning
authors: M. Sri Lakshmi, Mallireddy Varun Kumar Reddy, Pathan Moiz Ather Khan, Shaik Mohammed Feroz
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The explosion in growth of Android malware has driven the need for accurate, efficient and interpretable Android malware detection frameworks. This paper introduces EvoMal-Net, an evolutionary artificial intelligence-driven Android malware detection system that combines the genetic algorithm (GA)-based optimization with hybrid ensemble learning. The proposed framework uses a two-stage GA process where optimal feature subsets are determined in the first stage followed by optimal ensemble voting weight optimization in the second stage to get maximum classification performance. EvoMal-Net integrates Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) and Deep Neural Network (DNN) classifiers and adds a SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) based explainability layer to the model for improving model transparency. Features extracted from static, dynamic, and contextual data sources are fused to increase the robustness of the detection. Experimental evaluation using a synthetically constructed dataset of Android malware shows that EvoMal-Net achieves 96% accuracy with a 0.95 F1-score and a 0.97 AUC while having a low false positive rate and outperforms conventional baseline models. The results confirm the effectiveness of evolutionary optimization and hybrid learning in enhancing the detection of Android malware with enhanced interpretability.

title: Dual-Discriminator Adversarial Learning for 2D-to-3D Motion Style Transfer with Remote Information Sharing and Collaboration
authors: Xu Yaoyao
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This study proposes a 2D to 3D motion style transfer framework based on dual-discriminator adversarial learning. It also combines the remote information sharing and collaborative training mechanisms as the communication optimization to improve cross-node consistency. The proposed method introduces topological regularization on the generator side and, at the algorithmic level, constrains local and global geometric consistency on high-dimensional motion manifolds through Chern-Simons functionals. The discriminator side features a dual-discriminator structure to record global motion style information and local joint motion features, respectively. Additionally, this model presents a geometric field averaging remote collaboration technique for multi-node distributed training settings. Effective consistency optimization and crossnode feature sharing are made possible by this tactic. The experimental design of this work made use of a multi-source motion capture dataset from several labs. The information includes 3D motion annotations for 2D skeleton video clips. Comparative experiments included direct regression (DR), single-discriminator generative adversarial network (SD-GAN), and federated averaging (FedAvg) methods. Experimental results show that the proposed Dual+Remote method achieves the lowest motion reconstruction accuracy (MPJPE) of 18.6, the lowest degree of naturalness of motion generation (FID-M) of 12.4, a smoothness of 0.72, and a motion energy deviation of only 3.1. Furthermore, in the style consistency (SCI) test, the proposed model achieved scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 8 1}-\mathbf{0. 8 5}$</tex>. In the remote collaboration experiment, communication overhead was reduced by 92% and it achieves a collaboration efficiency (CE) of 0.61. At the algorithm training level, only 101 training epochs were required, and crossnode consistency reached 0.81.

title: Multi-Temporal Machine Learning-Based Land Use and Land Cover Change Analysis Using Landsat Imagery (1994-2024)
authors: Manasa Kolusu, Suneetha Manne, Sreekar Movva
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The temporal and spatial effects of urbanization, industrialization and agricultural expansions on the ecosystems of the regions can only be understood by monitoring Land Use and Land Cover (LULC) changes. This paper examines the three decades of LULC changes between 1994 and 2024 in Tamil nadu, India, based on the multi-temporal Landsat imagery and machine learning on the Google Earth engine platform. Cloud masking, atmospheric correction and clipping to state boundary were done in order to make Landsat 5, 7 and 8 datasets consistent. Two supervised algorithms Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Random Forest (RF) were used to create the classified LULC maps that identified the six categories of the land surface which included Water, Forest, Built-up, Agriculture, Fallow Land and Mines. Comparative accuracy Testing showed that the RF classifier had a greater overall accuracy and kappa value than that of SVM especially in spectrally mixed areas. As indicated by the time analysis, the build-up and fallow land areas have increased significantly with a sharp reduction in the forest and agricultural lands. These results indicate the high rate of urbanization and resource overexploitation in Tamil Nadu with the necessity to apply sustainable land use and environmental planning based on evidence-based data.

title: Design and Simulation of the Metamaterial Based Antenna Array to Reduce Mutual Coupling
authors: Shruthi M, Rajini V Honnungar, Deekshitha S, Harshitha MD, Roopa N, Shwetha C
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: In this paper, a systematic procedure is presented to streamline and simplify the design of metamaterial structures using Ansys HFSS simulation tools. A Mutual coupling is a critical limitation in closely spaced micro strip antenna arrays, particularly when the inter element distance is reduced below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5 \lambda$</tex>. Such coupling degrades return loss, gain and affecting overall system performance. In this work a single 10 GHz micro strip patch antenna is designed and extend into a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times 2$</tex> array to study coupling behavior under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda / 2$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<\lambda / 2$</tex> spacing conditions. To mitigate this, Split Ring Resonator metamaterial unit cell is developed and arranged into a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 6$</tex> periodic structure. The metamaterial exhibits negative permittivity and permeability, verified through S-parameter retrieval, enabling suppression of surface-wave propagation. When integrated between array elements, enhancing return loss to −36 dB while maintaining stable gain around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 7.7 \text{dB}$</tex>. the proposed metamaterial-assisted array provides and effective and compact solution for mutual coupling reduction in high frequency antenna system.

title: Novel Energy-Centric Fossa Optimization-Based Energy-Aware Clustering and Routing in IoTenabled Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: T. Kiruthika, E. Karthikeyan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The rapid expansion of Internet of Things (IoT)enabled Wireless Sensor Networks (WSN) demands efficient energy utilization and delivery of data in constrained resources. Traditional cluster-based routing schemes, such as LEACH and HEED, often suffer from unbalanced energy consumption (EC) and unstable routing due to static cluster formation and inefficient cluster-head (CH) selection. To overcome the challenges, this paper develops a Novel Energy-Centric Fossa Optimization (NECFO)-Based Clustering and Routing framework for IoT-WSN. The NECFO method used a lightweight Energy-Distance-based clustering mechanism to balance node energy and proximity to the base station (BS) for optimal cluster formation. After clustering, the Fossa Optimization Algorithm (FOA) is applied to fine-tune the cluster heads selection by evaluating factors, such as residual energy, intra-cluster distance and communication overhead, and to find the efficient multi-hop paths between CH and the sink. The NECFO decreased transmission EC, routing delays and network lifetime. Simulation results show that NECFO provides better energy efficiency, higher packet delivery rates and increased network stability compared with traditional methods and FOA-only approaches. These make it a strong candidate for large-scale IoT monitoring systems.

title: Balancing Transparency and Privacy with Explainable AI and Federated Learning in Fraud Detection
authors: P. Phanindra Kumar Reddy, Fahimuddin Shaik, T. Pavani, B. Vijaya Durga, M. Sai, B. Sai Gowtham Reddy
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The recenx't approaches supplemented with explainable AI (XAI) and Federated Learning (FL) include transparency and privacy particularly to the high-stakes financial fraud detection. In our project, we are working with the Paysim1 data of Kaggle in order to prepare a model that will not only be in a position to predict the accuracy of the bank transactions successfully, but also be generalized to identify the presence of a fraudulent transaction with the high degree of accuracy. Though the ensuing outcomes of the conventional fraud detecting models such as Deep Neural Networks (DNN), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN), Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) are handy, they may be considered as problematic in terms of interpretability and privacy since they assume a centralized data processing. To address these weaknesses, the alternative machine learning models will be utilized in the proposed system, which are more understandable, and can address more intricate patterns of fraud the Decision Trees, Random Forests and Gradient Boosting Machines (GBMs). Second, Federated Learning (FL) is also applied to ensure that the models would be trained locally without the need to transmit sensitive data to comply with privacy. Not only will this ensure privacy of the information of the users, but also achieve collaborative learning that can be engaged further to perfect the model of detecting the fraud. The project will additionally be useful in creating a more ethical and effective version of financial fraud identification, the precision of the model with regards to privacy and transparency which incorporates XAI and F.

title: Fluid Aerial Networks: UAV Rotation for Inter-Cell Interference Mitigation
authors: Enzhi Zhou, Yue Xiao, Ziyue Liu, Sotiris A. Tegos, Panagiotis D. Diamantoulakis, George K. Karagiannidis
abstract: With the rapid development of aerial infrastructure, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) that function as aerial base stations (ABSs) extend terrestrial network services into the sky, enabling on-demand connectivity and enhancing emergency communication capabilities in cellular networks by leveraging the flexibility and mobility of UAVs. In such a UAV-assisted network, this paper investigates position-based beamforming between ABSs and ground users (GUs). To mitigate inter-cell interference, we propose a novel fluid aerial network that leverages ABS rotation to increase multi-cell capacity and overall network efficiency. Specifically, in the line-of-sight channel model, the spatial beamforming weights are determined by the positions of GUs. In this direction, we examine the beamforming gain of a two-dimensional multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) array at various ground positions, revealing that ABS rotation significantly affects multi-user channel correlation and inter-cell interference. Based on these findings, we propose an alternative low-complexity algorithm to design the optimal rotation angle for ABSs, aiming to reduce inter-cell interference and thus maximize the sum rate of multi-cell systems. In simulations, exhaustive search serves as a benchmark to validate the optimization performance of the proposed sequential ABS rotation scheme. Moreover, simulation results demonstrate that, in interference-limited regions, the proposed ABS rotation paradigm can significantly reduce inter-cell interference in terrestrial networks and improve the multi-cell sum rate by approximately 10% compared to fixed-direction ABSs without rotation.

title: Silicon Controlled Rectifier (SCR)-Embedded Identification of Field Winding, Damper Bar, and Eccentricity Faults in Wound Field Synchronous Machines
authors: Sungwoo Choe, Muhammad Faizan Shaikh, Namhyuk Byun, Kyunghoon Kim, Kyeongyeol Kim, Anyuan Chen, Arne Nysveen, Hossein Ehya, Sang Bin Lee
abstract: It is crucial to detect faults in the rotor of wound field synchronous machines (WFSM) such as shorted field winding turns, broken damper bars, and rotor eccentricity, before they escalate into failures that cause forced outage of the machine. The off-line test methods available for detection of these faults require machine disassembly and/or physical access to the machine, which lead to high maintenance costs. On-line monitoring options require installation of sensors on the stator bore or on the machine frame and are not sensitive to the above-mentioned rotor faults. In this paper, a new test concept for remote and automated detection of WFSM rotor faults is proposed as a solution. The silicon-controlled rectifier (SCR) of the static field excitation system is used for injecting test signals into the rotor winding for testing, whenever the WFSM is de-energized. It is shown that the equivalent rotor impedance at 6 times the 50/60 Hz supply frequency at varying saturation levels of the core can be used for reliable detection and classification of shorted field winding turns, broken damper bars, and eccentricity. An experimental study on 20, 30, and 100 kVA WFSMs under the 3 fault conditions is given to validate the effectiveness of the proposed test method.

title: IEEE Dataport
authors: N/A

title: Open-Circuit Fault Diagnosis for Power Converters Under Variable Operational Conditions: From a Source-Free Domain Adaptation Perspective
authors: Jiusi Zhang, Kai Chen, Fan Wu, Gen Qiu, Yuhua Cheng, Shen Yin
abstract: As the core component of modern power electronic systems, power converters have widespread applications in renewable energy generation, smart grids, and aerospace. The open-circuit fault of power converters is highly concealed and easily derived from secondary faults, whose diagnosis technology has become a key research direction to ensure safe operation. Considering that power converters usually need to operate under different load conditions and complex environments, it is of great significance to achieve efficient open-circuit fault diagnosis while protecting data privacy. Therefore, this article proposes an open-circuit fault diagnosis approach for power converters from the perspective of source-free domain adaptation. In detail, this article proposes an attention-guided power converter fault diagnosis model to enhance the ability to capture key features. Without the need for source domain data to participate in joint training, this article proposes a dynamic clustering loss to achieve pseudolabel generation and iterative self-training, so that the model can make full use of target domain data. Furthermore, this article proposes uncertainty entropy loss and diversity entropy loss to decrease the uncertainty of fault diagnosis output while preventing the model from being over-confident. To demonstrate the reliability of the open-circuit fault diagnosis methodology, this research implements a functional power conversion system.

title: Time Synchronized Deterministic 6G Middleware Supporting AI Driven English Education in Cyber Physical Systems
authors: Xiaohui Zhang, Jia Guo
abstract: The combination of artificial intelligence (AI), cyber-physical systems (CPS), and next-generation wireless communication is changing digital education, especially in language learning environments that need real-time interaction and feedback that changes based on it the learner is demonstrating. AI-powered English language learning platforms are increasingly using multimodal data streams, such as speech, gestures, eye gaze, and learner engagement metrics. These streams need precise timing, low latency, and communication guarantees that are constantly identical. But most middleware solutions lack the rigorous time synchronization and dependability that these interactive, CPS-enabled educational systems require. This study develops up a Time-Synchronized Deterministic 6G Middleware architecture intended to facilitate AI-driven English instruction within cyber-physical systems. This study develops a Time-Synchronized Deterministic 6G Middleware (TSD-6G-EDU) architecture intended to facilitate AI-driven English instruction within cyber-physical systems. The proposed middleware uses 6G-native features like ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC), sub-microsecond time synchronization, and edge intelligence to make it easy for physical learning devices to work with AI-based educational services. A deterministic scheduling method guarantees limited latency and jitter for essential learning interactions, including real-time voice evaluation, pronunciation correction, and adaptive tutoring feedback. The middleware uses AI to help manage resources and time-aware message orchestration to dynamically allocate network, computation, and sensor resources based on the learner’s context. The system keeps digital copies of learners and instructional processes in sync, which makes it possible to have consistent perception–action phases that are necessary for individualized and immersive English learning experiences. Performance assessments show that the proposed middleware greatly lowers end-to-end latency variance and improves synchronization accuracy compared to other middleware methods. The findings demonstrate that time-synchronized deterministic 6G middleware establishes an effective structure for next-generation AI-enhanced English teaching, adaptive, and context-sensitive learning within cyber-physical educational environments.

title: Reflections on the 25th Anniversary of the IEEE Circuits and Systems Magazine [Editorial]
authors: Keshab K. Parhi

title: EdgeGuard: Blockchain-Enhanced Secure Data Circulation via Aggregatable Distributed Key Generation
authors: Boyang Liao, Jianwei Liu, Xinxin Xing, Jian Cui, Qianhong Wu, Willy Susilo, Robert H. Deng, Yizhong Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Data has become a critical driver of innovation in artificial intelligence and the evolution of 6G technologies. The explosive growth of data volume accelerates the convergence of cloud and edge computing, while simultaneously posing heightened challenges to data security and privacy. The emerging cloud-edge-device collaborative paradigm enables dynamic and large-scale data circulation across heterogeneous entities, exposing systems to complex threats such as malicious edge nodes and eavesdropping over untrusted communication channels. In response to these issues, we propose EdgeGuard, a secure and decentralized framework for cloud-edge-device data circulation. EdgeGuard is specifically designed for highly dynamic environments and ensures robust data confidentiality, integrity, traceability, and resilience against both malicious external attackers and compromised edge servers. To underpin its cryptographic foundation, we develop two core primitives. Specifically, we introduce an Aggregatable Publicly Verifiable Secret Sharing (APVSS) scheme that enables efficient sharing of field elements while supporting aggregation and public verifiability. Furthermore, we construct AggDKG, a distributed key generation (DKG) protocol. AggDKG achieves public verifiability and bias resistance with an expected total communication cost of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\mathcal {O}}(\kappa n^{3})$</tex-math></inline-formula> effectively overcoming the scalability limitations inherent in traditional complaint-based protocols. Collectively, these components form a comprehensive framework that strengthens secure and efficient data circulation in cloud-edge-device systems. Experimental data show that AggDKG delivers clear performance gains: across all tested scales, its total running time is only about 8%–65% of that of the DKG of Gurkan et al., and at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n=256$</tex-math></inline-formula>, it reduces per-node runtime by approximately 27% compared with the DKG of Gennaro et al. These results highlight EdgeGuard's superior scalability, lower latency, and stronger Byzantine resilience for secure large-scale deployments.

title: Development and Modeling of a Soft Array Actuator for Elderly Assistive Robotic Exoskeleton
authors: Yinan Jin, Yixiao Zheng, Junhao Lin, Zetong Li, Shibo Cai, Guanjun Bao, Prashant Jamwal, Shahid Hussain
abstract: The global elderly population is increasing rapidly, and more and more countries are stepping into the aging society. Because of deterioration in gait-related functions, such as muscle activity, most elderly people have difficulty walking, which is one of the most basic activities of daily living. A wearable lower limb robot exoskeleton offers a viable solution to this issue. Rigid exoskeletons usually have fewer degrees of freedom and require a quite big power supply for actuation, resulting in difficulties during wearing and motion. Soft exoskeletons could be an alternative choice for elderly assistance, due to their advantages of lighter weight, simpler mechanism, and compliance. Therefore, this research proposed a soft array actuator for a lower limb assistive exoskeleton for elderly people. The fully compliant actuation mechanism was designed using flexible Thermoplastic Polyurethane fabric to power the soft exoskeleton, making it compact, light, and comfortable to wear. The proposed array actuator was modeled on kinematics, contacting force, output torque, and dynamics. Mechanical performance of the compliant actuation mechanism during inflation/deflation at different air pressures and during static force testing has been evaluated. The internal air pressure of the soft array actuator reaches 100 kPa in 0.2 s and fully deflates in 0.48 s. It can generate 53.7–55.7 N to output 8.1–11.2 N·m of torque at the endpoint, which is 13.5% –18.7% of the maximum human hip joint torque during natural walking. The proposed soft actuation mechanism, with good wearability and performance, could be a good fit for a robot exoskeleton that improves natural gait and reduces human metabolic consumption during walking among the elderly. Its lightweight design, good invisibility, and high power-to-weight ratio enable the proposed soft array actuator to provide compliant, continuous, and consistent gait assistance without imposing a psychological burden on the elderly.

title: Focus on Finding Deepfakes: A Robust Proactive Detection Method Based on Orthogonal Moment Watermarking
authors: Chunpeng Wang, Wenlong Ma, Shanshan Zhang, Jie Gui, Qi Li, Yunan Liu, Zhiqiu Xia
abstract: Deepfake detection remains a challenging research topic, especially when the quality of forged images degrades, leading to unreliable detection results. In this paper, we propose a watermarking-based proactive method for robust proactive deepfake detection. First, we embed a watermark into the Fractional-order Quaternion Exponent Moments (FrQEMs) space of the host face image, achieving a balance between imperceptibility and robustness of the watermarking algorithm. Then, we introduce the Frequency Mamba (FreMamba) block to enhance feature extraction by leveraging correlations between frequency-domain subbands, thereby enabling the extraction of more discriminative feature representations. Finally, at the detection stage, we construct a dual-branch framework comprising a watermark extractor and a forgery discriminator. Through knowledge distillation, the watermark extractor guides the forgery discriminator to perceive forgery traces. Specifically, the integrity of the extracted watermark is compromised only when the host image is subjected to a deepfake attack, while conventional attacks do not affect the integrity. Experimental results on benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior deepfake detection accuracy. In particular, when images are subjected to conventional attacks, our method surpasses state-of-the-art approaches by more than 5.3% in terms of ACC.

title: Towards an Oracle for Binary Decomposition Under Compilation Variance
authors: Ang Jia, He Jiang, Zhilei Ren, Xiaochen Li, Zhipeng Yang, Yaxin Duan, Ming Fan, Ting Liu
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: Efficient LLM Adaptation to Low-Resource Languages via Cross-Lingual Semantic Anchoring
authors: Bekhzod Shukhratov, Shokhrukh Baydadaev, Jang Woo Kwon
abstract: Most Large Language Models (LLMs) remain heavily English-centric, resulting in high tokenizer fertility, where a single word in a low-resource language decomposes into multiple sub-tokens. This inefficiency inflates computational costs and weakens adaptation performance. We introduce bilingual exchange for optimized dense (BXOD) embeddings, a multi-stage framework for efficient cross-lingual adaptation based on cross-lingual semantic anchoring. BXOD employs a cold-start bilingual embedding initialization that anchors new vocabularies for low-resource languages within the semantic space of a high-resource model, guided by the multilingual LaBSE encoder. The pipeline expands the tokenizer to reduce fertility and initializes new embeddings through semantic alignment rather than sub-token composition. BXOD was evaluated using Llama 3.2 (1B, 3B) models adapted to Uzbek and Malay, and Gemma 2 (2B) models adapted to Uzbek, Malay, Korean, and Spanish. It achieved significant gains in low-resource settings, such as improving Malay news classification accuracy from 29.95% to 50.35% (+68.1%, p < 0.001) and English-to-Uzbek translation BLEU from 3.22 to 4.74 (+47.2%, p < 0.001), while preserving English measuring massive multitask language understanding (MMLU) performance and accelerating convergence. Results highlight BXOD’s strength for typologically distant, low-resource languages, while showing reduced benefits for lexically similar, high-resource languages such as Spanish. BXOD thus establishes an effective and computationally efficient paradigm for extending LLMs across linguistically diverse settings.

title: DMPT: An efficient Dual-Mamba Framework for Pedestrian Trajectory Prediction
authors: Kai Wu, Shiyi Tang, Haoyi Zhang, Yong Zhong, Weihua Li
abstract: Accurate and efficient pedestrian trajectory prediction is crucial for autonomous driving system, enabling safe and timely decision-making in complex urban environments. Despite much progress made in deep learning models, existing methods still struggle to simultaneously model intricate spatiotemporal interactions and inherent uncertainty in future trajectories, while maintaining computational efficiency. To address these limitations, we propose an efficient Dual-Mamba framework (DMPT), integrating intrinsic motion modeling and extrinsic social interactions into a unified encoder-decoder Mamba-based architecture. Specifically, we construct motion hypotheses for representative motion patterns from observed trajectories and employ a bidirectional Motion-Mamba encoder (BiMME) with learnable positional embeddings to thoroughly capture inter-pattern dependencies in both forward and backward directions. A classification head is then used to produce probabilities over motion patterns for selection and multimodal prediction. Conditioned on the selected motion hypotheses, a social Cross-Mamba decoder (SCroMD) incorporates neighbor information via a cross-selective scan mechanism, where a neighbor-dependent system matrix is aggregated with distance-based weights and used to decode the system states within the target motion hypotheses. Finally, a regression head generates multiple socially plausible future trajectories, each with a corresponding probability. This dual-head prediction allows the model not only to forecast diverse possible futures but also to provide an estimation for each prediction. Extensive experiments on three datasets demonstrate that our method has the best average minADE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">20</sub>/minFDE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">20</sub> of 0.19/0.30 on ETH/UCY and the lowest ADE of 6.72 on SDD, surpassing existing baseline models. Our method also demonstrates superior computational efficiency, achieving the highest inference speed while maintaining the lowest FLOPs of 1.11G and a competitive parameter size of 0.12M. This optimal balance between prediction accuracy, computation, and memory efficiency ensures reliable real-time trajectory prediction in autonomous driving.

title: AMTFusion: boosting 3D object detection by adaptive multi-modal temporal fusion and augmentation
authors: Wei Tian, Zhenglin Du, Xiaolong Zhao, Qiankun Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Accurate perception of surrounding 3D objects is indispensable for ensuring the safety of autonomous driving systems. Furthermore, the integration of multi-modal sensors has emerged as a pivotal research area within detection paradigms based on Bird’s Eye View. In this paper, we introduce a sparse query-based 3D object detection framework by adaptive multi-modal temporal fusion of camera and LiDAR sensor data. Initially, we leverage the spatial-aware cross-modal feature aggregation to adaptively fuse instance-level point cloud and image features. We further elaborate a sequential temporal modeling approach, which facilitates an enhanced multi-modal 3D object detection within constant spatial and temporal complexity. To mitigate the challenge posed by limited high-quality training data, we incorporate a fragment-wise multi-modal temporal data augmentation strategy, aimed at bolstering the model’s performance for detecting long-tail distributed objects. Our approach achieves an NDS detection score of 75.5% on the nuScenes test set (without test time augmentation and model ensembling). These results underscore the efficacy of our approach and highlight its superiority compared to prior state-of-the-art methods. We further show that our approach remarkably improves the accuracy of multi-object tracking on nuScenes dataset.

title: HGMRec: A Hypergraph-Based Medical Knowledge-Enhanced Literature Recommendation Model
authors: Hao Ding, Weiwei Zhu, Yuanyuan Shu
