abstract: The rapid expansion of medical literature has intensified the challenge of providing researchers and clinicians with accurate and personalized knowledge recommendations. This paper introduces HGMRec, a hypergraph- and medical knowledge graph-enhanced recommendation model designed for heterogeneous and multimodal medical information. The model leverages UMLS (Unified Medical Language System)-based entity recognition and TransE embeddings to inject domain-specific knowledge, and constructs a heterogeneous hypergraph that jointly represents literature, entities, and images. A hypergraph attention mechanism captures higher-order relationships, while a contrastive alignment module ensures semantic consistency across modalities, and a user-side dynamic attention network adapts to evolving user interests. Experiments on two real-world datasets demonstrate that HGMRec significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. HGMRec achieved an NDCG@10 of 0.442, representing a 4.25% improvement over the strongest baseline, and an F1@10 of 0.402, surpassing others by at least 4.7%.

title: Dual-Verifiable Federated Learning with Vector Commitments Against Collusion Attacks_supp1-3674195.pdf
authors: Xia Feng
abstract: Collusion attacks, where the server and malicious clients collaborate to bypass gradient source confirmation or tamper with aggregation results, cause a fundamental damage to the training process in federated learning (FL). However, existing verifiable FL frameworks typically adopt a split-verification model-clients can independently validate the correctness of aggregation results, while the server is responsible for confirming the legitimacy of gradient sources. Thus, the collusion attack has emerged as a critical and intractable vulnerability, as it completely collapses this split-verification model, thereby invalidating such verification mechanisms. To tackle this fundamental issue, we propose an innovative dual-verifiable FL framework. Specifically, by leveraging vector commitments, our scheme first integrates both gradient source confirmation and aggregation result verification into a unified framework. Based on this unified design, our scheme implements two targeted strategies to defend against collusion attacks. To prevent collusion-enabled gradient source spoofing, our scheme introduces a semi-trusted verification cluster in place of unreliable server-side validation and embeds an anonymized identity-check strategy to collaboratively confirm gradient source legitimacy. To counter collusion-driven manipulation of gradient aggregation results, our scheme customizes auxiliary verification proofs with a computational one-wayness for client-uploaded gradients. This renders it infeasible for adversaries to tamper with the aggregation result through reverse engineering. Under experiments and security analyses, our scheme achieves reliable dual-verification and robust resistance to collusion attacks. Moreover, it reduces computation and communication overhead by at least 40.83% and 50.47%, respectively, compared to state-of-the-art verifiable FL schemes.

title: 3DVidar: A Single Mmwave Radar Based 3D Vibration Sensing Method Via Multi-Point Multi-Path Multi-Antenna Enhancement
authors: Yulong Zhang, Xuanheng Li, Yi Sun
abstract: Vibration sensing is crucial for machinery health monitoring, but traditional contact sensors face deployment challenges. Recently, millimeter wave (mmWave) radar has emerged as a promising contact-free alternative. However, since radar is mainly sensitive to the vibrations perpendicular to its antennas, existing works can only achieve 1D/2D vibration sensing based on single radar, or 3D sensing by multiple ones. In this paper, we propose 3DVidar, a single-radar 3D vibration sensing system without external reference target. Considering the insufficient information provided by single radar, we introduce a multi-point multi-path multi-antenna signal enhancement strategy to compensate for the lack of 3D vibration information. Furthermore, we develop two dedicated mechanisms to selectively filter the most informative radar signals for subsequent processing. Based on the enhanced signals, we design 3D-VRNet, a deep learning framework that incorporates positional priors and fuses multi-view signals through multi-scale convolutions and an attention mechanism. We implement 3DVidar on a commercial mmWave radar, and the results on two type of vibration targets show that it can accurately reconstruct 3D trajectory across various conditions.

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)

title: A Reinforcement Learning Control Strategy for Non-affine Systems
authors: Xinran Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: To address the challenges of modeling uncertainties and mismatched disturbances in non-affine nonlinear systems, a reinforcement learning-based adaptive tracking control method is proposed. First, an auxiliary integral system is constructed to transform the original system into an affine form, overcoming the design difficulties posed by the non-affine structure. On this basis, a reinforcement learning network is introduced to estimate and compensate for uncertain nonlinearities in the system online, while a disturbance estimator is integrated to suppress the effects of mismatched disturbances. Furthermore, by combining reinforcement learning, disturbance estimation, and adaptive backstepping control, an intelligent adaptive controller is designed. Lyapunov stability analysis demonstrates that all signals in the closed-loop system are bounded and the tracking error converges. Numerical simulations verify the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method.

title: Detecting Sign Language Using LSTM-Based Algorithm for Inclusive Communication
authors: Vishal Singh Khati, Gaurav Sharma, Pawan Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This study presents an Indian Sign Language (ISL)-specific real-time sign language recognition system. The system uses OpenCV for skin segmentation, focusing on hand motions, to find and monitor Zones of Interest (ZOI). Hand landmarks are recorded using Media Pipe, and their important points are kept in a NumPy array. Model training is facilitated using TensorFlow, Keras, and LSTM. A live webcam feed is used for evaluating the model in real time. This creative method offers a way to communicate with the outside world, which is promising for the deaf and dumb community. Accuracy, precision, and recall metrics are used to assess the system’s efficacy using a dataset of frequently used Indian sign language motions. The LSTM model obtains a perfect accuracy of 1. The output emphasizes the usefulness of the technology by providing both text and audio representations of the identified sign gestures.

title: Reliability Evaluation Method for Data Flow Control in Substation Interval Renovation Based on Multi-agent Algorithm
authors: Ning Chen, XiaoYong Sun, HongBin Wang, Hang Lv, Yu Yan, Bin Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: In digital distribution substations, the real-time sharing and fusion of multi-bay data can effectively improve the protection, measurement and control performance of the substation. Firstly, it is necessary to address the high real-time performance, stability and reliability of cross-bay data exchange in the substation process bus. From the perspective of the data sharing requirements of relay protection in distribution substations, this paper evaluates the communication characteristics when setting the process bus to transmit SAV and GOOSE messages in a “bay-oriented” and “location-oriented” manner, and analyzes and discusses the problems existing in “function-oriented” process bus communication settings as well as their solutions. The research shows that when the network traffic is within the operating range and the message length of each bay is the same, the maximum number of bays capable of data sharing is approximately inversely proportional to the signal sampling rate of a single bay; under a 100M network, the process layer networking modes oriented to bays and locations can basically meet the data sharing needs with a sampling rate below 400 points per cycle, and the main delay and data sharing limitations stem from the performance characteristics of switches.

title: VOAPI2: a Vulnerability-Oriented Testing Framework for RESTful APIs
authors: Lübin Xu, Zhuolin Zeng, Zhen Tang, Biao Xiao, Dong Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the rapid proliferation of Web services, RESTful APIs have become a fundamental building block of modern software systems. As APIs increasingly expose critical business logic and sensitive data, ensuring their security has emerged as a pressing challenge. However, most existing RESTful API testing tools primarily emphasize functional correctness and robustness, and thus often fail to effectively uncover security vulnerabilities that do not manifest as obvious crashes or server errors.In this paper, we present VOAPI2, a vulnerability-oriented inspection framework for RESTful APIs that explicitly targets security flaws by leveraging the intrinsic relationship between API functionality and vulnerability types [1]. Unlike conventional API fuzzers that explore endpoints indiscriminately, VOAPI2 adopts a guided testing strategy that first identifies security-critical endpoints by analyzing semantic cues in API specifications. Based on these cues, the framework generates vulnerability-specific attack payloads and embeds them into state-aware request sequences that respect API execution dependencies.To accurately determine whether a vulnerability is successfully triggered, VOAPI2 further incorporates a feedback-driven verification mechanism that analyzes API responses and observable side effects rather than relying solely on generic server error codes. We implement a prototype of VOAPI2 on top of an existing stateful API testing engine and conduct a preliminary evaluation on real-world APIs. The results demonstrate that VOAPI2 is capable of discovering security vulnerabilities that are missed by state-of-the-art API testing tools, including previously unknown security issues that were later confirmed by developers. These findings indicate that vulnerability-oriented testing is a practical and effective direction for improving the coverage and efficiency of RESTful API security testing.

title: RFETR: Residual Frequency-Enhanced Two-stage Reconstruction for Time Series Anomaly Detection
authors: Shilong Jing, Jun Wu, Pengfei Zhan, Tianfeng Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Reconstruction-based methods are mainstream paradigms for unsupervised time series anomaly detection. However, with increasing expressiveness of deep models like Transformers, these methods face the over-generalization problem where high-capacity models produce low reconstruction errors even for anomalies. Additionally, existing methods exhibit low- frequency bias in frequency domain modeling, neglecting high- frequency details that contain discriminative anomaly information. To address these issues, we propose RFETR, a Residual Frequency-Enhanced Two-stage Reconstruction framework. The core innovation is a Residual Frequency Enhancement mechanism that modulates the spectral residual between original and reconstructed sequences rather than directly masking original spectra, enabling adaptive anomaly amplification while preserving normal patterns. We further design a parameter-shared two-stage reconstruction framework with input-target asymmetry that amplifies reconstruction errors for anomalies, along with a feature-driven adaptive error fusion strategy for handling diverse anomaly types. Experiments on five public datasets demonstrate that RFETR achieves state-of-the-art performance with 90.0 percent average F1-score and 94.7 percent average AUC-ROC.

title: Wavelength-Adaptive CNN Feature Encoding with Linear SVM Meta-Classification to Differentiate Multiple Photosensitive Skin Dissorders
authors: Lokeshwar S, Madhavan S, D. Ramalakshmi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Identification of photosensitive skin disorders becomes difficult because different conditions share similar symptoms and their lesions change appearance when exposed to different wavelengths of light. The research develops a Wavelength-Adaptive Convolutional Neural Network (WA-CNN) that works with a linear SVM meta-classifier to achieve better diagnostic results. The WA-CNN uses a spectral-selective modulation layer to modify its input channels based on estimated lesion reflectance, which helps extract specific spectral-spatial features from the data. The LSVM receives compressed embeddings that it uses to perform high-margin classification. The proposed framework achieves better results than standard CNNs and deep learning models when testing on a dermatological data set with wavelength information. The system enables users to understand its decision process through spectral-gradient saliency mapping, which shows how different wavelengths affect diagnosis. The combined method shows promise for accurate identification of photosensitive skin disorders through wavelength-dependent feature extraction.

title: Automated Control of Product Labeling System Based on Computer Vision
authors: Maxim Bogachev, Margarita Rudyy, Daniil Inechkin, Gleb Shvets
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: The increasing requirements for product traceability and digital labeling impose stricter demands on the correctness of applying unique identifiers within national and supranational regulatory systems (e.g., the "Chestny Znak" system in the Russian Federation, EUDAMED in the European Union for medical devices). Violations such as duplication, absence, or incorrect placement of labels complicate logistics processes and lead to risks of regulatory penalties, product batch returns, repeated re-sorting, and reduced trust from partners and consumers. This paper addresses these challenges by proposing an additional in-line control mechanism based on an automated machine vision system. The proposed solution enables continuous verification of labeling quality directly on the production line and transfers the responsibility for release decisions from human operators to an automated inspection system, thereby improving process reliability under conditions of high throughput and product variability.

title: DR-ILS: High-Precision RFID Indoor Localization via Differential RSSI and Iterated Local Search
authors: Mingfeng Lv, Zhijing Wang, Yibin Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) RFID systems enable cost-effective indoor localization but suffer from severe performance degradation due to the stochastic nature of Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) and environmental multipath effects. Existing heuristic approaches typically rely on absolute signal values and rigid spatial partitioning, making them susceptible to distribution shifts and global drift. To address these limitations, this paper proposes DR-ILS, a robust localization framework that integrates an Uncertainty-Weighted Differential Strategy with a Diversity-Driven Iterated Local Search (ILS) algorithm. Unlike traditional methods, we construct differential features weighted by signal variance to eliminate common-mode interference and suppress unstable readings. We employ a correlation-aware ILS mechanism to dynamically optimize reference tag selection, effectively preventing the system from being trapped in local optima. This is followed by a coarse-to-fine hierarchical strategy for precise coordinate estimation. Extensive experiments in complex indoor environments demonstrate that DR-ILS achieves a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 7.55 cm, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines (e.g., SWHG) by approximately 11.4%. Notably, the proposed method reduces the Y-axis error by 50.4% (to 1.33 cm), confirming its superior robustness against environmental dynamics.

title: DF-DDQN: Resource-Aware Dual-Flow Scheduling for Multi-Queue DAG Systems
authors: Zhenkun Zheng, Ying Guo, Longfei Yue, Di Wang, Yuanhe Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the rapid growth of parallel computing and data-intensive applications, DAG-based task scheduling has become a critical challenge in modern computing systems. In heterogeneous, high-concurrency environments, scheduling constraints extend beyond computational resources to include storage-related factors such as data locality, access paths, and bandwidth latency. Traditional heuristic methods often struggle with dynamic workloads, resource heterogeneity, and complex task dependencies, especially under tightly coupled computation–data flows. To address these challenges, we propose DF-DDQN (Dual-Flow Double Deep Q-Network), a deep reinforcement learning framework that decouples computation and data flows for joint modeling and adaptive optimization. The framework integrates structure-aware task representations (e.g., critical paths, dependency vectors) with resource-aware state features (CPU and memory utilization, queue loads) to enable efficient scheduling in multi-queue environments. A dual-flow simulation environment with multi-objective rewards is designed to balance makespan minimization, resource utilization, and dependency satisfaction. Experiments on DAGs of various sizes demonstrate strong performance: task duration prediction achieves R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.98, CPU usage prediction reaches R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.99; workloads with 95 tasks complete 5% faster than HEFT, reduce resource usage variability by 27%, and lower CV/Gini coefficients by 25–50%, resulting in smoother and more balanced queue utilization. These results confirm DF-DDQN’s superior scheduling intelligence and robustness across heterogeneous computing scenarios.

title: Event-Aware Relabeling for Addressing Future Leakage in End-to-End Autonomous Driving
authors: Haechul Chang, Siewoo Kim, Adeeb M. Islam, Seong-Jun Kim, Seung-Hyun Kong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Recent advances in autonomous driving have shifted the field from modular pipelines to End-to-End (E2E) frameworks. E2E frameworks directly map raw sensor inputs to control commands, leveraging large-scale data to learn robust driving policies. However, the conventional data generation process relies on future actions and trajectory information as Ground Truth (GT). This procedure introduces a “Future Leakage” problem, where information unavailable at inference time unintentionally enters the training process. This leads to causal confusion, where the model learns spurious correlations instead of true causality, thus compromising driving safety. In this paper, we propose an Event-Aware Relabeling technique to address this problem. The proposed method detects interaction events with obstacles and vehicles by utilizing traffic lights and the ego-vehicle's state information. Then it effectively eliminates the leakage of future information by performing appropriate relabeling on the data prior to the event occurrence. Experimental results demonstrate that our method effectively mitigates the causal confusion problem and achieves a driving score improvement of approximately 14% compared to baseline. This study highlights that a high-quality data curation process is essential to ensure the safety of E2E autonomous driving systems and validates the potential of data-centric performance optimization.

title: Lightweight Yet Powerful: Optimized Tightly Coupled Hyperdimensional Computing Accelerators
authors: Mohsen Asghari, Sebastien Le Beux, Ron Mankarious
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Hyperdimensional Computing (HDC) is a hardware-friendly machine learning paradigm that encodes data into high-dimensional binary hypervectors, enabling efficient computation through lightweight bitwise operations. Existing HDC accelerators, often implemented on FPGAs, typically rely on custom instruction sets and co-processor designs to optimize performance. However, these architectures face programmability challenges and incur significant control overhead when interacting with the main processor. To overcome these limitations, we present a methodology for designing and optimizing tightly coupled HDC accelerators. The proposed method produces accelerators enhanced with a controller capable of decoding instructions corresponding to key HDC kernels, thereby reducing communication overhead and improving throughput. To validate the approach, we implemented the accelerator at the RTL level and modeled the entire system in SystemVerilog. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to state-of-the-art architectures, the proposed method reduces software code size by up to 98% while achieving a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(3 \times$</tex>) speedup.

title: PureCertificate: A Privacy-Preserving Small and Local VLM Framework for Secure Certificate Analysis
authors: Odinachi Udemezuo Nwankwo, Heejae-Shin, Victor Ikenna Kanu, Chigozie Athanasius Nnadiekwe, Ihunanya Udodiri Ajakwe, Kalibbala Jonathan Mukisa, Nanteza Adah Lubwama, Dong-Seong Kim, Jae Min Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This work introduces PureCertificate, a privacy-preserving certificate analysis framework that uses locally deployed vision-language models instead of cloud services to meet military-grade confidentiality requirements. PureCertificate employs lightweight fine-tuning, dataset preprocessing, and GPT-Generated Unified Format (GGUF) conversion for efficient ondevice inference through OLLAMA. Among the tested models, qwen3-vl:8b-instruct-q8_0 delivered the most reliable performance with a model size of about 9.8 GB. The framework supports similarity checking, information extraction, discrepancy detection, content recognition, and certificate comparison through a Flutter-connected inferencing module. Experiments show fluctuating desktop performance. Overall, PureCertificate is a promising solution to offline multimodal certificate analysis.

title: Optimizing Inventory Decisions in Finance-Driven Supply Chains Using Data Mining
authors: Haoran Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: Inventory decisions in finance-driven supply chains are plagued by high inventory holding costs, stockout losses, and low turnover rates. This study proposes a framework that integrates a multidimensional demand forecast matrix, an inventory-financial sensitivity model, and an improved EOQ optimization engine. This framework improves demand forecast accuracy using an ARIMA-LSTM hybrid model, quantifies the dynamic relationship between inventory and financial indicators using a random forest, and generates a Pareto-optimal strategy set using Monte Carlo simulation. Experimental results show that this framework achieves an average monthly turnover rate of 7.2 times per year, reduces average costs to 14.3%, and reduces the average annual stockout rate from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4. 9 \%}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 2 \%}$</tex>. This framework provides a scientific and practical solution for inventory decision-making in finance-driven supply chains.

title: Design of Non-Gaussian Receivers for Ultra-Low Frequency Channels
authors: Wenwei Ying, Xun Li, Xiaobo Wang, Zijie Niu, Anbo Zhang, Zhen Qiao
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: Conventional receivers experience severe performance degradation in ultra-low frequency channels due to non-Gaussian noise interference. To ensure reliable communication for underwater vehicles and to support mission-critical operations, a non-Gaussian receiver design is proposed. The proposed approach includes two main components. The first component develops a nonlinear processor that estimates the derivative behavior of the noise probability density function through a nonparametric method. A dedicated search algorithm simplifies the estimated curve into a three-segment linear model, enabling Gaussian mapping of non-Gaussian signals. The second component optimizes the Turbo decoder using a maximum a posteriori iterative decoding structure. The received signal is divided into information and parity bits, which are processed by two sub-decoders that exchange a priori and extrinsic information through multiple iterations. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed receiver achieves comparable performance to the conventional Gaussian receiver under Gaussian noise conditions, while providing significant improvement in communication reliability under non-Gaussian impulsive noise environments.

title: Intelligent Early Warning Model for Financial Public Opinion Risk Based on Multimodal Feature-Level Fusion
authors: Yining Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: The core challenge of financial public opinion risk early warning is the difficulty of segmentation and coordination of multi-source heterogeneous data (text, image, video and other modalities) at the feature level, which leads to the one-sidedness and lag in risk signal identification. To this end, this paper proposes an intelligent early warning model for financial public opinion risk based on multimodal feature-level fusion. First, a dedicated module is designed to deeply extract the essential features of each modality. Then an adaptive feature-level fusion framework for the financial field is constructed. Finally, the gated fusion technology is used to deeply integrate the optimized aligned and weighted multimodal features at the feature level. Experimental results show that the proposed model has an accuracy of 92.5 %, an F 1 value of 90.7 %, and a recall rate of 91.8% on a real financial public opinion dataset. Cross-modal attention and weighted fusion mechanisms are proven to be the key to improving performance (the F1 value of the model drops to 80.1 % and 82.5 % respectively after removal). The model also shows good adaptability in different risk scenarios. These results verify the effectiveness of multimodal feature-level fusion methods in financial public opinion risk warning, which can significantly improve the recognition accuracy and adaptability of the model.

title: An Efficient Deep Learning Approach for Face Detection Using Eigenfaces
authors: Singanamalla Vijayakumar, G. Abhinav, G. Sai Krishna, T. Mahender Reddy, Somula Ramasubbareddy
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The practical applications of real-time face recognition systems include their use in smartphone unlock functions and automated attendance systems as well as secure access control and airport identity checks together with surveillance operations and smart home automation systems. Real-time applications in this field demand identification methods which operate quickly while maintaining both scale and precision. This project applies Eigenfaces combined with Incremental Principal Component Analysis (IPCA) to build a face recognition system that stand out with both large datasets and real-time data processing. IPCA stands apart from traditional PCA because it performs data analysis through smaller batches which results in more efficient memory usage and better scalability. The continuous update of eigenfaces in IPCA enhances system performance by producing more accurate results and better stability which results in improved quality capabilities for dynamic environments. The system performs image preprocessing alongside IPCA feature extraction before implementing distance metrics for recognition which offers an effective solution for real-time face recognition tasks.

title: A NARX-Inspired Dual-Branch Neural Network Calibration for Pipelined ADC
authors: Qintian Zhu, Zongmin Wang, Tieliang Zhang, Wenxiao Feng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This paper presents a digital calibration method based on a Nonlinear Auto-Regressive with Exogenous Inputs (NARX) neural network with a dual-branch architecture, which helps overcome the limitations of conventional methods in compensating complex non-idealities. Specifically, the static branch uses raw binary code to model code-dependent errors like capacitor mismatch, while the dynamic branch employs a causal tapped-delay-line regressor to model complicated dynamic distortion. Two shallow Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) estimate the error components, which are subsequently fused to correct the raw output. Experimental results on a commercial 14-bit 1 GS/s pipelined ADC demonstrate that the proposed method improves SFDR from 69.88 dB to 84.78 dB at 120 MHz and from 44.66 dB to 81.70 dB at 830 MHz. Furthermore, the calibrator maintains an SFDR of approximately 80 dB across the 1<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">st</sup> and 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nd</sup> Nyquist range.

title: Context-Aware Enterprise Vulnerability Prioritization and Remediation Recommendations Using Semantic Learning
authors: Sumit Prakash Dubey, Soumya Maity
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Enterprise vulnerability management needs prioritization and remediation decisions that need attention beyond generic severity scores and keyword mappings. CVSS frameworks offer standardized severity estimates, but they do not provide the necessary organizational context or understanding of vulnerabilities and remediation details that are available as text. This paper presents a framework for context-aware vulnerability prioritization and remediation recommendations which help organizations prioritize and remediate vulnerability based on its risk it poses to organization and not its technical severity score. It combines semantic learning with risk modeling specific to the organization. This research takes vulnerability descriptions and remediation texts using dense semantic representations from a Sentence-BERT model, allowing for meaning-based retrieval. A machine learning-based risk scoring model includes contextual factors such as asset importance, exposure, and the likelihood of exploitation. Finally, it re-ranks remediation candidates within a unified learning-to-rank framework and provides clear and actionable guidance to cyber analysts for remediation. Experiments on a large enterprise vulnerability dataset show that our approach consistently outperforms lexical, semantic-only, and heuristic riskbased methods regarding Mean Reciprocal Rank and Recall@K, while still keeping query latency low. These results demonstrate the advantages of merging semantic learning with context-aware risk modeling for managing vulnerabilities in enterprises. This paper explicitly incorporates enterprise context into remediation ranking; the proposed framework bridges the gap between vulnerability prioritization and actionable mitigation. In addition, source attribution and decomposed scoring components improve explainability and auditability, supporting practical adoption in enterprise security operations.

title: The Analysis of Next-Generation HPC Architecture Based on CXL Through RAMSES-HR5 Performance Optimization
authors: Hyun Mi Jung, Hyunjo Lee, Cheol-Joo Chae
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: High-performance computing (HPC) is an essential tool for solving complex and massive computational problems in advanced scientific research fields such as application simulations in astronomy, climate science, and particle physics. This is because simulations require maximizing floating-point operations and extensive parallel processing based on large-scale data. As HPC systems evolve into heterogeneous environments combining CPUs and GPUs, new performance bottlenecks beyond simple computational overhead have emerged as major challenges. Specifically, overhead from data memory copying between CPUs and GPUs, system-wide data bottlenecks, and increased computational latency and energy consumption are becoming pervasive. Resolving these inefficiencies to save time and costs while enhancing research efficiency is the primary goal. Therefore, this study analyzes performance optimization strategies for the HPC (High-Performance Computing) application simulation code RAMSES-HR5 to design and analyze a CXL-based nextgeneration HPC architecture. First, code profiling analysis revealed that the primary bottlenecks in the existing system stem from synchronization and communication delays based on OpenMP/MPI, rather than computational overhead. Excessive wait times were particularly observed in the kmp_fork_barrier and pmpi_allreduce functions. To address this issue, we propose solutions including merging OpenMP parallelization regions, utilizing asynchronous MPI communication, and changing the AMR (Adaptive Mesh Refinement) index structure to a hash-based approach. We also explore leveraging nextgeneration devices like CXL (Compute Express Link) to resolve memory bottlenecks and maximize data sharing efficiency. These approaches are expected to enhance the performance of RAMSES-HR5 and further contribute to building a simulation environment optimized for next-generation HPC system architectures.

title: Lattice-Based Functional Encryption for Next-generation Privacy-Preserving Computation
authors: S. Vimalalochana, M. Sakthivanitha, S. Cyciliya Pearline Christy, Vishwa Priya V, Vedavalli S, S. Janani
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: With the rise of cloud-based data processing and multi-party computations, the demand for secure, sturdy, and effective privacy-preserving tasks has risen. Homomorphic Encryption (HE) and Bilinear Functional Encryption (FE) users face computational and properties constraints in the form of high computation overheads, high ciphertext expansions, and lower functionality accuracy reducing usability. This research wishes to avoid such limitations by developing Lattice-Based Functional Encryption (FE) framework, which takes into consideration the neighborhood of security, efficiency and accuracy. Constructing a series of structured lattice based schemes for key generation and ciphertext formatting, allows for encrypted data evaluation without any need for decrypting, with worst-case hardness security properties. Experimental evaluations based on structured datasets, time of encryption, time of queries evaluation, ciphertext length, and accuracy of functions were observed. Lattice-Based FE achieved a total time of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.5 ~\text{ms} / 1 ~\text{KB}$</tex> encryption time, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$14.3 ~\text{ms} /$</tex> query evaluation time, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.1 ~\text{KB} / 1 ~\text{KB}$</tex> ciphertext size and resulted in 99.0 % functional accuracy as compared to HE and Bilinear FE methods. The findings show that the lattice-based FE was able to deliver scalable and effective privacy-preserving computations. This framework moreover will provide a promising approach for secure data processing tasks based on the IoT, finance and cloud interest, ushering an expansion into more cryptographic methods for years to come.

title: Dualscan.AI: A Unified Platform for Brain Tumor and Pneumonia Recognition from Medical Images
authors: N. Bhuvaneswary, B. Hari Krishna, S. Vivek Kumar, P. Siva Prakash
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: In effective patient care, brain tumor and pneumonia must have an early and appropriate diagnosis. But interpreting chest X-ray and manual MRI images is frequently resource-intensive, time-intensive, and susceptible to variation inherent in clinical practice. Assisting physicians in making diagnoses, this project focuses on creating DualScan.ai, an fully integrated, AI-based medical image analysis platform that Automatically detects pneumonia on chest X-rays and brain malignancies from MR images. Convolutional neural networks DualScan.ai couples both Visual Analysis and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). Vision Transformers (ViT) have been carefully fine-tuned per application. imaging technique utilized alongside sophisticated preprocessing methods techniques like segmentation, noise reduction, and anatomical Verification of structure. A backend server based on Flask Trusted API service and a React front-end with an easily understandable To perform model inference, the platform uses TensorFlow 2.x. The system has JWT-based authentication to provide safe access to users, stringent image validation pipelines to ensure input integrity, and cloud deployment on Render with containerization on Docker for high availability and scalability. Full training and testing achieved encouraging performance which had good accuracy, recall and AUC score but lung at 95% of recall rate and brain tumor accepted only 92% accuracy. The software also includes confidence scoring for clinical interpretation and to record history analytics for Longitudinal monitoring. Leveraging the most advanced deep learning technology and cloud-based system architecture, DualScan.ai is an end-to-end solution, fulfilling the need for efficient, accurate and low-cost medical imaging Diagnostic tools. The study can contribute towards the execution of AI in healthcare and enhance diagnostic workflow.

title: Contextual Feature Extraction for Personality Prediction Using Transformer-Based Models
authors: Suman A. Patil, Dr.Shivaleela Patil
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Personality inference from written text has emerged as a prominent interdisciplinary area, connecting the fields of computational linguistics, psychology, and artificial intelligence. Its applications span personalized recommendation engines, mental health monitoring tools, and adaptive human-computer interfaces. Early approaches largely depended on self-reported surveys and simple textual features, including Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) metrics and n-gram representations. In recent years, the field has shifted toward deep learning solutions, utilizing architectures such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. This research investigates the capabilities of transformer-based models, particularly DistilBERT, to extract detailed contextual cues from social media content. By leveraging multi-head self-attention and contextualized embeddings, the approach captures intricate semantic and syntactic relationships, enabling a more sophisticated characterization of personality traits. The proposed methodology not only achieves superior predictive accuracy compared to conventional techniques but also provides practical benefits in terms of computational efficiency and scalability. Overall, these results highlight the significant impact of advanced natural language processing techniques on personality modeling, while also emphasizing critical concerns related to interpretability, ethical considerations, and potential biases in the data.

title: An Error-Resilient Area-Efficient Ultra-Low-Power 4-Bit Flash ADC Design with Multi-Vt Transistors
authors: Riyanka Banerjee, Jay Nangia, M. Santosh, Jai Gopal Pandey
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: In high-speed, low-resolution conversion applications, Flash analog-to-digital converters (ADCs) are most suitable. In the proposed work, a Flash ADC is designed using multithreshold transistors offered in sub-micron CMOS technology, effectively leveraging low-threshold voltage transistors (LVT), nominal-threshold voltage transistors (NVT), regular-threshold voltage transistors (RVT), and high-threshold voltage transistors (HVT) transistors to optimize performance and power efficiency. The designed Flash ADC architecture incorporates calibrated multi-threshold transistors based comparators to balance between gain-bandwidth trade-off, reduced area, desired figure of merit (FoM), and ensure bubble-free encoding. The proposed design achieves a sampling rate of 2 MS/s at a 1.2 V supply, consumes only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$17.48 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$478.6788 \mu \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> active area. Key performance metrics include a peak differential non-linearity (DNL) of 0.4 LSB, INL of 0.7 LSB a signal-to-noise-and-distortion ratio (SNDR) of 22.83 dB, an effective number of bits (ENOB) of 3.5, and a resulting FoM of 273.13 fJ/conversion-step.

title: An Efficient Fingerprint Extraction and Matching Algorithm for Imaging Device
authors: Yikun Geng, Yushi Li, Xuefeng Zhang, Along Jin
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of how to efficiently extract and accurately match the fingerprint information collected by the imaging device, an efficient fingerprint extraction and matching algorithm is proposed. Firstly, the preprocessed fingerprint image is extracted using the trained convolutional neural network (CNN), and the extracted feature vectors are compared using the elastic deformation matching algorithm. The matching degree between the two fingerprint images is evaluated by calculating the similarity and distance between the feature points. The algorithm adjusts the position of the feature points within a certain range to achieve a more accurate matching effect. The simulation results show that under different imaging device types and fingerprint image quality conditions, the average matching accuracy of the proposed algorithm is $94.53 \%$, and the extraction speed is also faster.

title: A Geometric-Analytical Algorithm for Hybrid AOA/TDOA Localization
authors: Jindian Lv, Feifei Pang, Haiyan Wang, Yong Liang
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: Safety management of low-altitude airspace is a critical prerequisite for fostering the high-quality development of the low-altitude economy. Accurate positioning technology has emerged as a central challenge in mitigating collision risks associated with the deployment of high-density aircraft. This paper proposes a collaborative positioning algorithm that integrates time-difference-of-arrival (TDOA) and angle-of-arrival (AOA) information. The proposed algorithm rigorously exploits the inherent geometric constraints present in the TDOA and AOA observation models. By incorporating AOA measurements into the nonlinear equations of TDOA, the algorithm effectively transforms the nonlinear localization model into a pseudo-linear formulation. Unlike conventional nonlinear Taylor-series expansion methods, the proposed approach derives a closed-form solution directly from the pseudo-linear equation, thereby completely eliminating the iterative process and its associated convergence issues. Simulation results demonstrate that maintaining a low Horizontal Dilution of Precision (HDOP) during sensor deployment leads to superior localization performance.

title: EfficientNetV2 and Grad-CAM++ Enabled Cloud Framework for Automated Thoracic Disease Diagnosis From Chest X-Rays
authors: K. Radhika, S. Annapurna Shobitha, Gousia Thahniyath, Damodharan D, Fenita F
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: A quick and effective diagnosis of Thoracic diseases is a critical challenge in today's professional environment, particularly in resource limited environments with minimal access to highly qualified radiologists. This paper suggests an end-to-end cloud enabled artificial intelligence-based automatic classification of multi-label disease of the chest using chest Xray images. The system is effectively classifying 14 popular thoracic diseases using high-level structural and spatial characteristics in the X- rays using the EfficientNetV2-L convolutional neural network to reach precise detection. Grad-CAM++ model is then utilized to visualize heatmaps of the X-ray areas that are maximally contributing towards the model's predictions to make its decision- making process as transparent and interpretable as possible, thus resulting in situations where clinicians can trust the models. Its design also involves the utilization of Flask as a web interface where medical practitioners are able to utilize the software real- time and integrate it into the cloud safely. Moreover, there is a clinician feedback loop that allows the iterative improvement or optimization of model predictions after expert verification, thus enhancing diagnostic reliability and flexibility. Various experiments on the ChestX-ray14 dataset show that the proposed model is very effective in reaching state-of-the-art multi-label classification, as well as transparency and explainability. As a result of the AI methods being improved and integrated into clinical practice as the main factors, the diagnostic processes can be considerably expedited, the risk of error-prone human judgment in complicated diagnoses can be reduced, and the making of informed health decisions can be easily performed in different healthcare settings.

title: Beam Alignment for Massive MIMO Transmission Based on Multi-Dimensional and Multi-Particle Quantum Walks
authors: Maki Arai, Tomoki Yamagami, Takatomo Mihana, Ryoichi Horisaki, Mikio Hasegawa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper proposes a multi-armed bandit (MAB) algorithm based on a quantum walk (QW) with multiple dimensions and multiple particles. By extending our fast beam selection method using one-dimensional discrete-time QW to multi-dimensional and multi-particle systems, we aim to improve the search efficiency for optimal beam combinations for multiple users and enhance the total channel capacity. Numerical analysis demonstrates that the proposed method improves the average channel capacity even when the number of users is three or more. The proposed method provides an effective framework for achieving low latency and large capacity in multi-user beam allocation problems.

title: Lightweight Quantum-Inspired Metaheuristic for Real-Time Resource Scheduling in Heterogeneous Edge Environments
authors: Vignesh M, Dharanya A P, GandhimathiNathan T, Nareshkanna S, Sobika M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Edge computing has become a fundamental architecture for supporting latency-sensitive and computationally intensive Internet of Things (IoT) applications. However, dynamic workload fluctuations, device heterogeneity, and limited energy capacity pose significant challenges for efficient task scheduling. Traditional optimization methods-including classical heuristics and metaheuristics-often suffer from premature convergence, scalability limitations, and inability to adapt to rapidly changing edge conditions. This paper proposes a Lightweight Quantum-Inspired Metaheuristic (LQIM) designed for real-time resource scheduling in heterogeneous edge environments. The proposed algorithm leverages quantum-inspired probability amplitudes, Q-bit encoding, rotation operators, and measurement-based sampling to enhance global exploration while maintaining computational efficiency suitable for resource-constrained devices. Experimental evaluations conducted in a controlled multi-node simulator demonstrate that LQIM reduces average latency by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$27-31 \%$</tex>, decreases energy consumption by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8}-\mathbf{2 2 \%}$</tex>, and improves resource utilization by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5}-\mathbf{3 3} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> compared to Genetic Algorithms (GA) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). These results indicate that quantum-inspired mechanisms can significantly enhance adaptive scheduling in edge environments without requiring quantum hardware.

title: Design of a PI-Controller ASIC for a Multilevel Converter Using High-Level Synthesis and LibreLane
authors: Francisca Donoso, Nelson Salvador, Christian A. Rojas, Jorge Marín, Gonzalo Carvajal
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and validation of a fixed-point PI controller for power electronics applications, introducing a design flow based on high-level synthesis tools. The proposed design methodology tackles the long-standing challenge of migrating high-level floating-point representations of control algorithms into hardware-efficient fixed-point implementations suitable for FPGA prototyping and ASIC integration. A threelevel flying capacitor converter (3L-FCC) serves as the case study to demonstrate the approach. Using software-in-the-loop verification, the fixed-point design is shown to maintain functional equivalence with its floating-point reference. Furthermore, wordlength exploration is conducted to analyze trade-offs between accuracy, area, power consumption and resource utilization. The proposed design flow is validated using both proprietary and opensource high-level synthesis tools to generate RTL descriptions, which are later used for FPGA prototyping and verification and also processed through an open-source ASIC design framework with minor modifications. This flow complements the traditional RTL-to-GDSII flow by reducing HDL development time for designs derived from high-level algorithms.

title: Fuzzy-Based Fractional Control Structure for Enhanced Load Frequency Control
authors: Akhilesh Kumar Mishra, Anukaran Khanna, Vanya Arun, Arpit Varshnry
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: In this work, the performance of the multi- area interconnected power system whose turbine system contains an intrinsic nonlinearity in its performance, particularly in the generation rate, has been improved through a fuzzy based fractional order control structure. The fuzzy controllers, as we all know, are superior to the traditional controllers in the management of parametric variations of the system. The paper has also used fractionality and the adoption of fuzzy logic to provide a superior outcome in the rejection of disturbances. The fuzzy based FO controller was superior to a standard PID controller by 51.7%. Also, the proposed controller was tested on random step load perturbation and shown to be more effective than the existing control structure.

title: Readiness Assessment and Barriers Identification in Adopting Sustainable Smart Warehousing Practices in Sri Lanka'S FMCG Sector
authors: Nethmini A.P.P, Kavirathna C.A., Withanarachchi A.
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: The rapid shift toward digital transformation and sustainability has elevated the importance of Sustainable Smart Warehousing (SSW) across global supply chains. This study investigates the barriers influencing the adoption of SSW practices in Sri Lanka's Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG) sector and evaluates organizational readiness using a statistical model based on Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modelling (PLS-SEM). A Systematic Literature Review (SLR) identified core technological, operational, financial, organizational, and industry-related barriers. A survey of 144 FMCG logistics and warehouse professionals was analyzed to test the impact of these barriers on SSW adoption. Findings reveal that Financial & Economic barriers, Technological Infrastructure gaps, Warehouse Operations issues, Human Resource & Organizational limitations, and Industry-Related constraints influence adoption at varying degrees. The research proposes a SSW Readiness Assessment Model tailored to Sri Lanka's context and presents actionable strategies for phased adoption. The study contributes to the development of a unified empirical framework for SSW integration in developing economies.

title: Dual-Strategy Localization and Patch-Based Perceptual Evaluation for Industrial Print Inspection
authors: B.G.V.A.A.S. Jayasinghe, L.M.A.P. Cabral, A.U.P.H. Athukorala
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Smart manufacturing environments require quality inspection systems that exhibit human-aligned perceptual sensitivity and robustness to variable production conditions. Traditional Automated Optical Inspection (AOI) systems often struggle with surface deformation on flexible packaging, while conventional objective metrics fail to correlate reliably with human visual judgment. This paper introduces a hybrid, perceptually aligned inspection framework designed for highspeed and variable industrial workflows. The proposed system employs a hierarchical dual-strategy localization architecture, using Normalized Cross-Correlation (NCC) for uniform surfaces and contour-based shape matching for deformed substrates. To enhance evaluation reliability, a Sensitivity-Enhanced Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity (LPIPS) module is incorporated to align automated decision boundaries with human quality perception. Experiments on a dataset of 2,000 samples, followed by a real-world production evaluation of more than 98,000 images, demonstrate strong adaptability and stability. In low-contrast defect scenarios, the sensitivity-enhanced metric maintained a perfect stability score (1.00) for acceptable prints while reducing to below 0.7 for defective cases, providing decisive separation that conventional metrics fail to achieve. These results validate the framework's ability to support consistent, humancentric quality assurance and contribute to scalable, resilient, and agile manufacturing inspection ecosystems.

title: A DAE-Transformer Framework for Predictive Maintenance of Lithium-Ion Batteries in Intelligent Battery Management Systems
authors: Siyuan Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: The increasing adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) and large-scale energy storage systems has highlighted the critical importance of accurate battery health monitoring and predictive maintenance. This study proposes a novel framework for predicting the Remaining Useful Life (RUL) of lithium-ion batteries using a combination of key aging interval extraction, feature engineering, denoising autoencoder (DAE), and a linearcomplexity Transformer network. The framework first identifies two critical points-knee-onset and knee-point-to define the most informative segment of the degradation curve. Battery health indicators (voltage, current, temperature, and internal resistance) are extracted and preprocessed to handle missing values and outliers. The DAE captures latent features from noisy input data, while the Transformer models long-range dependencies to generate accurate RUL predictions. Experimental validation on NASA 18650 and CALCE battery datasets demonstrates that the proposed method outperforms existing LSTM, CNN-LSTM, and standard Transformer models across multiple metrics, including Relative Error (RE), RMSE, and MAPE. The results indicate that focusing on the critical aging interval and leveraging advanced deep learning architectures significantly improves predictive maintenance capabilities, enhancing safety, reliability, and operational efficiency in intelligent Battery Management Systems.

title: Content-Based Image Retrieval using Deep Learning for Medical Images
authors: Novy Jacob, Soumya George, Deepa Mathew K, Laxmi, Rooby M
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This project introduces an advanced framework incorporating GLCM, CNN, and Euclidean distance metrics for improved medical image retrieval. Focused on the efficient retrieval of medical images like Xrays, CT scans, MRIs, and histopathological slides, the application leverages deep learning techniques. By integrating GLCM for texture analysis and CNN for feature extraction, the system aims to enhance the accuracy of content-based image retrieval. Euclidean distance metrics will be utilized for similarity comparisons. This project aims to significantly improve medical professionals’ image search capabilities and contribute to enhanced healthcare outcomes.

title: A Survey on Semantic Encoder Technology Trends for 6G Task-Oriented Semantic Communications
authors: Chang gyo Jeong, Woongsoo Na
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: In the era of 6G communications, massive data traffic and ultra-low-latency service requirements expose the limitations of conventional Shannon-based communication approaches that prioritize bit-level accuracy. Semantic communication has emerged as a key enabling technology to address these limitations, and the semantic encoder plays a central role by extracting task-relevant semantic information from source data and generating a compact semantic representation. This paper analyzes technology trends of semantic encoders, focusing on their architectures, learning paradigms, and extensibility, based on nine representative recent studies. Our analysis shows that semantic encoders are evolving along multiple directions, including knowledge-driven encoders, multimodal semantic relay structures, self-supervised (low-label) semantic encoders, LLMenabled knowledge-augmented encoders, generative-model-based semantic encoding, metasurface-based physical-layer semantic encoding, and multi-user semantic separation approaches.

title: TSR-VLM: A Large Multimodal Model for Traffic Sign Recognition in Intelligent Traffic Management
authors: Boshuo Peng, Tangyi Guo, Yifan Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Traffic sign recognition (TSR) remains a critical challenge in intelligent transportation systems due to persistent issues such as cross-modal semantic disconnects, limited anomaly sensitivity, and non-compliant decision-making. While existing vision-based models excel in controlled environments, they often fail to adapt to real-world complexities where contextual reasoning and regulatory adherence are paramount. This paper proposes TSR-VLM, a vision-language framework that addresses these gaps through a unified architecture integrating multimodal learning, domain knowledge, and causal reasoning. The framework combines a deformable attentionenhanced visual encoder for multi-granularity feature extraction with a dynamically updatable knowledge graph encoding traffic regulations (GB 5768.2-2022), enabling contextaware and rule-compliant sign interpretation. A novel causal intervention mechanism further enhances anomaly detection robustness by quantifying model sensitivity to critical sign regions under perturbations. Evaluated on a hybrid dataset (Tsinghua-Tencent <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${1 0 0 \rm{K}}$</tex> and TSR-Anomaly), TSR-VLM achieves state-of-the-art performance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ (9 4. 2 \%}$</tex> accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${9 0. 3 \%}$</tex> F1 score) while demonstrating superior compliance validation capabilities (91.7% rule-matching accuracy on average). The framework also supports continuous adaptation through human feedback, making it a scalable solution for evolving traffic management needs. TSR-VLM establishes a scalable paradigm for interpretable and regulation-compliant traffic management systems.

title: RMC-YOLO: A Small Object Detection Framework with Routing Attention and Multi-Scale Context Modulation
authors: Jimeng Chen, Xiaodong Wang, Lianjun Zhang, Yuli Liu, Min Luo
abstract: Detecting small objects in UAV images is challenging due to their limited size and the complexity of background. This work introduces an improved YOLOv8nbased approach designed to enhance performance in such environments. Specifically, a feature modeling framework, named RMC (Routing-aware Modulated Context) is designed, which integrates a routing-aware attention mechanism with a multi-scale context modulation strategy to better capture and distinguish small object features. The backbone of YOLOv8n is restructured by introducing the Omni-dimensional Dynamic Convolution (ODConv) module and the Routing Attention Spatial Aggregator (RASA) module, both aimed at improving the model's sensitivity to target regions. Meanwhile, the original SPPF module is replaced by the Multi-Scale Context Modulation (MSCM) module to strengthen the modeling of complex contextual information. Further improvements are made in the neck by introducing an extra feature layer and replacing the detection head with the Adaptive Spatial Feature Fusion (ASFF) module, which is incorporated to enable effective multi-scale feature fusion. Validation on the VisDrone2019 dataset demonstrates that the RMC-YOLO method reaches 38.9% <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP}@0.5$</tex>, representing a 4.9% improvement over the original YOLOv8n.

title: FESL-YOLO: Improved YOLOv11 Small Object Detection Algorithm for Aerial Images
authors: Wei-Qing Ge, Heyrim Ju, Wang-Su Jeon
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: To address the challenges posed by UAV flight conditions such as long distances from targets, significant variations in object sizes, and occlusions this paper introduces FESL-YOLO (Feature Enhancement and Small-object detection Layer YOLO), a YOLO-based algorithm built upon YOLOv11s and augmented with feature enrichment and a small object detection layer, aiming to advance small object detection efficacy from a UAV perspective. A novel Feature Enhancement Convolution module (FEConv) is designed to replace the original convolutional structures in the backbone network, strengthening feature acquisition and representation capabilities with minimal increase in parameters. Given the typical characteristics of small objects such as small size, sparse information, and dense distribution and the structural limitation of YOLOv11 due to its aggressive down sampling, a dedicated small object detection branch is further introduced. This branch extracts fine-grained target information from shallow feature maps and fuses it with deep features, hence enhancing the model's ability to perceive small objects and considerably improving detection precision. Experimental results on the VisDrone2019 dataset demonstrate that our algorithm achieves 44.6% in mAP@0.5 and 27.1% in mAP@0.5:0.95, representing gains of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.1 \% \mathrm{p}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.3 \% \mathrm{p}$</tex>, respectively, over the original YOLOv11s model. These results verify the effectiveness and superior performance of our method for small object detection tasks in UAV-based scenarios.

title: The Value and Methodology of Blue-and-White Porcelain Decorative Patterns: A Technical and Theoretical Framework for Purification, Classification, and Restoration
authors: Yujie Cheng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: The study of blue-and-white porcelain patterns has long been challenged by two core issues: the high adhesion between patterns and backgrounds, and the complexity of categorical systems. This paper proposes a computational framework for a complete research pipeline to systematically address these challenges. The framework consists of two implemented pillars and one forward-looking direction. First, the GrabCut algorithm is employed to achieve high-precision segmentation of complex patterns, effectively resolving feature confusion and providing clean data for subsequent analysis. Second, through a benchmark comparison of seven deep learning models,ResNet101 is established as the current optimal model for classification (with a test accuracy of $\mathbf{9 1 . 9 8 \%} \boldsymbol{)}$,providing a solid performance baseline for the intelligent recognition of patterns. Building on the achievements in segmentation and classification, this paper further outlines a prospective digital restoration path centered on generative models, thereby forming a complete research loop from “data purification” and “pattern recognition” to “content regeneration”. This work not only resolves key technical challenges in pattern segmentation and classification through empirical research but also lays a theoretical foundation and practical framework for an end-to-end computational analysis system for blue-and-white porcelain patterns, promoting a shift toward quantifiable and scalable research paradigms in the field.

title: A Smart Identification and Hierarchical Diagnosis Method for Grassland Ecological Degradation Integrating Multimodal Surface Sensors and Polarimetric SAR Images
authors: Yan Liu, Ming Zhao
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Grassland ecological degradation is a continuous evolutionary process driven by multiple coupled factors, and its state representation exhibits significant multi-scale, nonlinear, and spatial heterogeneity. Existing monitoring methods mostly rely on single data sources or static classification models. This model paradigm struggles to simultaneously address the dynamic continuity of the degradation process and the need for hierarchical diagnosis. To address these issues, this paper proposes an intelligent identification and hierarchical diagnosis method for grassland ecological degradation that integrates multimodal surface sensor and polarimetric SAR imagery. At the algorithm design level, a unified state-space description model for grassland degradation is first constructed. This process incorporates vegetation structure, soil physicochemical properties, biological stress, climate environment, and human disturbance into a high-dimensional continuous state vector. Secondly, to address the discreteness and scale inconsistency issues of multi-source surface sensor observations, a spatial interpolation model based on thin-plate splines and an exponentially weighted fusion operator are designed. In terms of remote sensing feature modeling, this study introduces a multibranch feature modeling strategy constrained by polarimetric scattering mechanisms, modifying the Lur joint amplitude-phase dual-morphological representation and Freeman-Durden polarimetric decomposition features. Furthermore, a composite attention-driven cross-modal feature fusion mechanism consisting of the coordinate-aware channel attention and crosschannel transposed self-attention is proposed. Finally, a hierarchical degradation diagnosis model combining continuous degradation risk mapping and multi-threshold discrimination is constructed. The testing area was a typical temperate steppedesert steppe transition zone in central Inner Mongolia. Experimental data were validated based on 240 standard quadrats and 24 months of continuous observation data. Experimental results show that the proposed method improves overall recognition accuracy by approximately 5.5% – 15.1% compared to single-sensor methods and traditional fusion methods. Furthermore, the F1-score improvement is most significant in identifying moderate and severe degradation levels. Under conditions of 30% sensor loss on the surface and SAR coherent noise perturbation, the overall accuracy of the proposed algorithm decreases by only 3.8%.

title: Multi-Level Pelican Optimization with Fuzzy Thresholding and Deep Residual CNN for Enhanced Fatty Liver Classification in Abdominal CT Scans
authors: R Radha, Blessy Boaz
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Fatty liver disease is one of the most common liver problems in the world; however, it is frequently overlooked in its early stages since the variations in abdominal CT scans tend to be compact. This paper introduces a novel hybrid framework that integrates Multi-Level Pelican Optimization (MLPO), Fuzzy Thresholding, and a Deep Residual Convolutional Neural Network (ResCNN) to facilitate the identification and classification of fatty liver. The proposed framework uses MLPO in two different ways: first, to improve the quality of the image by choosing the best contrast parameters, and second, to find the most distinguishing characteristics after liver segmentation. Fuzzy thresholding is different from typical binary segmentation algorithms since it gives pixels membership values based on their intensity levels. This renders it easier to find soft boundaries, which makes it easier to delineate the liver region even in low-contrast or noisy images. The segmented liver area is achieved through a ResCNN architecture, utilizing deep feature extraction and skip connections to preserve spatial and contextual information. This approach assists the classifier in telling the difference between normal and fatty liver tissues, even when there is a lot of variation in classes and intensities overlap. Many tests have been performed on annotated abdominal CT datasets, and the results were based on accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, F1-score, and the Dice similarity coefficient. The results show that the new MLPO-Fuzzy-ResCNN framework does an excellent job of classifying things better than other methods. This study provides a strong, automated pipeline that can be used in clinical environments to help with early diagnosis and successful monitoring of fatty liver disease utilizing non-invasive imaging methods.

title: Human Perception of AI-Generated Digital Avatars: Exploring Uncanny Valley and Ethical Dimensions
authors: Akshat Thakur, Mayon Rajpoot, Harminder Kaur
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The paper offers an in-depth, multidimensional analysis of human perceptions with regard to AI-generated digital avatars. Key themes involve the uncanny valley effect, perceived usefulness, ethical awareness, and cultural sensitivity. In all, 12 participants took part in the survey using a validated 23-item instrument whose reliability was excellent for all factors, Cronbach's α>0.78. Key findings show that ethical awareness coexists with, rather than opposes, technology acceptance. Regression modeling showed that age and experience with AI significantly predict perceptions of usefulness, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.42, at a probability level below 0.05. Cluster analysis uncovered two distinct user clusters, namely, tech enthusiasts exhibiting high levels of acceptance and cautious evaluators preoccupied with cultural sensitivity. The correlations among factors reveal the role of ethical awareness as an acceptance moderator. These findings provide empirical guidance into culturally sensitive deployment and ethical governance of synthetic resurrection technologies. The currently conducted study falls within the scope of AI ethics, human-computer interaction, and policy development, further combining the approaches of psychological, utilitarian, normative, and contextual frames regarding the perception of synthetic avatars.

title: Research on a Blockchain-Based Encrypted Transmission Method for Network Communication Data
authors: Dong Wang, ZhengHong Zhang, Xiaoqi Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Based on blockchain technology, this paper proposes a highly secure data encryption transmission method for computer network communications. A layered system architecture is constructed, in which cryptographic hash algorithms and dynamic key management strategies are integrated to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and traceability of data transmission. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method exhibits significant advantages in improving data transmission efficiency, reducing network latency, and resisting man-in-the-middle attacks. The research findings provide a feasible reference for the construction of secure and reliable network communication systems.

title: Metadata-Aware Agentic Rag Framework for Disaster Response and Task Synthesis
authors: Savinu Aththanayake, Chemini Mallikarachchi, Sajeev Kugarajah, Janeesha Wickramasinghe
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Timely and contextually accurate information retrieval is a critical challenge in disaster response, where fragmented citizen help requests must be converted into field-executable tasks. While Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) offers a promising solution, standard implementations often lack the operational precision required for high-stakes scenarios. This paper presents a comparative study of a standard RAG and a proposed Agentic RAG framework designed for disaster coordination. The Agentic RAG enhances conventional retrieval by integrating adaptive query reformulation, metadata-aware filtering, and iterative self-assessment to improve semantic precision and operational relevance. Both workflows were evaluated over a curated knowledge base of disaster-response documents using an LLM-based judge with a structured rubric to assess outputs on relevance, safety, and specificity. This evaluation methodology itself contributes a reproducible framework for measuring the operational readiness of RAG outputs in this domain. Comparative results demonstrate that the Agentic RAG consistently achieves higher performance, particularly for complex or ambiguous help requests, producing more reliable and actionable guidance. This study provides strong evidence that agentic reasoning is a crucial component for building dependable AI-assisted decision-support systems in critical domains. Compared to the Standard RAG, the Agentic RAG shows a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.73 \times$</tex> fold increase in 'Excellent' judgments, consistent with the fold-change measure commonly used for comparative performance analysis.

title: A Gamified Knowledge-Guided Graph Learning Framework for Early Detection of Digital Cognitive Decline
authors: Surruthisha Sundareswaran, Banuka Athuraliya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The early identification of cognitive impairment among young adults (13-25 years old) is a critical yet under-studied area of neuro-epidemiology. Current studies indicate a rise in attention fragmentation and working memory impairment in younger cohorts, often attributed to the “Brain Rot” effects of chronic digital hyper-stimulation [1]. However, standard clinical tools like the MMSE and MoCA exhibit poor sensitivity to these subtle prodromal changes in high-functioning populations due to ceiling effects. To address this, a conceptual framework for a Knowledge-Guided Graph Neural Network (KG-GNN) is proposed. This study makes a threefold contribution. First, a gamification model validated by 15 clinical experts is designed to extract biomarkers while minimizing sensory overstimulation. Second, a novel knowledge injection strategy utilizes population-level priors from the massive NCPT dataset (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=757,427$</tex>) to augment local gameplay graphs, distinguishing individual behavior against a global baseline. Third, Explainable AI (SHAP) is integrated to render predictions clinically interpretable. This paper demonstrates a viable path for non-invasive, data-efficient cognitive screening.

title: Exploring Charge-Based Mosfet Compact Models with ACM-2 as a Design-Oriented Paradigm
authors: Rafaella Fiorelli, Matías Miguez, Juan Núñez
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Charge-based MOSFET compact models provide a physically consistent framework to describe transistor charges and capacitances across operating regimes. Unlike current-based approaches, they enforce charge conservation and yield reliable predictions of dynamic and RF behavior. This paper reviews the main charge-based formulations, ranging from industrial standards (BSIM, PSP, HiSIM) to academic compact models such as EKV and the recent ACM-2 five-parameter approach. We contrast their philosophies, complexity, and accuracy, highlighting the trade-offs between highly parameterized industrial models and compact analytical formulations oriented to design and education. Representative applications in analog/RF design, digital timing and power estimation are discussed. Particular attention is given to the lightweight ACM-2 model as a paradigmatic example of simplicity and analytical clarity. We conclude by outlining current challenges-advanced device architectures, quantum effects, and automated parameter extraction-and perspectives for future compact modeling in deeply scaled technologies.
