title: Speech and Text Based Students' Mental Health Detection System Using Natural Language Processing with Explainable Ai
authors: Janidu Himansa Welarathna, Pubudu Nallaperunma, Gayana Fernando
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Mental health challenges among students have emerged as a pressing global issue, with students experiencing serious psychological distress and concentration difficulties due to stress. A significant problem is that many students cannot identify their mental health struggles or pinpoint what causes their academic pressure, resulting in unnoticed difficulties that escalate gradually. Existing evaluation approaches do not effectively detect the underlying factors behind academic pressure or offer transparent reasoning suitable for clinical applications. This research presents a dual-modality system that combines speech emotion recognition with text-based mental health classification using Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Explainable AI (XAI) techniques. The system analyzes emotional patterns in students' voices to identify eight emotions while simultaneously processing spoken content to classify mental health concerns into academic, non-academic, or mixed categories. Seven machine learning (ML) algorithms and one deep learning (DL) model were evaluated for emotion recognition, with Random Forest (RF) achieving 83.66% accuracy. For mental health classification, a fine-tuned BERT model achieved 98.91 % accuracy. XAI techniques including LIME and SHAP provide transparent interpretations by highlighting which words support academic versus non-academic classifications, making the system suitable as a clinical decision support tool. The system operates effectively in uncontrolled environments with language-independent audio features, providing clinicians with a dependable supplementary assessment resource that preserves their professional judgment authority.

title: Design and Experimental Investigation of an Enhanced Solar Dryer with Polycarbonate Sheet and Thermal Energy Storage
authors: I Rahamathullah, T Jayakumar, A Kumaravadivel, S. Sugumar
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Solar drying is a good and a sustainable method used in preserving agricultural produce though the traditional open sun drying method has issues based on low efficiency, lack of quality control and limited weather dependence. The present research offers the design, fabrication and experimental study of an improved type of cabinet-based solar dryer that is combined with a polycarbonate sheet and thermal energy store to dry brinjal (Solanum melongene). The suggested system integrates a polycarbonate sheet drying chamber, forced air circulation and each of the following thermal stability enhancements such as a combined latent sensible heat storage system which uses phase change material (PCM) and high heat capacity material (HHCM) which is incorporated to enhance the thermal stability. Similar experimental studies involved three drying setups, that is, open sun drying, cabinet drying without PCM, and cabinet drying with PCM-HHCM. The dryer was tested on the basis of temperature change, solar radiation, rate of removal of moisture and time taken in the course of drying. Findings reveal that PCM-HHCM integrated dryer has gained the higher and more constant drying temperature and the highest drying temperature of about 72° C in contrast with the other settings. The increased thermal retention was a great boost in the speed of moisture removal and the time taken to completely dry brinjal was only a day long in a daylight cycle. The paper shows that the integrated impact of the polycarbonate sheet refraction and thermal energy storage is effective in enhancing its efficiency in drying, including the quality of products and thus the proposed system is applicable in the small scale of farming. (by using polycarbonate sheet the UV rays are not enter into the dryer so it will maintain the vitamin content present in the food product why because the UV rays affect the vitamin content in the food). Using PCM-HHCM to increase the drying time it will reduce the drying time and give more efficiency compare to open sun drying and cabinet solar drying).

title: Hybrid Genetic Algorithm and Fuzzy Control Approach for Lunar Lander Soft Landing
authors: Vitaly S. Oleynikov, Guan Tianyi
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: Lunar landing requires precise control of the descent trajectory to ensure both safety and fuel efficiency. This paper develops a practical control solution by combining the global optimization capability of genetic algorithms with the adaptive nature of fuzzy logic control. The initial conditions are set based on the Chang'e-3 mission profile, with an initial altitude of 15 km and horizontal velocity of 1.7 km/s[1]. The genetic algorithm generates an optimal reference trajectory by considering fuel consumption and various operational constraints. Fuzzy control rules are then formulated based on this trajectory to handle the real-time control task during landing. The fuzzy controller continuously adjusts the thrust to maintain trajectory tracking while accommodating disturbances and model uncertainties. Simulation results indicate that the proposed method achieves consistent landing performance with good accuracy and robustness, making it suitable for autonomous lunar landing missions.

title: Integrating Transformer and Generative Models for Intergenre Text Reframing
authors: Alina Sungurova, Yaroslava Isaeva, Ekaterina Isaeva
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: In the context of rapidly increasing volumes of specialized information, the problem of adapting complex texts to the cognitive characteristics of different user groups is gaining particular importance. The effectiveness of information perception largely depends on the reader’s background knowledge, education level, and familiarity with domain-specific terminology. This paper proposes an architectural framework for a system designed to perform cognitive adaptation of specialized texts. The suggested model integrates several modules, including text analysis, audience cognitive profiling, adaptive text generation with semantic preservation, and quality evaluation. The use of transformer-based language models in combination with corpus-driven audience representations enables the generation of texts aligned with the cognitive load and comprehension level of the target group. To solve the problem, we consider Ch. Fillmore’s frame semantics as the cognitive linguistic foundation of the study, and develop the workflow, which compiles a two-genre corpus, simulates framing profiles in the genre subcorpora using the FrameSemanticTransformer model, and finally generates the reframed texts according to the expected framing profile using the gigachat-0.1.43 model. The proposed architecture can be applied in the development of intelligent educational platforms, information and analytical systems, and media services aimed at delivering complex materials to non-specialist audiences without loss of meaning or accuracy.

title: Deep Learning Models for Detecting AI-Generated Faces: A Comparative Study
authors: Fahad Nahes, Saif Alharbi, Saleh Alsuhaimi, Abdullah Aljohani, Wael M.S. Yafooz
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: A major threat to digital trust and security is the proliferation of the highly realistic, synthetically generated media, especially faces of human beings developed by Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs). Automatically differentiating genuine and AI-generated facial images is essential to use in digital forensics and fighting fake news. The given research project is based on a strict comparative study of two prominent deep learning paradigms, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViT), in the context of the task of identifying faces generated by AI. In particular, the paper compares the results of a state-of-the-art CNN, EfficientNetB0 with that of a baseline Vision Transformer model, vit-base-patch16-224. Based on a high-quality publicly available face dataset of real and AI-generated faces. The empirical findings were conclusive: the EfficientNet-B0 model achieved accuracy of 98.4 %, while the ViT-Base-16 model achieved 98.7 %. As a result, both models delivered to strong performance. EfficientNet-B0 reached an AUC-ROC of 0.997, while ViT-B16 achieved 0.989. These results are higher than commonly reported baselines for this task.

title: AI-Driven Digital Pathology for Automated Disease Detection and Diagnosis: A Comprehensive Survey of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Innovations
authors: Roshan Sanu Y, N Prakash
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Digital pathology has enabled scalable analysis of histopathological data through whole-slide imaging (WSI) and computational methods. Recent advances in artificial intelligence (AI) have significantly improved automated disease detection, grading, segmentation, and prognostic modelling from histological images. This paper presents a structured narrative survey of AI-driven approaches in digital pathology, covering traditional machine learning methods, convolutional neural networks, multiple instance learning, transformer-based architectures, and emerging self-supervised and foundation models. The survey distinguishes between patch-level and slidelevel evaluation paradigms, emphasizing their respective roles in model development and clinical decision-making. Public datasets, preprocessing techniques, and commonly used evaluation metrics are summarized to contextualize model benchmarking. Key clinical applications across major cancer types are reviewed, alongside critical challenges including data variability, annotation scarcity, interpretability, computational complexity, regulatory readiness, and environmental sustainability. The maturity of foundation models for clinical deployment and their carbon cost implications for routine pathology laboratories is discussed. Finally, future research directions toward efficient, interpretable, and clinically deployable AI systems are highlighted.

title: Neural Network–Based Optimization of Base Station Placement for Local Positioning Systems
authors: Mikhail Vdovichev, Denis Bogdanov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: This paper deals with the problem of placing base stations of a local radio positioning system when the users are located inside a building and the infrastructure is deployed around it. In this situation, both the geometric configuration of the stations and the radio signal attenuation in the building must be taken into account. For a given floor plan, candidate layouts of three base stations are evaluated using a joint criterion based on the geometric factor and a coverage measure derived from a path-loss model with wall attenuation. The best layouts, found by exhaustive search, are then used as training data for a neural network that predicts near-optimal base station coordinates for new buildings.

title: Real-Time Multi-Modal Deepfake Detection Using Spatiotemporal Attention Networks and Cross-Domain Adversarial Training
authors: A P. Gopu, R. Rakesh, P. Swetha, P. Tharun, S. Gopinath, K. Baby
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The CNN-LSTM structures implemented for deepfake detection approaches have displayed effectiveness, but their accuracy has been limited and cross domain success has been limited due to less than optimal spatiotemporal modeling and ability to detectdeepfakes in real time. To improve upon these limitations, our work proposes MS-STAN (Multi-Scale Spatiotemporal Attention Network), a spatiotemporal framework for deepfake detection that utilizes attention mechanisms to improve understanding of spatial features and temporal coherence. To accomplish this, the proposed model integrates EfficientNetV2 for enhanced extraction of spatial features, Bi-directional LSTMs to better exploit temporal dependencies, and self-attention to better aggregate temporal dependencies. To reinforce generalization by training across domains and without further delay, cross-domain adversarial training is also employed. Experimental findings on FaceForensics++, Celeb-DF, DFDCshow a significant improvement in accuracy and real-time detection at 45 FPS with an accuracy of 77%.In addition, our proposed spatiotemporal detection implements heatmaps to visualize spatiotemporal features that contributed to the decision in the classification process.

title: MarkEditor: Precise and Controllable 3D Gaussian Editing with Text-Driven Optimization
authors: Cen Li, Peng Zhou, Hanfei Hu, Jianchao Wang, Rong Quan, Jie Qin
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has gained significant attention in the 3D field; however, achieving precise and controllable editing remains a challenging problem. This paper introduces MarkEditor, a framework for precise and localized editing of 3D Gaussians. It uses a text-driven approach, beginning with a marked region as a coarse editing guide. The process consists of three stages. First, we use text-to-3D model to generate an initial 3DGS from a textual description. Second, we align it with the marked area through geometric optimization. Third, we employ a freezing mechanism and utilize Score Distillation Sampling (SDS) for global optimization of the appearance, while updating only the content within the marked region, resulting in the final edited 3D Gaussian scene. MarkEditor provides a precise and controllable approach for 3D Gaussians editing, achieving results that outperform state-of-the-art methods.

title: Agentic AI for Recon, Threat Analysis and Mitigation Reporting
authors: Kalaivani T, Charanya P, Abhindhira KP, Barath Kumar L, Santhosraj BS, Srinithi S
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Digital attacks in the ever-changing digital environment are becoming highly complex, more common, and dynamic, and this has become a major threat to organization of all sizes. In this paper, the researcher introduces the design of an autonomous and intelligent cybersecurity system that will carry out reconnaissance, threat reporting, and mitigation of websites and IP addresses. Based on an agentic AI architecture, the system uses dedicated AI agents to perform tasks through information collection, vulnerability scanning, and threat pattern matching and result interpreting, which do not require the training of new models. Instead, it runs on the top of existing Large Language Models (LLM), such as GPT and open-source ones, to comprehend and argue about complex security situations A User Interface is used to enter missions, an Orchestrator is used to sequence tasks, Agent modules are used to interact with industry-standard pentesting tools (such as Nmap, Nikto, and SQLmap), a Safety module precludes actions that are beyond its purview, and a Reporter module provides actionable insights. Its framework is focused on automation, adaptability, and scalability, and also limits the use of manual analysis and allows quicker and more precise threat detection and remedies. By means of continuous learning and effective management of various penetration testing situations, this approach becomes a viable way of enhancing cybersecurity measures in small organizations and learning institutions.

title: Balancing Fairness and Accuracy in Credit Scoring: Logistic Regression Vs Random Forest
authors: Akshant Mukesh, Mourad Messaadia
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: Credit scoring systems are very important for deciding whether to approve loans. They have a direct impact on household welfare, financial inclusion, and the overall stability of the economy. But as machine learning (ML) becomes more common in credit evaluation, worries about fairness and possible bias have grown. Models trained on historical data may mechanically perpetuate or impair socioeconomic inequalities, disadvantaging borrower demographics. This study examines the balance between prediction accuracy and fairness in two commonly utilized models, Logistic Regression (LR) and Random Forest (RF), employing the German Credit Dataset with a 70:30 train-test split. The findings reveal that Random Forest had marginally superior predictive performance, but Logistic Regression displayed enhanced gender parity, and Random Forest illustrated improved age-related fairness. No single model exhibited advantage across all criteria. So, the credit risk assessment needs to find a compromise between demographic fairness and prediction accuracy. This shows how important it is to use fairness-aware model evaluation to make sure that lending operations are fair, responsible, and open.

title: A New Implementation Form of Optical Neural Network based on Broad Learning Model
authors: Tiancheng Wu, Tao Li, Wei Li, Hong Yang, Jiao Shi, Yu Lei
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Optical neural network (ONN) systems are receiving increasing attention from researchers based on their rapid transmission speed, substantial information capacity, high parallelism, and robustness against interference. These qualities position ONNs as a promising solution to overcome the physical constraints faced by von Neumann architecture-based electronic neural networks, such as processing speed, energy consumption, and bandwidth limitations. Traditional ONN systems improve performance by cascading optical devices. However, this approach introduces errors at each layer and renders the system highly sensitive to alignment and manufacturing errors of individual components. Consequently, model building times tend to be relatively lengthy. To address these challenges, we have developed a novel approach, BLS-ONN, based on the broad learning system (BLS) theory for ONN systems. Our method constructs a spatial optical ONN architecture using a shallow-width optical diffraction structure, marking a significant advancement in the field. The proposed architecture perfectly avoids the inherent optical system error issues associated with deep cascades in traditional ONN systems while ensuring performance on par with baseline cascaded ONN systems. Experimental results across two different tasks demonstrate that the proposed BLS-ONN system exhibits superior feature extraction and information retention capabilities. Additionally, the test conducted on misalignment errors proves that the novel spatial optical ONN architecture has sufficient and superior anti-noise performance compared to traditional ONN systems

title: X-Ray Radiograph-Based Knee Osteoarthritis Severity Grading Using Efficientnet-B0
authors: R. Ramani, A. Selvakumar, A. Bajulunisha, S. Murugan, Aparna Jose, Monisha R
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Knee osteoarthritis (KOA) is a gradual degenerative joint disease that significantly impairs mobility and quality of life. X-ray radiographs are often used for osteoarthritis diagnosis; however, severity assessment using the Kellgren-Lawrence (KL) scale is subjective and significantly reliant on clinical skill, resulting in inter-observer variability. This paper presents an automated approach for rating the severity of KOA using the EfficientNet-B0 deep learning architecture to overcome this constraint. The model is trained and assessed using 9,786 knee X-ray images sourced from a publicly accessible Kaggle dataset, categorized into five KL classes from normal to severe osteoarthritis. Image preprocessing and data augmentation approaches are used to increase feature representation, improve generalization, and alleviate class imbalance. Experimental findings indicate that the proposed EfficientNet-B0 model attains an overall classification accuracy of 99.70 % across all severity categories. The model accurately detects essential radiographic characteristics, including joint space constriction and osteophyte development. EfficientNet-B0 outperforms traditional convolutional neural network (CNN) models while exhibiting less computational complexity. The proposed methodology provides a dependable, efficient, and impartial decision-support system for doctors, facilitating consistent and timely evaluation of KOA severity using X-ray images.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning–Based Data Privacy Framework for Real-Time Industrial Sensor Networks
authors: T. Saveetha, R. Ramya, S. Anto Martin Pradeep, Kalaivani Balaji, Jai Ganesh. B, Bakkiyalakshmi. R
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The paper introduces a profound reinforcement learning-based data privacy system that can be applied to real-time industrial sensor networks with very rigid performance and confidentiality requirements. The framework uses policy-optimization mechanisms to control the exposure of data whilst preserving the analytical utility of dynamic industrial processes. The analysis on the experiment shows that the average reward of 0.87, privacy leakage reduction of 42% and utility preservation of 91 percent are notable when the network loads vary. The system will be able to accommodate variation in sensors, different patterns of threats and high frequency data streams without compromising operational information. A stability index analysis also indicates the reliability of convergence in updated policies, making it possible to have similar behavior on long term industrial loads. The suggested solution will provide a fair trade-off between data safeguarding and operational efficiency, which is a crucial aspect of Industry 4.0 settings and secure and intelligent automation. The findings confirm the practicability of reinforcement learning as an online privacy controller of industrial complex ecosystems.

title: Enhancing Lung Cancer Detection Accuracy with CrossViT-Lung: A Vision Transformer-Based Approach
authors: Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed, Zinat Tasneem
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Lung cancer (LC) is the leading cause of death globally. Early detection of lung tumors is essential in reducing the number of LC cases and increasing the survival rate. CrossViT-Lung is a model that uses a Cross-Attention MultiScale Vision Transformer (ViT) that is specifically designed to increase the accuracy of LC diagnosis while being computationally efficient. It uses two parallel branches operating at different spatial scales, enabling the model to recognise both large and small features that are essential for diagnosing LC. In addition to the original feature extraction of the ViT, the model uses EfficientNet-B7, which is known for its global context understanding using self-attention. The accuracy of the CrossViT-Lung model is 99.41 %, which is higher than the accuracy of EfficientNet-B7, which is 94.53 %, in the detection of small and scattered patterns in LC images. This proves that the model is efficient in the early detection of LC and is great advancement in the field. The results demonstrate the potential of multi-scale transformer designs and cross-attention in creating trustworthy computer-aided diagnostic systems for early detection of LC.

title: LLM-TDA: A LLM-based Tabular Data Augmentation Framework
authors: Rui Lin, Zhiwei Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Tabular data augmentation is a critical technique for improving machine learning performance in data-limited scenarios. However, existing methods face inherent limitations in balancing quality and efficiency. Filter-based approaches tend to introduce low-quality or irrelevant features due to the lack of task-specific feedback, while wrapper-based methods incur prohibitive time overheads necessitated by massive downstream model training. Fundamentally, both paradigms rely solely on statistical properties, neglecting the rich semantic information in tabular data. To address these limitations, this paper proposes LLM-TDA, a novel framework that leverages the semantic reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) to optimize the feature augmentation process. LLM-TDA employs a coarse-to-fine pipeline: first, a table-level analysis rapidly prunes semantically irrelevant tables based on business logic; second, a fine-grained feature selection mechanism fuses semantic understanding with statistical assessment to identify high-value features. Extensive experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate that LLM-TDA significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in downstream task performance. Notably, it achieves this accuracy while delivering a 10-fold to 14-fold improvement in computational efficiency compared to wrapper-based methods, providing a robust and scalable solution for automated feature engineering.

title: A User-Centric Approach to Fitness Tracking and Nutrition Guidance Using Machine Learning Classification and Clustering
authors: Shiela David, Varsha Veronica A, Vandhana R
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The importance of health and fitness management in contemporary society has been risen significantly, with sedentary lifestyles and unhealthy eating patterns contributing to overall health concerns. Traditional means of assessing fitness and nutrition generally fail to take into consideration individual differences when creating a plan for managing one's health and fitness. Rather, they provide users with static, pre-generated recommendations based on a specific criterion that inhibit users from continuing to use the service for an extended period of time and do not provide users with the most accurate information about their health and fitness. In order to help solve the problem of traditional assessment methods, we will provide users with a web-based fitness tracking and nutrition guidance application that uses multiple users, machine learning classification and clustering models, and principles of gamification. Users will be able to input their health goals into the application and track their individual activity levels, including their daily steps taken, calories expended, exercise sessions completed, hours of sleep, heart rate and BMI. The classification models used to classify users based on their level of activity are Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Logistic Regression. All three classification models will classify users into levels of low, moderate and high activity levels, with Random Forest achieving at least 99.5 % accuracy. The Random Forest classification model has outperformed the other two models, as it accounts for nonlinear relationships that exist between data sets, and because it accounts for participants that have very similar physiological make-ups. Besides, the KMeans clustering system allows for participants who demonstrate similar patterns of fitness and health behaviours to receive simultaneous personalized prescriptions and to engage with other K-Means community members. The gamification principles include streaks, achievements, leaderboards, and points to increase long-term adherence. The fitness tracker system also incorporates a dashboard reporting a range of visualizations for users, which assists users with their workouts, alongside peer comparisons.

title: A Neural Network Approach for Speech to Text Translation of Low Resource Language
authors: Kumaresh Sheelavant, Preksha G P, Sakshi Shanbhag, Shreya B C, Sneha T, Shilpa Patil
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Developing speech translation systems for low-resource languages remains a major issue because lack of annotated datasets and linguistic resources. This work introduces a neural speech-to-text translation framework designed for low-resource Indian languages, with Konkani as a case study. The proposed system converts spoken Konkani into English text through Hindi as a pivot language, integrating Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) and Neural Machine Translation (NMT) models within a unified architecture. The methodology involves audio preprocessing, feature extraction, and pivot-based translation using pretrained models such as IndicConformer and Transformer-based NMT to ensure fluency and adaptability. By leveraging transfer learning and multilingual modeling, the approach effectively addresses issues of data scarcity, accent variation, and linguistic diversity. In comparison, our proposed pipeline attains 29.6% WER and 28.1 BLEU, demonstrating competitive performance despite operating in a low-resource setting. These results show that our model performs on par with, and in some cases surpasses, existing benchmarks for similar multilingual pipelines.

title: FreqHDR: Frequency-Enhanced HDR Video Reconstruction
authors: Tianxin Huang, Yun Zhou, Tian He, Zhixin Zheng, Aidong Men, Zhuqing Jiang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Reconstructing high dynamic range (HDR) video from alternating exposure sequences remains highly challenging. Existing methods primarily rely on optical flow or attention mechanisms for low dynamic range (LDR) sequence alignment and ghosting artifact suppression. However, optical flow integration alone proves insufficient for consistent performance improvement in dynamic/static scenes, exhibiting limited noise suppression and suboptimal detail recovery. To address these limitations, a novel exposure fusion framework, FreqHDR, is proposed, incorporating a dedicated Spatial-Frequency Integration Block (SFIB). The encoder adopts a stage-wise cascaded architecture for progressive multi-scale feature extraction, with each stage enhanced by the SFIB module. The SFIB follows a dual-path design: the spatial path employs convolutions and channel attention to capture local structural details, while the frequency path utilizes Fourier transforms for global semantic context. Features from both domains are dynamically fused and refined via a gated residual connection, significantly strengthening representation learning. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior or competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches on benchmark datasets.

title: Soft-Switching ZCS Commutated Current-Fed DAB DC/DC Converter with New Modulation Scheme
authors: Nil Patel, Sheron Figardo, Akshay Kumar Rathore
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This paper analyzes a soft-switching ZCS currentfed dual active bridge (DAB) isolated DC/DC converter. Through a novel secondary-modulation technique, the topology achieves natural voltage clamping across the primary-side devices and resolves the long-standing issue of voltage spikes across devices during turn-off, this solution eliminates unnecessary snubbers or clamping circuits. As a result, the converter is snubber-free, characterized by reduced hardware complexity, enhanced compactness, and improved costeffectiveness. By enabling zero-current switching (ZCS) on the primary side and zero-current turn-on switching on the secondary side, the converter effectively reduces switching losses. The feasibility of the proposed modulation scheme is verified through the hardware results of a 1.5 kW lab prototype.

title: The Concept of Constructing Normalized Scales for Diagnostic Results of Complex Objects
authors: Artem A. Geleta, Elena S. Suloeva
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: The report is devoted to issues of metrological assurance of diagnostics. Diagnostic methods aimed at assessing the state of complex objects are presented. Within the framework of the metrological approach, the problem of low informativeness of results reducible to a binary choice is investigated. A transition to the use of normalized scales for quantitative assessment is proposed as a solution. The result of the work is implementation variants for constructing scales based on the principles of metrological assurance, allowing for an increase in the informativeness of diagnostics.

title: Improvement on optical signal bit rate recovery algorithm based on multiple ultrashort optical pulse samplings
authors: Lin Zuo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: In software-synchronized optical sampling technology, the original optical signal is sampled twice using sampling pulses with different repetition frequencies. By performing fast Fourier transform and chirp Z transform on the sampled signals, the equivalent sampling bit number is obtained, and the rate of the original optical signal is recovered, which is called the signal rate recovery algorithm. However, when the equivalent sampling bit numbers of the two sampled signals satisfy specific conditions, the signal rate recovery algorithm fails, and the recovered rate is always less than the true rate, which is called the blind zone of the signal rate recovery algorithm. Through theoretical analysis and numerical simulation, the conditions for the existence of the blind zone are confirmed. An improved scheme is proposed, which involves using another sampling pulse with different repetition frequency besides the existing two sampling pulses for sampling. The equivalent sampling bit numbers of all the three samplings are obtained through the software synchronization algorithm, and three rates are recovered, the maximum value among which is taken as the correct original signal rate. The feasibility of this scheme is verified through numerical simulation and experimental investigation, which shows the blind zone is eliminated.

title: GRACE-RAG: Graph Retrieval with Adaptive Chunk Extraction for Long-Context Question Answering
authors: Tianwei Huang, Shuai Lei, Askar Hamdulla, Chunxiao Gao, Huaping Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) improves factuality by grounding large language models (LLMs) on external corpora, but it still struggles with multi-hop reasoning and long-context overload. We propose GRACE-RAG, a two-stage framework that (i) builds a fine-grained chunk–sentence–entity graph via dynamic chunking during offline indexing, (ii) performs query-aware entity activation and personalized PageRank for associative retrieval online, and (iii) adaptively compresses retrieved evidence under a token budget before answer generation. Experiments on seven QA benchmarks show consistent gains over strong graph-based RAG baselines.

title: Modeling a Reduced Hybrid Quantum-Inspired Framework for Optimized Speckle Denoising Filters
authors: M Su-Ada, S Shailesh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: This paper examines a hybrid classical-quantum workflow for speckle-oriented image denoising, in which denoising is formulated as a discrete filter-parameter selection problem over image batches. Candidate convolution kernels are evaluated using batch-averaged peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) and structural similarity (SSIM), and the best-performing kernel is deployed within a variance-guided adaptive filtering stage. The selected kernel configuration is then encoded into a quantum register and marked using a Grover oracle, followed by amplitude amplification to increase the likelihood of retrieving the target configuration upon measurement. In this formulation, the quantum circuit serves to demonstrate parameter encoding, oraclebased marking, and probabilistic retrieval consistent with the classically selected optimum, rather than replacing classical fitness evaluation. Experiments conducted on speckle-contaminated image batches from multiple imaging modalities show that the optimized kernel provides stable denoising performance, while the quantum subroutine reproduces the target configuration with high probability under practical circuit-depth constraints.

title: Machine Learning Models and Power BI Dashboards for Predicting Employee Attrition in a Manufacturing Company in Bahrain
authors: Hawra Nasser, Dana Alshoala, Mariam Alshaikh, Noor Hassan, Minwir Al-Shammari
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: Employee attrition is emerging as a growing concern for organizations, particularly in industrial sectors where skilled labor retention is critical to productivity and continuity. This research aims to investigate the determinants of employee attrition at Aluminum Bahrain (ALBA) using a combination of secondary data and primary sources. The study uses the IBM Human Resources (HR) Analytics Attrition dataset to identify key drivers of turnover with Python-based machine learning (ML) models, specifically logistic regression, decision trees, and random forests. Additionally, we directly asked ALBA employees what was on their minds by sending out a well-organized survey to gather their thoughts on their jobs, work-life balance, communication, and opportunities for advancement. Results were presented visually in Power BI dashboards to derive actionable insights. The findings recognize overtime, non-promotion, and job stress as key causes of attrition. The study concludes by offering strategic recommendations to reduce turnover and enhance employee engagement through fact-based decisionmaking.

title: Multi-Modal Longitudinal Framework for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis Using DPTPSAN and SHAP-Based Interpretability
authors: Virendra V. Bagade, Yashwant V. Dongre, Laxmi Bewoor
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: This paper is addressing AD diagnosis on pre-clinical stage by considering the longitudinal multimodal biomarker studies which reveal that the continuum of Alzheimer's disease (AD) includes a long latent phase, referred to as preclinical AD, which precedes the onset of symptoms by decades. Diagnosis treatment during the preclinical AD phase offers an optimal opportunity for slowing the progression of disease. Despite significant advancements in the field of Alzheimer's disease (AD) diagnosis, current diagnostic models still face several challenges that hinder their clinical applicability. Most existing methods focus either on neuroimaging or clinical data in isolation, without effectively leveraging the complementary strengths of multi-modal data. Moreover, many diagnostic frameworks rely on cross-sectional data, ignoring the temporal dynamics that are crucial for understanding the progression of AD. The integration of longitudinal clinical data with imaging information remains underexplored, and synthetic adjustment of such heterogeneous data is rarely addressed in the literature. Furthermore, issues like class imbalance, suboptimal feature fusion, limited interpretability, and lack of real-world deployment mechanisms-such as mobile-based tools-further exacerbate the challenge.

title: Achieving Secure and Reliable URLLC in Cell-Free Massive MIMO via Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Donggen Li, Shuo Sun, Xinxin Ma, Hengjie Ren, Chong Huang, Wenjiang Feng
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: The stringent requirements of ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC) contribute to an enhanced service experience for user equipments (UEs). However, it also poses a challenge to the design of security strategies when UEs require multiple businesses while the communication resources are constrained. This letter focuses on the security and reliability of URLLC for uplink transmission in cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) systems. We define a new metric, secure and reliable probability (SRP), to characterize the trade-off between secure and reliable services in the system. Furthermore, we propose a power control approach based on self-supervised learning, which adjusts the user's power allocation strategy to meet maximum security or maximum reliability requirements of different UEs and improve the performance of the whole system. Finally, extensive simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: DQN-Based Resource Allocation and Offloading (DQN-RAO) Algorithm for Multi-User Multi-Server MEC Network
authors: Hoa Tran-Dang, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper proposes a DQN-based resource allocation and offloading (DQN-RAO) Algorithm designed to minimize the average task delay in Multi-User Multi-Server Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) networks. We address the inherent complexity of the joint task-server assignment and resource allocation problem through a two-phase decomposition. First, a Deep Q-Network (DQN) learns the optimal global strategy for the discrete task offloading assignment. Second, a convex optimization model efficiently calculates the optimal CPU frequency allocation for the determined assignment. Comparative simulation results demonstrate that the DQN-RAO algorithm consistently achieves the lowest average task delay, significantly surpassing both the random offloading algorithm (ROA) and the greedy offloading algorithm (GOA) across varying server capacities and task complexities.

title: Automated Malaria Diagnosis Using a Fine-Tuned EfficientNetB3 Model on Blood Smear Images
authors: Mukesh Kumar, Sandeep Kumar, Gaurav Sharma, G Santhosh Kumar, Zaid Alsalami, Anurag Shrivastava, Deepak Banerjee
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Malaria is a major global health issue with great death especially in tropical and subtropical regions. Correct and quick diagnosis is essential for good prevention and control of diseases. Historically, the diagnosis of malaria has hinged on hand microscopic inspection of blood smears, a technique prone to human error as well as time-consuming and labour-intensive character. By building an automated malaria detection system using an upgraded EfficientNetB3 model, our work seeks to address these problems. Designed to split blood smear images into parasitised and uninfected groups, the EfficientNetB3 design celebrated their harmonic scaling of depth, width, and resolution. With an F1-score of 0.97 the model proved dependability and durability by showing amazing general accuracy of 97% for both classes. Particularly in environments with limited resources, these findings underline the promise of the customised EfficientNetB3 model to greatly increase malaria diagnosis, hence improving patient outcomes and supporting more efficient disease control.

title: Deep Learning-Based Bird Species Classification Using ResNet-50
authors: Girish S, Yukesh S, Balaji Kannan K, Vigneshwar B, Vishnu Sekhar R
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Taxonomic classification of biological species plays a vital role in biodiversity monitoring, ecological research, and conservation efforts. Recent advances in deep learning have enabled automated species identification using visual data, reducing reliance on manual expertise. In this work, we investigate the effectiveness of transfer learning-based convolutional neural networks for bird species classification using a curated dataset of Indian birds. A systematic experimental study is conducted using ResNet architectures pretrained on ImageNet, focusing on the impact of feature extraction strategies, partial fine-tuning, and data augmentation. We compare a frozen backbone baseline against fine-tuned models with varying degrees of trainable layers and evaluate their performance across multiple training configurations. Additionally, a comparison between ResNet-50 and ResNet-152 is performed to analyze the trade-off between network depth and classification accuracy. To further assess the influence of dataset complexity, experiments are conducted on a reduced subset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex> visually distinctive bird species. The results demonstrate that partial fine-tuning of higher network layers combined with data augmentation significantly improves classification performance, achieving high validation accuracy while maintaining stable training behavior. This study highlights the effectiveness of controlled fine-tuning strategies for fine-grained taxonomic classification tasks.

title: Early detection method for high-risk diabetic foot based on infrared thermal imaging
authors: Xiao Wu, Zhen Ma, Xi Chen, Diming Liu, Huarui Liu, Yang Xu, Xianjun Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Diabetic foot ulcers represent a severe complication of diabetes, associated with high rates of amputation and mortality risk. Consequently, early identification of high-risk foot lesions is crucial for prevention. Since abnormal plantar temperature changes often precede ulcer formation, infrared thermography has emerged as a promising screening tool. However, its clinical application is limited by uneven temperature distribution and a primary focus on established ulcers. This study integrates the deep features from infrared images, and plantar temperature characteristics to construct a machine learning-based classification model for high-risk diabetic foot lesions. After evaluating multiple classifiers and feature fusion strategies, the XGBoost model trained using fused ResNet-101 Global Average Pooling features and temperature features demonstrated optimal performance. This confirms the potential of multimodal infrared thermography models for clinical early screening and preventive management.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Real-Time Student and Tutor Matching: A Design Science Implementation Through FocusDesk
authors: MAVKT Muramudali, Nirosha Wedasinghe
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: The demand for timely, topic-specific academic support is increasing among university students in Sri Lanka, while existing digital learning platforms provide limited mechanisms for real-time, micro-level tutoring. In response, this study adopts a Design Science Research (DSR) methodology to design, implement, and evaluate FocusDesk, a web-based micro-tutoring platform that integrates real-time peer-to-peer interaction with a hybrid recommendation mechanism. The recommendation model combines content-based similarity modeling using TF-IDF with collaborative filtering to support tutor matching under sparse interaction conditions. Experimental evaluation was conducted using a combination of pre-deployment model initialization and interaction data collected during system usage, complemented by a controlled pilot study to assess usability. The results indicate stable matching behavior under early-stage deployment conditions and a positive user experience for short, topic-focused tutoring sessions. This study demonstrates the technical feasibility and practical viability of integrating hybrid machine-learning based matching with real-time micro-tutoring in a resource constrained higher education context. The results highlight the potential of such systems to support timely, topic-focused academic assistance and provide a foundation for future investigations into learning outcomes, fairness-aware recommendation, and scalable peerlearning platforms.

title: A Novel Multi-Factor Authentication Technique Using DNA Cryptography to Safeguard Oil Companies' Data
authors: Amal Khaleel Hamad, Mishall Al-Zubaidie
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: Cyber transaction protection is one of the largest concerns to oil firms due to the increasing security and privacy threats. Rivest Cipher 4 (RC4), and ElGamal cryptographies are only two among the many frameworks and measures that are already being implemented to secure transactions in the cyber world. Nevertheless, they possess their drawbacks like slow performance, breakability, obsolescence, and susceptibility to the whale-phishing attack. This paper introduces an efficient means of guarding Internet of Things (IoT) communications and oil industry cyber transactions depending on zero trust architecture (ZTA). It is an amalgamation of advanced practices such as tokenization (TK), data integration/DNA cryptography, GPS (global positioning system)-based authentication, password salting and hashing. This strategy will enhance the security of online transactions in the oil industry by enhancing the degree of privacy and reliability of cyber transactions through incorporation of extensive, robust, and effective verification frameworks that can withstand complex cybers attacks. The proposed framework is assessed theoretically to prove the security and performance of the system. In this study, we have been able to provide a theoretical security analysis against various threats and comparisons to show that our proposal would be better than the past research.

title: AI-Driven Dual CNN System for Automated Tomato Ripeness Classification and Defect Detection
authors: L.P.G.K.G. Lankawarna, K.A.P.M. Perera, S.L.D.P. Pramodya, A.A. Lakshitha, E.R.J. Samarakoon
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Tomato is one of the most perishable food commodities, and post-harvest handling of tomato is very crucial. In most commercial supply chains, tomato sorting and grading are done manually using manpower. Manual sorting of tomatoes is usually subjective, inconsistent, and laborintensive, leading to misclassification and economic losses. This research study was conducted to address the above challenges by developing a dual Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) system that simultaneously classifies tomatoes based on their ripening stages and outer surface defects. Images of Gowri F1 hybrid tomatoes were used for this study. The tomato grading criteria used in this study align with those of the Keells supermarket chain. The images were captured under diverse lighting and background conditions. At the end of the study, the ripening stage classification model achieved 97% accuracy, while the defects detection model achieved 55% accuracy. This dual-output model processes each tomato image once and predicts both ripening stage and defect status in a single pass. Findings of this study demonstrate that deep learning can improve post-harvest quality management in tomato supply chains, though challenges remain in detecting subtle defects.

title: Delay Tolerant Precaching Scheme Based on Proximal Policy Optimization in Content-Centric Vehicular Networks
authors: Jeongtak Na, Jongpil Youn, Irina Em, Euisin Lee, Youngju Nam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The surge in multimedia demand from autonomous driving and infotainment stresses RSUs; mobility and limited wireless capacity cause delays and QoS degradation. While CCVN precaching has been studied, delay-tolerant services remain underexplored. We present a PPO-based delay-tolerant precaching scheme featuring path-aware RSU discovery, proportional precaching decisions, and opportunistic transmission within deadlines. A tailored reward balances delivery success, backhaul reduction, and traffic efficiency. Simulation results in Large-scale SUMO experiments show that our scheme outperforms a heuristic scheme in content delivery success ratio and traffic overhead, demonstrating scalable and adaptive learning for delay-tolerant vehicular content delivery.

title: Hybrid Beamforming for Subarray-Level Movable Antenna Enhanced MU-MIMO Communications
authors: Shanshan Zhang, Songjie Yang, Wenxuan Zhang, Youzhi Xiong, Siya Yao
abstract: This study investigates subarray-level movable antenna (MA) architecture for multi-user MIMO (MU-MIMO) systems. Unlike conventional systems with fixed-position antennas (FPAs), the proposed scheme harnesses the additional positional degrees of freedom (DoFs) of movable subarrays to enhance spatial multiplexing capabilities for both multi-user and multi-stream communications. Our objective is to maximize the overall system spectral efficiency by jointly optimizing the hybrid beamforming design and the positions of all subarrays. To tackle this challenging non-convex optimization problem, we first adopt a block diagonalization (BD) based digital precoder to effectively eliminate multi-user interference. Subsequently, the joint optimization of the analog beamformer and the subarray positions is efficiently solved using a sequential interference cancellation (SIC)-based algorithm. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed SIC-MA method significantly outperforms the benchmark SIC-FPA scheme where subarrays are fixed.

title: Industry 4.0 Design Principles in Continuous Production Processes: A Case Study
authors: Aline M. Culchesk, Vagner L. Gava, Ana L. F. Facin, Rodrigo F. Gonçalves, Mauro de M. Spinola
abstract: This study investigates the application of Industry 4.0 design principles in a continuous production environment, specifically in an iron grinding plant. The research aims to understand how principles such as interconnection, information transparency, technical assistance, decentralized decisions, and sustainability, are implemented in the mining sector, and to identify the associated challenges. A case study was conducted in a plant that received financial support to adapt to Industry 4.0 technologies, supported by interviews with industry experts. The results revealed a focus on interconnection and sustainability, which were considered key to optimizing mining processes. Technological implications include the need to move beyond traditional automation by integrating smart sensors, IoT platforms, AI tools, and sustainability-focused solutions. Regarding process type, the study highlights continuous processes, typical in mining, facing greater challenges for Industry 4.0 implementation compared to discrete ones, due to their rigidity and complexity. Managerial implications include the analysis of implementation costs, scalability of technologies, modularization feasibility, and economic viability, especially for small and medium-sized enterprises. These findings provide valuable insights into the practical application of Industry 4.0 in mining and emphasize the need for continuous innovation and strategic adaptation to overcome barriers and fully benefit from digital transformation in continuous production industries.

title: KidMesh: Computational Mesh Reconstruction for Pediatric Congenital Hydronephrosis Using Deep Neural Networks
authors: Haoran Sun, Zhanpeng Zhu, Anguo Zhang, Bo Liu, Zhaohua Lin, Liqin Huang, Mingjing Yang, Lei Liu, Shan Lin, Wangbin Ding
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Pediatric congenital hydronephrosis (CH) is a common urinary tract disorder, primarily caused by obstruction at the renal pelvis-ureter junction. Magnetic resonance urography (MRU) can visualize hydronephrosis, including renal pelvis and calyces, by utilizing the natural contrast provided by water. Existing voxel-based segmentation approaches can extract CH regions from MRU, facilitating disease diagnosis and prognosis. However, these segmentation methods predominantly focus on morphological features, such as size, shape, and structure. To enable functional assessments, such as urodynamic simulations, external complex post-processing steps are required to convert these results into mesh-level representations. To address this limitation, we propose an end-to-end method based on deep neural networks, namely KidMesh, which could automatically reconstruct CH meshes directly from MRU. Generally, KidMesh extracts feature maps from MRU images and converts them into feature vertices through grid sampling. It then deforms a template mesh according to these feature vertices to generate the specific CH meshes of MRU images. Meanwhile, we develop a novel schema to train KidMesh without relying on accurate mesh-level annotations, which are difficult to obtain due to the sparsely sampled MRU slices. Experimental results show that KidMesh reconstructs CH meshes in an average of 0.4 seconds, and achieve comparable performance to conventional methods without requiring post-processing. The reconstructed meshes exhibited no self-intersections, with only 3.7% and 0.2% of the vertices having error distances exceeding 3.2mm and 6.4mm, respectively. After rasterization, these meshes achieved a Dice score of 0.86 against manually delineated CH masks. Furthermore, these meshes could be used in renal urine flow simulations, providing valuable urodynamic information for clinical practice.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Enabled Joint Task Offloading and Trajectory Optimization in Multi-UAV Systems
authors: Ju Li, Fengjun Shang
abstract: UAV-assisted multi-access edge computing (MEC) enables flexible task offloading in emergency communication networks, but joint trajectory planning and task offloading optimization remain challenging due to UAV mobility and dynamic network conditions. This paper proposes a multi-agent deep deterministic policy gradient (MADDPG)-based framework to jointly optimize UAV trajectory planning and task offloading decisions. The proposed approach follows a centralized training with decentralized execution (CTDE) paradigm, enabling UAVs to learn coordinated computation and communication strategies in dynamic environments. Each UAV is modeled as an autonomous agent, and an actor–critic architecture is designed, where distributed actors generate offloading and movement policies, while a centralized critic evaluates global state–action values to guide cooperative learning. To improve training stability and convergence efficiency, experience replay and soft update mechanisms are incorporated. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the proposed MADDPG framework significantly reduces task offloading latency and computational overhead while improving system throughput, outperforming conventional optimization methods, and validating its effectiveness in emergency communication networks.

title: Enhanced Disease Susceptible Variant Identification via Short Identity by Descent Segments
authors: Chonghao Wang, Werner Pieter Veldsman, Yufen Huang, Xiaodong Fang, Lu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Rare diseases affect millions of individuals worldwide, yet diagnostic yields for them still remain low. Among variant identification approaches, identity by descent (IBD) mapping is used to identify disease susceptible variants originating from a recent common ancestor among affected individuals, but existing IBD detection models struggle to identify these variants in short IBD segments. Here, we introduce SILO, a novel model to detect disease susceptible variants in both short and long IBD segments. SILO employs a two-stage procedure to detect IBD segments. In the first stage, SILO identifies long IBD segments based on common variants. In the second stage, SILO utilizes rare variants to detect short IBD segments using a seed-and-extend algorithm. We evaluated SILO in simulated data and real data from the 1000 Genomes Project. Our results demonstrate that SILO outperforms existing models in detecting disease susceptible variants within short IBD segments, and show comparable performance in detecting these variants within longer IBD segments. These findings highlight the potential of SILO to increase diagnostic yields for rare diseases by enhancing the identification of previously overlooked disease susceptible variants in short IBD segments. Nonetheless, we note that the detection of short IBD segments remains challenging due to limited precision, leaving room for future improvement.

title: From List-Decodability to Proximity Gaps
authors: Yiwen Gao, Dongliang Cai, Yang Xu, Haibin Kan
abstract: Proximity testing for linear codes is a fundamental problem in coding theory with critical applications in cryptographic protocols, blockchain, and distributed storage systems. This work addresses the proximity gaps for linear codes, a crucial aspect for efficiently verifying whether a batch of words is close to a given code. We present a general framework for deriving proximity gaps from the list-decodability properties of the underlying linear code. Our main result show that for any (p,L)-list-decodable code C ⊆ Fnq with minimum relative distance ΔC > p, the probability that a random combination of a batch of t words containing a δ-far word (for δ ≤ 1 − √ 1 − p + ε) remains δ-far from C is bounded by O( tL2pn/q + t/εq ). This result also establishes a form of (mutual) correlated agreement for linear codes, which can be used to strengthen soundness analyses in protocols that rely on proximity testing, thereby reducing query complexity and enabling practical instantiations over smaller finite fields. In particular, we apply our main result to randomly punctured Reed–Solomon codes and folded Reed–Solomon codes—both of which are known to achieve list-decodability up to capacity—and derive linear proximity gaps for these families of codes under the Johnson bound.

title: Angular-Domain Control of Electromagnetic Waves Through Scattering Manipulation
authors: Ran Song, Tao Hong, Jiangcheng Ge, Zhexun Zhang, Wen Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article addresses two primordial questions concerning angularly dispersive periodic structures. First, how does the scattering response govern electromagnetic functionality at specific angles? Second, under what conditions can perfect transmission, reflection, or absorption be achieved at given angles? To this end, a unified framework is proposed to systematically integrate angular-selective transmission, reflection, and absorption. By decomposing the total scattered field into independently controllable antenna mode scattered field (AM-SF) and structural mode scattered field (SM-SF), the framework establishes a systematic link between scattering characteristics and electromagnetic functionality. This approach offers a clear physical foundation for angular manipulation of electromagnetic waves. The proposed framework is validated through simulations of three types of angle-selective surfaces (ASSs), namely, reflection–absorption (RA), transmission–reflection (TR), and transmission–absorption (TA). Experimental measurements are further carried out for TM-polarized TR-ASS and TA-ASS, along with a dual-polarized TA-ASS. These results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method and demonstrate a scalable design framework for diverse angular-domain filtering functionalities in RF front-ends.

title: The Role of AI in Climate Change Prediction: Analysing Machine Learning Models for Extreme Weather Forecasting
authors: Nanda Gopal Parise
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: ML and AI are relevant in climate change prediction, more so in extremal weather conditions observation. In terms of ability of the technologies are being applicable in spatial and temporal analysis, estimation, and climatic data. AI models, especially LSTM and YConvLSTM deep learning structures, outperform numerical weather prediction methods. These models are more effective in identifying fragile climate parameters and crossectional patterns. Nonetheless, the LSTM (Long ShortTearm Memory) technology can come in handy in identifying severe weather occasions because of its temporal endurance. YConvLSTM contributes to the enhancement of the regressionbased modeling for the purpose of minimizing the prediction errors, hence enhancing the predictability of the model. The fact is that YConvLSTM helps improve decent predictability of dependability predictions. They help in development of efficient disaster response plans, distribution of resources and even in policy formulation by producing good forecasts. However, rising computational costs, data integrity concerns, and ethical concerns remain. Climate research using artificial intelligence can handle the increasing level of complexity of rising temperatures and extreme weather occurrences. This makes it vital to climate resiliency measures and disaster recovery planning to make people aware of the climate.

title: Modeling of Ventilation and Air Conditioning Control Systems for Processing Plants
authors: Ludmila G. Musta, Yuriy K. Kozhubaev, Valeria O. Lozhkina, Yin Changwen, Abbas Ali
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: This article is devoted to the modeling of ventilation and air conditioning control systems for processing plants. A study of automated ventilation and air conditioning systems for processing plants is conducted.For this study, an automatic control system suitable for processing plants and enterprises was modeled. Equipment control algorithms were implemented.The following software was used to achieve this result: SMLogix, SMLogix SMH2010, and MASTER-SCADA.

title: Accident Prone Hotspot Detection Using YOLOv8 Integrated with HDFS
authors: Dheeraj Chandra, Rashi Bagrecha, Ishita Aryasi Karwariya, Gouthaman P, T. Preethiya
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Emergencies often occur in rapidly developing urban areas. However, delays in identifying and responding to these emergencies increase the fatality rate and worsen the outcome. This may be prevented if there exists an appropriate execution framework concerning the situation. It takes too long to respond to road accidents since crucial data is distributed over various sources such as CCTV cameras and sensors along with manual reports. This work aims to give a solution to the problem by combining the information from surveillance camera footage and IoT sensors to spot critical events and respond to the issue more effectively and accurately. By utilizing YOLOv8n, which supports the methodology to locate accidents in real time. Images from the CCTV camera and IoT sensors provide additional details, like how severe the wreckage is along with determining whether it is an accident or not. To evaluate and prioritize the incidents, machine learning models are utilized for concluding how critical the condition is and which incident requires help at the earliest so that a response can be provided. The system furthermore discovers accident prone regions using hotspots and processes real time details using multi source data. It helps emergency teams to act faster and make better decisions which helps save lives by giving them clear and timely information and reducing the response time.

title: Four Families of Binary Sequences with Low Ambiguity Function Properties
authors: Furong Bao, Zhengchun Zhou, Yang Yang, Ping Deng
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: Binary sequence families with low ambiguity function properties serve as fundamental building blocks in advanced wireless communication and radar systems. In this paper, we present a systematic characterization of the ambiguity function properties of four classes of binary sequence families. First, we adopt the convention that the quadratic character <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\eta$</tex> satisfies <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\eta(0)=1$</tex> or −1, which provides a unified representation for binary sequences. Under this definition, we refine the Weil bound for multiplicative character sums and establish their equivalence to the ambiguity functions of sequences constructed via quadratic characters. Through rigorous mathematical analysis of specific exponential sum structures, we theoretically demonstrate that all four sequence classes exhibit excellent low ambiguity function properties.

title: TransGate-RiceNet: Attention-Fused Transformer-Convolutional Model for Early and High-Precision Detection of Rice Leaf Pathologies
authors: M. Rajeswari, R. Jeena, P. Sangeetha, G. Mary Valantina, P. Muneeshwari
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Rice is an important cereal crop in the world and its production is usually influenced by diverse leaf pathologies causing immense agricultural and economic losses. These diseases are very important in crop health and sustainable food production, since early and accurate detection is of great importance. TransGate-RiceNet, which is presented in this paper, is a new hybrid deep learning model that combines the advantages of transformer encoders with gated convolutional neural networks (GCNNs) to identify and classify rice leaf diseases at fine grain. The model takes advantage of a dual stream feature extraction pipeline; the transformer branch extracts the long-range contextual dependencies whereas the gated CNN branch highlights the localization of lesion patterns. An attention fusion module multi-head is used to fuse both global and local representations that boost discriminative features learning in various lighting and environmental conditions. According to experimental assessment of the RiceLeaf-2025 benchmark data, the classification accuracy of the model is 98.84, which is even better than the state-of-theart CNN-only and transformer-only models. Furthermore, the suggested model has strong generalization on untested test sets, and it has high sensitivity and specificity in the detection of diseases at the early phase. The findings show that TransGate-RiceNet can be an effective diagnostic device to support precision agriculture based on AI that ensures that interventions are implemented on time and can be used to maximize yields.

title: A Resilient LLM-driven Agent Framework for Multi-UAV Mission Planning and Control
authors: Haoyang Chen, Shaoxun Liu, Jinfeng Zhang, Yupeng Zhang, Shiyu Wang, Chenchen Liu, Peng Fu, Yuezhong Zhang, Dongchen Ji
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) offer a new paradigm for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) mission planning and control. However, their inherent probabilistic nature and hallucination tendencies present significant challenges for safety- critical applications. Despite efforts to mitigate these issues through prompt engineering and post-generation validation, existing open-loop and code-oriented frameworks remain inherently unsafe. To address these limitations, we introduce Decomposer-Executor-Replanner (DER), a closed-loop agent framework designed to enhance the resilience and interpretability of LLM-driven UAV control. The Decomposer injects dynamic UAV states to transform high-level objectives into executable subtasks under explicit safety invariants. The Executor performs sandboxed tool invocations with runtime guards and feedback-aware parameter adjustments, ensuring bounded and interpretable execution. The Replanner monitors mission states, detects anomalies, and triggers recursive replanning based on contextual reasoning and mission memory. Together, these modules form a resilient and verifiable lifecycle for LLM-based UAV autonomy. Experiments conducted in Gazebo with PX4-ROS and up to 30 UAVs demonstrate that DER achieves superior safety, interpretability, and recovery capability compared to monolithic LLM and ablated variants. While current results are simulation-based, DER establishes a foundation for extending LLM-driven UAV control toward real-time deployments.

title: Integrated On-Board Chargers for Electric Vehicles: Topologies, Control Strategies, Challenges, and Future Research Directions
authors: Malik Qamar Abbas, Lassi Aarniovuori, Pasi Peltoniemi, Michele Mengoni, Luca Zarri
abstract: The rapid adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) is increasing the demand for charging solutions that are compact, cost-effective, and grid-friendly. Conventional off-board charging stations face challenges such as high infrastructure cost, distribution-grid stress from high-power charging, and limited flexibility for RE integration. Integrated on-board chargers (IOBCs) offer a promising alternative by reusing the traction inverter and motor for charging, thereby reducing system size, weight, and cost, while improving hardware utilization. Despite growing research interest, a consolidated and critical assessment of IOBC architectures and control strategies remains limited. This paper presents a comprehensive review of IOBC technologies, covering key topologies and control approaches for charging, traction, and vehicle-to-grid (V2G) operation. It critically examines major implementation challenges, including unintended charging torque and torque ripple, harmonic current distortion and grid-code compliance, thermal stress due to high power density, and safety and isolation constraints. Finally, future research directions are discussed, with an emphasis on advanced converter topologies, wide-bandgap devices, enhanced thermal management, cybersecurity, and data-driven resilience for secure and reliable EV charging systems.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence in Software Engineering: Current Trends, Gaps, and Future Directions
authors: Adam Khan, Asad Ali, Muhammad Ismail Mohmand, Mahdi Zareei, Rajesh Roshan Biswal
abstract: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) has gained increasing attention for improving the transparency of artificial intelligence systems. However, there is a less clear understanding of its adoption and research patterns in the field of Software Engineering (SE).This systematic literature review focuses on the use of XAI within SE and the prospects of cross-domain learning. To achieve this, twenty-nine studies applying XAI in Software Engineering (SE), published between March 2020 and April 2024, were reviewed, and their findings were compared with XAI practices reported in healthcare and finance. The findings show that SE research mostly uses local explanation techniques: 86% of the studies employ instance-level methods, such as Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME), whereas global explanation techniques that provide an overall view of the model are used less frequently. In terms of application domains, XAI studies in SE are mainly focused on software defect prediction, while other domains, such as software testing, and effort estimation remain comparatively underexplored. Regarding implementation tools, scikit-learn is the most frequently used machine learning (ML) library in XAI-based SE studies, and less often, TensorFlow, PyTorch, and R based-frameworks are used. The current study is more comprehensive in terms of scope compared to the previous review, as it addresses four research questions, examines the types of explanations, and clearly mentions the utilization of ML libraries. The findings indicate that future SE studies should incorporate global explanation techniques, diversify XAI methods, and extend the research to underrepresented SE tasks. In addition, clear reporting of tools and libraries is essential to enhance reproducibility. Overall, although XAI in SE is progressing, the domain remains at an early stage, and insights from other fields may help improve interpretability, reliability, and trust in SE systems.
