title: A Quantum Internet Protocol Suite Beyond Layering
authors: Angela Sara Cacciapuoti, Marcello Caleffi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Layering, the organization principle of the network protocols underpinning the classical Internet, is ill-suited to the Quantum Internet, built around entanglement, which is non-local and stateful. This paper proposes a quantum-native organizational principle based on dynamic composition, which replaces static layering with a distributed orchestration fabric driven by the node local state and “in-band” control. Each node runs a Dynamic Kernel that (i) constructs a local Plan of Actions (PoA) of candidate steps to advance a given service intent, and (ii) executes the PoA by composing atomic micro-protocols (MPs) into context-aware procedures, namely the meta-protocols. Quantum packets carry an in-band control-field – the meta-header – containing the service intent and an append-only list of action-commit records, termed as stamps. Successive nodes exploit this minimal, authoritative history to construct their local PoAs. As quantum packets progress, these local commits collectively induce a network-wide, direct acyclic graph that certifies end-to-end service fulfillment, without requiring global synchronization or an external controller. In contrast to classical encapsulation, which prescribes a fixed vertical processing order, the proposed suite enforces order by certification: dependency-aware local scheduling decides what may run at a certain node, stamps certify what did run and constrain subsequent planning. By embedding procedural control within the quantum packet itself, the design ensures coherence and consistency between entanglement-state evolution and control-flow, preventing divergence between resource state ad protocol logic, while remaining MP-agnostic and implementation-decoupled. The resulting suite is modular, adaptable to entanglement dynamics, and scalable. It operates correctly with or without optional control-plane hints. Indeed, when present, hints can steer QoS policies, without changing semantics. We argue that dynamic composition is the organizing principle required for a truly quantum-native Internet.

title: Simultaneous Localization and Synchronization in Distributed MIMO-OFDM Systems
authors: Boyu Teng, Xiaojun Yuan, Rui Wang
abstract: Time-of-arrival (ToA)-based user localization typically requires precise clock synchronization between base stations and user equipments, making the localization and synchronization problems tightly coupled with each other. This paper considers a distributed multi-input multi-output (MIMO) orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) system and addresses joint multi-user localization and clock synchronization within an integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) framework. Unlike existing works that only consider clock bias, we account for the impacts of both clock bias and clock skew on MIMO-OFDM signals. Specifically, clock bias introduces a constant offset in path delays, whereas clock skew causes a mismatch in the OFDM symbol durations between the transmitter and the receiver, resulting in linearly varying delays across OFDM symbols. We formulate the joint localization and synchronization problem within a Bayesian framework. Based on variational message passing and the sum-product rule, we propose a message passing algorithm, termed Bayesian Localization and Clock Synchronization (BLACS), which jointly estimates the positions, clock parameters, and velocities of multiple users. Simulation results show that accounting for clock skew significantly improves localization accuracy compared to the baseline methods. Moreover, the proposed BLACS algorithm achieves performance close to the Bayesian Cramér–Rao Bound, demonstrating its effectiveness and near-optimality.

title: Dynamic Spatiotemporal Measurement Uncertainty-Aware Detection for Robust Power System State Estimation Against Stealthy FDIA
authors: Shutan Wu, Qi Wang, Jianxiong Hu, Yujian Ye, Yi Tang, Wei He
abstract: In cyber-physical power systems, state estimation (SE) serves as a critical function for operational monitoring but is increasingly vulnerable to functional failures induced by stealthy external attacks. To overcome the limitations of traditional attack detection methods for SE, particularly their heavy reliance on prior attack models and static measurement infrastructures, this paper proposes a robust SE model that proactively introduces dynamic spatiotemporal measurement uncertainty (DSTMU) to detect stealthy false data injection attacks (FDIAs) without requiring an explicit prior attack model, thus improving the resilience and security of power system operations. First, to disrupt the attacker's static perception of the measurement configuration, a robust SE model with structural uncertainty and adversarial resilience is constructed by dynamically reconfiguring the measurement sampling pattern via spatial selection and temporal weighting matrices, leveraging the inherent redundancy of hybrid SCADA and WAMS measurements. Subsequently, a consistency-based detection index, the random measurement selection consistency index (RMSCI), is designed to quantify the deviations in SE results caused by varying measurement configurations. The statistical characteristics of RMSCI are analytically derived under normal and adversarial conditions, verifying its sensitivity to stealthy attacks. Furthermore, an offline robust detection-oriented optimization model is formulated, jointly optimizing the measurement selection strategies and detection thresholds. A Benders decomposition-based algorithm is employed to achieve efficient offline solution convergence for the detection-oriented deployment problem. Finally, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively detect FDIAs of varying intensities without requiring additional hardware or external detection modules.

title: MicroSDF: Microfacet-Driven Hybrid Neural SDFs for Mixed-Reflectance Surface Reconstruction
authors: Lejia Ye, Yuhua Xu, Yulan Guo, Lian Xu, Xiaohu Zhang
abstract: Accurate 3D reconstruction in real-world environments remains a significant challenge due to the coexistence of reflective and non-reflective surfaces, which pose distinct modeling demands. Existing methods often treat these surface types separately, limiting their generalizability and physical plausibility. To bridge this gap, we propose MicroSDF, a novel neural implicit framework that facilitates geometry and reflectance modeling through microfacet theory. Our approach incorporates three core innovations: (i) a microfacet-guided geometry model that extracts multi-scale surface normals (macroscopic and microfacet) from a signed distance field (SDF), regularized by a proposed microfacet normal consistency loss to enforce physically plausible surface orientations; (ii) an enhanced dual-branch color model, where the specular branch leverages the microfacet normals to model high-frequency reflectance, and the vanilla branch, unlike prior works, uses reflection direction (instead of viewing direction) to better model diffuse and low-frequency specular components; and (iii) a detection-guided color blending strategy that adaptively fuses the color outputs based on reflection priors, providing more physically intuitive blending than implicitly learned blending weights. Combined with a tailored multi-stage optimization scheme, the proposed MicroSDF achieves robust and high-fidelity reconstruction across reflective and non-reflective surfaces. Extensive experiments on DTU, Shiny Blender, Ref-NeRF, and DeepVoxels datasets demonstrate state-of-the-art performance, establishing a new direction for physically grounded neural reconstruction.

title: Score-Based Conditional Flow Models for MIMO Receiver Design with Superimposed Pilots
authors: Ruhao Zhang, Yupeng Li, Yitong Liu, Shijian Gao, Jing Jin, Hongwen Yang, Jiangzhou Wang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Accurate channel state information (CSI) is vital for multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems. However, superimposed pilots (SIP), which reduce overhead, introduce severe pilot contamination and data interference, complicating joint channel estimation and data detection. This paper proposes a conditional flow matching receiver (CFM-Rx), an unsupervised generative framework that learns directly from received signals, eliminating the need for labeled data and improving adaptability across diverse system settings. By leveraging flow-based generative modeling, CFM-Rx enables deterministic, low-latency inference and exploits model invertibility to capture the bidirectional nature of signal propagation. This framework unifies flow matching with score-based diffusion modeling via a moment-consistent ordinary differential equation (ODE), replacing stochastic differential equation (SDE) sampling with a deterministic and efficient process. Furthermore, it integrates receiver-side priors to ensure stable, data-consistent inference. Extensive simulation results across various MIMO configurations demonstrate that CFM-Rx consistently outperforms conventional estimators and state-of-the-art data-driven receivers, achieving notable gains in channel estimation accuracy and symbol detection robustness, particularly under severe pilot contamination.

title: BCEDAS: A Blockchain and Chameleon Hash-Based Efficient Decentralized Authentication Scheme for Internet of Vehicles With Multi-Scenario_supp1-3670901.pdf
authors: Ruizhong Du
abstract: The Internet of Vehicles (IoV) is a crucial component of Intelligent Transportation Systems, enhancing the intelligence and connectivity of transportation networks through comprehensive data support and communication capabilities. However, since each communication entity within the IoV operates in an open network environment, it is vulnerable to various threats, including user data leakage and vehicle operational security issues. Moreover, the IoV is highly time-sensitive, necessitating the design of a secure and efficient authenticated key agreement (AKA) protocol in IoV. Unfortunately, most current solutions only achieve one-to-one authentication and lack scalability for one-to-many or cross-domain scenarios. As a result, their efficiency remains limited, while alternative AKA schemes that support these use cases tend to introduce linearly increasing costs and computational overhead. To address these challenges simultaneously, we propose BCEDAS that utilizes chameleon hash function, physical unclonable functions and blockchain technology, which minimizes reliance on centralized trusted party while meeting essential security features, including unlinkability, non-repudiation, and side-channel attack mitigation. It also enables cross-domain authentication and multi-vehicle authentication across diverse scenarios. We conducted a comprehensive security deployment analysis by employing widely recognized Real-Or-Random model, along with the ProVerif tool. Furthermore, it exhibits competitive performance metrics in terms of computational efficiency, communication overhead, energy consumption, and average packet loss rate, which demonstrate the potential usability of BCEDAS in various environments.

title: Monocular Optical-Flow Method for Gait Event Detection and Foot-Strike pattern Evaluation in Parkinsonian Gait
authors: Luca Ceriola, Diego Torricelli, Francisco Molina-Rueda, Fabrizio Patanè, Marco Donati, Ilaria Mileti
abstract: Gait event detection and foot strike pattern (FSP) evaluation are critical in clinical, rehabilitation applications, particularly in monitoring pathologies such as Parkinson's disease (PD). While traditional gait analysis methods such as force platforms and optoelectronic systems offer high accuracy, they are limited by cost, setup complexity, and environmental constraints. This paper presents a novel, low-cost, vision-based system that uses a single sagittal-view RGB camera and neural network modules to identify and track subjects, segment the foot closest to the camera and estimate optical flow. The proposed modular pipeline, named Foot Otsu Optical Flow (FOOF), can automatically detect initial contact and toe-off events, as well as classify foot strike patterns (heel, midfoot, and toe strikes) while walking. The method was validated with respect to an optoelectronic system involving 25 participants, 15 with PD and 10 controls, walking on flat terrain and on irregular terrain. The results demonstrate high sensitivity 0.99 ± 0.04, and specificity 1.00 ± 0.00, and temporal errors up to 0.02 ± 0.01 s for gait event detection. Foot strike pattern analysis on flat terrain indicated excellent heel strike identification (sensitivity: 0.93, precision: 1.00). However, midfoot and toe strike detection presented greater challenges due to misclassifications, particularly in complex walking scenarios and among participants with PD. Heel strike identification remained robust on irregular terrain (sensitivity: 0.83, precision: 0.96), though midfoot (sensitivity: 0.95, precision: 0.67) and toe strikes (sensitivity: 0.68, precision 0.59) were less reliably detected. This study demonstrates a new method for accurately detecting gait events and evaluating FSP with a single camera across different terrains in multiple people environments.

title: FD-SCU: Frequency Decomposition-based Spectrum Collaborative Upsampling for Point Cloud Color Attribute
authors: Hao Liu, Wenchao Wang, Hui Yuan, Raouf Hamzaoui, Weiqing Yan, Junhui Hou
abstract: Existing point cloud color upsampling methods typically treat color upsampling as an interpolation problem within a local color or implicit feature domain. This largely overlooks the ability of the frequency domain to capture color correlations in local point sets. To address this limitation, we propose a spectrum collaborative strategy that uses frequency decomposition on voxel blocks (VBs) to enhance point cloud color reconstruction. We first voxelize the low-resolution (LR) color point cloud to generate multiple VBs and introduce a virtual filling strategy that adaptively assigns colors to empty voxels in each VB, ensuring that the irregularly distributed color information fully occupies the VB. We then apply the discrete cosine transform, known for its strong frequency-domain representation of locally smooth signals, to each color-filled VB to obtain frequency coefficients. These frequency coefficients are separated into high-frequency (HF) and low-frequency (LF) components. The LF coefficients, together with the LR color point cloud, are fed into a multi-scale cross-domain feature extraction module to capture deep features. Next, a Gaussian perturbation-based feature expansion generates upsampled color features, which are used to regress a coarse upsampled color point cloud. Finally, a high-frequency-guided residual refinement module uses the HF coefficients to refine the coarse upsampled result and produce a high-fidelity color point cloud. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods. Our code will be publicly available at https://github.com/wangwenchaoxx/FD-SCU.

title: Machine Learning Adversarial Attacks using Partial Sinkhorn Optimization
authors: André Bertolace, Konstantinos Gatsis, Kostas Margellos
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Control Systems
abstract: Adversarial attacks are often modeled as pointwise perturbations of individual samples, which can miss structured distributional effects and may waste perturbation budget on examples that are already misclassified. We study a data-driven Wasserstein attack model in which the adversary shifts the empirical distribution under a label-preserving transport budget. Starting from this formulation, we derive a finite-dimensional transport surrogate and an equivalent lifting that makes the role of transport couplings explicit. We then introduce an entropic regularization, obtaining a difference-of-convex formulation that penalizes attacks which mainly amplify the loss of already misclassified samples. This leads to Partial Sinkhorn, an iterative algorithm that combines convex-concave linearization with Sinkhorn-type updates, such that any limit point of a convergent subsequence is a KKT stationary point of the penalized problem. Experiments on synthetic and MNIST tasks show that the proposed method generates stronger attacks than FGSM under comparable perturbation budgets, particularly in the low-distortion regime. The framework also highlights links between adversarial attack, optimal transport, and distributionally robust control.

title: Quantum-Enhanced Variational Stacked LSTM Network for High-Dimensional Threat Detection in Cloud Computing Environments
authors: Pullaiah Chowdary Vutla, Triveni Yenugu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Generally, cloud computing environments (CCE) involve large, dynamic, and high-dimensional data, which makes them an easy target of sophisticated cyberattacks that are difficult to deal with by conventional detection algorithms. To ensure cloud security, this research develops a Quantum-enhanced Variational Stacked Long Short-Term Memory Network (QVSLSTM-Net), a hybrid Deep Learning (DL) framework with quantum processing to achieve better threat detection and minimize false positives on the complex, high-dimensional cloud telemetry. The framework feeds on multi-source cloud logs, which are preprocessed to ensure the data quality, and uses a lightweight temporal encoder to identify significant feature vectors. Now, the significant features are mapped toward a quantum Hilbert space through parameterized quantum feature maps. The Variational Quantum Classifier (VQC) operates on these encodings to make use of quantum-enhanced expressivity to the fine patterns of anomalies, and the stacked LSTM is used as an ensemble of outputs to model the temporal attack signatures. The evaluation shows that the proposed QVSLSTM-Net exposed improvements in AUC (0.98) and minimized detection latency (0.2s) compared to classical baselines such as VQC and LSTM.

title: Deep-Machine Learning Integration for Enhanced Pest Detection in Mangifera Indica (Mango)
authors: Sankeetha Vamadevan, Agalya Vinoharan, Veerapathirapillai Vinoharan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Mango (Mangifera indica), the third most widely consumed fruit in Sri Lanka after banana and papaya, has experienced steady growth in cultivation and yield; however, pest and disease infestations still cause substantial yield losses and degrade fruit quality. Since most pests inhabit mango leaves, early and accurate leaf-based detection is crucial for effective crop management, yet traditional manual inspection remains time-consuming, labor-intensive, and often unreliable due to pest evolution and limited expert availability. To address these challenges, this study proposes an automated pest detection framework that integrates deep learning-based feature extraction with machine learning classification. The research uses ten classes from the MangoPestClassification dataset, consisting of real-world images of healthy and pest-affected mango leaves captured under diverse environmental conditions to represent realistic field scenarios. Pre-trained convolutional neural network (CNN) models, VGG19, ResNet50, InceptionV3, DenseNet121, MobileNetV2, and UNet, are employed to extract discriminative image features. These features are then classified using Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifiers. This hybrid deep-learning-machine-learning approach ensures both strong feature representation and efficient classification. A 10-fold cross-validation strategy is applied to ensure reliable performance evaluation. Experimental results show high accuracy and robustness across model combinations. ResNet50 features with Logistic Regression achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6. 6 5} \pm \mathbf{1. 0 6 \%}$</tex> accuracy using 200 images per class, while ResNet50 features with an SVM (RBF kernel) yielded <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.09 \pm 0.30 \%$</tex> accuracy with 1,000 images per class. Overall, the findings highlight that ResNet-based feature extraction combined with either SVM (RBF) or Logistic Regression offers an effective and practical solution for automated mango pest detection, reducing dependence on manual monitoring.

title: A Modified Transformation for Solving Complex Ordinary Differential Equations
authors: Amal Khalaf Haydar, Radhi A. Zaboon, Shatha S. Alhily
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: This paper suggests a complex differential transformation in the one-dimensional space by modifying the real differential transformation. Thus the modified complex differential transformation is defined for complex valued functions of complex variable. A table of proposed transformation for many important complex functions has been included. In addition, solution of complex differential equations and also solution of the complex hypergeometric differential equation by the modified complex transformation are presented directly without converting to real space.

title: Modeling of Disturbance-Aware Multipath Mechanism and Causal Inference Framework: A Semantic Vector-Based Analysis of Behavioral Systems
authors: Suofei Zheng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In complex systems subject to ongoing environmental disturbances and information shocks, the perception capability of individuals or organizations has become a key factor affecting system behavior generation and evolutionary efficiency. To systematically capture how perception influences observable behaviors through latent structural mechanisms, this paper proposes a semantic perception-oriented causal reasoning framework for multipath mechanism modeling. The framework constructs a disturbance perception vector through semantic dictionary expansion and text mining, and identifies three representative transmission paths-structural regulation response, external signal absorption, and capability evolution adjustment-enabling a comprehensive view of perception-to-behavior transformation. At the algorithmic level, the study integrates bidirectional fixedeffects modeling with instrumental variable techniques, propensity score matching, and dynamic GMM to enhance the robustness and credibility of causal inference. Empirical validation based on climate-related semantic disturbance corpora and behavioral panel data confirms that perception intensity significantly impacts behavioral outcomes, with all mediating paths jointly contributing to this effect. This research offers three key contributions: (1) It provides a novel framework for modeling disturbance-aware behavior formation in intelligent systems; (2) it enriches causal inference methodologies under semantic uncertainty; and (3) it delivers practical insights for designing adaptive and cognitively intelligent control systems operating under dynamic, information-rich environments.

title: Asymmetric Dual-Encoder and Width-Dependent Feature Interaction for Road Extraction in Remote Sensing Images
authors: Ruyi Liu, Junhong Wu, Qiguang Miao, Zhigang Sun, Lei Wang, Panshi Guo
abstract: Road extraction from remote sensing images provides basic data for urban planning and intelligent transportation, aiding efficient management and decision-making. Traditional methods struggle to maintain road continuity and integrity due to varying road widths, complex shapes, uneven feature distribution, and occlusions. Despite the powerful feature extraction capabilities of CNNs in this field, their single-branch structure has limited effectiveness in handling roads of varying widths and often results in boundary blurring and disconnections in complex scenes. To address these challenges, we propose an Asymmetric Dual-encoder Feature Interaction Network (ADFINet). This model integrates the local feature extraction capabilities of CNNs with the global context modeling of Transformers. It uses dual encoders to separately extract features for narrow and wide roads and integrates them via a Dual-branch Feature Interaction Fusion Module (DFIFM). Additionally, a Feature Refinement and Redundancy Removal Module (FR3M) enhances feature expression and detail retention. Experiments demonstrate that ADFINet achieves superior performance on the DeepGlobe dataset, significantly improving road extraction accuracy and robustness.

title: Wideband Frequency-Coupling Impedance Identification Under All Operating Points of Grid-Connected Inverters Based on PSO-GRNN
authors: Zilin Zhang, Zhiwei Xie, Wenhua Wu, Peng Liu, Zhihao Dai, Jiawei Xu
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: The acquisition of wideband frequency-coupling impedance in grid-connected inverters serves as a critical prerequisite for analyzing wideband oscillation phenomena. This paper proposes a data-driven all-operating-point frequencycoupling impedance identification method. First, the wideband frequency-coupling impedance data of the grid-connected inverter is obtained through online impedance measurement. Then, a frequency-coupling impedance identification model under all operating points is identified based on the generalized regression neural network (GRNN) algorithm. The particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm is utilized to dynamically adjust the smoothing factor of GRNN, thereby building an optimal GRNN model for accurate identification of frequency-coupling impedance under all operating points. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified through simulation.

title: Market Bidding and Optimal Control of Distribution Transformer Areas Based on Virtual Power Plant Risk Management Model
authors: Bing Wang, Zhihui Wang, Lei Chen, Fei Xu, Ling Hao, Aoqian Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: As market bidding and optimal control participants, virtual power plants (VPPs) employ commonly centralized uniform clearing bidding involving price and quantity processes. Existing literature overlooks price bidding risks, focusing only on quantity-related revenue risks. This study addresses this gap by constructing a risk management model-based bidding risk-return function, quantifying price risks via historical clearing price probability matrix with conditional value-at-risk (CVaR). A two-dimensional risk framework merges price and quantity metrics into unified assessment for distribution transformer areas. Methods for market bidding and optimal control in energy and ancillary markets evaluate profit potential and control regulation capability. Cases show VPPs should adapt strategies to market traits and risk preferences. Profit analysis confirms the multi-market coordinated approach enhances profitability: total system revenue rises by 22.1% vs. single-energy market maximum; conservative VPP4’s risk loss ratio drops from 4.50% to 1.83%. The framework improves system coordination efficiency and supports VPPs in balancing risk and return under uncertainty.

title: IoT Based - Fall Detection System
authors: V. Karthikeyan, R. Rajapriya, J. Sherlin, Varshikadevi. A. M, Yuvarani. D
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Falls pose serious health risks to elderly individuals and those with mobility impairments, often resulting in injuries, hospitalization, or loss of independence. With the global rise in aging populations, the need for real-time, reliable fall detection is more critical than ever. This paper presents a wearable fall detection system utilizing the ESP32-WROOM microcontroller and MPU6050 sensor, which combines a 3-axis accelerometer and gyroscope. The system continuously monitors body motion and uses a threshold-based algorithm to detect falls based on sudden changes in acceleration and orientation. When a fall is detected, alerts are sent via the Blynk IoT platform and logged to Firebase, enabling timely caregiver intervention. Designed for comfort, low power usage, and portability, the device achieved over $\mathbf{9 0 \%}$ accuracy in diverse test scenarios, with minimal false positives. Its integration of IoT communication and potential for machine learning enhancement make it a scalable and effective solution for personal health monitoring and elderly safety.

title: Fast Quasi-Optimal Control of On-Board Energy Storage Systems for Regenerative Braking Energy Recovery Based on Area-Equivalence Principle
authors: Jiayu Mi, Zhongping Yang, Yajie Zhao, Zhongbei Tian, Fei Lin, Zhihong Zhong
abstract: Existing threshold-based control strategies for on-board energy storage systems (OESSs) often fail to achieve optimal energy-saving performance, while AI-based strategies typically suffer from low computational efficiency. To address these limitations, this article proposes a fast quasi-optimal control strategy for OESSs by leveraging the electrical characteristics of supercapacitors and the area-equivalence principle. Specifically, during train braking, OESSs should absorb as much regenerative braking energy as possible. By applying the area-equivalence principle, this goal is reformulated as a geometric problem-maximizing the area bounded by the train power curve and the OESS power curve. Thus, the optimal charging current and initial charging voltage of OESSs can be determined. Correspondingly, a discharging strategy is developed to ensure that the OESS voltage at the end of train traction reaches the previously obtained optimal initial charging voltage. Case studies reveal that the proposed area-equivalent strategy can achieve 98% of the optimal energy-saving performance while maintaining low root-mean-square (rms) current and average di/dt. Meanwhile, its computational time is only 1/92 of that of dynamic programming (DP) method. With a well-balanced trade-off among energy recovery performance, computational efficiency, and OESS lifetime, the superiority of the area-equivalence-based control strategy is verified.

title: IP-UNet: Intensity Projection UNet Architecture for IoMT-enabled 3D Medical Volume Segmentation
authors: Nyothiri Aung, Tahar Kechadi, Liming Chen, Sahraoui Dhelim
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Areas in Sensors
abstract: The proliferation of high-resolution medical imaging data, increasingly generated and transmitted via Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) infrastructure, presents significant computational challenges. Limited memory capacity remains a common drawback when processing these high-resolution 3D volumetric datasets, as direct cropping or down-sizing often results in a loss of critical diagnostic detail, exacerbates class imbalance, and degrades segmentation performance. 3D volumes are usually cropped or downsized first before processing, which can result in a loss of resolution, increase class imbalance, and affect the performance of the segmentation algorithms. In this paper, we propose an end-to-end deep learning approach called IP-UNet. IP-UNet is a UNet-based model that performs multi-class segmentation on Intensity Projection (IP) of 3D volumetric data instead of the memory-consuming 3D volumes. IP-UNet uses limited memory capability for training without losing the original 3D image resolution. We compare the performance of three models in terms of segmentation accuracy and computational cost: 1) Slice-by-slice 2D segmentation of the CT scan images using a conventional 2D UNet model. 2) IP-UNet that operates on data obtained by merging the extracted Maximum Intensity Projection (MIP), Closest Vessel Projection (CVP), and Average Intensity Projection (AvgIP) representations of the source 3D volumes, then applying the UNet model on the output IP images. 3) 3D-UNet model directly reads the 3D volumes constructed from a series of CT scan images and outputs the 3D volume of the predicted segmentation. We test the performance of these methods on 3D volumetric images. Experimental results show that IP-UNet can achieve similar segmentation accuracy with 3D-UNet but with much better performance. It reduces the training time by 70% and memory consumption by 92%.

title: Enhancing Elastic Full-Waveform Inversion with Envelope Cosine Similarity: A Multiscale Frequency Strategy for Walkaway VSP Data
authors: Huiyuan Quan, Xiaobo Liu, Jun Lu, Ran Zhang, Zhe Yang, Zhonglin Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Conventional elastic full-waveform inversion (EFWI) using the L2-norm objective function is highly sensitive to phase errors and prone to local minima, which often results in cycle skipping when low-frequency components are absent in field data. Although Walkaway vertical seismic profiling (VSP) offers higher-resolution constraints than surface seismic data, the lack of low-frequency energy in VSP recordings still limits the stability and accuracy of conventional EFWI. To address these challenges, we propose a novel multiscale inversion framework that employs envelope cosine similarity (ECS) as the misfit function for EFWI of Walkaway VSP data. Compared to the L2-norm, the ECS is less sensitive to phase mismatches and emphasizes structural similarity of signal envelopes via cosine similarity rather than absolute envelope differences, thereby mitigating cycle skipping and enhancing low-wavenumber recovery. Synthetic and field experiments confirm that the proposed method is effective, yielding stabilized convergence and improved velocity reconstruction accuracy in scenarios with low-frequency data deficiency, thus offering a robust framework for multiparameter VSP inversion.

title: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Computing
authors: N/A

title: Spectrum and Power Resource Allocation for C-RAN Enabled Dual-Connectivity Networks
authors: Jixing Liu, Xinwei Yue, Jinhe Zhou
abstract: In 6G wireless networks, the substantial increase in user equipment (UE) density and wireless resource utilization has made spectrum and power resource allocation challenges. Currently, limited research exists on spectrum and power resource allocation when UE employs dual-connectivity mode within the cloud radio access network architecture, particularly in scenarios where unmanned aerial vehicle base station is rapidly deployed to enhance network performance during peak traffic periods. Therefore, we propose a spectrum and power resource allocation scheme for this scenario. The scheme formulates the problem as two separate optimization problems to maximize utility functions, allocating spectrum and power resources based on system throughput, UE service priority, and operator's revenue. Simulation results show the proposed scheme's effectiveness in improving system throughput, spectrum utilization, and operator's revenue, especially under high-load conditions, while ensuring quality of service requirements for high-priority UEs.

title: Spectral-Routed Mixture-of-Experts for High-Fidelity Complex-Field Measurement in Single-Frame Lens-Free On-Chip Microscopy_supp1-3677977.pdf
authors: Zhengjun Liu
abstract: Single-frame lens-free on-chip microscopy (LFOM) offers a compact and cost-effective solution for biomedical diagnostics and micro/nanoscale inspection but suffers from severe twin-image artifacts and frequency-dependent degradation due to numerical aperture (NA) limitations. Existing reconstruction methods often neglect the heterogeneous frequency sensitivity of LFOM and fail to explicitly suppress artifacts, leading to suboptimal complex-field measurement quality. To address these challenges, we propose SEMNet, a spectral-expert measurement network that introduces frequency-aware modeling into single-frame LFOM reconstruction. Specifically, SEMNet transforms captured holograms into polar Fourier space, partitions them into directional subbands, and adaptively routes each subband via a soft top-k gating mechanism to lightweight expert networks specialized for low-, mid-, and high-frequency restoration. A physics-consistent loss jointly enforces complex-field fidelity, Fresnel-likelihood consistency, twin-image suppression, and spectral load balancing, thereby aligning the reconstruction objective with physical imaging principles. Extensive experiments on 404 high-resolution hologram–complex field pairs from seven types of biological tissues demonstrate that SEMNet improves peak signal-to-noise ratio by up to 1.7 dB over strong single-frame baselines, with statistically significant gains in structural similarity indices (p < 0.001). SEMNet restores high-frequency textures while suppressing NA-boundary artifacts. It delivers 47.1 FPS end-to-end inference with 100× fewer parameters than a representative CNN baseline and 5× fewer MACs than a representative Transformer baseline, highlighting a frequency-structured, physics-aligned design for efficient LFOM.

title: Enhancing Performance of Doubly Fed Induction Generator Under Unbalanced Voltage
authors: Jitendra Kumar Mahawar, Saikat Chakrabarti
abstract: Unbalanced voltage at the terminals of the doubly fed induction generator (DFIG) leads to double grid frequency oscillation (DGFO) in its operating quantities. The operating quantities include stator and rotor current, electromagnetic torque, DC-link voltage, and DFIG power. These oscillations de grade the performance and lifespan of the DFIG. The magnitude of DGFO in these operating quantities can be effectively reduced by regulating the negative-sequence (NS) current and the stator terminal voltage of the DFIG through coordinated control be tween the rotor-side converter (RSC) and the grid-side converter (GSC), rather than independent NS control of each converter. This paper presents a comprehensive investigation of several NS coordinated control strategies derived from the individual NS control of RSC and GSC for various operating conditions of the DFIG. Along with it, the impacts of NS-coordinated control on voltages at the PCC and stator terminal, as well as on the magnitude of DGFO under weak grid conditions are analyzed, which have not been addressed in the existing literature. Further, the suitability of the evaluated strategies for DFIG is suggested based on operating conditions, DGFO magnitude, and converter current-handling capability. The effectiveness of the proposed strategies is verified through real-time digital simulator (RTDS) testing and validated using hardware-in-loop (HIL) experiments, demonstrating their practical applicability in real-world DFIG based wind energy systems.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)

title: Robust Self-Localization of Wireless Acoustic Sensor Networks in the Presence of TDoA Outliers_supp1-3676429.pdf
authors: De Hu
abstract: Today, we are surrounded by numerous intelligent terminals (e.g., smartphones, tablets, and laptops) that can collectively form wireless acoustic sensor networks (WASNs), which are expected to be the next-generation platform for sound processing. Since the geometric structure of WASNs is critical for tasks like source localization and acoustic beamforming, automatic self-localization of WASNs (SL-WASNs) is indispensable. Although several SL-WASN methods have been developed, most of them fail to account for the impact of time-difference-of-arrival (TDoA) outliers induced by noise and reverberation. To address this issue, we first propose a centralized-robust self-localization (CRSL) method for WASNs with a centralized network topology. Specifically, we develop a novel probabilistic model leveraging the zero-sum property of TDoAs to derive the probability of a TDoA measurement being an inlier (or outlier). Then, we construct a weighted cost function based on TDoA measurements, in which the weights are controlled by the computed inlier probabilities. Afterward, the Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno (BFGS) algorithm is employed to simultaneously estimate the positions of nodes and sources, as well as the inter-node time offsets. In addition, we extend the CRSL to a distributed framework, termed distributed-robust self-localization (DRSL), which incorporates distributed computation of inlier probabilities, distributed cost function construction, and distributed optimization. Furthermore, the computational complexities of both CRSL and DRSL are analyzed in detail. The main contributions of this work can be summarized as follows: a novel TDoA outlier recognition model, the CRSL method, the DRSL method, and their complexity analysis. In real-world experiments with M = 10 nodes and N = 8 sources, the proposed methods achieved over 8% higher localization accuracy than state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Design of Power Allocation and Device Grouping in PD-NOMA Communication Systems_supp1-3678581.pdf
authors: Min Hua
abstract: Power domain non-orthogonal multiple access (PD-NOMA) is considered an effective means of enabling massive connectivity and improving spectral efficiency in next-generation (6G) wireless communication networks, particularly for the massive Internet of Things (mIoT). This paper optimizes power allocation and device grouping to address energy efficiency and resource scarcity challenges. We investigate optimal power allocation under two distinct successive interference cancellation (SIC) decoding orders at the receiver, incorporating constraints on limited transmit power, quality of service (QoS), and SIC decoding requirements. Using Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions, we derive rigorous criteria for grouping IoT devices on shared subchannels and determine their optimal power coefficients. Analytical results prove that channel-gain-ascending SIC maximizes downlink subchannel capacity, while channel-gain-descending SIC optimizes uplink performance. Furthermore, we propose a resource-efficient device grouping scheme that reduces the required number of orthogonal subchannels compared to conventional orthogonal multiple access (OMA). Simulations demonstrate that the proposed scheme not only conserves the number of orthogonal subchannels compared to the OMA system, but also exhibits superior overall system capacity. This work directly contributes to energy-efficient resource management in sustainable IoT communications by minimizing spectral resources and enhancing network longevity.

title: Cycle Time Optimization in Scheduling Automated Bench Cleaning Tool for Semiconductor Manufacturing
authors: Luetao Li, Siwei Zhang, Yan Qiao, Naiqi Wu, Jie Li, Bai Yang
abstract: In the semiconductor wafer manufacturing process, wafer cleaning operation is responsible for removing the residues on the surface of wafers after processing. The cleaning time accounts for 30% of the wafer fabrication work, which is significant as other wafer production processes. Wafer cleaning equipment consists of a single-arm robot for transportation and multiple processing tanks that are organized in a linear topology structure. Unlike cluster tools with its processing modules being arranged in a radial structure, the moving time of the robot between the tanks in a cleaning tool cannot be treated as the same, making its scheduling problem more difficult. The purpose of this paper is to solve the scheduling problem of automatic bench cleaning tools to make the wafer residency time same among the tanks for a processing step while maximizing the tool productivity. In order to achieve this goal, a backward strategy for wafer cleaning is proposed and its characteristics under steady state are studied. Based on the analysis results, scheduling algorithms aiming at minimizing the wafer cleaning cycle are established. Finally, the practicability of the algorithm is proved by case analysis.

title: Adaptive Game-Based Learning for Artistic Anatomy: Effects on Learning Achievement and Creative Self-Efficacy among First-Year University Art Students
authors: Guo Xinming, Chau Kien Tsong, Wan Ahmad Jaafar Wan Yahaya
abstract: This study investigates whether interpretable adaptive difficulty regulation in game-based learning enhances learning achievement and creative self-efficacy in artistic anatomy education. An Adaptive Game-Based Learning (AGBL) system was developed using the ADDIE model and grounded in constructivist theory, the adaptive learning framework, and social cognitive theory. The system implements a rule-based, transparent difficulty adjustment mechanism driven by a performance index derived from accuracy and response time. A quasi-experimental design was conducted with 109 first-year university art students, who were assigned to either the AGBL condition or a Non-Adaptive Game-Based Learning (N-AGBL) condition. Analysis of covariance (ANCOVA) controlling for pretest scores revealed that the AGBL group significantly outperformed the N-AGBL group in both learning achievement (F(1,106) = 42.56, p < .001, ηp² = .286) and creative self-efficacy (F(1,106) = 56.90, p < .001, ηp² = .349). Furthermore, creative self-efficacy was positively correlated with sketching test scores (r = .587), and the association remained significant after controlling for group assignment (partial r = .370), providing convergent validity evidence. Offline benchmarking against fixed, random, and Bayesian knowledge tracing scheduling strategies demonstrated that the rule-based mechanism achieved more stable difficulty trajectories while maintaining comparable accuracy. The findings suggest that structured and interpretable adaptive control is associated with improvements in cognitive mastery and motivational belief. This study contributes empirical evidence for rule-based adaptive game-based learning in visual-spatial domains and offers replicable design principles for integrating adaptive regulation into creative learning environments.

title: Decoupling Energy Stability and Thermal Response in Thermoelectric Atmospheric Water Generators
authors: María C. Salgado-Gutiérrez, José L. López-Moreno, Perla Y. Sevilla-Camacho, Gilbert F. Pérez-García, Rubén Martínez-Revuelta, Horacio I. Solís-Cisneros
abstract: This work experimentally evaluates the dynamic reproducibility of a thermoelectric atmospheric water generation (AWG) prototype operated under six control strategies. Three independent trials were conducted per strategy, and time-series data of cold-side temperature, hot-side temperature, energy consumption, and coefficient of performance were analyzed. Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) was used to quantify temporal similarity and capture non-linear deviations across repetitions. Results show that energy consumption remains highly reproducible, with average DTW distances below 1.15 Wh in most cases. In contrast, thermal variables exhibit significant dispersion, with DTW values reaching up to 113.53 for the Thermal Lift Coefficient. These findings demonstrate a clear dynamic decoupling between energy stability and thermal response, indicating that stable electrical input does not guarantee consistent thermal behavior. The observed variability is attributed to the intrinsic thermal inertia and non-linear dynamics of the Peltier effect, which amplify minor operational fluctuations. The results highlight the limitations of average performance metrics and emphasize the need for time-resolved analysis and advanced control strategies to ensure reliable operation of thermoelectric AWG systems.

title: EnCANet: A Network with Entropy-Guided Saliency and Cross-Spatial Attention for Urban Building Change Detection
authors: Tiankai Ge, Xianglong Qiao, Yanyan Zhang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Remote sensing change detection (RSCD) is essential for urban building monitoring, yet accurate building change extraction is often limited by the structural complexity of buildings and spatial heterogeneity. To address these challenges, we propose EnCANet, a supervised RSCD framework for urban building change detection that explicitly models spatial information density and cross-spatial dependencies. The framework comprises three key components: (1) an Entropy-guided Saliency Prior (ESP) module that computes local channel-wise entropy to quantify spatial information density, generating adaptive weights to guide early-stage feature modulation; (2) an Entropy-Focused Cross-Spatial Attention (EnFocSA) mechanism that integrates entropy priors into attention calculation to suppress pseudo-change responses while enhancing cross-region contextual discrimination; and (3) a Uni-FiRE decoder that employs a unified fusion-and-refinement strategy to restore multi-scale details and ensure structural consistency. Extensive experiments on the LEVIR-CD and WHU-CD building change detection datasets demonstrate that EnCANet achieves F1 scores of 92.25% and 94.27%, surpassing the closest state-of-the-art competitors by 0.46% and 1.01%, respectively. These results quantitatively demonstrate the effectiveness of integrating entropy-driven spatial priors and cross-spatial attention for accurate and robust urban building change detection in complex heterogeneous environments. Our code is available at https://github.com/SkyeGe04/EnCANet.

title: Reliability-Based Design and Control of Crystallization Systems Under Parametric and Process Uncertainties_supp1-3670402.pdf
authors: Zoltan Nagy
abstract: Population balance model-based approaches are widely employed in crystallization process design to meet the industry-specific critical quality attributes (CQAs). However, their reliability is often limited by parameter uncertainties arising from noisy experimental data, which if, unaccounted for, can lead to suboptimal or failed designs. This study introduces a novel reliability-based design optimization (RBDO) framework for open-loop design of crystallization systems under uncertainty. The framework formulates CQA constraints as probabilistic constraints with user-defined reliability thresholds, enabling quantified, risk-informed process design. To solve the resulting RBDO problems efficiently, a surrogate modeling-based, nested simulation-optimization workflow is developed. The proposed methodology is applied to both batch and continuous pharmaceutical crystallization systems, demonstrating its ability to generate robust design trajectories with significantly higher probabilities of meeting CQAs under both parametric and operational uncertainties compared to the deterministic designs. The computational efficiency and accuracy of the approach are further validated through benchmarking against back-mapping-based solutions of the underlying chance constrained formulations.

title: Background-Noise-Driven Detection of Diffusion-Generated Images_supp1-3678476.pdf
authors: ou weihua
abstract: Diffusion-based image generation has proliferated, making robust detection of synthetic imagery critical. We propose a background-noise-driven detector motivated by the observation that real camera images preserve physical noise traces, whereas model-generated images tend to exhibit algorithm-induced residual statistics. Starting from sRGB, we apply a deterministic inverse ISP to obtain an approximate Bayer RAW representation, train a lightweight RAW-domain denoiser, and extract a noise residual by subtracting the denoised reconstruction from the inverse-ISP signal. A detector trained only on ADM residuals generalizes in a zero-shot manner to a wide range of unseen diffusion generators. By emphasizing noise-domain cues rather than semantic content, the proposed method offers a simple and practical approach to diffusion-generated image detection.

title: Research on Fault Reasoning and Risk Assessment Method of Mountain Photovoltaic Power Station Based on Fault Tree and Dynamic Fuzzy Bayesian Network
authors: He Yaoxi, Hu Junjie, Ji Fangxu, Li Wentao, Liu Xiuyi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In this paper, a fault reasoning and risk assessment method based on fault tree (FTA) and dynamic fuzzy Bayesian network (DFBN) is proposed to solve the problems of strong dynamic and high uncertainty caused by complex terrain and changeable environment in mountain photovoltaic power stations and the limitations of traditional fault diagnosis methods. Firstly, the fault propagation logic is defined by FTA and mapped to the polymorphic fuzzy structure of DFBN, and the state of fault events is extended to polymorphic fuzzy sets such as “normal”, “slight degradation”, “serious degradation” and “fault” to adapt to the continuous changing events such as efficiency attenuation of photovoltaic modules. Secondly, using the dynamic time series modeling ability of DFBN, the hierarchical time slicing mechanism is introduced to capture the regular characteristics of fault probability changing with seasonal change and day and night cycle, and the fault probability of each node is dynamically corrected through environmental covariant nodes. In addition, the model parameters are learned and dynamically modified by combining expert fuzzy evaluation with multi-source data adaptive mechanism to improve the uncertainty and dynamic adaptability of the model. Empirical results show that the proposed method significantly outperforms traditional FTA, LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory), and Static Bayesian Networks (SBN) in fault diagnosis accuracy, achieving a Fault Detection Rate (FDR) of 0.89, a False Alarm Rate (FAR) as low as 0.07, and a Risk Evaluation Error (REE) reduced to 0.12. Meanwhile, the warning-driven maintenance strategy based on the model effectively reduces maintenance costs, with the average fault repair time shortened by 62% and the number of preventive maintenance actions decreased by 58%. The results validate the efficiency and practicality of the proposed method in fault diagnosis and risk assessment under complex conditions, providing a new technical approach for intelligent operation and maintenance of mountain photovoltaic power plants.

title: Predictive Analytics of Tesla and Google Stock Prices Using Advanced Machine Learning Frameworks
authors: Ganeshvel K P, Harshavardhana D, P. Gopika
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Stock market forecasting remains a challenging task due to the nonlinear, volatile, and stochastic nature of financial time- series data. This study aims to (i) comparatively evaluate statistical and machine learning approaches for stock price prediction, (ii) assess the impact of technical feature engineering on forecasting accuracy, and (iii) analyze the suitability of different models for volatile technology-driven stocks. A comparative framework is developed using ARIMA, Prophet, and Random Forest regression to forecast the daily closing prices of Tesla and Google stocks. Historical price data are preprocessed and enriched with technical indicators, including moving averages, momentum indicators, and volatility measures. Model performance is evaluated using Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Coefficient of Determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>). Experimental results demonstrate that the Random Forest model consistently outperforms ARIMA and Prophet, achieving lower prediction errors due to its ability to capture nonlinear relationships through engineered features. ARIMA and Prophet serve as effective statistical baselines for trend and seasonal analysis. The findings confirm that feature-driven ensemble learning significantly enhances predictive accuracy, highlighting its applicability in algorithmic trading and decision-support systems.

title: Multimodal Representation Learning for Modeling New Quality Productivity in Complex Systems
authors: Runhan Fang, Rui Sun, Jingchong Yang, Yulin Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In response to the growing need for intelligent analysis of productivity dynamics, this paper presents a computational framework for modeling and predicting new quality productivity using machine learning and data-driven techniques. A multi-dimensional productivity index is constructed by integrating heterogeneous features from economic, social, and environmental datasets. Advanced algorithms, including gradient boosting machines, graph neural networks, and attention-based temporal models, are employed to capture the complex nonlinear relationships and temporal dependencies. Feature selection, model interpretability, and sensitivity analysis are conducted to identify the key digital transformation and human-capital-related factors contributing to productivity evolution. The proposed framework demonstrates high predictive performance and offers an interpretable basis for understanding how digital systems and human-centric variables interact within productivity models. This research bridges the gap between data science and productivity intelligence, offering scalable tools for automatic productivity assessment and real-time decision support in digital ecosystems.

title: Resource Allocation Leveraging Dual Traffic and Channel Predictions in Open-RAN Architecture
authors: Yeonghun Jeong, Dongho Ham, Minhyun Kim, Jungmo Moon, Jeongho Kwak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: In this paper, we first envision an agentic-AI framework for open-RAN architecture that enables autonomous and context-aware network control by integrating predictive intelligence into key resource-management loops. Building upon this architectural vision, we develop a dynamic resource-allocation mechanism that leverages dual traffic and channel predictions to guide power-budget decisions across cells. By jointly forecasting cell-level traffic demand and channel conditions using transformer-based AI models, the proposed mechanism acquires predictive knowledge of spatial-temporal cell environments, allowing power resources to be proactively aligned with expected demand. Within this framework, we design an adaptive power budget allocation algorithm that dynamically distributes the total transmit-power budget across cells based on anticipated traffic load and channel quality. Simulation results demonstrate that this predictive strategy achieves a 27.1 % improvement in Geometric Average Throughput (GAT) compared to a baseline scheme without predictive information, confirming the effectiveness of embedding predictive and agentic intelligence into open RAN resource control.

title: An Intelligent, Non-Invasive Framework for Automated Oral Lesion Detection and Risk Stratification Using Intraoral Imaging and Hybrid Machine Learning
authors: Srihariharan R, Surya V V, Saraswathi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Oral lesions are among the commonest antecedents of fatal oral diseases, such as oral cancers but even early detection is still not readily achievable due to the lack of access to special care and the subjectivity of diagnosis. In an attempt to overcome the drawbacks of the wrong diagnosis as well as their diagnosis late in the case of oral lesions, the current project presents a smart, non-invasive building, which combines the utilization of high-definition intraoral imaging with machine learning-powered detection of peril and recognition of the lesions as hybrids. The architecture employs the best techniques to improve the quality of images through the utilisation of appropriate preprocessing, such as the median sharpening and top-hat transformation in an attempt to isolate clinically significant regions. Additional characteristics that detail the texture, color, as well as morphology, are also utilized. The diagnostic accuracy is brought about by GLCM-based characteristics, where the LightGBM as well as decision trees are utilized, giving strong as well as interpretable results. The novelty is the integration of automated feature-based diagnosis as well as future therapeutic regimens for lesions like erythroplakia as well as lichen planus; also, the project promotes low-cost, inexpensive, as well as remote diagnostic care for the mouth.

title: Market Transaction Method of Source-Network-Load-Storage Integration Project Based on Smart Contract
authors: Jihe Xu, Zongmin Jiang, Mingtao Shi, Ning Zhu, Yanyi Ye, Ben Chen, Jianlei Wang, Huiying Yu, Chunlin Xie, Yumin Zhang
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: With the rapid global transition to sustainable energy systems, the integration of Source-Grid-Load-Storage (SGLS) in electricity markets has become crucial for optimizing energy distribution and balancing supply and demand. This paper presents a market transaction method based on smart contracts for the SGLS integration project, aiming to address key issues such as transparency, efficiency, and automation in electricity trading. Blockchain technology enables a decentralized, transparent, and secure trading system, facilitating seamless interactions between electricity generation, transmission, storage, and consumption. We describe the process flow of a blockchainbased electricity trading system, including energy and green certificate trading, and propose a methodology to integrate load and storage systems efficiently. The implementation of smart contracts reduces intermediary costs, enhances trust and traceability, and provides a reliable mechanism for resource scheduling. Simulation results demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of this approach in optimizing energy market transactions.

title: Aqua Sense IoT and AI-Powered Water Quality Monitoring System
authors: Raju K, Sreemathi G, Subi Nanthana Devi N. K
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The availability of clean and safe water has been greatly impacted by rising pollution, industrial discharge, and environmental changes. Traditional water quality monitoring techniques rely on laboratory-based analysis and manual sampling, which are labor-intensive and inappropriate for real-time evaluation. This paper introduces Aqua Sense, an IoT and AI-driven water quality monitoring system intended for automated and ongoing assessment of water resources. Using integrated sensors, the system keeps an eye on important variables like <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, turbidity, temperature, dissolved oxygen, and electrical conductivity. To increase the accuracy of water quality assessment, machine learning-based data fusion techniques are used. Aqua Sense is an affordable and dependable solution for contemporary water quality monitoring since it allows for prompt decision-making through real-time alerts and visual analytics.

title: Quantum Cryptography Simulation
authors: Goutam R, Shwetha S, T R Loukya, Sowjanya M S, Sandhya S
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: By utilizing concepts from quantum physics like entanglement and superposition, quantum cryptography is revolutionizing data security. This study focuses on the application of classical computational models for simulation-based analysis of Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) protocols, namely BB84 and E91. By taking use of quantum features, QKD makes secure key exchange possible and guarantees that any effort at eavesdropping may be identified because of the disruption of quantum states. In order to assess how quantum communication channels behave under different noise and attack scenarios, the study models and simulates these channels. The study evaluates the efficacy and robustness of BB84 and E91 protocols in practical network settings by contrasting their performance and error tolerance. In order to determine if integrating quantum-secured systems with current classical infrastructures is feasible, scalability issues are also investigated. These simulations’ outcomes shed light on feasible security levels, resource needs, and workable deployment tactics. In the end, this research highlights how quantum cryptography may advance the basis for next-generation secure information networks by bridging the gap between theoretical quantum security models and real-world communication systems with the use of strong simulation frameworks.

title: Smart Alumni-Student Community Platform Using RAG
authors: Deva Prasath P S, Dhanush Shankar U, Nithya Jayakumar, Ramya Jayakumar, Kalaivani P
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Traditional alumni-student networking platforms rely on static profiles and keyword-based search, which limits contextual understanding, personalization, and effective engagement between students and alumni. To address these limitations, this work proposes a smart alumni-student community platform that integrates Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to enable context-aware information access, mentorship discovery, and knowledge sharing. The RAG framework combines semantic retrieval from structured institutional databases and vector-based knowledge stores with generative response mechanisms to deliver accurate, relevant, and personalized outputs for user queries. The system is evaluated through comparative analysis against a conventional keyword-based alumni platform using metrics such as retrieval accuracy, response relevance, engagement rate, and administrative efficiency. Experimental results indicate improvements of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$55-65 \%$</tex> in information retrieval accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40-50 \%$</tex> growth in user engagement, and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60-70 \%$</tex> reduction in manual administrative effort, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: A Lightweight Smart Multimodal Document Question-Answering System with Embedding-Based Retrieval
authors: Kamparapu V V Satya Trinadh Naidu, Moturi Sandilya, Budde Venkata Satya Tejesh, Setti Narendra Kumar, Sk. Ahmed, K. Devipriya
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: The increase in the rate of digital resources in educational and organizational settings has challenged effective information retrieval. Traditional searches by use of key-word may result in the wrong or incomplete searches, due to lack of semantic meaning. The given paper provides a lightweight smart multimodal document question-answering (QA) system that utilizes embedding-based retrieval and large language models (LLMs) to answer questions correctly and in a context-sensitive manner. The documents uploaded by a faculty or an organization are embedded and stored in a vector database (ChromaDB) and can be searched by similarity effectively. The query of users is processed into embeddings, and the appropriate document snippets are retrieved and synthesized into sensible responses with the help of LLMs. The system also uses Gemini and Sarvam APIs to use text-to-speech features and speech-to-text functions to provide responses in both text and audio formats to promote increased accessibility. Being lightweight, the system can be deployed in low-resource environments and therefore is feasible in the case of educational institutions and organizations that have a small computational infrastructure. The solution proposed illustrates a high potential of managing knowledge which has the ability to save manual work and enhances the efficiency and inclusiveness in the access of information.

title: Fatigue Driving Detection System Based on the Optimized YOLOv8 with BiFormer Attention Mechanism
authors: Haoxiang Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: This paper presents an enhanced algorithm for detecting fatigue states during driving. This paper introduces a novel standard for fatigue detection, which refers to the traditional blink frequency judgment, and a method based on yawning frequency judgment is proposed, the two methods are incorporated to promote the accuracy of fatigue detection. Furthermore, based on the mainstream YOLOv8 algorithm, this paper improves the backbone network of YOLOv8 by combining the BiFormer attention mechanism. BiFormer is a dual-layer routing attention module that utilizes dynamic sparse attention to filter out less relevant key-value pairs, thereby boosting the model's ability for global feature extraction. To improve the accuracy and robustness of the YOLOv8 algorithm, efficient computation allocation is dynamically optimized based on queries. Finally, based on the above algorithms and criteria, a simple decision-making system is designed to facilitate straightforward decisions for the driver. The final experimental results demonstrate that the improved algorithm exhibits better performance, yielding faster and more accurate judgment results, which provide valuable insights and research implications for detecting tired driving.

title: Explainable Machine Learning Framework for Behavioral Sentiment Analysis Using ASD-Related Data
authors: S. M. Jeya Ramani, J. Ida Christy
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Behavioral data analysis plays a vital role in understanding patterns within structured human response data. Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD)-related datasets contain rich behavioral attributes that can be explored from an analytics-driven perspective without clinical or diagnostic intent. This paper presents an explainable machine learning framework for behavioral sentiment analysis using ASD-related questionnaire data. Structured behavioral response scores are aggregated and mapped into sentiment-oriented categories, namely positive, neutral, and negative, enabling sentiment classification of behavioral patterns. The proposed framework employs Random Forest, XGBoost, and Neural Network models to evaluate classification performance using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC metrics. To enhance transparency, Explainable Artificial Intelligence techniques, including SHAP and LIME, are integrated to provide global and instance-level interpretability of model predictions. Experimental results demonstrate that ensemble-based models, particularly XGBoost, achieve superior classification performance. Explainability analysis highlights the influence of key behavioral attributes related to social interaction and communication. The study emphasizes the importance of interpretable machine learning for behavioral sentiment analysis and demonstrates the potential of explainable analytics frameworks for structured ASD-related data without engaging in clinical diagnosis. This study focuses exclusively on analytics-driven interpretation of behavioral data and does not perform clinical diagnosis or medical decision-making.

title: Emergency Communication System for Hikers Using LoRa
authors: Sravani Nalluri, G. Gayatri, G. Abhinav, A. Sai Nagendar, Somula Ramasubbareddy
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Difficulties in communication in forest, hilly, and isolated areas are also a major danger to the hikers and small rescue groups since the cellular networks are either unreliable or even non-existent. To address this shortcoming, we have developed a small, inexpensive handheld tool that enables off-grid text messaging and SOS-assisted location sharing via LoRa technology, eliminating the need for towers, gateways, or internet connections. The proposed system will comprise an ESP32 microcontroller, LoRa RA-02 transceiver, GPS receiver, and user interface based on a minimal 16x2 LCD, backed up by custom low-power firmware and a store-and-forward communication model. Assessment was done in varied environments such as open fields, semi-urban areas, and thick forest hill areas, totaling more than 120 hours of tests. Findings have shown a consistent range of communication of 8-10km with a packet success rate of more than 93% and battery life of 18-72 hours, which depends on duty cycle, and the ability to propagate emergency alerts in network denials.

title: Evaluating the Adversarial Stability of Classical Fake News Classifiers Using Transfomer Embeddings
authors: Nakul Chhimwal, Shivani Tufchi, Arpit Gola Bennett, Kartik Bhat
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The spread of fake news has become a significant concern in today’s digital landscape, calling for reliable and robust detection methods. This study investigates the adversarial robustness of classical machine learning models for fake news detection, emphasizing simplicity, interpretability, and resistance to manipulation. A balanced dataset of real and fake news articles was sourced from Kaggle’s Fake and True News dataset [8]. After preprocessing the text through tokenization, lemmatization, and TF-IDF vectorization, a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier was employed for classification. To enhance semantic representation, contextual embeddings were generated using DistilBERT to capture deeper contextual meaning in the news content.To evaluate robustness, a WordNet-based adversarial attack was applied by replacing words with contextually similar synonyms. The model achieved an accuracy of 99.25% on both clean and adversarially perturbed data. This consistency reflects the model’s robustness against minor lexical variations, such as synonym substitutions, without sacrificing classification quality. While these results are promising, further evaluation with stronger adversarial techniques is recommended to fully assess real-world applicability.

title: Hybrid EfficientVit Model for Plant Disease Classification Using Leaf Images
authors: A Adithyan, T.H. Abdulla, Aljasir Muhammed, Christy Babu, B Sivakarthik, Rajeev Rajan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Plant diseases represent a persistent challenge to global food security, causing substantial crop losses each year. While traditional machine learning approaches have shown promise in automated disease detection, they often struggle with computational efficiency and capturing complex disease patterns. In this work, we introduce EfficientViT, a next-generation vision transformer specifically designed for agricultural applications. Our approach combines the global reasoning capabilities of transformers with computational efficiency through a novel cascaded multi-scale attention mechanism. We demonstrate that this architecture not only achieves superior accuracy in classifying diseases across multiple plant species but does so with significantly lower computational demands. The model’s lightweight design makes it particularly suitable for deployment in resourceconstrained agricultural settings, potentially enabling real-time disease monitoring systems that could revolutionize crop management practices.

title: Source Code Vulnerability Detection Technology Based on Code Semantic Contribution
authors: Chunling Liu, Yonghe Tang, Jian Lin, Le Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This research is dedicated to developing source code vulnerability detection technology based on code semantic contribution to address software security challenges. The research preprocesses source code functions by constructing a dictionary library of common programming naming conventions, builds data dependency graphs and control dependency graphs using the Joern tool, adopts BPE word segmentation technology, and combines sentence embedding variants and LSTM graph embedding for semantic feature extraction and embedding to achieve precise detection. The construction of the experimental environment takes into account both hardware performance and software compatibility. The Big-Vul, SARD and NVD datasets were selected and used for the experiment after cleaning and stratified sampling processing. Compared with methods such as Checkmarx, SySeVR based on BLSTM, VulCNN based on CNN, Devign based on graph neural networks, and LineVul based on Transformer, the results show that: The semantic contribution method of this study performed outstandingly in the comprehensive performance indicators, with a recall rate of 87.52%, an accuracy rate of 90.13%, a precision rate of 88.76%, and an F1 value of 0.881. It shows obvious advantages under different types of vulnerability detection, different code scales and training data volumes, and has lower false positive and false negative rates. This technology provides a more effective way for source code vulnerability detection and is expected to enhance the overall security of the software.

title: Toward the Conduction of Automated Systematic Literature Reviews: The Role of Large Language Models
authors: Marco Fiore
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)
abstract: The conduction of Systematic Literature Reviews is an essential yet labor-intensive process for researchers, requiring meticulous compilation and analysis of literature to answer predefined Research Questions. Despite attempts to streamline this process, automation of reviews remains a significant challenge. This paper explores the potential of Generative AI and Large Language Models to revolutionize the review process by automating key tasks such as identifying, extracting, and synthesizing relevant literature. By leveraging open-source Large Language Models, we aim to reduce the time and effort traditionally required for comprehensive literature reviews. Our study involves comparing various models, including lightweight and more robust ones. We replicate a published review on Blockchain technology in agri-food traceability to evaluate the performance of these models. Results indicate that while high-parameter models successfully generate relevant search strings and classifications, they also exhibit trade-offs in terms of response times. Our findings highlight both the promise and limitations of current Large Language Models in automating Systematic Literature Reviews.

title: ADLOB: An attention based deep learning model for predicting midprice in Limit Order Books
authors: Pulyapudi Aditya, Yalavarthi Roshan Sai, D Saicharan, Chevula Sai Anivesh, Anup Aprem
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: Predicting price movements in Limit Order Books (LOB) has historically been a challenging task due to the high frequency, complexity, and non-linear nature of the LOB data.
