This paper proposes ADLOB, a deep learning model incorporating convolutional neural network, an inception module, a LSTM layer and a custom weighted attention layer - the convolutional neural network extracts hierarchical and spatial features in LOB data, the LSTM layer extracts long term temporal dependence and the weighted attention layer selects the appropriate context among the hierarchical features extracted by both convolutional and LSTM layers. Numerical experiments on the FI-2010 benchmark dataset shows an accuracy improvement of 8% compared to the state-of-art model for LOB, along with similar improvements in precision, recall and f1-score. Backtesting results demonstrate improved performance in all metrics such as the cumulative return, maximum drawdown, Sharpe ratio, profit factor and win rate, compared to the state-of-art models for LOB.

title: Research on Indicator Timing Selection and Anomaly Detection Based on Multivariate Mixed-Effects Models and Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Wencheng Ma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: This study focuses on data point selection and anomaly detection, integrating multivariate statistical models with machine learning algorithms to construct a multidimensional analytical framework for precise decisionmaking. First, to analyze the correlation between core variables and feature variables, a multivariate mixed-effects model was constructed. This model incorporated linear terms, quadratic terms, and interaction effects of variables. Model parameters were estimated using the least squares method to quantify the impact of each variable on core indicators. Simultaneously, it accounted for individual differences and residual interference to reveal the nonlinear association patterns among variables. Second, K-means clustering optimizes feature grouping, with validity verified via one-way ANOVA. A logistic regression model predicts compliance rates, while a risk quantification function iteratively identifies optimal timing for each group. Detection error impacts are analyzed to enhance model robustness. Finally, we incorporate multi-class feature variables for in-depth analysis. LASSO regression identifies key variables, while Cox proportional hazards models and the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) determine weights to construct a comprehensive cost function for solving optimal timing. Concurrently, Gaussian mixture models and confidence interval analysis enable anomaly detection. A “gray zone” mechanism handles borderline samples, enhancing the rationality and reliability of classification outcomes.

title: Multi-component NIR Spectral Quantitative Analysis via GAF Encoding and Multi-scale 2D-CNN
authors: Jingpeng Hu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This paper proposes a GAF-2D-CNN framework for multi-component Near-Infrared (NIR) spectral quantification. We map 1D spectra to 2D textures via Gramian Angular Field (GAF) and perform regression using a multi-scale 2D-CNN. To address high spectral similarity (p95 ≈ 0.9999) and prevent template memorization, we introduce a physics-based data generation pipeline with "multi-style/near-orthogonal" perturbations (baseline drift, micro-shifts, mixed noise) and a strict Group-based splitting strategy. Experimental results on a synthetic aromatic hydrocarbon dataset (0–500 ppm) show that the 2D model achieves an RMSE of 13.8–14.9 ppm, significantly outperforming a strong 1D-CNN baseline (RMSE ≈ 38.4 ppm). Furthermore, Sanity-Permutation verification confirms the model learns effective physical mappings rather than nearest-neighbor matching.

title: A Machine Learning Based Approach for Detection and Classification of Fault and Fault Location in Medium Voltage Power System
authors: W.A.S.U. Gunathilaka, R.M.Y.R. Chandrasiri, Eng. A.I.S. Juhaniya, Eng. M.G.N.D. Weerarathna
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Reliable and safe electrical power systems are essential to sustain modern living and industrial operations. Quick and accurate detection and localization of faults are essential for minimizing their impact and restoring normal operation. This paper presents a machine-learning-based pipeline for fault detection, multi-class classification, and fault-location estimation on a 33 kV distribution feeder in Nintavur, Sri Lanka. Real measurements from the CEB Nintavur Customer Service Centre (CSC) were digitized and modeled in ETAP to create realistic simulation data. Two stages of training were performed: initially using sample datasets generated under two feature regimes—faulty-phase-only and all-phases—and subsequently using a real-data-derived dataset. On the sample datasets, the CNN and SVM achieved 100% accuracy in the all-phase regime but less than 50% in the faulty-phase-only regime, confirming the advantage of full-phase information. On the real-data-derived dataset (240 buses, 560 samples), CNN reached 99.40% classification accuracy with a fault-location RMSE of 0.46 km, while SVM achieved 98.81% accuracy with 1.01 km RMSE. Both models provided real-time inference capability with fault-clearance decisions within 1 s. Although CNN required a longer training time compared to SVM, it consistently delivered superior performance and robustness, validating its suitability for deployment in medium-voltage feeders.

title: A SASNPA Algorithm based Robust Controller for 3-Phase Grid PV System
authors: Dheeraj Kumar, Sanjeev Singh, Shailendra Kumar, Rahul Arora
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a robust controller based on the sparse Andrew sin norms prompting adaptive (SASNPA) algorithm for 3-Phase grid-photo-voltaic (GPV) system. To limit the large error of voltage and current, an Andrew sin function (ASF) is used, along with a reweighted zero attracting (RZA) technique for reduction of number of equations. The complete controller is modeled in MATLAB-SIMULINK and applied on a 3-Phase grid connected solar photovoltaic (SPV) system for efficient transfer of available SPV power to the grid. Further, a comparative analysis of the proposed SASNPA algorithm based system and other existing controllers, is presented to demonstrate superiority of this controller. A scaled down laboratory hardware setup is also presented for validation of proposed concepts.

title: A Random Forest Regressor Model for Forecasting Agricultural Yield Using Agronomic and Climatic Parameters
authors: Gayathri S, P. Alagu Manoharan, S. Durai
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: This project builds a data-based system to predict crop yield per hectare in Tamil Nadu, India. Crop yield prediction is difficult because it depends on many changing factors like rainfall, temperature, soil type, and nutrient levels. To handle this, the system uses a machine learning method called the Random Forest Regressor, which is strong and can find complex patterns in data. The model uses six main inputs rainfall, temperature, soil <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, nitrogen <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{N})$</tex>, phosphorus <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{P})$</tex>, potassium (K) content, district, and crop type. It is trained and tested using past data and synthetic data, and it performs well based on MAE(Mean Absolute Error), RMSE(Root Mean Square Error), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> scores. A MySQL database is used to manage data efficiently and support real-time updates, creating a full system from data collection to prediction. This system gives useful insights to farmers and policymakers, helping them choose crops wisely and use resources effectively. It supports sustainable farming and food security in Tamil Nadu. In the future, it can include real-time weather data, satellite images, and profit analysis to make predictions even better.

title: A Two-Stage Robust Operation Method for Microgrid Considering Charging and Discharging Control of Electric Vehicles
authors: Runru Zhai, Zhiyuan Tang, Youbo Liu, Hongjun Gao, Weiting Xu, Zhengwei Chang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs), due to their energy storage capabilities, can serve as flexible resources to provide various control services. The large-scale integration of EVs into microgrid creates new opportunities for optimal operation. This paper proposes a two-stage robust optimal operation method for microgrid considering EVs charging and discharging control. In the proposed method, the feasible operation region (FOR) of EV charging station (EVCS) is firstly estimated. Then, based on the FOR estimation, a two-stage robust optimization problem is constructed to calculate the optimal dispatch signals for EVCSs in microgrid. Finally, a pseudo-perturbation-based broadcast control (PBC) strategy is employed for each EVCS to allocate the obtained dispatch signal to EVs. Case studies demonstrate that the proposed method can maintain microgrid voltage within safe limits while ensuring economic operation of the system.

title: Microservice Failure Propagation Simulator: Survey and Gaps
authors: Sarthak Kalyankar, Rishikesh Chaudhari, Siddhi Bodake, Tushar Rane
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: The adoption of microservice architectures has introduced unprecedented scalability and agility, but at the cost of increased operational complexity and susceptibility to cascading failures. A localized fault can propagate through complex dependency chains, leading to widespread outages. To address this, the industry uses two primary paradigms: reactive Root Cause Analysis (RCA) and proactive Chaos Engineering. This paper systematically surveys academic research and industry tools for analyzing and mitigating failure propagation in microservices. We categorize and evaluate approaches from dependency graph analysis and causal inference RCA to fault injection frameworks. Our analysis reveals a critical gap: the absence of tools for proactive, offline simulation of failure propagation, which contrasts with existing reactive and in-vivo proactive methods. Current solutions explain failures after they occur or test them in live environments but cannot predict the blast radius of potential failures at design time. This survey delineates this gap and proposes a research agenda for developing dedicated failure propagation simulators to enable predictive resilience engineering.

title: Improved Discrete Spider Monkey Optimization Using a Dynamic Penalty Function with Partial Constraint Violation Acceptance for Vending Machine Column Optimization Considering Sales and a Replenishment Cycle
authors: Riko Hasegawa, Yoshikazu Fukuyama, Takuya Watanabe, Naoto Ishibashi, Tatsuya Iizaka
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper proposes an improved discrete spider monkey optimization based method using a dynamic penalty function with partial constraint violation acceptance for vending machine column optimization considering sales and a replenishment cycle. The conventional method has a challenge that it only searches for feasible regions. To address the challenge, the proposed method added a dynamic penalty function in order to search for infeasible regions. Moreover, partial constraint violation acceptance rate is introduced to obtain high quality solutions. Effectiveness of the proposed method is verified by comparing with the conventional method, a method with only a static penalty function, a method with only a dynamic penalty function with actual vending machine data.

title: A DBN-Based Reliability Analysis of Aircraft Braking Systems
authors: Jihui Xu, Rong Liu, Wenjie Tian
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: A reliability analysis and fault diagnosis method for aircraft braking systems based on dynamic Bayesian networks (DBN) is proposed to address the reliability and fault diagnosis issues of such systems. First, conditional probability tables and marginal probability tables for each node are given by integrating failure rates and repair rates, based on the dynamic logic gates of DBN and combined with probability table modeling methods. Second, a multi-level DBN model for typical fault modules of the braking system is constructed by considering standby components and historical fault data, in accordance with the dynamic operational characteristics of the aircraft braking system. The reliability functions of components in each module are derived through the inference algorithm of DBN. Finally, laboratory simulation data are used for verification. The simulation results provide the reliability curves of each subsystem module, achieve system fault assessment and positioning of weak components, and validate the practicality of this method.

title: Automated Snow Depth Measurement and Its Transmission
authors: Praveen Kumar Mishra, Sachin Kumar, Rajesh Kumar Garg
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Accurate snow depth measurement is critical for avalanche forecasting and for safeguarding communities and infrastructure in the snow bound regions. Monitoring snow depth enables assessment of snowpack stability and supports timely issuance of warnings and advisories to mitigate risks. This paper presents an automated method for snow depth measurement using images of snow stakes captured remotely by a fixed-position camera. The system employs a cost-effective yet efficient combination of a fixed camera and a processing unit. While largely autonomous, the method requires minimal user input for initializing a reference image patch. The approach integrates image processing and machine learning techniques, including image patch similarity analysis and optical character recognition (OCR), to determine snow depth. After detection of snow depth, measured snow depth is transmitted through a GSM modem using protocol description unit (PDU) mode over short message service. The proposed method offers a low-cost solution for image transmission in bandwidth constraint environment and in remote areas where there are no operators available for manual measurement.

title: A Novel Automatic Modulation Recognition Methodology based on Attention Mechanism
authors: Yue Hu, Yu Long, Jinchao Hu, Yongyang Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Automatic modulation recognition is a key technology in cognitive intelligence, which can be used to distinguish signal modulation modes, especially for non-cooperative signals. The development of deep learning empowers automatic modulation recognition to be more intelligent and demonstrates better performance than traditional methods. However, it should be noted that automatic modulation recognition based on deep learning still has shortcomings. Firstly, the existing methods still cannot fully extract signal features. Secondly, the extracted features are directly used for brute force classification in most methods, which ignores the deep processing of features. These issues limit the further improvement of recognition accuracy. To solve the problems mentioned above, a novel automatic modulation recognition methodology based on attention mechanism is proposed. Firstly, a feature extraction enhancement module is designed to optimize feature extraction of signal. Then, a feature cross fusion module is proposed to fuse the enhanced features extracted from In-phase signal and Quadrature-phase signal respectively. The experiment is conducted to prove the effectiveness of our proposed methodology based on the benchmark dataset RadioML2016.10A. Compared to the existing popular deep learning methods, our methodology exhibits better performance, with recognition accuracy increased by 1.82% to 14.40%.

title: Investigating Slow Multiplicative Noise and its Influence on ECG Signals
authors: Gokul Prakash, Libya Thomas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: The influence of slow multiplicative noise (MN) on signal quality has been studied mainly from a theoretical perspective. Earlier work derived closed-form expressions for the energy spectrum of the noise modulation function (NMF), showing that such noise introduces both discrete and continuous spectral components. In this paper, we build on that foundation and move toward practical validation. Using synthetic electrocardiogram (ECG) signals created with NeuroKit2, we simulate amplitude and phase distortions modeled as Gaussian processes. The time and frequency domain behavior of the ensuing noise-modulated signals are investigated with particular focus on energy spectra, pulse fluctuations, and phase changes. To emphasize the distinctive role played by MN, we also contrast its action with that of additive noise. The overall goal is to introduce insight into the way MN deforms biomedical signals like ECG, and why its action has to be taken into account in clinical and wearable monitoring scenarios.

title: Research on Fault Traveling Wave Location of Medium-Voltage Cables Based on DWT-VMD and Time-Domain Cross-Correlation
authors: Aoyu Liu, Xiaotong Jing, Xiangjun Zeng, Jian Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Medium-voltage cables are the core equipment of 35kV collection lines in new energy power stations, and their fault location accuracy directly affects the operation and maintenance efficiency and power supply stability of the stations. To address the problems of traditional location methods being affected by noise interference and inaccurate time delay calculation, this paper proposes a traveling wave location method based on DWT-VMD decomposition and time-domain cross-correlation. Through wavelet denoising and VMD feature extraction, the optimal decomposition layer is selected by combining a dynamic weight scoring system, and the traveling wave time delay is accurately calculated using time-domain cross-correlation. Verified by simulation and experimental platforms, the method effectively suppresses noise and refraction-reflection interference, featuring high positioning accuracy and strong robustness, which provides reliable technical support for the rapid fault location of cables in new energy power stations.

title: Dual-Phase Graph Refinement: Pruning Noisy Edges and Augmenting Tail Links for Recommendation
authors: Ziyuan Zhu, Wentao Huang, Jinhui Zhang, Haibo Ye
abstract: Implicit feedback recommender systems face significant performance challenges due to noisy interactions and sparse data from long-tail users. Noisy edges propagate errors through graph convolutions, while sparse interactions of long-tail users bias recommendations toward popular items, compromising fairness and accuracy. This paper proposes a novel Dual-Phase Graph Refinement (DPGR) framework, built upon GNN, to optimize the interaction graph structure through synergistic denoising and long-tail link augmentation. In the first phase, DPGR employs node neighborhood similarity with a dynamic thresholding mechanism to accurately identify and remove unreliable edges, constructing a high-quality subgraph to enhance embedding robustness. In the second phase, targeting longtail users-defined as the bottom 80% by interaction count-DPGR selectively adds high-confidence links via a probabilistic sampling model, enriching their neighborhood representations and mitigating sparsity. Comprehensive empirical studies on three diverse datasets demonstrate that DPGR outperforms other methods in both overall and long-tail performance, achieving average improvements of 7.98% and 6.53% over the baseline, respectively, across Recall and NDCG metrics. Ablation studies confirm the necessity of the dual-phase design, and noise injection experiments further highlight the method's robustness. By jointly optimizing noise reduction and long-tail augmentation, DPGR offers an efficient and equitable solution for graph-based collaborative filtering.

title: Smart Assistance System for Alzheimer's Patients Using the Internet of Things
authors: Anita Dombale, Ishika Mahadar, Heramb Mahadik, Dhruv Madderlawar, Amruta Magar, Aditya Loya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease leads to memory decline and creates significant challenges for caregivers. This paper introduces a Smart Assistance System that uses IoT and AI to bring together various support modules into one mobile platform. The system combines real-time GPS tracking with geofencing, fall detection, medication reminders, appointment reminders, behavioral logging, and a digital family tree. It uses dual Firebase databases for better data handling. A notable feature is the AI caregiver chatbot, which offers guidance and helps reduce the burden on caregivers, an area that has not been fully explored in previous research. We validated the hardware implementation with ESP32, NEO-6M GPS, and MPU6050 sensors in controlled trials. These trials showed 95.6% accuracy in geofencing and 92% accuracy in fall detection. Caregiver alerts were sent out within 3 to 5 seconds. Unlike existing systems that focus on single functions, NeuroCare provides a unified, affordable, and scalable solution for Alzheimer's care. Future work will include predictive analytics, biosensor integration, and improved data security.

title: Adaptive Channel Switching Algorithm for Enhanced Resilience in Hybrid OCC-RF Drone Systems
authors: Yukang Kim, Ki-Hyung Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) communications face dual challenges: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R F}$</tex> channels are vulnerable to interception and jamming attacks [1], while Optical Camera Communication (OCC) offers security but suffers from physical disruptions [2] [3]. This paper proposes a Hybrid Adaptive Channel Switching Algorithm that designates OCC as the main secure channel with RF as emergency backup. The algorithm employs hierarchical defense: (1) cryptographic filtering of logical attacks (replay, tampering) via nonce and MAC verification without channel switching [7]; (2) adaptive FEC (LDPC/Reed-Solomon) redundancy adjustment based on the experimentally derived physical collapse threshold of BER 0.02 and (3) autonomous RF switching upon physical collapse detection. Simulation across six attack scenarios demonstrates successful logical attack filtering <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<1 ~\text{ms}$</tex> Response Time), adaptive resilience under degraded conditions, and reliable failover during jamming/blockage <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{(}\boldsymbol{\sim}\mathbf{500ms}$</tex> switching response time), ensuring continuous drone control even under severe cyber-physical threats.

title: Structured Prompt Optimization for Few-Shot Text Classification via Semantic Alignment in Latent Space
authors: Jiasen Zheng, Zijun Zhou, Huajun Zhang, Junjiang Lin, Jingyun Jia, Qi Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This study addresses the issues of semantic entanglement, unclear label structure, and insufficient feature representation in few-shot text classification, and proposes an optimization framework based on structured prompts to enhance semantic understanding and task adaptation under low-resource conditions. The framework first uses a pretrained language model to encode the input text and obtain basic semantic representations. It then introduces structured prompts composed of multi-dimensional semantic factors and integrates them with text features through a learnable combination mechanism, which forms task-related representations with clear boundaries in the latent space. To further strengthen the consistency between text representations and label semantics, the method constructs a structured label embedding matrix and employs a cross-space alignment mechanism to ensure stable matching between textual features and label attributes. In addition, the model applies prompt orthogonality constraints and a joint optimization objective to maintain independence across different semantic factors in the prompts, allowing the structured prompts to provide transparent and controllable guidance for classification decisions. Three types of sensitivity experiments, including learning rate sensitivity, prompt length sensitivity, and data scale sensitivity, are designed to evaluate the stability and robustness of the framework under different conditions. Experimental results show that the proposed structured prompt optimization framework effectively alleviates semantic conflicts and label ambiguity in few-shot text classification. It significantly improves performance on accuracy, precision, recall, and AUC, and demonstrates strong cross-task applicability. Overall, the method achieves stable semantic modeling under limited data and offers an efficient and interpretable optimization pathway for few-shot text classification.

title: Embedding-Only Federated Edge Learning for Privacy-Preserving eHealth
authors: Bharathwaj Vijayakumar, Samyukta Alapati, Sahana Varadaraju
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The next wave of digital health innovation lies in combining federated learning and edge intelligence for secure, low-latency physiological monitoring. This paper introduces a federated self-supervised edge framework for wearable devices that track physiological transitions such as heart-rate variability, skin temperature, and galvanic skin response. The system enables decentralized learning directly on devices without transmitting sensitive data, addressing key challenges in privacy and communication efficiency. Each client employs a lightweight one-dimensional convolutional encoder trained via contrastive self-supervised learning to capture temporal patterns in local signals. Instead of sharing raw data or model weights, each device uploads a compressed embedding centroid to a central aggregator that computes a global reference embedding for anomaly scoring. A prototype simulation across five edge clients achieved Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (AUROC) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.86$</tex> while reducing communication overhead by 82 %. Visualization via t-SNE revealed separable manifolds of normal and anomalous physiology, demonstrating a novel, communication-efficient paradigm for privacy-preserving, federated IoT-based healthcare.

title: A General Framework for Neural Adaptive Sensing of Dynamical Fields
authors: Felix Köster, Atsushi Uchida
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Physical dynamical systems can be considered as natural information processors, where their trajectories encode past inputs in high-dimensional continuous states. Reservoir computing exploits this property by driving a fixed nonlinear dynamical system and learning only a linear output mapping, however traditional approaches assume fixed measurement locations and weights, which limits robustness across different regimes and substrates. This work introduces a general framework for adaptive measurement of continuous physical dynamics using a partial-differential-equation-based reservoir, where both the spatial positions of the measurements and the output weighting are learned jointly. The method dynamically determines how and where to observe the state of the system and optimizes sensor placement end-to-end. Experiments on forecasting tasks involving multiple chaotic systems demonstrate substantial improvements in accuracy compared to fixed-measurement reservoir computing approaches, indicating that adaptive measurement provides an effective interface for extracting task-relevant information from continuous dynamical systems.

title: Cross-view UAV Geo-localization via Wavelet-based Local Feature Enhancement
authors: Chen Zhang, Rui Zhao, Zhiping Lin, Zhenguo Gao, Qing an Peng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Cross-view image matching serves as a pivotal technology for UAV autonomous localization and navigation. Yet, given the severe viewpoint disparities between UAV and satellite imagery, current approaches often fail to balance the extraction of global semantics with the preservation of fine-grained local details. To overcome this limitation, we present an enhanced feature model built upon a lightweight backbone. We design a fusion architecture that leverages the Unit Spatial Attention Module (USAM) from Lin et al. to target salient regions, followed by the innovative integration of the Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) to establish a local feature enhancement module. This module explicitly captures high-frequency components—such as edges and textures—thereby effectively mitigating spatial information loss during downsampling. On the University-1652 benchmark, our method delivers highly competitive performance, achieving 81.63% R@1 in the localization task with a lightweight ConvNeXt-Tiny backbone. This validates the efficacy of our strategy in synergizing frequency-domain details with spatial semantics.

title: Research on Construction Methodology of Digital Twins for Communication Infrastructure
authors: Yujing Zhang, Yu Zhang, Yang Yu, Rui Zong, Jingzhong Zhang, Xiang Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This paper systematically analyzes the current data challenges and their underlying root causes within communication infrastructure systems. It presents a comprehensive overview of Digital Twin (DT) technology and examines its specific implementation requirements. The core contribution lies in proposing a robust, integrated construction methodology for DTs, alongside prospective application scenarios. This research establishes a comprehensive technical foundation for DT implementation in communication infrastructure environments.

title: Zero-Knowledge Proof-Based Verification System Based on Environmental Sensing for Reliable Operation of AI-Driven Autonomous Robots
authors: Arata Nakajima, Hideaki Miyaji, Hiroshi Yamamoto
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The smart cities that collaborate with AI-driven autonomous robots are attracting attention for supporting various social activities in the real world. In facilities that provide such services, various systems managing the facility and robots may coexist in the common area. By enabling the systems to interoperate and share information about the status of the facility and robots, it becomes possible to realize a variety of services that support safety and security within the facility. However, while the operators of the facility want to monitor the detailed conditions of the robots, the operators of the robots are cautious about providing the information about the status of robots such as the moving trajectory and various sensor data. To resolve this dilemma, we propose a new system that enables estimation of the operational conditions of the robots by verifying the positions and trajectories at landmarks in the facility without disclosing their internal information. In the proposed system, we focus on the observation of environmental information that accurately reflects the real-world situation for estimating the proximity between the robot and each landmark. As the environmental information, both systems on robots and a facility measure CSI (Channel State Information) and acoustic information. In addition, by utilizing zero-knowledge proof (ZKP) technology, the system for the facility confirms the reliability of the process for estimating the proximity of the robots to the landmark without exchanging detailed internal information. Through the proof-of-concept experiment, applying the proposed system achieved high-accuracy proximity detection with both methods (CSI and acoustic information) yielding precision and recall rates exceeding 0.90.

title: FPGA-Based File Level AES-Encryption System with Volatile Physical Key
authors: Diego Penaflor, Jimmy Tarrillo
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Traditional file encryption methods face security limitations due to vulnerabilities to side-channel attacks in both software and hardware implementations. In addition, most commercial solutions are designed for storage-level encryption and are typically embedded in storage devices. This paper presents a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)-based hardware encryption system that provides improved resistance to side-channel attacks. The system performs file-level encryption (FLE) using the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) with a 256-bit key. The encryption key is entered through a physical keypad and stored only in a volatile BRAM block, ensuring that it is erased at power-off and cannot be extracted remotely. A USB-UART interface is integrated into the system, enabling connection to any conventional computer. Experimental results showed that the device and its interface operated as expected, ensured that the encryption key remained volatile, and successfully encrypted and decrypted files, which could only be restored with the corresponding key.

title: $H_{\infty}$ Dynamic Output Feedback Control for the IPT System with External Disturbances
authors: Shuangxin Zhu, Qinglong Feng, Yi Zhang, Yuanjun Hou, Jinbin Zhao, Zhixin Wang
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: Due to unpredictable variations in actual operating environments, it is necessary for inductive power transfer (IPT) systems to dynamically adjust their output signals based on realtime demands under different conditions. To address this issue, an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> dynamic output feedback (DOF) control strategy is proposed to guarantee accurate tracking of time-varying reference outputs while maintain robust performance against environmental disturbances in this paper. Firstly, the dynamic mathematical model of the IPT system with the series-series (SS) compensation network is built via the extended describing function (EDF) method. Then, a closed-loop tracking control scheme with external disturbances is employed to enhance the regulation adaptability of the output voltage under engineering operational conditions. Subsequently, the gains of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> DOF controller are determined via sufficient conditions that ensure the asymptotic stability of the augmented closed-loop tracking control system and disturbance suppression index. Finally, simulation results of the SS-IPT system verify that the effectiveness and practical feasibility of the proposed control approach.

title: Intelligent Computer Vision Model for Brain Sub-Region Segmentation and Classification in Alzheimer's Disease Detection
authors: Akash Kumar Bhagat, Sumukh Pandey, Malepati Chandra Sekhar
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a neurodegenerative disorder that requires early and exact analysis to improve the patient. This research presents a computer vision methodology to classify brain MRI scans as Normal Control (NC), Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI), and AD stages. The method applies Adaptive Dragonfly Optimization (ADO) to determine the hyperparameters of a Back Propagation Neural Network (BPNN) with the aim to enhance the performance of classification. MRI scans are preprocessed with a Gaussian filter to eliminate noise, improving the image quality for analysis. Comparative outcomes illustrate that the novel ADO-BPNN model performs better compared to current algorithms, such as CAD, CNN, and RF, with 97.62% accuracy, precision of 98.79%, recall of 97.47%, and F1-score of 98.32%. Such results show the promise of the model for the finding of AD at a previous level and decision support in a clinical setting. The model was deployed using Python 3.8 on a Windows 10 operating system. Through combining swarm intelligence by the Dragonfly Algorithm and tuning a neural network for brain sub-region classification, the method provides a robust and efficient solution to Alzheimer's diagnosis. The research proves the effectiveness of the integration of Deep Learning (DL) methods and optimization algorithms for developing the accuracy and interpretability of AD finding systems.

title: AnonymEyes: Lightweight and Affordable Privacy-Enhanced Human Detection via Depth Sensing on Edge Devices
authors: Sercan Yeşilköy, Mohsen Ali Alawami, Yoon-Ho Choi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Privacy concerns in human monitoring systems have become increasingly critical as surveillance technologies proliferate in public and private spaces. Conventional detection solutions rely on RGB-based systems, raising serious privacy concerns due to potential identity disclosure. This paper introduces AnonymEyes, a lightweight, affordable, and privacyenhanced human detection system utilizing Time-of-Flight (ToF) depth cameras with edge computing devices such as the Raspberry Pi. By exclusively leveraging depth data while discarding RGB-based data, AnonymEyes enhances individual anonymity while maintaining high detection performance. The system employs YOLOv8 Nano architecture based framework to process ToF depth frames, achieving real-time detection on resourceconstrained environments. The system was trained and validated on over 1,500 annotated ToF depth frames encompassing diverse poses, clothing, and appearances. The system demonstrates exceptional metrics: 99.90% precision, 100.00% recall, 99.95% F1-score, 99.50% mAP@0.5, and 92.50% mAP@0.5:0.95 across stricter IoU thresholds. AnonymEyes represents a robust and scalable solution for applications requiring accurate, real-time human presence monitoring in privacy-sensitive, resourceconstrained environments.

title: Realtime Sign Language to Text Conversion with AI Sentence Completion
authors: R. P. Narmadha, V. Ajay, S. K. Dharaneesh, S. Hareini
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: This paper is going to present a real time sign language translation system it can convert hand gestures into text using a webcam-based interface. User show a hand gesture and hold it steady for two seconds, which the system automatically recognizes the gesture and outputs the respective alphabet character. The proposed system is designed to enhance communication for individuals with speech and hearing impairments. Tracking and Hand detection are performed using the default MediaPipe hand landmark model, which enables accurate visual capture from live video streams. The extracted hand landmark features are processed using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) trained to classify sign language gestures at the character level. To enhance user experience and communication speed, a word and sentence prediction module is interfaced using the LLaMA 3.1 language model via API access on the Groq platform. The system is developed using Tensorflow and Python. This helps the system run directly in a web browser and provides real-time visual feedback through a simple user interface. Overall, unlike other sign language convertors, this paper presents on prediction of the words based on the sentence context which reduces a significant amount of time for the conversion of long sentences compared to traditional sign language conversion.

title: An Improved Deep Learning Framework for Arterial Stiffness–Based Heart Disease Risk Prediction in Diabetic Patients
authors: R Parameswari, P Sudarsanam, Mikkilineni Vasudevarao, Yadala Sucharitha, M Nirmala, Erla Mani
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Diabetic patients may be able to use arterial stiffness (AS) as a biomarker to gauge their risk of cardiovascular disease (CVD). Still lacking are AI-based clinical prediction models for AS detection, and the nature of the link between diabetes and cardiovascular risk is not entirely apparent. By keeping an eye on AS, diabetes patients can get early warning signs of problems, allowing for prompt treatment. In order to better forecast the risk of CVD, this study suggests an optimized DL-approach to evaluate the risk of AS in diabetic individuals. The model's diagnostic parameters include fasting blood glucose (FBG) and brachial-ankle pulse wave velocity (baPWV). To begin, we use the LASSO approach to extract important characteristics from patients' medical data. Then, we use PCA to eliminate outliers by calculating their Mahalanobis distance and reconstruction error. The Gaussian LASSO Cat Optimization Algorithm (GLCOA) is then used to perform a feature selection process. The last one is DKNN-FL, which stands for Deep Kronecker Neural Network optimized with the Frog Leap Algorithm. On top of that, we use cross validation and SMOTE augmentation to prevent overfitting. An analysis of variance (ANOVA) demonstrates that the suggested work outperforms the state-of-the-art on a dataset containing discoveries made in the Dryad digital repository. Area under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUROC) for DKNN-FL with GLCOA is 0.989, and the accuracy is 98.7 percent, according to the comparison study. Applying the suggested model to a different diabetic's prediction dataset yields a 95.8% success rate.

title: Adaptive ship target recognition method based on deep learning
authors: Rongxin Liu, Fan Yu, Chuanyang Ge
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Ship target recognition is one of the key contents in underwater acoustics research. and how to make full use of ship radiated noise for feature extraction and classification recognition is an important topic in the field of underwater acoustic signal processing. This paper proposes an adaptive feature extraction and target classification method based on deep learning. This method employs Improved Complete Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition with Adaptive Noise (ICEEMDAN) to adaptively decompose the signal into components across different frequency bands, yielding various orders of Intrinsic Mode Functions (IMFs). The signal to be demodulated is reconstructed by summing the strongly modulated components through a judgment of envelope entropy. The reconstructed signal is then analyzed to obtain both the continuous spectrum and line spectrum features of the target noise modulation spectrum. Concurrently, each IMF is subjected to smoothing filtering, and all components are subsequently summed again. The reconstructed denoised signal is analyzed using Low Frequency Analysis Recording (LOFAR) spectrograms. Finally, a multi-feature fusion model is utilized for target classification and recognition. Experimental results indicate that the proposed method demonstrates robust performance on the dataset, exhibiting favorable classification effectiveness.

title: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome Risk Prediction System using Transparent Machine Learning
authors: Rabeeya M, SreeVarshini B, Jeyanthi P
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Strong and trustworthy diagnostic tools are required for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), a very common and frequently misdiagnosed endocrine condition. The creation of the PCOS Risk Predictor, an AI-powered tool intended for the transparent and early evaluation of PCOS risk, is described in this paper. Vite, React, and TypeScript make up the modern, effective, and component-driven web stack used in the system's construction, guaranteeing a scalable and responsive user interface. Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui are used to manage styling and user experience, resulting in a polished and user-friendly clinical front-end. Crucially, the system's core processes pertinent clinical features and generates a risk prediction using a Machine Learning (ML) model. The application is based on Explainable AI (XAI) principles, which offer a concise, convincing explanation for each prediction in order to address the inherent “black-box” problem in clinical AI. The goal of this openness is to empower patients and promote trust between healthcare providers. The integrated system represents a major breakthrough in the noninvasive early diagnosis of PCOS by providing a quick, objective, and comprehensible way to support clinical decision-making. According to experimental results, the suggested Gradient Boosting model outperforms baseline models with an accuracy of 94.2 % and a recall of 92.8 %. Clinical relevance is confirmed by SHAP-based explanations, which show that hormonal and metabolic characteristics like HOMA-IR, testosterone levels, and the LH/FSH ratio are the most significant predictors.

title: Dividend Policy, Investment Opportunities, and Firm Value: A Moderated-Mediation Analysis in Indonesia's Coal Sector
authors: Ayu Yunita, M. Shabri Abd. Majid, A. Sakir
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: This study explores how dividend policy mediates the effect of investment opportunity set on firm value, with firm size as a moderator, using evidence from 15 Indonesian coal companies during 2015–2024. Data were drawn from financial reports and stock prices. The results show that investment opportunity set significantly influenced dividend policy, while both directly affected firm value. Dividend policy effectively mediated the relationship, and firm size strengthened the impact of dividend policy on firm value. These findings highlight the importance of dividend decisions and firm characteristics in shaping value creation in Indonesia's coal industry.

title: An Adaptive Ensemble-Based Multi-Focus Image Fusion Framework Using Classical Methods and Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: M S Santhi Krishna, D Harikrishnan, C R Shiyas
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: Multi-Focus Image Fusion is a computational imaging approach to fuse multiple depth focused images into an all-in-focus representation. Classical fusion methods like Laplacian, Wavelet and PCA-based fusion can capture low level features efficiently but high level semantic features cannot be preserved. Deep learning methods (such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)) can be used to learn complicated spatial features but are more likely to ignore fine-grained texture details. Inspired by the success of classical fusion as well as deep learning technique, we develop a novel composite ensemble based architecture to better solve this problem. This model produces pixel-wise adaptive weights to fuse the pixel wise output of multiple fusion strategies. Comprehensive experiments on the benchmark MFIF datasets show that this framework has a significant and steady superiority over their classical and CNN counterparts in standard metrics such as SSIM, PSNR, Entropy, Standard Deviation and Spatial Frequency. The experimental results show that the method can obtain high-quality all-in-focus images so that the proposed method is capable for high-level image fusion.

title: S2VAD: A Self-Supervised Framework for Unsupervised Visual Anomaly Detection
authors: Mariam Ishtiaq, Jongun Won
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: In open world environments, vision anomaly detection (VAD) is inherently complex due to diverse and unpredictable anomaly manifestations. To overcome the limitation of the availability of an inclusive training data, unsupervised anomaly detection (UAD) methods provide a solid baseline. Principally, using only normal distribution patterns for training, any deviations can be flagged as anomalies during inference. Following the same analogy, we propose a self-supervised framework for unsupervised anomaly detection termed S2VAD. We explore the potential of DINOv2 as an encoder for normal feature extraction, a bottleneck as a compression head, and a transformer decoder using self-attention for VAD. We use a global cosine loss for comparing the test image to the learned normality and detecting anomalies. Using the benchmark MVTec-AD dataset, our work shows state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance on texture classes with an image AUROC of 99.8 %. To explore the model further, we share interesting future work directions.

title: Rat Swarm Optimization-based CSD for Design of Circular Symmetric 2D FIR Filter and Hardware Architecture Implementation
authors: Venkata Krishna Odugu, Merina Jaladi, Harish Babu Gade, Pinjala N Malleswari, S. Harivardhagini, Malla Reddy Amreddy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Using Rat Swarm Optimization (RSO) and Canonical Signed Digit (CSD) representation, this work proposes an optimized architecture for a circular symmetrical Two-Dimensional Finite Impulse Response (2DFIR) filter. The filter is developed in 45 nm CMOS technology and deployed on Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) also for real-time signal processing applications. A novel modified McClellan transformation approach is called $\mathbf{P 5}$ transformation is considered for the design of sharp circular symmetric filter. Minimizing the error between the desired and actual filter responses is achieved through the efficient optimization of the filter coefficients in the suggested design, which makes use of RSO. By converting the optimized coefficients to CSD form, we minimize the amount of non-zero terms, which in turn reduces the number of needed multiplications and, in turn, the hardware complexity. This architecture is perfect for adaptive filtering, communication systems, and image processing since the filter is implemented on FPGA, which guarantees high-speed parallel processing. Genus, a synthesis tool, is used to obtain the real area, power, and timing of the design. Using simulation and real-time FPGA testing, we were able to evaluate the design’s performance and see that it strikes a good balance between power efficiency, speed, and hardware resource utilization.

title: Evaluating VGG Network for Autism Spectrum Disorder Identification via Facial Images
authors: C. M. Maind, P. P. Vast
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental condition that affects a child's nervous system, developmental progress, and social interaction skills. Due to its complex nature and the resource intensive diagnostic process, early and accurate identification remains a significant challenge. This study investigates the use of deep learning models, specifically convolutional neural networks (CNNs), to support ASD identification through facial images. VGG16 and VGG19 architectures were trained and evaluated on a publicly available Kaggle dataset namely ASD_V4 comprising 2,886 facial images. VGG16 achieved a classification accuracy of 91.43%, while VGG19 reached 90.00%. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of VGG-based models in aiding early ASD identification. Future work will focus on expanding the validating model performance across diverse populations to improve generalizability.

title: A Universal Domain Knowledge Self-Embedding Framework for Remote Sensing Building Change Detection
authors: Hong Yang, Tiancheng Wu, Xiaozheng Zhang, Chunhui Tan, An Xu, Nan Zhang, Yu Lei, Jiao Shi
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Building change detection is crucial for urban planning and sustainable development but faces challenges due to building morphology diversity, environmental interferences, and dependency on labeled datasets. To address this, we propose a domain-knowledge self-embedded training method that integrates prior insights on planar and vertical building features without relying on pre-annotated data. The approach introduces a pre-processing multi-layer feature extraction module and a post-fusion edge feature analysis module into conventional change detection frameworks, emphasizing high texture contrast and morphological shadows. Specifically, it employs multi-feature fusion and a deep edge guidance loss to enhance discrimination between building and non-building areas, utilizing non-maximum suppression and dual-threshold processing for improved accuracy and robustness. Evaluated on several remote sensing datasets, the approach demonstrates superior performance in reducing false detections and maintaining edge integrity, achieving reliable results even in unsupervised settings.

title: An Integrated Transformer-Based Framework for Multilingual Summarization and Automated Question Generation in Education
authors: V. Harshini, T.N. Jeyashree, S. Karthiga, S. PadmaDevi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The increased use of digital resources, means learners are finding it a challenge to search and analyze the details from the documents, slides, and recorded lectures because there is a lot to choose from. Although the use of the transformer model in some languages, like summarizing a piece of writing, producing study questions, most systems perform these types of tasks in a separate system. In highly controlled environments. The concept of integrating these systems in one place that can deal with more than one language, as well as other multimedia such as a learning process, is a concept that has never really been explored. Learners need a system that will assist them, while the use of language models needs to be integrated in a manner that will make the learning processes easier. The resources, such as documents, slides, and recorded lectures, among many more, are very essential to learners. This paper presents EduCraft, an AI-assisted educational framework designed to generate study materials automatically. EduCraft uses computer programs to make summaries, practice questions, flashcards, and audio and video materials out of all sorts of things we learn at school. EduCraft was evaluated to assess its effectiveness in summarization, question generation, and multimedia content creation. We looked into how well it summarizes, what people think of the questions it produces, and what users think about the audio and video it produces. Results show that people using a variety of ways of learning-text, audio, and video-can study more quickly and be more interested in what they are learning than by studying text alone. EduCraft is able to produce all these study materials out of all the things we learn in school. The current study also discusses challenges related to multilingual consistency and content reliability, and thus highlights considerations when such systems are deployed in a real-world educational setting.

title: A Simplified Controller for Speed and Position Sensorless Controlled PMBLDC Motor Drive
authors: Anupam Das, Rahul Arora, Sanjeev Singh, Dheeraj Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Speed and position sensorless operation of PMBLDC motor, requires estimation of rotor position and speed to obtain the commutation points of the voltage source inverter (VSI). There are various methods of estimation, out of which the back-emf waveform-based methods are mostly preferred but the back-emf waveforms suffer from false zero crossings (ZC) resulting in faulty commutation. Physical RC filters are mostly used to tackle this problem but suffer with performance issues in wide rangeof speed. This paper presents a simplified controller with use of a digital filter specially designed for avoiding the false ZC points in back EMF waveforms and obtaining a smooth position sensorless operation of PMBLDC motor. The speed and rotor positions are extracted from the line back-emf across the motor terminals. The performance simulation of the designed system is obtained using MATLAB-Simulink platform which is successfully validated on a laboratory hardware setup having 1 kW PMBLDC Motor.

title: Adaptive IVMD-LSTM Model for Ultra-Short-Term Wind-Solar Power Forecasting
authors: Zhou Can, Li Jian, Luo Bin, Li Xiaopeng, Shi Peng, Nie Zhuang, Zeng Xueyang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: To fully leverage meteorological elements for enhancing wind and solar power forecasting, an ultra-short-term prediction model integrating Improved Variational Mode Decomposition (IVMD) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) artificial neural network is proposed. First, considering the volatility of renewable power and meteorological input sequences, VMD is employed to decompose the sequences into different frequency components. To avoid mode mixing and improve decomposition efficiency, the VMD process is optimized using a center frequency-based method, resulting in IVMD. Second, differential correlation analysis is applied to optimize the vectorization process of different modal components of the meteorological sequences. Each modal component is processed via sliding window vectorization and used as input to the LSTM model, so as to explore temporal correlations between meteorological modes and power sequences. Finally, to improve the adaptability and generalization of the model, an adaptive parameter optimization framework is introduced to match the best optimization algorithm for different power stations. Simulation analyses were conducted using real-world data from photovoltaic and wind farms in Southwest China. The results show that at the Ganbazi wind farm, the forecasting error (MAPE) was reduced by $13.7 \%$ and $1.6 \%$ compared to the LSTM and IVMD-LSTM models, respectively. Similarly, the Yimen solar farm saw improvements of $7.4 \%$ and $1.1 \%$, respectively.

title: Automatic Parameter Identification for Electric Drives: Ensuring High Accuracy and Adaptive Control Systems
authors: Alexander Korolev, Andrew Senkevich
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: Achieving high accuracy, stability, and adaptability in electric drive control systems requires precise knowledge of the drive’s dynamic parameters. This paper presents an automatic frequency-domain identification algorithm for real-time parameter estimation without interrupting the technological process. The method employs a linear second-order transfer function model that represents either a DC drive or a vector-controlled induction motor. By injecting low-amplitude sinusoidal test signals into the control loop across a broad frequency range (0.1–5000 Hz) and recording the amplitude and phase response of the output signal, the algorithm extracts key parameters—system gain and inertial time constants—with a typical accuracy of ±1–2%. This approach eliminates the need for dedicated offline testing, thereby increasing system availability and enabling continuous performance monitoring and adaptive controller tuning. Experimental validation confirms the algorithm’s robustness in industrial environments, establishing a reliable foundation for optimized control design, early fault detection, and predictive maintenance strategies

title: Threshold Voltage Variability Induced by Localized Ferroelectric Phase Distributions in FeFET and Its Mitigation Strategies
authors: Omkar Phadke, Minji Shon, Stuart Wodzro, Halid Mulaosmanovic, Stefan Dünkel, Sven Beyer, Asif Khan, Suman Datta, Shimeng Yu
abstract: Threshold voltage variability is a key concern in ferroelectric field effect transistor (FeFET) technology. In this work, a study spanning experiments, modeling, and SPICE simulations is conducted to evaluate the impact of localized FE/ dielectric (DE) distributions on the threshold voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {TH}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of FeFET. The device characterization is performed on the 28-nm FeFET foundry platform. The experimental study on device-to-device (D2D) and cycle-to-cycle (C2C) variations reveals that the FeFET can be an asymmetric device, with the V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</sub> showing dependence on the direction of current flow, as well as the magnitude of applied drain voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {DS}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) to the device. The TCAD modeling study reveals that the cause of asymmetry is the spatial localization of DE phases that may occur due to the random nature of the distribution. SPICE simulation for two-FeFET-based ternary content-addressable memory (TCAM) reveals that as few as two devices exhibiting the localization-induced threshold voltage shift can lead to a 64-bit search failure. Various strategies are investigated to minimize the impact of such localized phase distribution-induced threshold voltage shift (LPTS) effect. Notably, the capacitive readout of FeFET and the insertion of a floating metal electrode in the gate-stack improve the robustness.

title: Direct measurement of transient temperature at the tool–chip interface using a 3D-printed sensor-integrated cutting tool
authors: Chao Wang, Tatsuki Saito, Haruka Yamamoto, Zhitong Xie, Toru Kizaki, Tanibuchi Takahito, Hirosaki Kouji, Kumai Kenji, Keisuke Nagato, Naohiko Sugita
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Due to the extreme thermal-mechanical stress at tool-chip interface, built-in sensors in the cutting tool are prone to failure during machining process despite their high sensitivity. Eroding thermocouple (ETC) can operate at high stress and high wear conditions; however, traditional adhesive bonding methods introduce significant thermal inertia because the low-thermal-con-ductivity bonding materials reduce response speed and cause sys-tematic measurement errors. In this study, we demonstrate for the first time the integration of an ETC at the tool–chip interface using a solid-state diffusion-bonding approach, which creates direct joints between the sensor and substrate materials. A 3D-printed stainless-steel tool was co-sintered with the ETC, achieving seam-less bonding, rapid response, and robust temperature monitoring based on solid-state diffusion. The performance of this sensor-in-tegrated cutting tool was first evaluated through calibration ex-periments and subsequently verified through real cutting tests. To process the measurements, an extended Kalman filter was em-ployed to filter and regulate the data into an equivalent first-order model. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed sensor exhibits superior response speed and robustness compared with the conventional sensor, achieving a 67.8% mean reduction in thermal time constant and improving quasi-steady-state robust-ness by as much as 36.2%. Furthermore, the method enhances measurement accuracy, as evidenced by a distinctly narrowed un-certainty band.

title: Distance-Security Tradeoffs for Repeaterless End-to-End QKD Networks_supp1-3678029.pdf
authors: Janis Notzel
abstract: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) offers provably secure, information-theoretic key exchange, but in long-distance scenarios without quantum repeaters, Trusted Nodes (TNs) are commonly employed despite introducing critical security risks. We propose a redundant key management method for QKD network that combines Twin Field QKD (TF-QKD) (or Measurement-Device Independent (MDI)-QKD) with a novel key-routing scheme to eliminate the need for truly trusted TNs. Quantum measurements are handled entirely within the network, minimizing end-user hardware requirements. Multiple QKD links connect intermediate nodes such that a successful attack requires the collusion of multiple adversarial nodes, greatly enhancing security over the traditional TN model. In this contribution, we discuss the tradeoff between security, key rates, and distances supported by the new method. Our analysis reveals that the improved redundant key management system may enable true end-to-end connectivity over several thousand kilometers while maintaining high security standards.

title: The Franklin Stove: An Unintended American Revolution—Joyce E. Chaplin (New York, NY, USA: Farrar, Straus and Giroux, 2025, 422 pp.)

title: Top-K-Aware Set Optimization for Component-Sharing Multiobjective Optimization_supp1-3678952.pdf
authors: Qingfu Zhang
abstract: Component-sharing multiobjective optimization (CSMOO) is an emerging research topic that seeks to identify a set of optimal solutions with shared components in the decision space. This task can be modeled as a bi-level structured multiobjective optimization problem (MOP), where the upper level is a single-objective optimization problem, and the lower level is an MOP parameterized by upper-level variables. However, existing methods often generate an unnecessary large set of solutions when solving each lower-level MOP. This not only complicates decision-making, but also leads to significant resource waste when only a small number of top-K solutions are ultimately required. To mitigate this issue, we adopt a concatenated vector to encode the size-K solution set and introduce a new problem formulation that explicitly incorporates the top-K requirement in CSMOO. To efficiently solve the newly formulated problem, we develop a top-K-aware bi-level search (TopK-BLS) algorithm. It leverages similarities among the top-K-aware lower-level MOPs to solve multiple lower-level tasks in a cooperative manner, thereby improving computational efficiency. The effectiveness of our proposed TopK-BLS is demonstrated through experiments on benchmark and real-world problems. Experimental results indicate that the proposed method can not only generate superior top-K solution sets but also significantly reduce the number of function evaluations compared to other state-of-the-art methods.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Spatio-Temporal Fusion Model Accounting for Strong Phenological Changes
authors: Aijing Li, Qunming Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Spatio-temporal fusion enables the generation of time-series data with both fine spatial and temporal resolutions, which are essential for timely, precise monitoring of land surface processes. However, existing spatio-temporal fusion methods face significant challenges in handling large phenological changes, especially when there is a large spatial resolution difference between fine and coarse spatial resolution images, leading to noticeable spectral distortion and spatial blurring during in the fusion predictions. To address this issue, we proposed a deep learning-based spatio-temporal fusion model designed for scenarios with strong phenological changes, namely, PC-STF. The proposed network adopts a dual-stream architecture, where one branch captures spectral variations across different times, while the other compensates for sensor differences and extracts fine spatial details. The features from both branches were then fused by a U-shaped architecture with ConvNeXt V2 blocks as the backbone for multi-scale feature extraction. Moreover, we employed a pyramid pooling module (PPM) to capture global contextual information and a local consistency block to preserve spatial coherence. Additionally, a phenological-aware loss function was applied during training to maintain fine visual details across phenological changes. In the experiments on fusion of 10 m Sentinel-2 MSI and 300 m Sentinel-3 OLCI data, PC-STF was compared with seven benchmark methods, including three traditional and four deep learning-based methods. It is found that PC-STF can consistently produce more accurate fusion results, and it is more robust under different phenological changes.

title: Design and Implementation of an Autonomous Neural Network Subsystem for Monitoring Computer Systems Digital Components
authors: Alexander K. Krutikov, Dmitry A. Strabykin, Vasily Yu. Meltsov, Aleksei A. Golovin
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: The presented study examines the most common techniques used to automate monitoring and diagnostics of the functioning of computing equipment. The novelty of the approach proposed by the authors lies in the segmentation of the initial diagnostic information collected in real time and the formation of the structure of the neural network subsystem, taking into account the interdependencies of digital components. The sequential connection of neural modules into tiers makes it possible to identify not only potential failures and failures of system elements, but also to predict cascading shutdowns of blocks, nodes, and entire subsystems. The paper describes in detail the key features of the architecture of an intelligent diagnostic and monitoring module operating in automatic mode.

title: MHCFNN: Multihead Attenuated Neuro-Fuzzy based Deep Learning Model for Cybersecurity in Internet of Things
authors: Anand Prakash Dwivedi, Omkar Agrahari
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Cybersecurity is crucial for securing the information or data generated in the Internet of Things paradigm, as it experiences certain challenges, including the problem of data scarcity, data breaches, and increased network traffic. Multiple machine learning and deep learning strategies were designed to overcome such downsides, but their inability to mitigate the computational complexity, overfitting, and poor integrity issues affected their utilization in the attack determination process in the IoT networks. Therefore, in order to address such drawbacks, an effective Multihead attention-enabled Neuro Fuzzy Convolutional Neural Network (MHCFNN) model is proposed in this research. The utilization of the preprocessed and log-transformed data increased the stability and the attack determination accuracy of MHCFNN. Further, the multi-head attention incorporated aided in the accurate determination of the cyber threats by concentrating only on the relevant attack features. Also, the Adam Optimizer included in MHCFNN fine-tuned the model parameters and stipulates the proposed model to attain optimal convergence. The efficacy of the proposed MHCFNN is evaluated using numerous performance metrics using the BoT-IoT dataset, and the proposed research is found to obtain superior performance by attaining 97.09% accuracy, 97.66% sensitivity, and 95.73% specificity for a training percentage of 80.

title: Smart AR-Based Sign Language Recognition System for Real-Time Communication and Translation
authors: Parminder Singh, Kawal Preet Kaur, Samriti
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Sign language users have not yet been properly connected to the rest of the world, through effective communication. In this paper, we present a state-of-the-art real-time sign language recognition and translation system that not only labels continuous hand gestures but also renders them into AR via dynamic speech bubbles. The recognized gestures are mapped into coherent English sentences which are translated into multiple languages (Hindi, Arabic, etc.) and vocalized via TTS synthesis. Our main framework incorporates hand tracking with MediaPipe, a sequential modelling of LSTM-based classification, and Google Translate API for multilingual translation. User interaction is controlled by the Tkinter toolkit within a graphical user interface (GUI). One important feature added to the system is the use of augmented reality (AR). In this setup, AR is used to show the translated or recognized sentence in a small floating bubble next to the user's hand. This appears in real time and makes it easier for users to see and understand the translation. The system uses buffering methods to avoid recognizing gestures too early, which helps the translated sentences flow more smoothly. Tests show that the system recognizes gestures accurately, responds quickly, and works well even with different languages. User feedback also shows that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A R$</tex> makes the experience more responsive and satisfying. Overall, this mix of technologies creates a helpful and easy-to-use tool for communication across different languages, with AR making it more engaging and practical.

title: Threshold Anonymous Credential Scheme Based on Attribute-Based Encryption
authors: Zhengxi Zhong, Hongyang Yan, Weichu Deng, Gongming Zhang, Zhexuan Ni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Decentralized anonymous credentials (DACs) enable users to prove possession of specific identity attributes without disclosing additional information or relying on a centralized authority. However, existing DAC schemes commonly rely on complex zero-knowledge proofs, resulting in high computational overhead. They also lack sufficient flexibility and efficiency in handling multi-authority environments and supporting complex access policies, while facing limitations in trust assumptions and scalability. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel threshold anonymous credential scheme. Specifically, we introduce a ciphertext-policy attribute-based encryption (CP-ABE) scheme that supports threshold key distribution and aggregation, and leverage it to construct the threshold anonymous credential scheme. Experimental results and security analysis demonstrate that the proposed scheme exhibits high efficiency and flexibility in constructing authentication for complex access policies.

title: A Hybrid Attribute-Content-Based Access Control Model for Medical Information Systems
authors: Ke Ma, Xinyu Xu, Yizhen Yang, Pingping Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Access control is fundamental to facilitating secure data sharing in Medical Information Systems (MIS). While prevalent models like Role-Based Access Control (RBAC), Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC), and Mandatory Access Control (MAC) provide foundational security, their granularity and flexibility are often insufficient for dynamic and collaborative clinical scenarios, such as multi-disciplinary consultations. This limitation creates a critical challenge: balancing stringent security with operational practicality, as overly restrictive policies impede workflows while excessively lenient ones risk sensitive data leakage. To address this, we propose a novel hybrid access control model that enhances the ABAC framework by integrating data content and security level as core attributes for authorization decisions. This composite model enables context-aware and content-driven permissions, effectively supporting secure yet practical data sharing in healthcare environments.

title: Advanced Learning Based Quantum Key Distribution Framework for Practical Secure Communication Networks
authors: ShivaPrasanna. K, B.Rajesh Shyamala Devi, Ravichand S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The integration of Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) with Machine Learning (ML) has developed a high-end, secure model for accurate data transmission and network security. Many existing systems face several issues due to advances in quantum computing. Thus, integrating these models improved the efficiency, accuracy, and feasibility of QKD systems. In this paper, an Advanced Learning Approach (ALA) is combined with the Quantum Key Distribution Framework to refine key generation, error correction, and intrusion detection in quantum communication channels. The proposed ALA also used reinforcement learning algorithms to predict quantum channel fluctuations, dynamically adjust key exchange parameters, and strengthen authentication protocols. Simulation results demonstrate improved key rates, reduced Quantum Bit Error Rates (QBER), and enhanced resilience against eavesdropping attacks. The study contributes to the realization of practical, scalable, and intelligent quantum-secure communication systems—bridging the gap between theoretical quantum security and real-world network deployment.

title: A Study on Extract Bits of Pseudorandom Sequences Generated by Me Operation on the Elliptic Curve
authors: Renta Yamauchi, Natsuo Hayashi, Takeru Miyazaki, Shunsuke Araki, Satoshi Uehara
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: In this paper, we propose an extracting method of pseudorandom bits from sequences generated by Shirase's Me operation on elliptic curves over finite fields, whose security is based on the hardness of the Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithm Problem (ECDLP). In Shirase's original pseudorandom number generator, only the least significant bit (LSB) of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex> coordinate of each point generated by the Me operation was extracted. However, since this approach is time-consuming and impractical for real-world use, we conclude a new method that extends the number of extracted bits.
