title: Automatic Generation Method of Secondary Work Tickets for Substations Based on SDD Model and Self-learning Fuzzy Reasoning
authors: Zutao Shen, Dan Luo, Peng Xu, Zeyin Song, Yuteng Wu, Hongling Wu
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: A substation secondary work ticket automatic generation method based on SDD model and self-learning fuzzy reasoning is proposed to address the problems of low efficiency and possible missing items in manual work ticket preparation. Firstly, the overall architecture of the automatic generation system for secondary work tickets in substations is constructed, including the model management layer, operation support layer, and production management layer. Then, combining CAD mapping technology and substation secondary circuit information model, a secondary circuit digital description model of the entire station is constructed. Finally, a secondary operation library is generated using an expert system, and a fuzzy matrix is established between the work symptom vector and the work reason vector. The secondary equipment maintenance work ticket is automatically generated through fuzzy learning inference. Based on experimental verification of the proposed method at a certain 220 kV substation, the results show that the average accuracy of the secondary work ticket generation is about 98.5%, and the average reliability coefficient is 0.942, which is superior to other comparative methods.

title: Explainable AI Models for Predicting Major Complications in Thalassemia Patients
authors: Sithumi Bhagya, Thosini Kumarika, Shiromi Perera, Anuja Premawardhena
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Thalassemia is an inherited blood disorder in which hemoglobin is synthesized abnormally and requires lifelong blood transfusions and continuous clinical monitoring. Secondary complications such as diabetes and leg ulcers are common, significantly reducing quality of life and increasing healthcare costs. Early detection of these complications is crucial for early intervention. However, the black-box nature of most machine learning algorithms hinders their application in clinical practice. In this study, predictive models for diabetes and leg ulcer complications in thalassemia patients were developed using a clinical dataset with 357 real patient data including demographics, laboratory values, and treatment histories. The models tested were Random Forest, XGBoost, and a Neural Network implemented in PyTorch. The neural network performed best for both complications, with excellent F1-scores and AUC scores. To ensure interpretation, Explainable AI methods were used using SHAP values to identify important clinical risk factors, LIME to produce case-specific explanations, and adversarial reasoning to test operational “what-if” scenarios. The results identified hypogonadism, serum ferritin, hemoglobin, ALT and AST as the best predictors of diabetes and leg ulcers. Through the application of explainable high-performance neural networks, this study demonstrates the potential of Explainable AI systems in improving personalized risk stratification, risk identification, and clinical decision-making for thalassemia management.

title: Adversarial Robustness Analysis of Deep Learning-Based Automatic Modulation Classification in Wireless Communication
authors: Sunjun Hwang, Eunho Choi, Dohyun Hwang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Deep learning-based Automatic Modulation Classification (AMC) has emerged as a critical technology for spectrum sensing and cognitive radio applications. However, the vulnerability of these models to adversarial attacks poses significant security concerns in wireless communication systems. This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of adversarial robustness for a VTCNN2-based AMC model using the RadioML2016.10A dataset. We systematically analyze three representative adversarial attacks-FGSM, DeepFool, and C&W-and evaluate two defense mechanisms: adversarial training and Denoising Autoencoder (DAE). Our experimental results demonstrate that the baseline model is highly susceptible to adversarial perturbations, with accuracy dropping from 54.02% to as low as 10.80% under DeepFool attack. FGSM-based adversarial training improves robustness across multiple attacks, increasing accuracy under FGSM from 17.59% to 33.47% and improving robustness under DeepFool <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(10.80 \% \rightarrow 15.36 \%)$</tex> and C&W <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(29.41 \% \rightarrow 40.67 \%)$</tex>, with a modest clean-accuracy drop <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{5 4. 0 2} \boldsymbol{\rightarrow} \mathbf{5 1. 4 8} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex>). In contrast, DAE preprocessing preserves clean accuracy(53.94%) but provides negligible improvement under FGSM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(17.59 \rightarrow 17.48)$</tex> and only limited gains under iterative L2 attacks such as DeepFool <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(10.80 \% \rightarrow 21.16 \%)$</tex> and C&W <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(29.41 \% \rightarrow 32.81 \%)$</tex>. These findings highlight the urgent need for robust defense mechanisms in deep learning-based wireless communication systems.

title: Design of RSSI-Only UAV Path Planning for Search and Rescue: Greedy, Prudent, and DRQN-Based Algorithms in GPS-Denied Mountains
authors: Jiwoong Jeon, Hyerim Jeon, Jonghyeon Bae, Juyeol Park, Hoki Baek
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper proposes three algorithms for received signal strength indicator (RSSI)-only UAV path planning in SAR for GPS-denied mountainous environments. RSSI is attractive for early path planning due to low energy cost, implementation simplicity, and ubiquity, but severe shadowing causes large fluctuations and unstable localization. To cope with this, we design: (i) a Greedy method that chooses the initial heading via the strongest RSSI and proceeds while adjusting using prior branch comparisons; (ii) a Prudent method that performs an initial cross-shaped scan and fork-shaped local searches at each decision point for reliability under heavy shadowing; and (iii) a deep recurrent Q-network (DRQN)-based method that adaptively selects between Greedy and Prudent at each step according to changing shadowing. Simulations show the DRQN approach balances both strategies, matching Prudent's high success rate while maintaining decision-step counts close to Greedy, yielding strong adaptability and robustness in challenging shadowing conditions.

title: One-Shot Training for Reduction of Performance Variance in LLM Rotation Quantization
authors: Sangki Park, Chanhoon Kim, Ki-Seok Chung
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Rotation-based quantization techniques have emerged as effective methods for compressing Large Language Models by mitigating the impact of activation outliers. However, existing approaches face significant challenges: random rotation methods like QuaRot exhibit substantial performance variability across different random seeds, while learned rotation methods like SpinQuant require hundreds of calibration samples, increasing the training cost. To address these limitations, we propose OneRot, a novel one-shot rotation-based quantization technique that achieves stable performance with only a single calibration sample. OneRot introduces random perturbations to layer activations during training, which amplify gradients and enable effective rotation matrix learning in a single attempt while simulating diverse activation distributions. Experimental results on Llama-2 7B, Llama-3.2 3B, and Llama-3.2 1B models with 4-bit quantization demonstrate that OneRot reduces perplexity by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.25,2.88$</tex>, and 21.48, respectively, compared to QuaRot, and reduces standard deviation by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.01,0.24$</tex>, and 1.41, respectively, while achieving comparable performance to SpinQuant using 800 times fewer samples. OneRot provides a practical solution for efficient LLM deployment by combining the stability of learned rotations with the computational efficiency of random approaches.

title: Cyber-Resilient Load Frequency Control in PV-Thermal Hybrid System via 1+FD ADRC
authors: Raj Kumar Yadav, Ajit Kumar, Rajdeep Kumar, Chetna Sagar
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: The dynamic performance of power supplies for pulsed power loads necessitates rapid and precise output voltage and frequency adjustment. The model independence and parameter design of active disturbance rejection control (ADRC) with a linear extended state observer (LESO) make it special. Although the LESO's crossover frequency restricts its tracking speed, it effectively records total disturbances with minimal system information. The Quasi opposition whale optimiser (QOWOA) approach is used to attain the best tuning 1+FD ADRC settings. A comparison study of reported controllers in the recent literature, PIR and FOPIR, taking into account the impact of loading conditions, resonance cyber-attack (RsA), and system parameter uncertainty, validates the effectiveness of the created <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1} \boldsymbol{+} \mathbf{F D}$</tex> ADRC. When compared to alternative approaches, such as the PIR and FOPIR controller, the performance test results show that the 1+FD ADRC demonstrates improved dynamic response and resilience under investigated circumstances.

title: Semi-Supervised Learning and Blockchain Integration for Transparent Smart Contract Security
authors: Muhammad Sannan Khaliq, Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: With the rapid proliferation of blockchain technology and smart contracts in consumer IoT systems, ensuring digital trust and security remains a persistent challenge due to vulnerability diversity, data scarcity, and limited on-chain auditability. To address these issues, this paper presents PureChain, a scalable trust framework that integrates semi-supervised deep learning for smart contract vulnerability detection with blockchain-based audit logging for transparent security assurance. The framework employs a lightweight Conv1D neural architecture trained via iterative pseudo-labeling and a Mean Teacher strategy, effectively leveraging tens of thousands of unlabeled contracts to minimize annotation requirements. Experimental evaluation shows that PureChain achieves macro F1-scores exceeding 99% and significant improvements in recall for rare vulnerabilities, outperforming both supervised and state-of-the-art baselines. The integration of blockchain ensures all detection events are immutably recorded, enabling accountable and verifiable device operation. These results demonstrate that adaptive semi-supervised learning, combined with on-chain transparency, provides a robust and efficient foundation for secure smart contract monitoring across IoT and edge environments, with future work targeting more adaptive pseudo-labeling, semi supervised learning (SSL) techniques and cross-chain generalization.

title: Fuzzy-DRL: Cost and Energy Efficient Task Scheduling in Cloud-Fog Computing Environment
authors: G Sreenivasan, Pasupuleti Prathima, Johnson Kolluri, Kanneboina Ashok, Raveendrababu Vempati, Santhosh Kumar Medishetti
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Task Scheduling (TS) is a key feature of Cloud-Fog Computing (CFC) that ensures the efficient execution of latency-restrictive and computation-intensive applications and ensures the efficient utilization of the system and its sustainability. In this paper, a hybrid Fuzzy Logic and Deep Reinforcement Learning (FL-DRL)-based task scheduling model that co-optimizes makespan, energy cost, and execution cost will be proposed to operate in heterogeneous cloud-fog systems. The utilization of fuzzy logic is used to deal with uncertainty and dynamically prioritize tasks depending on the nature of workload and resource conditions and a deep reinforcement learning agent is used to learn the best policies of task-to-resource allocation through ongoing interaction with the environment. The suggested framework is tested on Alibaba GPUs workload traces and analysed in the SimPy simulation environment and evaluated in terms of its performance in comparison with ACO, pure DRL, and EEOA algorithms. Experimental outcomes show that FL-DRL can achieve as much as 60 % of makespan reduction, 57 % energy savings, and 45 % baseline method execution costs reduction with different task loads. These significant improvements confirm that integrating uncertainty-aware fuzzy reasoning with learning-driven decision-making enables robust and scalable multi-objective scheduling. The suggested FL-DRL model is an efficient framework that can be applied to energy-efficient, low-cost, and high-performance tasks scheduling in the next-generation cloud-fog computing systems.

title: Optimizing Quantum Decision Trees (QDT) Circuits Using Hybrid Golden Eagle Optimization with Adaptive Gradient Descent
authors: L. Maria Michael Visuwasam, Sudha B, G. Kalpana, Viswanathan Ramasamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: This research centers on enhancing quantum circuits for Quantum Decision Trees (QDT) a highly promising algorithm in the realm of quantum machine learning aimed at classification and pattern recognition-via a hybrid metaheuristic optimization framework that integrates Golden Eagle Optimization (GEO) with Adaptive Gradient Descent (AGD). Although QDTs provide interpretability and quantum-enhanced classification capabilities for high-dimensional data sets, their effectiveness is frequently limited by subpar circuit configurations, excessive gate depth, and susceptibility to quantum noise. The suggested hybrid approach utilizes the global search capability of GEO and the local convergence accuracy of AGD to optimize QDT parameters such as entanglement structure, placement of quantum gates, and decision thresholds. Experimental findings indicate that the proposed technique achieves quicker convergence (38 iterations), superior classification accuracy (97.8 %), decreased quantum circuit complexity (39 quantum gates), and enhanced noise resilience (fidelity: 0.986), surpassing traditional methods including Genetic Algorithms (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Simulated Annealing (SA). These results affirm the promise of hybrid metaheuristic optimizers in improving quantum circuit efficiency for real-world quantum machine learning applications.

title: Design and Implementation of a Deep Learning Embedded Cryptosystem for IoT Applications
authors: Muhammad Ismail, Daniel Tadros, Mahdi Madani, El-Bay Bourennane
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)
abstract: Securing communications in the Internet of Things (IoT) is a critical challenge, particularly for resource-constrained devices operating on local networks. This paper presents a comprehensive cryptosystem for text-based message transmission between an IoT device and a connected server. We propose a deep learning embedded cryptosystem that integrates several advanced security techniques. Our system leverages the DiffieHellman (DH) key exchange to securely establish a shared secret between the server and devices, which is then used with Advanced Encryption Standard in Galois/Counter Mode (AESGCM) encryption scheme to safeguard messages. JSON Web Token (JWT)-based authentication ensures that only devices with valid credentials can access the system. At the same time, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based machine learning model further analyzes device-specific logs to classify devices as trusted or malicious. Qt-based applications on both the IoT device and the server facilitate real-time interaction, monitoring, and control. Experimental results demonstrate key exchange times of 150 to 200 ms, encryption/decryption latencies of approximately 50 ms per message, and JWT verification within 20 ms, while the CNN model achieves over 90 % classification accuracy, with precision, recall, and F 1 -scores of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$95.2 \%, 94.7 \%$</tex>, and 94.9 %, respectively. This integrated solution not only provides robust security but also lays the groundwork for future real-time anomaly detection and scalable IoT deployments.

title: A Conditional Variational Autoencoder-Enhanced Virtual IMU Contrastive Learning for Wearable Human Activity Recognition
authors: Qingrui Wang, Chengshuo Xia
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) with Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs) sensor is often constrained by the high cost of labeled data and the domain gap between virtual and real signals. To address this, we propose a virtual-to-real domain adaptation framework that integrates a Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE) with contrastive learning. The CVAE generates label-conditioned virtual IMU samples, while contrastive pretraining learns transferable representations, compatible with various self-supervised frameworks. We evaluate on a custom anaerobic exercise dataset with six activities, using three-axis accelerometer signals. Results show that our method improves accuracy from 56.8% to 81.7% with only 40 seconds of labeled data per class, demonstrating its effectiveness in few-shot cross-domain HAR.

title: Static Code Vulnerability Detection Framework for Secure Compiler Assisted Software Development Pipelines
authors: Arivazhagan P, Sunitha. P, M. Sakthivanitha, M. Vijaya Maheswari, S.K. Ramani, T. Thirumalaikumari
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: With the growing use of software systems in critical areas, securing the code while it is being developed has become a top priority. Traditional manual code reviews and testing can't often find subtler vulnerabilities for a significant security potential risk. This study fills the gaps of automated and accurate detection of static code vulnerabilities in secure compiler-aided software development pipelines. The goal is to have a strong structure that combines the static code analysis, the anomaly detection using machine learning, and the compiler-based instrumentation in order to detect potential vulnerabilities early in the development. The proposed framework uses the combinations of hybrid techniques of pattern-based vulnerability scanning, control-flow and data-flow analysis, and reinforcement learning models for prioritizing critical issues. Experiments were performed on commonly used benchmark datasets, such as the Juliet Test Suite, among several programming languages, and the detection accuracy of 96.3%, precision of 94.8%, recall of 95.1%, and F1-score of 94.9% were obtained, which were <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 - 12 \%$</tex> better than existing static analysis tools on all metrics. The results show the effectiveness of making compiler-level insights work along with machine learning models in proactive vulnerability detection. Conclusively it does not only improve the security posture of software development pipeline but also lowers the overhead of remediation of vulnerability post deployment which is a practical solution for secure software engineering.

title: Dual-Horizon Bandit Learning for UAV Path Planning with Trajectory Prediction
authors: Juner Qiu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: Finding routes for drones in complex, dynamic environments is the main problem for autonomous navigation systems. Traditional algorithms like A* and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) have some limitations. These limitations show in real-time response speed, ability to adapt to new environments, and computational speed. This article proposes a path planning framework. It is based on Dual Horizon Multi-Armed Bandit (DH-MAB) technology. This framework also includes trajectory prediction technology. It is designed to deal with dynamic obstacles and uncertain environments. This framework has two decision-making perspectives. One is short-term returns, and the other is long-term value estimation. With these two perspectives, the framework can balance decision-making. It also combines a trajectory prediction module with an encoder-decoder structure. This can help avoid obstacles and enable online learning in advance. Researchers did experiments. They built a dynamic simulation environment using real meteorological data. The experimental results show that the DH-MAB algorithm works better. In areas like success rate (95.2%), path cost (98.3%), and computational speed, it does better than traditional static algorithms and standard Multi-Armed Bandit (MAB) methods. This shows it is really useful and practical in dynamic path planning.

title: Cross-Modal Consistent Augmentation Bridging 2D–3D Transformations
authors: Donguk Kim, Soonmin Hwang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: LiDAR-camera fusion models are essential for robust 3D scene understanding in autonomous driving. However, existing multi-modal augmentation methods are typically developed independently for the LiDAR and image domains, which often leads to misalignment between 3D geometric transformations and corresponding 2D visual information. In this paper, we present a geometry-aware framework bridging 2D-3D transformations. With a diffusion inpainting model conditioned on transformed 3D bounding boxes and object appearance, augmented images are generated to preserve LiDAR object geometry and align with the surrounding scene context. Experiments on the nuScenes dataset with BEVFusion demonstrate that our augmentation improves geometric alignment and yields consistent gains in 3D detection performance over both object sampling and object paste baselines. Our findings highlight the importance of geometry-aware, multi-modal augmentation for advancing LiDAR-Camera fusion models.

title: Benchmarking the Symmetry of MOSFET Compact Models with Emphasis on ACM2
authors: Deni Germano Alves Neto, Márcio Cherem Schneider, Manuel J. Barragan, Sylvain Bourdel, Carlos Galup-Montoro
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: The symmetrically built MOS transistors of integrated circuits exhibit symmetric electrical behavior if the source and drain terminals are interchanged. Additionally, a series association of transistors is electrically “equivalent” to a single transistor. However, some of the compact MOSFET models do not comply with the requirements of symmetry and transistor equivalence. This paper reports tests of symmetry and of series association of transistors of some compact models available in circuit simulators. We show that the ACM2, a chargebased model in which the terminal voltages are referred to the substrate, is fully compliant with the transistor symmetry, but that some popular models are not. To test the symmetry property, we show examples of transistor current-voltage characteristics and derivatives up to the fifth order, and capacitance-voltage characteristics, all tests around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{D S}=0$</tex>. A MOSFET binary current divider is employed to test the consistency of the model applied to a series association of transistors.

title: AI-Enhanced Breast Cancer Screening with Deep Transfer Learning
authors: Mohini Kumbhar, Mayuresh Gulame, Renuka Wakhare, Punam Sawale, Priya Khune, Trupti Sonkusare
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Malignancy is a deadly ailment that distresses many advanced and rising nations globally. Explicitly, the commonness of breast melanoma in womenfolk is intensifying daily, in part because of a shortage of attentiveness and early discovery. Only by precisely pinpointing and categorizing breast cancer in its very primary stages can a suitable initial treatment be administered. By cultivating and supporting less skilled medical specialists, the application of computer-assisted diagnosis (CAD) and medical image exploration practices may add to the automation and acceleration of cancer recognition and categorization. Convolutional neural networks are crucial for accurately identifying and categorizing cancer in big databases of medical images. Using the publicly accessible BreakHis dataset and a variety of deep neural networks (DNN) (ResNet 18, Shuffle-Net, and Inception-V3Net), this research offerings a distinctive computer-aided opinion approach for breast malignancy categorization. Proposed method proved to be the utmost perfect and proficient classifier, with the highest average accuracy, sensitivity and specificity are 99.64%, 98.80%, 97.12 individually.

title: Design and Analysis of Natural Gas Pressure Energy Recovery System of Underground Gas Storage Coupled with Organic Rankine Cycle and Proton Exchange Membrane Electrolyzer
authors: Qianshan Jiang, Wei Yang, Kang’Kang Wang, An’An Zhang, Wei Wang, Peiyu Yin
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: To address the challenges of utilizing natural gas pressure energy in underground gas storage and insufficient utilization of cold energy after natural gas (NG) expansion power generation, an organic rankine cycle-proton exchange membrane electrolyzer-natural gas pressure recovery (ORC-PEME-NGPR) hybrid system was designed for underground gas storage scenarios under the ‘dual-carbon’ context. First, based on the principle of energy cascade utilization and considering the production characteristics of the underground gas storage, an ORC-PEME-NGPR integrated system structure was designed. Subsequently, guided by engineering thermodynamics theory, a coupled simulation model of the integrated system was constructed, and key indicators for, thermal analysis, economic analysis, and environmental analysis were established. Finally, a case study was conducted on an underground gas storage in south-west China to validate the feasibility and economic viability of the system. The influence of NG flow rate and pressure on system efficiency and economic performance was analyzed. The study demonstrated that the integrated system can effectively recover NG cold energy while utilizing its pressure energy, achieving energy efficiency and exergy efficiency of $82.53 \%$ and $55.30 \%$, respectively.

title: Optimizing Real Estate Investment Allocation in the UAE Market: A Spatial Machine Learning Framework
authors: Zahra Radhi A. Salman
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: The real estate market in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) presents significant complexity due to rapid expansion, speculative influences, and geographic dependencies. Traditional investment methodologies often rely on basic heuristics that fail to capture spatial relationships and market dynamics. This study presents a spatial machine learning framework for real estate investment allocation, analyzing 22,676 property listings across UAE emirates. Spatial autocorrelation analysis was conducted using Moran's I statistic, confirming significant geographic dependencies (Moran's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{I}=\mathbf{0. 1 7 8}, p<0.001$</tex>). Ensemble methods including Random Forest (RF) and eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) were assessed through 5-fold cross-validation. The Random Forest model achieved coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex>) of 0.930 with Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 183,744 AED, representing 882.9% improvement over area median baseline (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2} =0.095$</tex>, MAE =2,949,763 AED). Feature importance analysis using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) revealed location encoding (35.0%) and price normalization (35.0%) as dominant predictive factors. An investment scoring framework integrating rental yield, capital appreciation, property quality, and location attributes was developed and applied to identify 4,276 highpotential investment opportunities with projected 66.5% fiveyear Return on Investment (ROI). Geographic allocation analysis identified Jumeirah Village Circle as the optimal investment location, offering 9.79% rental yield, 76.58% five-year ROI, and 603 opportunities at median price of 910,000 AED. ROI decomposition analysis revealed rental income comprises 54.2% of total returns, challenging speculation-focused investment narratives in emerging markets.

title: Improving the Classification Accuracy of Breast Cancer Through Ensemble Learning Methods
authors: Saba Tahseen, Apurva Chaudhari, Nidhi Joshi Parsai, Anaswara Venunadh
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Strong diagnostic methods must be developed because breast cancer is still a main health concern. The effectiveness of ensemble learning techniques in categorizing breast cancer according to unlike clinical characteristics is examined in this work. With an emphasis on characteristics like average appearance, average diameter, and average perimeter, mean area, and mean smoothness, Breast Cancer dataset is used. This data set was separated between training and evaluation categories sets to guarantee a comprehensive evaluation process. To develop a performance baseline, two separate classifiers-Random Forest and LightGBM were first used. The Voting Classifier approach with soft voting was then used to create an ensemble model integrating these classifiers. For checking each model's performance, Accuracy, classification reports, and matrix of confusion are used. The results obtained revealed that the ensemble model outperformed the individual classifiers, achieving higher accuracy and more balanced classification metrics. The work emphasizes how ensemble approaches may improve the diagnostic precision of breast cancer classification, which may lead to better patient care and therapeutic approaches.

title: Development of CAM-System for Laser Equipment
authors: Andrew Senkevich, Alexander Korolev
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: In this article described process of developing CAM-system for high accurate laser machines. These systems provides ability of creating receipts for automatic MCU-based systems and in some cases provides operator control process execution. This software is necessarily for high accurate systems, especially in laser industry. This software development includes the variety of mathematical methods like optimization algorithms and high level mathematical models. All this methods should help to fill the need in this kind of software for high accurate laser machines and automation systems that includes these machines. In result were developed high level program for simulating process execution and tracking machine state in real time.

title: Dynamic Response Analysis Based on Neural Network and Genetic Algorithm
authors: Tingkai Wang, Dongxuan Han
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In this paper, a framework of algorithms for response modeling and structure optimization of multivariate systems is proposed, focusing on state prediction under highdimensional input conditions, modeling of time-series evolutionary processes, and mapping relationships between parameter structures and system performances. First, a neural network model is constructed to jointly predict multiple continuous response variables, which improves the accuracy and stability of high-dimensional output-consistent modeling. Second, a discrete-time evolution model is introduced to simulate the dynamic changes of the system state under external intervention, spontaneous adjustment and neighborhood propagation mechanisms, and the regulation parameters are optimized by genetic algorithms to maximize the response effect under cost constraints. Finally, combining weighted correlation analysis and hierarchical aggregation methods, the multiindicator structural feature vector is constructed, which is used to portray the coupling relationship between input factors and system performance. The model integrates deep learning, propagation modeling and intelligent optimization methods with high adaptability, strong scalability and good interpretability, and is suitable for response prediction and strategy regulation scenarios of multi-stage and multi-variable systems.

title: Capacity Analysis of Frequency Reuse Schemes for Steerable Beam in LEO Satellite Systems
authors: Weilong Ren
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: The construction of low earth orbit (LEO) satellite system is in great demand in the world today, where the interference due to the limited bandwidth is one of the difficulties. In the paper, we analyze the steerable beam capacity of different frequency reuse schemes in LEO satellite systems, including two traditional frequency reuse schemes (reuse factor =1,3) and soft frequency reuse (SFR). Besides, the influence of static inter-cell interference coordination (ICIC) technology on capacity is simulated at the same time, where we set up two scenarios: one LEO satellite forming one beam, and one LEO satellite forming three beams. Through the simulation and analysis, we get visual results and make a suggestion on the application of frequency reuse and ICIC in LEO satellite systems with steerable beam.

title: Digital Beamforming Method Based on Space-based Distributed Array
authors: Tianlin Xu, Wupeng Xie, Ruilin Pei, Zhuang Li, Zhijie Qu, Xinzhu Liu, Yanan Guan, Yunfei Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Beamforming technology has been widely applied in radar systems and related fields. To overcome the limitations of traditional fixed arrays, such as restricted electrical aperture and high vulnerability to enemy detection, a digital beamforming method based on a space-based distributed array is proposed. A joint optimization cost function is established for the array element positions and the antenna array weight vectors, aiming to achieve a unit response in the target direction and null responses in the jamming directions while simultaneously minimizing the sidelobe level. The Lawson criterion is employed to transform the original minimax problem into a tractable weighted optimization form. Based on this formulation, an alternating iterative optimization strategy is adopted, in which the antenna array weight vector and the array element positions are updated sequentially and repeatedly until convergence. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reduces the peak sidelobe level (PSLL), suppresses strong jamming, and forms a high-performance beam pattern, indicating that the detection performance of space-based distributed arrays can be significantly enhanced.

title: Design, Control, and Experimental Validation of a Regulated Flyback Converter for Automotive Auxiliary Applications
authors: Om Prakash Bairwa, Naveen Yalla, Narendrababu A
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Flyback converters are widely used in automotive systems to provide isolated, regulated 12 V power to auxiliary loads such as infotainment, sensors, and dashboard electronics. These applications demand reliable operation across a wide input voltage range. This paper presents the design and closed-loop control of a 100 W flyback converter delivering 12 V output from a variable DC input. The topology offers simplicity, galvanic isolation, and cost-effectiveness—well-suited for automotive needs. A voltagemode PI controller ensures tight regulation under dynamic input and load conditions. Simulation and experimental results validate the converter's performance, demonstrating effective voltage regulation, transient response, and efficiency for automotive auxiliary applications.

title: Evaluating GRAD-CAM Explainability Across Deep Learning Models
authors: Akhil Shaju, Akshay A C, Madhav Murali V, Nandan Krishna, Manoj V Thomas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence(AI) and Machine Learning(ML) have modified every field of modern life. However, due to the black-box nature, the lack of interpretability in deep learning models, particularly in Convolutional Neural Networks, limits real-world adoption. Explainable AI(XAI), on the other hand, improves the black-box nature of deep learning models by enhancing the transparency, making AI-driven predictions more reliable, especially in the fields of healthcare, education, etc. This research project integrates Grad-CAM(Gradient Weighted Class Activation Mapping) Lightweight CNNs and their architectures will enhance interpretability in classifications, generating visual explanations, aiding professionals in understanding AI predictions. The study evaluates various real-world datasets that leverage Grad-CAM for better visualizations. While applied in the efficient CNN the Grad- CAM can improve the model by increasing its interpretation. Experimental results demonstrate a high accuracy of $95 \%$ and above while ensuring efficient computation and transparent decision making. This work systematically evaluates Grad-CAM’s applicability in AI-driven fields.

title: Al-Driven Leaf Health Assessment, Damage Quantification, and Leaf Area Index Calculation
authors: Parth Shinde, Prathamesh Jagadale, Harshita Chandekar, Mohammad Khatib Shaikh, Priyanka More
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The given paper suggests a new method of plant leaf analysis that will combine disease detection and sophisticated biometric measurements. The proposed solution is a multidimensional pipeline with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to classify diseases, U-Net to implement semantic segmentation, and Generative Adversarial Networking (GAN) which are not very heavy. We do not follow the traditional method, which considers the disease identification in the process of evaluating the damage only, but we also employ the innovative and intact leaf reconstruction to determine the LAI of diseased leaves. The methodology can assist in practical applications in agriculture since it can offer a full-scale diagnostic solution that is compatible with edge devices.

title: Axial-iFormer: Robust and Efficient Wafer Map Defect Classification via Modulated Axial Attention
authors: Jaeho Song, Juhyeon Noh, Jihoon Lee, Yonggwan Won, Jaehyung Park, Jinsul Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Reliable classification of wafer map defect patterns helps process engineers identify yield-limiting issues in semiconductor fabrication plants. Hybrid CNN-Transformer models such as iFormer, which combine local convolutions with global self-attention, have recently achieved strong results on this task. However, iFormer's Single-Head Modulation Attention (SHMA) still performs expensive global attention over all spatial tokens and provides little inductive bias for thin, line-shaped defects such as scratches. We introduce Axial-iFormer and a lightweight companion model that replace SHMA with a Modulated Axial Attention block. The new block factors 2D attention into row-wise and column-wise operations and modulates the resulting context through a gating branch, preserving long-range dependencies while lowering computational cost. On the WM-811K wafer map benchmark, the performance-oriented Axial-iFormer-S increases Scratch Recall to 82.85%, a gain of 3.35 percentage points over the iFormer-S baseline. The efficiency-oriented Axial-iFormer-S-Lite reduces the parameter count by 29% yet still surpasses the baseline on overall defect detection, indicating that Axial-iFormer is well suited to yield monitoring scenarios with tight computational budgets.

title: Research on Numerical Recognition of Mine Gas Sensors Based on PP-OCRv5
authors: Chuan Luo, Lei You, Yanchi Liu, Xiaoyu Yang, Tao Li, Xinglin Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Gas inspection in mines is a critical task for ensuring safe coal mine production. Manual meter reading by gas inspectors may involve negligence or concealment, leading to inconsistencies between recorded sensor readings and actual values, which seriously affects safety supervision. To address this problem, this paper proposes a numerical recognition method for mine gas sensors based on PP-OCRv5. The core algorithm adopts PP-OCRv5, and the model is fine-tuned using a dataset of gas sensor images collected from real mine environments. The trained model is then converted using the TPU-MLIR tool to generate a format suitable for deployment on BM1684X edge computing devices. The system is validated on a test dataset. Experimental results show that when deployed on a single BM1684X device, the numerical recognition algorithm achieves a maximum average processing frame rate of 12.6 frames per second with a recognition accuracy of up to 97.5%. When multiple algorithm instances are deployed simultaneously, the average frame rate remains at 1.8 frames per second or higher. Overall, the proposed method meets the requirements of low power consumption, high speed, and high accuracy for real-time monitoring in underground mine environments, and provides practical evidence for the application of character recognition systems in edge computing scenarios.

title: Intelligent Educational Robots Aided Integration of Design Fundamentals and Specialized Courses Path Optimization and Software Implementation
authors: Zhi Fu, Tiancheng Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: With the booming of intelligent educational technology, educational robots have become key tools for curriculum reform. Design science, an interdisciplinary field blending art and engineering, faces a common issue in its basic courses: disjointed general knowledge and professional skills, failing to meet compound talent cultivation needs in the new era. This study uses intelligent educational robots as a carrier to explore optimizing the integration of general and professional content in design basic courses and proposes a software implementation scheme. It first analyzes the course's knowledge system and teaching features to identify integration pain points, then constructs an optimization model with strategies for course content reconstruction, modular design, and evaluation mechanisms. Finally, it develops educational robot teaching software with intelligent interaction, personalized recommendation, and real-time feedback, verifying its feasibility via teaching cases. Results show the integration path and software enhance teaching efficiency, boost interdisciplinary collaboration, and stimulate creativity, offering new ideas and technical support for intelligent reform of design courses.

title: A Novel Data-Driven State of Charge Estimation for Li-Ion Batteries
authors: Basant Kumar Sethi, Nivedika Kher, Debottam Mukherjee
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: An efficient state of charge (SoC) estimation is a crucial prerequisite for a reliable operation of the battery management systems (BMS). This study proposes a data-driven SoC estimation technique based on the 2-RC equivalent circuit model (ECM) utilising gradient descent-based optimisers. A detailed comparative analysis is performed between data-driven optimisers like the least squares (LS), conventional gradient descent (GD), and the gradient descent with momentum (GDM) to assess the convergence characteristics, accuracy, and robustness of the proposed SoC estimator across various learning rates. The results demonstrate that GDM not only achieves a superior estimation accuracy over LS and GD but also converges significantly faster, thus showing a lower computational burden. The proposed estimator demonstrates a robust performance under the hybrid pulse power characterisation (HPPC) tests and is capable of handling small-scale noises. Simulation results demonstrate that momentum-based SoC estimators can be implemented in real time and may lead to the development of advanced BMS for electric vehicles.

title: BanINT: A Bengali Dataset for Multiclass Intent Classification with BanglaSeqNet
authors: Md. Tanvir Ahammed Shawon, Fariha Haq, Md. Ayon Mia, Muhammad Ibrahim Khan, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin, M. M. Musharaf Hussain
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Detecting user intent in Bengali social media text is a critical challenge, particularly given the linguistic ambiguity, semantic overlap, and low-resource nature of Bengali NLP. To address this, we introduce BanINT, an innovative dataset comprising 4,817 manually annotated comments and descriptions collected from Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube. The dataset spans six intent categories: Emotion, Query, Request, Complaint, Statement, and Suggestion, reflecting a broad spectrum of real-world communication. We perform a comprehensive evaluation of transformer-based models, including BanglaBERT, mBERT, and XLMRoBERTa, under standard fine-tuning protocols. Furthermore, we propose BanglaSeqNet, a transformerbased architecture that integrates multi-head attention and multi pooling strategies to enhance representation learning. BanglaSeqNet, leveraging XLM-RoBERTa, achieves a promising 84.5 % accuracy with a macro F1-score of 0.844, demonstrating its effectiveness for multiclass intent detection. Our findings highlight key challenges in distinguishing semantically overlapping intent categories and underline the need for more context-aware modeling. This work establishes a strong foundation for advancing intent understanding in Bengali and other low-resource, linguistically complex languages.

title: Automatic Publication Summarization using a BERT-based Natural Language Processing Model
authors: H K Susheelamma, R Madhavi Salai, Utkarsh Singh, Udaybhaskar Konagalla, H T Shabareesh
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Faculty members in academic institutions invest significant time manually creating and updating research publication summaries for profile building, grant applications, and institutional reporting. This manual process is labor-intensive, inconsistent, and increasingly difficult as publication counts grow. This paper presents an automated Publication Summary Generator system that employs Natural Language Processing techniques to extract publication data from multiple sources and generate concise summaries for faculty profiles. The proposed system integrates with Google Scholar, institutional repositories, and PDF documents to automatically collect publication information. It implements text extraction, preprocessing, and summarization algorithms using BERT-based transformer models to create meaningful summaries. The system provides a web-based interface built with React and Flask where faculty members can upload publications, customize summary parameters, and export formatted summaries in PDF, Word, and HTML formats. Evaluation with faculty members across different departments demonstrates that the system reduces profile building time by seventy-eight percent while maintaining summary quality comparable to manually written abstracts. The system successfully generates publication summaries that are grammatically correct, factually accurate, and suitable for professional academic profiles. This work contributes a practical solution for academic profile management and demonstrates effective application of modern NLP techniques in higher education administration.

title: Fabrication of High-Resolution Digital Torque Wrenches With Quartz Crystal Force Sensors
authors: Cheng-Yi Wu, Qiushi Liu, Kun Li, Gangyin Ding, Jung-Chieh Chen, Jiahui Liang, Chin-Yin Chen, Chang-Chun Zhao, Min-Chiang Chao
abstract: A sensor head of quartz crystal resonant chips was developed as a force/torque sensor, incorporating parameters such as resolution, sensitivity, and response time. This sensor was applied to the torque detection technology of digital torque wrenches. The main body of the digital torque wrench was mechanically designed and simulated in advance to concentrate mechanical energy within the groove structure where the sensor head was installed. Compared with traditional strain gauge-based digital torque wrenches, which typically exhibit a measurement resolution of more than 1%-FS, the digital torque wrench utilising the quartz crystal force sensor head achieved a detection resolution of less than 0.2%-FS. Additionally, the reliability of the digital torque wrench was verified, and the results demonstrated its effectiveness.

title: Security-Trust-Determinism Co-Design Using Hybrid Intrusion Detection With Temporal Modeling for Real-Time Publish-Subscribe Middleware
authors: Ramakrishna Penaganti
abstract: This paper proposes a trust-aware hybrid intrusion detection (IDS) for real-time pub/sub middleware that jointly targets security effectiveness and determinism preservation. The proposed approach fuses a low-latency inline detector with a temporal convolutional network (TCN) through a calibrated score-level combination, and then aggregates the fused evidence using a lightweight Bayesian trust engine to maintain continuous trust scores for middleware entities. Trust-guided enforcement is constrained by deterministic quality of service (QoS) budgets to avoid degrading real-time guarantees. Experiments on the publicly available UNSW-NB15 benchmark demonstrate that the hybrid design improves separability and robustness to time-dependent attack behaviors while remaining compatible with real-time execution. The obtained results of the proposed work establish a practical security–trust–determinism trade-off for deploying hybrid machine learning-based IDS in real-time middleware environments.

title: Model Predictive Control of DAB Converters for RMS Inductor Current Minimization Under ZVS Constraints
authors: Nguyen Ngoc Nam, Young Il Lee
abstract: This paper introduces a model predictive control (MPC) method for dual active bridge (DAB) converters, which minimizes the root mean square (RMS) inductor current while ensuring zero-voltage switching (ZVS). First, the power losses that reduce DAB efficiency are analyzed. It is revealed that achieving optimal efficiency requires both minimizing the RMS inductor current and maintaining ZVS, thereby reducing switching, conduction, and magnetic component losses. Subsequently, an expression for the RMS inductor current is derived, which involves two decision variables: the inter-phase and outer-phase shifts. By formulating and solving an optimization problem that minimizes the RMS inductor current under ZVS constraint, the steady-state solutions for the inner and outer phase shifts are determined. To achieve fast-tracking performance and eliminate steady-state error, the MPC approach is designed based on the output filter. Additionally, Lyapunov stability theory is employed to guarantee system stability. The steady-state inner phase shift solution is used to satisfy the ZVS constraints, while the final outer-phase shift is determined based on the MPC approach and inter-phase steady-state solution, ensuring power transmission. To validate and highlight the effectiveness of the proposed method, extensive hardware experiments are conducted and compared with recent studies.

title: Connected Vehicular Network Ground-to-Air Target Tracking Based on Bearings Measurements: Pseudolinear Information-Guided Strategy
authors: Zhihao Xiao, Luyao Zhang, Ning Liu, Tong Guo, Luhan Jin, Yao Mao
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper investigates ground-to-air Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) tracking using bearing-only line-of-sight (LOS) measurements provided by electro-optical tracking systems (ETS) mounted on a connected vehicular network. The key challenges arise from asymmetric mobility constraints and limited observability in three-dimensional space. To address these issues, a closed-loop tracking framework is proposed that integrates state estimation with ground vehicle coordination. A distributed pseudolinear filter is developed to handle the nonlinear nature of bearing-only observations and improve estimation stability in a decentralized setting. On top of this, an information-theoretic guidance strategy is formulated by adapting the Fisher Information Matrix (FIM) to ground-to-air sensing geometry, enabling explicit quantification of the relationship between sensor positioning and estimation accuracy. The framework employs Voronoi-based spatial decomposition to achieve scalable coordination among multiple ground vehicles, balancing estimation precision with spatial coverage. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method enhances tracking accuracy and improves target observability, offering a practical solution for UAV tracking under realistic conditions.

title: Sparse SAR Separated Iterative Imaging for Stationary and Moving Targets
authors: Yufan Song, Fuxuan Cai, Cuijun Liu, Jingjing Zhang, Liping Wang, Wen Hong, Hui Bi
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imaging, moving targets often appear along with the stationary scene in the surveillance regions. The imaging results of moving targets are defocused by using traditional imaging methods since the velocities of them are unknown. Most ground moving target imaging (GMTIm) methods ignore the stationary targets when analyzing the signal of moving target. Therefore, effective imaging methods for both stationary and moving targets focusing in the same surveillance region are worth investigating. In this paper, the stationary and moving targets sparse SAR separated iterative imaging model and methods are proposed and used for the real data processing. Firstly, the two-dimensional (2-D) stationary and moving targets separated imaging model is constructed based on approximate observation. Then a novel sparse iterative reweighted algorithm (SIRA) is proposed to solve the constructed model and achieve the focusing of stationary and moving targets simultaneously. Furthermore, on the basis of SIRA, we improve the constraints on the stationary targets to enhance the recovery accuracy about them, and use alternating direction multiplier method (ADMM) to solve the novel optimization problem. Finally, the normalized relative speed estimation (NRSE)-based imaging method for moving target is introduced. Compared with traditional matched filtering (MF)-based and sparse-driven imaging methods, the proposed methods achieve separated reconstruction of moving and stationary targets, respectively while improving the imaging quality. Experiments based on simulated and real Fucheng-1 SAR dataset verify the proposed methods.

title: Load Disaggregation in Residential Appliances with Multiple States: Comparative Techniques
authors: Carlos A. Urzagasti, Isaac S. De Freitas, Oswaldo H. Ando, Euzeli C. Dos Santos, Fabiano Salvadori
abstract: This study presents a comparative analysis of load disaggregation techniques applied to residential appliances with multiple operational states, aiming to enhance Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring (NILM) capabilities. The research evaluates three appliances with multi-state consumption patterns (refrigerator, washing machine, air conditioner) using DATAPORT and UK-DALE datasets across four experimental scenarios: intra-household, inter-household, multi-household, and multi-dataset. The methodology employs the Torch-NILM framework to implement and compare five deep learning architectures: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Sequence-to-Point (Seq2Point), Denoising Autoencoder (DAE), and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU). Results reveal distinct technique-appliance specializations in the intra-household scenario: Seq2Point achieves optimal refrigerator disaggregation (F1-score: 0.92, MAE: 18.0W, RMSE: 32.0W), GRU excels for washing machines (F1-score: 0.76, MAE: 22.0W, RMSE: 90.0W), and CNN demonstrates superior air conditioner performance (F1-score: 0.85, MAE: 24.0W, RMSE: 80.0W). Cross-household transfer induces 15-20% performance degradation, with GRU exhibiting superior robustness (refrigerator: 0.78, washing machine: 0.68) compared to CNN and Seq2Point. Multi-household training recovers 8-10% of lost performance through implicit regularization from diverse usage patterns. multi-dataset evaluation (DATAPORT to UK-DALE) reveals catastrophic degradation (40-50% loss), with maximum F1-scores of 0.55 (GRU, refrigerator) attributed to sampling rate mismatch (1s versus 6s), regional electrical differences (120V versus 230V, 60Hz versus 50Hz), and cultural usage pattern divergence. These findings quantify fundamental limitations in current NILM architectures for cross-domain generalization and establish empirical benchmarks for technique selection based on appliance characteristics and deployment constraints.

title: FedAligns: A Federated Learning-Based IoT Intrusion Detection Method with Direction-Aligned Updates
authors: Keyuan Qiu, Zhejie Xu, Jianlin Lu, Xiaohui Xu, Shuangshi Zhao, Meifang Yan, Tao Luo, Dejie Yang, Zhigang Li
abstract: IoT intrusion detection faces critical challenges from device heterogeneity, non-iid data distributions, and malicious updates. To address these, we propose FedAligns, a robust and efficient federated learning intrusion detection framework. First, we design a Temporal Attention Autoencoder that integrates TCN, GRU, and self-attention to capture local patterns and global dependencies in complex IoT traffic, enhancing feature representation. Second, we introduce a dual-aggregation algorithm, AlignMSE, to tackle non-iid heterogeneity and malicious threats. Its first phase filters anomalous updates via temporal direction alignment and sign consistency to ensure robust aggregation. Its second phase dynamically allocates weights based on local Mean Squared Error, prioritizing high-performing clients to better adapt to non-iid data. Experiments on N-BaIoT and ToN IoT datasets show FedAligns significantly outperforms traditional methods in accuracy and robustness, demonstrating superior resilience against malicious updates in non-iid scenarios.

title: Distributed Generalized Distributionally Robust Equilibrium Seeking for Dynamical Games Under Unknown Time-Varying Interference
authors: Longcheng Liu, Shuai Liu, Yiguang Hong, Lihua Xie, Guangchen Wang
abstract: This paper investigates a distributed generalized Nash equilibrium-seeking problem in stochastic dynamical systems, focusing on two key challenges: 1) nonlinear coupled constraints and nonlinear dynamics, and 2) nonconvex objectives influenced by disturbances with unknown time-varying distributions. To address these challenges, a distributionally robust game framework with an exact penalty is proposed. We introduce a first-order equilibrium concept suitable for nonconvex-nons-mooth settings and ensure finite-sample guarantees. Furthermore, a distributed zeroth-order feedback algorithm is proposed to solve the problem. This algorithm utilizes gradient estimators for the objective functions and subgradient estimators for the exact penalty terms. We provide a detailed analysis of the relationship between communication errors and the dynamic energy of the system, along with an expected upper bound for the zeroth-order gradient estimation. Our findings indicate that the expectation of the time-accumulated regret grows at a sublinear rate. Furthermore, as the distribution stabilizes, we show that the empirical distribution converges with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O(1)$</tex> sampling complexity.

title: An EEG-Based Multi-Source Domain Knowledge Transfer Framework for Cross-Session and Cross-Subject Emotion Recognition
authors: Xiaoya Zhu, Haohao Zheng, Xiaolong Chen, Hong Zeng, Chenpu Li, Motonobu Hattori, Xiang Zhang
abstract: Multi-source domain adaptation (MSDA) aims to transfer knowledge from multi-source domains to an unlabeled target domain, making it highly suitable for cross-session and cross-subject emotion recognition based on electroencephalography (EEG). Nevertheless, current MSDA models primarily focus on the relationship between the global feature distribution of the source and the target domain while neglecting the relationship between local feature pairs, resulting in poor robustness. In this study, we propose an EEG-based Multi-Source Domain Knowledge Transfer Framework (MS-KTF) for cross-session and cross-subject emotion recognition. The MS-KTF uses a triplet loss combined with a maximum mean discrepancy (MMD) to achieve unbiased alignment at both the domain and data-pair levels of each pair data in the source and target domains. Moreover, the introduction of knowledge distillation enables MS-KTF to achieve better robustness without requiring additional training time. We evaluated the MS-KTF's performance on the DEAP, SEED and SEED-IV datasets for two-, three- and four-class emotion recognition, respectively. The experimental results demonstrated that MS-KTF achieves competitive performance in both cross-session and cross-subject scenarios.

title: Synchronization Control Based on Sequential Convergence
authors: Huan Li, Shuangsi Xue, Hui Cao, Dongyu Li
abstract: Dear Editor, This letter focuses on the synchronization of system state components under the constraint of convergence sequence. Firstly, we introduce the predefined-sequence-synchronized control problem, a unique synchronization challenge where all state components must be synchronized to the origin following a predefined convergence sequence. A controller is proposed to solve the predefined-sequence-synchronized control problem in first-order affine systems, and the proposed controller can regulate the convergence sequence of state components by adjusting their ratios. Finally, the effectiveness of the theoretical findings is demonstrated through a numerical examnle.

title: Swin-ResU-Net: Hybrid Transformer CNN for Spatial Segmentation of Satellite Imagery
authors: Tejas Chauhan, Rajakumar K, Charan Rajana, Kaviti Sravan, Sai Bhargav Kasetty
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The resolution of satellite imagery has significantly improved due to the unprecedented growth in Earth observation technologies, necessitating the use of suitable and efficient semantic image segmentation techniques to perform spatial analyses. Most traditional CNN-based segmentation models fail to model long-range spatial dependencies and multiscale contextual relationships, which are inherent in satellite imagery. In this paper, the authors present a hybrid deep learning framework that combines the Swin Tiny Transformer with a ResNet50 backbone within a UNet-style architecture. The suggested model maintains spatial localisation through encoder-decoder skip connections and models global dependencies by utilising Swin Transformer's hierarchical selfattention mechanism. With the ResNet50 backbone, this enhances feature reuse by enabling precise gradient flow. The OpenEarthMap dataset, which featured a varied collection of globally dispersed land-cover samples, was then used to train and validate the model. With a peak validation IoU of 0.54 and an accuracy of 0.67, the experimental results show a progressive improvement over epochs, outperforming a number of baseline architectures in terms of both boundary precision and spatial consistency. The Swin-ResU-Net framework that was suggested brings together hierarchical transformer representations and convolutional feature learning so that the satellite imagery can be segmented spatially with precision. The results of the experiment show that the proposed method is not only robust but also generalizable, as it has consistently improved its performance across different evaluation metrics.

title: Accuracy-Efficiency Trade-Off Analysis of Deep Learning Models for Breast Cancer Diagnosis Using Ultrasound Images
authors: Sunny Kumar, Abhijeet Dhanotiya, Jasmine Selvakumari Jeya I, Tushan Kumar Sinha, Anuj Gupta, Aldrin Joan Pandian W
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative analysis of four deep learning architectures on an ultrasound image dataset of 2104 training and 529 test images of human breast, differentiating between three classes (benign, malignant, or normal). These models are evaluated on multiple parameters -accuracy loss, Cohen's Kappa coefficient, Area Under Curve (AUC), and computational efficiency. The results show InceptionV3 achieves the highest overall accuracy of 94.14 % with a Cohen's Kappa score of 0.9121, followed by DenseNet121, which provided competitive performance with 93.01 % accuracy and the lowest test loss of 0.2024. VGG16 delivered an accuracy of 92.06 %, whereas MobileNetV2 showed a lower accuracy of 86.96 %. Robust comparisons between models are ensured using statistical significance testing and highly sophisticated evaluation metrics. By tackling issues with model selection, accuracy over computational efficiency trade-offs, and deployment considerations, this study offers helpful insights for well-informed choices in deep learning applications.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Recruitment: Frameworks and Innovations
authors: Pankaj Khurana, Jimmy Singla
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Now a days, the entire recruitment process is changed with the automation and artificial intelligence. There is no doubt that candidate screening, evaluation and shortlisting processes have been changed with the help of intelligent systems. In this paper, the latest developments of artificial intelligence - based frameworks and innovations for recruitment processes are reviewed. The existing studies use machine learning, natural language processing and deep learning techniques to improve resume parsing, job matching and interview analysis. Some existing studies are based on the combination of blockchain technology with gamification. In these studies, hiring process is more transparent, fair and engaging for all the stakeholders. The emerging trends from the existing studies are hybrid transformer-based models and smart recommendation systems. These models and systems are able to make sensible recommendations for employers of the organizations. Further there are some issues with these artificial intelligence-based systems for recruitment. These issues are data bias, privacy and explainability. Basically, this paper offers understanding of how intelligent systems are transforming the process of recruitment through automation and incorporation of artificial intelligence.

title: Towards Trustworthy LLMs: A Hybrid RAG-Enhanced, Attention-Driven Multi-Metric Framework for Model Evaluation and Comparison
authors: Suryaram S, Getzi Jeba Leelipushpam Paulraj, Stewart Kirubakaran S, Immanuel Johnraja Jebadurai
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The rapid development of Large Language Models has transformed the field of NLP, enabling a range of applications from auto-generated content to intelligent conversational systems. This work proposes an overall hybrid interpretability and evaluation framework for transformer-based Large Language Models (LLMs), including multi-model comparison, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), attention-based mechanistic analysis, and multimetric performance scoring. It processes user queries with optional contextual information extracted from PDF, text, or CSV files and enhances the input using an offline RAG mechanism built with SentenceTransformer embeddings and FAISS indexing. Clickable evidence links retrieved from a locally cached NewsAPI dataset provide additional external context to ground the responses and reduce hallucination, improving their factual reliability. The interpretability module computes token-level attention importance directly from the encoder, producing normalized attention maps and visual heatmaps. Output length and inference speed are also measured, and all four metrics contribute to a weighted overall score: 40% faithfulness, 30% length, 15% coherence, and 15% speed. A comparison engine runs each model in sequence, using strict memory management, attention fallback handling, and CPU/GPU switching to avoid serialization and CUDA memory issues. An end-to-end visualization layer through Streamlit is provided with a comparison table of the models, score breakdown pie chart, attention heat map, and RAG retrieval display. Experiments show that the Flan-T5-XL strikes the best balance among interpretability, detail, and speed: faithfulness scores were between 0.86, coherence scores averaged 4.5/5, and the inference times were rather moderate (2-10} seconds) outperforming the other Flan-T5 family of models.

title: Smart Pill Dispenser with Facial Recognition and Health Monitoring
authors: Ranjini R, Sabarigirinathan C, Sanjay V, H. Joseph Prabhakar Williams
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: With the increasing need for personalised healthcare and medication adherence, smart healthcare devices have become essential. This paper presents a Smart Pill Dispenser with Facial Recognition and Health Monitoring, designed to ensure accurate medication delivery and real-time patient monitoring. The system integrates facial recognition for patient authentication, a microcontroller (ESP32) for intelligent control, and sensor-based health monitoring to track vital signs such as heart rate, temperature, and blood pressure. Simulation and prototype testing confirm accurate medication dispensing, reliable patient identification, and continuous health monitoring. The proposed system demonstrates promise for enhancing medication adherence, patient safety, and remote healthcare management.

title: Context-Aware Explanation Drift Detection (CA-EDD): For Plant Disease Severity Estimation
authors: Sonu Varghese K, R Satheesh Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Deep learning models have shown high accuracy in automated plant disease classification; however, their black-box nature limits adoption in precision agriculture, where biological validity and interpretability are critical. Conventional Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methods, such as Grad-CAM, generate visual saliency maps that often lack alignment with true pathological symptoms, leading to predictions that are accurate yet biologically inconsistent. This paper proposes SymptomConsistency Guided Explainable AI (SCG-XAI), a novel ContextAware Explanation Drift Detection (CA-EDD) framework that validates model reasoning against established plant pathology principles. The framework integrates an explanation generator, an explanation embedding module that encodes attribution maps into structured symptom descriptors capturing lesion color, texture, and spatial distribution, a temporal drift analyzer to detect shifts in model reasoning across disease severity stages, and a context integration layer that constrains explanation validity using agronomic criteria, specifically the Relative Lesion Height (RLH) and the Standard Evaluation System (SES) for rice sheath blight caused by Rhizoctonia solani. By evaluating the semantic consistency between model explanations and physiological disease symptoms, SCG-XAI enables the detection of logic drift that is not reflected in conventional performance measures. Experimental results on a multi-severity rice sheath blight dataset demonstrate that SCG-XAI maintains competitive classification accuracy while ensuring that model explanations are biologically consistent and trustworthy for real-world field deployment.

title: Bearing-Only Formation Control of Nonholonomic Unicycles: A Conditional Disturbance Utilization Method
authors: Qin Wang, Hanyu Yin, Yanmeng Zhang, Yang Yi, Wenbing Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Maintaining a stable formation among nonholonomic unicycles presents a significant challenge, particularly when subjected to real-world disturbances such as wheel slipping and motor noise. To tackle this issue, we propose a conditional disturbance utilization (CDU) framework that selectively uses favorable disturbances rather than simply rejecting all. First, the disturbance observer (DO) is employed to establish the informational foundation for subsequent conditional utilization decisions. And, the CDU mechanism dynamically decides whether the disturbance aligns with the desired control objective. Moreover, incorporating bearing rigidity achieves global stability with fast convergence. Specifically, the proposed potential function, constructed from bearing information, mitigates disturbance driven collision risks and ensures safety. Finally, simulations and experiments validate the proposed CDU-based formation control. The results demonstrate substantial improvements in responsiveness, and efficiency when compared to disturbance suppression method, highlighting the superiority of the proposed framework.

title: SP $^{2}$ Join: Secure and Practical Multi-Table Pairwise Equi-Join Queries over Encrypted Databases
authors: Sen Zhao, Siyi Lv, Zheli Liu, Fuchun Guo, Xiaofeng Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Practical equi-join queries over encrypted databases aim to provide clients with flexible query capabilities while safeguarding data confidentiality. So far, the most promising and practical solution is CHT, which is based on Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs). However, such schemes can only support two-table equi-join queries, which are rather limited compared to Multi-Table Pairwise Equi-Join (MTPEJ). Furthermore, in terms of efficiency, throughput, and access pattern, the join implemented in TEEs is still problematic compared to a practical and secure scheme. In this work, we focus on this important type of equi-join, which facilitates join across any two tables among multiple tables. Specifically, we propose SP <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> Join in this paper, the first MTPEJ framework that supports secure pairwise equi-join among multi-table by leveraging a novel oblivious pipeline join algorithm in TEEs. To validate our approach, we evaluate SP <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> Join in MySQL using the benchmarks and real datasets, and the results demonstrate that SP <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> Join is superior to the state-of-the-art in terms of secure equi-join. Particularly, our pipeline join query time is only 2.68% of CHT, yet throughput can reach up to 16.56 × of CHT. Even for MTPEJ, it is still 14.95% of CHT when implementing pairwise equi-join among 10 tables, with a time cost of approximately 140.30 ms.

title: A Unified Modeling Framework for Cognitive Wildlife Monitoring
authors: P. Venkateswarlu Reddy, D. Ganesh, Sivakala Sridevi, Thupakula Venkata Srikala, Ukkusuri Arun Kumar, V. Yugesh
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Automatic identification of common species plays a crucial role in biodiversity monitoring, ecological research, and agricultural management. However, existing approaches often struggle with inter-class similarities, intraclass variability, and limited generalization across diverse datasets. To address these challenges, this work proposes an automatic species identification method based on ensemble learning integrated with deep learning. The proposed framework leverages multiple deep neural architectures to capture complementary feature representations and employs an ensemble strategy to improve classification robustness and accuracy. By combining convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with advanced ensemble techniques such as stacking and weighted voting, the model effectively mitigates individual model biases and enhances generalization across species categories. Experimental validation of image datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance compared to single deep learning models with improvements in accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score.

title: Detection of Algorithmic Bias in AI-Based Recruitment Systems Using Explainable AI
authors: P. Hari Krishna, K. Uday Kumar Reddy, G. Leela Aravinda, V. Anjali, C. Bhavana Reddy, SK. Abdur Rehman
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
