abstract: This project is about an intelligent recruitment forecasting system that uses advanced machine learning models to evaluate and score candidates for hiring. The system combines Logistic Regression, Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), and XGBoost to make dependable predictions from candidate facts like age, experience, and education level, the skills, certifications, and requirements of the job. In order to raise the quality of data, the system gets job and candidate details ready, calculates skill intersections, and changes the categorical features into suitable numerical formats. The problem with the class that was solved was by using Random Over Sampling, so that the models remain stable and not biased during the training. The performance of the models is very well measured through the use of the different metrics like accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score together with the visualization tools such as confusion matrices and ROC curves, thereby enabling the recruiters to benchmark the algorithms and pick the one that is most effective for candidate evaluation. In order to open up more, the system uses Explainable AI methods like SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations). SHAP offers both global and local feature importance, while LIME clarifies how individual candidate features influence a prediction. These explanations make it possible for the recruiters to know the “hired” or “rejected” decisions' logic and thus help in establishing fairness, trust, and accountability. To sum up, this project is a proof of concept that the use of machine learning and explainable AI in recruitment can lead to a more secure decision-making process, fewer biases from algorithms, and a more consistent and morally right way of choosing candidates.

title: WirelessEV-OPT: Metaheuristic Optimization of Resonant Frequency and Power Transfer Using PSO and GWO
authors: Anupriya S, K. Malarkodi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Wireless electric vehicle (EV) charging has become one of the potential successful solutions to traditional plug-in charging systems, but its efficiency is highly influenced by the misalignment of the coils, resonant frequency error, and thermal variations. In order to solve these problems, this paper presents WirelessEV-OPT; a hybrid metaheuristic optimization model combining Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO) to optimize power transfer efficiency and voltage stabilization in dynamic charging conditions. It has in place a comprehensive two-way wireless power transfer (WPT) model, which involves compensation networks, frequency restrictions and thermal limits. The hybrid algorithm PSO-GWO comes up with a multi-objective fitness criterion that combines coil alignment, resonant frequency selection as well as reduction of losses. Results of simulations show that simulation exhibits better convergence behavior and is more robust than single algorithm methods. WirelessEV-OPT is more efficient, less prone to misalignment, lower thermal spikes, and is more efficient in various loads. The next-generation wireless EV chargers infrastructure suggested has an optimization framework that is a scalable/intelligent togetherness that is capable of delivering a robust energy transfer in the face of real world uncertainties. The WirelessEV-OPT model is able to promote the sustainability of metropolitan mobility by using energy-efficient EV charging. Its wireless power transmission is optimized; it enhances transportation and urban energy infrastructure to encourage greener and smarter cities.

title: The Virtue of Hallucination: When AI Mistakes Make Software Safer
authors: Antonio Mastropaolo
abstract: “LLMorpheus: Mutation Testing Using Large Language Models,” published in IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering in 2025, proposes a large language model-driven mutation testing framework that moves beyond fixed operator catalogs by using context-aware code infilling.

title: 50 & 25 YEARS AGO
authors: Erich Neuhold
abstract: Summaries of articles presented in this issue of the publication.

title: From Structure to Semantics: Hypergraph-Based AR Assembly Guidance with LLM-Mediated Narration
authors: Xinda Liu, Bowei Zhang, Jiaju Xu, Jian Wu, Guohua Geng, Lili Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Effective Augmented Reality (AR) guidance for complex assembly faces a dual challenge: the inability of conventional liaison graphs to represent procedural logic, and the cognitive burden imposed by visual instructions. We argue that the solution requires a more expressive structure to overcome these representational deficits and a narration approach to mediate instruction complexity. Our method first employs an assembly hypergraph to capture the task's hierarchical information, from which an A* search algorithm generates an optimal assembly path. Then a Large Language Model (LLM)-mediated narration workflow is designed to address the ergonomic deficiencies of the machine-centric path. It employs an optimizer to improve fluency, followed by a narrator that crafts the steps into an intuitive instruction narration. A within-subjects user study (N = 24) revealed a progressive enhancement from our method's components. The transition from a liaison-graph baseline to the hypergraph alone improved objective outcomes by reducing task time and errors and improving subjective ratings (SUS, NASA-TLX, TAM, and ARI). Subsequently, augmenting the LLM-mediated narration maintained these gains while lowering cognitive load and elevating user experience and usability. Our findings indicate the value of our AR assembly design and discuss the opportunities of using LLM as a mediation layer for better user interaction.

title: Finite-Time Disturbance Observer-Based Adaptive Second-Order Sliding Mode Control for Parallel Connected DC–DC Buck Converters With Nonideal Switches
authors: Cong Wang, Li Li, Minghui Yao, Yan Niu, Qiliang Wu
abstract: Parallel connected DC–DC buck converters (PCBCs) are inevitably subjected to the disturbances caused by nonideal active switches and uncertain passive components, which poses a significant challenge for achieving both precise current sharing and robust voltage regulation. To address the coupling between inductor current and output voltage, a multilayer control framework equipped with a slow voltage controller and fast current controllers is developed, which can automatically deal with the load variations, and be easily extended to PCBC with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N$</tex-math> </inline-formula> modules. Subsequently, the nonideal characteristics in PCBC are modeled as disturbances. The constructed finite-time disturbance observer (FTDO) can effectively estimate the disturbances, and then a feedforward compensation is made by using the obtained estimates. To mitigate chattering with the further aim of enhancing control accuracy of output voltage, a finite-time stable adaptive second-order sliding mode (SOSM) control is proposed, in which the adaptation algorithm achieves rapid reaching of sliding manifold, and avoids excessive control action. The presented control strategy can obtain fast and accurate tracking of output voltage, as well as balanced current sharing. Experimental results are presented to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Virtual Reality Laboratory for Teaching Power Systems in Electrical Engineering Undergraduate Courses
authors: Daniel Rebouças Jaguaribe, Gustavo Alves, Fernando Lessa Tofoli, Ruth Pastôra Saraiva Leão, Lucas Silveira Melo, Raimundo Furtado Sampaio, Antônio José Melo Leite Júnior, Giovanni Cordeiro Barroso
abstract: The study of power substations is fundamental to the training of electrical engineers, as these facilities are crucial to power system operation, ensuring the reliability and efficiency of energy distribution. However, traditional teaching methods often face limitations in offering practical and immersive experiences. Given this gap, this work presents a Virtual Reality Substation Laboratory (VRSL), developed based on a 69/13.8-kV substation at the Federal University of Ceará, Fortaleza, Brazil. The VRSL provides a free-to-use educational platform that allows teachers and students to explore the structural and operational aspects of power substations, including equipment operation and fault simulations. Its integration into two substation-related courses over a three-year period engaged more than 120 students by combining virtual reality (VR) with conventional lectures. The triangulation of quantitative and qualitative analyses demonstrates substantial improvements in academic performance, student engagement, and comprehension of complex substation concepts. By bridging theoretical knowledge and practical simulation, the VRSL enhances engineering education and prepares students for real-world challenges in power systems.

title: Congratulations IEEE Fellows
authors: N/A

title: AI-Powered Community Elderly Care: An Intelligent Companion System Integrating Emotional-Computing and Health Monitoring
authors: Juan Yang, Mei Li, Haoxue Zhou
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: Addressing the dual challenges of accelerated aging and shrinking family structures, this paper proposes an intelligent companion system for community-based elderly care in Chongqing. Integrating artificial intelligence with applied psychology, the system establishes a tripartite framework of “emotional computing-health perception-service adaptation“ to tackle practical difficulties including insufficient service resources, heightened loneliness among the elderly, and lagging chronic disease management. The system constructs a dynamic health profile that synthesizes physiological indicators (e.g., heart rate via PPG) and psychological data (e.g., depression risk via voice analysis and PHQ-9) to enable synergistic intervention. A six-month empirical study conducted in typical Chongqing communities demonstrated the system's significant effectiveness in alleviating loneliness (p < 0.001), reducing depressive symptoms (p < 0.001), and improving chronic disease management adherence (from 65% to 88<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">%</sup>).

title: Low-Frequency First: Eliminating Floating Artifacts in 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Jianchao Wang, Peng Zhou, Cen Li, Rong Quan, Jie Qin
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) is a powerful and computationally efficient representation for 3D reconstruction. Despite its strengths, 3DGS often produces floating artifacts, which are erroneous structures detached from the actual geometry and significantly degrade visual fidelity. The underlying mechanisms causing these artifacts, particularly in low-quality initialization scenarios, have not been fully explored. In this paper, we investigate the origins of floating artifacts from a frequency-domain perspective and identify under-optimized Gaussians as the primary source. Based on our analysis, we propose Eliminating-Floating-Artifacts Gaussian Splatting (EFA-GS), which selectively expands under-optimized Gaussians to prioritize accurate low-frequency learning. Additionally, we introduce complementary depth-based and scale-based strategies to dynamically refine Gaussian expansion, effectively mitigating detail erosion. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that EFA-GS substantially reduces floating artifacts while preserving high-frequency details, achieving an improvement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1. 6 8 ~ d B}$</tex> in PSNR over baseline method on our RWLQ dataset. Furthermore, we validate the effectiveness of our approach in downstream 3D editing tasks. We provide our implementation in https://jcwang-gh.github.io/EFA-GS.

title: A Wideband Single Stage LNA Using a 200-nm GaN HEMT Process
authors: Jiyong Chung, Ickhyun Song
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: In this paper, we introduce a single-stage wideband low-noise amplifier (LNA) implemented in gallium-nitride (GaN) High-electron-mobility transistor (HEMT) technology. The amplifier operates over the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-5 \text{GHz}$</tex> frequency range and exhibits <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{21}$</tex> of 12.4 dB. To achieve such a wide bandwidth with a single stage, a bandwidth-enhancement technique is employed. By using one capacitor and two inductors in the input matching network with feedback resistor, the proposed design provides improved input matching (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\vert \mathrm{S}_{11}\vert < -9$</tex> dB) over a wider bandwidth compared to a single-inductor matching approach. Also, wide output matching <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\left\vert \mathrm{S}_{22}\right\vert <-10 ~\text{dB}\right)$</tex> is achieved in operation frequency. With a minimum noise figure (NF) of 2 dB and a maximum 1-dB compression point (Output P1 dB) of 11.5 dBm and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3{ }^{\text {rd }}$</tex> output intercept point (OIP3) of 33.4 dBm, the proposed amplifier offers a clear advantage in chip area compared to wideband LNAs implemented using multiple stages.

title: MT-MO: Efficient and Robust Non-Profiled Side-Channel Analysis Using Multitask Learning
authors: Huy-Thanh Le, Van-Phuc Hoang, Ngoc-Tuan Do, Xuan-Nam Tran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Multi-Output Deep Learning based Side Channel Analysis (MO-DLSCA) with its variants have demonstrated their efficacy in non-profiled scenarios. However, current MO approaches remain limited to sequential single-byte recovery, failing to exploit shared leakage characteristics across the cryptographic key and resulting in redundant training overhead. This letter introduces a Multitask Multi-Output (MT-MO) deep learning architecture to mitigate this issue. By leveraging a shared feature extraction backbone with vectorized output heads, our model simultaneously recovers multiple key bytes in a single training session. Both Multitask Classification (MT-MOC) and Regression (MT-MOR) variants are introduced to target masking, noise injection, and desynchronization countermeasures. Experimental results obtained from three randomly selected target bytes indicate that the proposed models reduce the execution time by approximately 11 times compared to the sequential single-task model, while maintaining success rates of at least 73.33 %.

title: AI-Powered Reasoning Framework for Multilingual Fake News Detection
authors: S. Prasanna, Fahimuddin Shaik, A. Charan Kumar Reddy, P. Kamaniya Sai, K. Chaitanya, M. Dinesh
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Rapid detection of fake news, a major global issue, is the root of the problem of the quick spreading of false information through digital channels, which endangers the trust of the public. This paper presents an AI reasoning framework as a method to expose and offer an easy and trustable way for different user groups. The cross-lingual challenge of finding fake news is the problem with which the framework is dealing. After word embeddings have captured the conceptual core of the news articles, the system proceeds to represent the semantic relationships with the texts. Next the use of a gradient-boosting classifier gives more flexibility in decision-making on the same accuracy level, thus benefiting from the best of both worlds. The framework reaches breakthrough accuracy of 98.9%, thus making it comparable to traditional approaches. Hence, the system is not only successful in the prediction phase, but it also utilizes transparent AI methods, which allow the end users to follow the logic of each prediction, thereby giving an indication of fairness and trust. Moreover, a real-time interface has been developed so that the public can quickly verify the trustworthiness of the news flow.

title: Mitigating Bias and Hallucinations in LLMs Through Prompt Engineering and Knowledge-Grounded Approaches in Healthcare Domain
authors: A.P.D. Ishadya, A. Withanaarachchi, S. Jayalal
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied in healthcare for clinical question answering and information support, yet their deployment is constrained by risks of hallucinated content and embedded bias that can compromise patient safety. This study proposes an integrated mitigation framework that combines advanced prompt engineering with knowledge-grounded approaches, specifically Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and the Model Context Protocol (MCP), without modifying underlying model parameters. Using a mixed-methods design, parallel RAG and MCP pipelines were implemented with shared retrieval and prompting logic and evaluated on NIH-derived MedQuAD subsets (CancerQA and NIDDKQA). Structured prompting strategies including role-based prompts, chain-of-thought, and chain-of-verification were applied consistently across both pipelines. Quantitative evaluation using accuracy, semantic similarity, evidence-backed rate, abstention, recall, and demographic drift metrics shows that MCP consistently outperforms or matches conventional RAG, achieving higher F1 and ROUGE-L scores, stronger evidence grounding, and lower abstention while maintaining minimal demographic drift. Expert review further confirmed improved clinical relevance and reduced unsupported claims. These findings demonstrate that coupling structured prompting with knowledge grounding produces more reliable, safer, and fairer healthcare LLM outputs, offering a practical and scalable pathway for trustworthy AI deployment in high-stakes clinical settings.

title: Quantitative Readability Assessment of Sinhala Handwriting Using Visual Feature Consistency and Sinhala Morphological Feature Matching
authors: M.D.K.L. Gunathilaka, L. Ranathunga
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Handwriting legibility is a critical factor in human visual interpretation, distinct from machine recognition accuracy. While readability assessment frameworks exist for Latin scripts, complex Indic scripts like Sinhala remain under-researched. This paper proposes a novel framework for quantitatively evaluating the readability of handwritten Sinhala text using visual and spatial features. The study introduces a feature-driven model incorporating stroke thickness, size consistency, spacing uniformity, slant angle, baseline alignment, and a novel Sinhala morphological feature matching method grounded in the script's unique anatomy. Validation was conducted using a dataset of handwritten samples, each evaluated with subjective ratings provided by native readers on a Likert scale. The proposed framework achieved a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.113, Mean Squared Error (MSE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.019, R^{2}$</tex> of 0.233, and a Pearson correlation of 0.509 against human ratings. These results demonstrate the feasibility of automated readability scoring for pedagogical and forensic applications in Sinhala.

title: Research on Energy Storage Configuration and Dynamic Networking of Hybrid Energy Microgrid Cluster Based on Source-Load Matching Degree
authors: Shuming Zhou, Ye He, Yalu Fu, Chaoming Zheng, Qiang Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: To solve the power supply reliability problem of hybrid energy microgrids (HEMs) and their clusters under extreme conditions and faults, this study explores HEMs' source-load matching, energy storage configuration, and fault dynamic networking. It first clarifies the differences between HEMs and traditional microgrids, proposes the “source-load coincidence rate” to evaluate new energy's power supply capacity under extreme conditions, and defines the source-load matching degree as the minimum value of the annual typicalday source-load coincidence rate to quantify distributed power's load support capability. Then, it builds a source-load matching model with energy storage, derives energy storage capacity and power formulas (with the boundary condition that “the source-load matching degree <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 1$</tex> during upper-grid faults”), and puts forward a microgrid cluster energy storage allocation method. It also analyzes dynamic networking for two cluster fault scenarios and optimizes the configuration formula. A 5-sub-area distribution feeder case shows that the faultlocation scheme cuts total energy storage by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$29.4 \%(2380 \text{kWh}$</tex> to 1880 kWh) while ensuring critical load supply, balancing economy and reliability. This study supports HEM (cluster) energy storage planning and safe operation.

title: Analyzing the Impact of Time-Marked Twitter Sentiment on Intraday Stock Prices Using ARIMA Models: A Case Study of Nvidia and Microsoft
authors: Anil Chandrasekaran, Abdulla Atayer, Ahmed AlMarri, Abdulla AlDoobi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: This study examines the influence of real-time Twitter sentiment on intraday stock movements of Nvidia (NVDA) and Microsoft (MSFT). Tweets containing company-related keywords were collected using Python's snscrape module and analyzed with the VADER sentiment model. Aggregated sentiment scores were time-aligned with minute-level stock data from Yahoo Finance. Two econometric models—ARIMA and sentiment-augmented ARIMAX—were compared using RMSE, MAE, and directional accuracy. Results show that integrating Twitter sentiment improves short-term forecasting performance, highlighting social media's measurable impact on market behavior and the value of sentiment-based econometric modeling.

title: Leveraging Synthetic Multimodal Health Data and Federated Learning to Develop Explainable ML Models for Population-Level Precision Medicine
authors: Gokul Narain Natarajan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The increasing availability of heterogeneous health data from wearables, electronic health records, imaging, genomics, and patient-reported outcomes presents a unique opportunity to build predictive models capable of supporting population-level precision medicine. However, privacy constraints, institutional silos, and imbalance across demographic subgroups limit the creation of robust and generalizable models. This paper introduces a privacy-first framework that integrates synthetic multimodal health data generation with federated learning to enable collaborative model development without exposing real patient information. The approach uses high-fidelity synthetic data to augment underrepresented populations, harmonize modality distributions, and stabilize cross-site learning. Federated optimization enables multiple institutions to jointly train models while maintaining strict data locality. An explainability layer— combining temporal attention, modality-specific attributions, and global feature ranking—supports interpretable insights for clinicians and public health stakeholders. The proposed framework demonstrates a scalable pathway toward equitable, trustworthy, and reproducible precision-medicine analytics for large heterogeneous populations.

title: Bio-Inspired Ultra High Frequency (UHF) Sensor Design for Partial Discharge Measurement in High Voltage Apparatus
authors: Jyotirmoy Paul, Arijit Baral, Soumya Chatterjee
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel bio-inspired ultra-high frequency (UHF) sensor design for partial discharge (PD) measurement in high voltage apparatus. The proposed UHF sensor incorporates a design inspired by the Bougainvillea leaf with wideband frequency response spanning from 413 MHz to above 3 GHz ideal for PD measurement in HV apparatus. The bio-inspired UHF sensor is modeled using ANSYS HFSS software with the parameters of the UHF antenna optimized to achieve return loss (S11 less than −10 dB) and VSWR (less than −2). The UHF sensor designed in this paper can be used for online PD monitoring of any high voltage equipment.

title: Temporal-Aware Crowd Anomaly Detection Framework for Violence Recognition Under Video Frame Discontinuities
authors: K Karthicka, M Saranya Meenatchi, G Varshini Ramithaa, S Santhi, S Kalaiselvi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: Real world video surveillance systems frequently encounter frame discontinuities due to network failures, occlusions and data corruption which usually degrade the performance of crowd anomaly detection algorithms. Traditional approaches tend to lose temporal consistency that results in lower accuracy for real-time applications. To overcome this limitation, this paper introduces a Temporal Aware Crowd Anomaly Detection Framework (TA-CADF) based on YOLOv8 based spatial feature extraction augmented with a K-Fold Temporal Consistency Module (KTCM) for video frame discontinuities. The proposed model is targeting the extraction of spatiotemporal edge features in order to bridge discontinuities between frames and provide smooth interpretation of scenes. The K-Fold Temporal Consistency Module improves the learning stability and generalization by rigorously testing performance on multiple folds. The experimental results indicate that the proposed approach achieves 97.34% accuracy while effectively reducing the impact of frame discontinuity. These results show the potential of the framework in live surveillance applications with an effective solution for edge-preservation video processing and public surveillance safety.

title: Research on Wireless Communication Based on Quantum Cryptography Technology
authors: Yuehua Xing, Hui Li, Jiaying Zhao, Yiming Lu, Miaomiao Du, Hua Shi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the upgrading of rail transit towards high speed, intelligence and networking, the demand for quantum attack resistance of train-ground communication systems has become increasingly urgent. To address the problems of traditional optical Quantum Key Distribution (QKD), such as high deployment costs, insufficient security strength of lightweight encryption algorithms, and poor scalability of hardware trusted encryption chips, this paper constructs a quantum-encrypted wireless communication technology architecture centered on the closed-loop management and control of the full life cycle of keys. It designs an on-board quantum security board integrating components including a quantum random number generator and an algorithm module, and proposes a key derivation and replacement algorithm based on the PBKDF2 function as well as a random salt transmission mechanism relying on Shannon's Perfect Secrecy Theorem. The results of the on-board test show that this scheme can realize the secure encrypted transmission of train-ground service data, with the average handshake delay being less than or equal to 300 ms and the average decryption and forwarding delay being less than or equal to 0.5 ms. It does not interfere with the operation of the original communication link and achieves both security and real-time performance. This research provides a feasible technical path for resisting quantum computing attacks and building a solid security defense line for smart rail transit, which has important theoretical significance and engineering application value.

title: Experimental Investigation to Find the Potential of Regenerative Suspension System Using Piezoelectric Energy Harvester
authors: V L Athul, A P Amal Raveendran, S Sandra, A Akhil, R Sailesh, Subin Michael
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: The conventional vehicle suspension system dissipates mechanical energy produced due to vibrations caused by road irregularities as heat to its surroundings, leading to significant energy losses. This study focuses on recovering the lost portion of energy by converting it into electrical energy using piezoelectric materials like lead zirconate titanate (PZT4). This recovered electrical energy has been used to power several vehicle sensors. The research involves designing and fabricating an optimized experimental apparatus modeled as a cam irregularity setup, simulating real-world road irregularities. Experimental procedures have been conducted under varying motor speeds using a variable frequency drive (VFD), and energy outputs have been measured. The results demonstrated that a considerable portion of the mechanical energy from the suspension system could be converted into usable electrical energy, validating the feasibility of integrating a piezoelectric energy harvester (PEH) in automotive systems. The findings support the integration of piezoelectric energy harvester (PEH)- based regenerative suspension systems to improve energy efficiency in modern vehicles.

title: StarGS: Towards Real-Time Dynamic Scene Rendering via Gaussian Splatting with Spatiotemporal-Aware Density Control
authors: Mingwei Cao, Zhihao Liu, Haifeng Zhao
abstract: The 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) technique has yielded significant achievements in scene rendering. However, it encounters difficulties when representing dynamic scenes marked by complexity, high degrees of freedom, and limited viewpoints. To overcome these challenges, we introduce StarGS, an innovative method for real-time dynamic scene rendering that employs spatiotemporal-aware density control in Gaussian splatting. StarGS features an adaptive density mechanism that dynamically modifies the Gaussian distribution based on spatiotemporal characteristics, thereby precisely capturing structural variations and intricate texture details in areas with complex motion. Firstly, we devise a motion-aware keypoints mechanism, guided by spatio-temporal features, which isolates notable moving objects within the scene and promotes local density augmentation around them, enhancing their structural representations. Secondly, we incorporate a pixel-wise error-guided pruning module to eliminate low-contribution Gaussians and noise, effectively minimizing computational redundancy and enhancing rendering efficiency. Thirdly, we suggest a structural feature-anchor rigidity constraint that bolsters the local consistency of Gaussians, reducing motion artifacts in dynamic scenes. We assess StarGS using the HyperNeRF and Neu3D datasets and compare it with state-of-the-art methods. Experimental results reveal that StarGS achieves 86 FPS at a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1352 \times 1014$</tex> resolution, while outperforming state-of-the-art methods in image quality. Source code available at: https://github.com/caomw/stargs

title: A Framework for Digital Twin Runtime and Lifecycle Management
authors: Edmund Widl, Marc Dünser, Jort A. Groen
publish: 2026 Open Source Modelling and Simulation of Energy Systems (OSMSES)
abstract: Digital twins (DTs) are playing an increasingly important role in the implementation of complex energy applications. This paper addresses the challenge of managing DT development and operation across diverse use cases and deployment contexts. It introduces a structured lifecycle schema and a technical framework that combines a common runtime environment with automated model generation. By applying a modular architecture, containerization, and workflow automation to DT models and services, a consistent and streamlined process for the development and operation of DTs is enabled. Based on an open-source implementation of this technical framework, a proof-of-concept example for the development and deployment of a DT is presented. This approach provides a practical foundation for efficient DT implementation, fostering rapid adoption and adaptability in heterogeneous energy systems.

title: SRE-Med: A Saliency-Aware Illumination Preprocessing Pipeline for Medical Imaging AI Models
authors: Sasi Vadan Muppalla, Lokesh Deekonda, Sriram Deveragatla, V. Harini
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Medical image acquisition in modalities such as chest radiography, retinal fundus imaging, and microscopy is often affected by poor or uneven illumination, which can obscure clinically significant features and reduce the reliability of automated diagnostic systems. Conventional preprocessing methods, including histogram equalization and Retinex-based models, perform global adjustments that may introduce artifacts, amplify noise, or distort color balance. Deep learning approaches have improved overall brightness but frequently fail to preserve localized diagnostic details that are essential for medical interpretation.To address these challenges, this work presents SRE-Med, a saliency-aware illumination preprocessing pipeline designed for medical imaging applications. The method integrates U<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>-Net and FastSAM to extract diagnostically relevant regions, which are processed separately from background areas using a dual-branch encoder–decoder architecture. A U-Net-based fusion stage then restores global consistency while maintaining smooth boundaries and color fidelity. The pipeline was implemented in Python with PyTorch and evaluated on chest X-ray and retinal fundus datasets. Quantitative results, measured by PSNR, SSIM, and F1 score, demonstrate consistent improvements over baseline enhancement techniques, while perceptual metrics such as BRISQUE and MUSIQ confirm the naturalness of the restored images.By preserving fine diagnostic structures under challenging lighting conditions, SRE-Med provides a reliable preprocessing stage that enhances the performance of AI-based medical imaging models. The proposed approach is adaptable across imaging modalities and has the potential to be integrated into both clinical decision support systems and real-time embedded diagnostic devices.

title: Detection of False Data Injection Attacks in Interconnected Power Networks Using Hybrid Deep Learning Approach
authors: Prasanjit Dey, Shailendra Singh, Padmagirisan P., S. R. Mohanty, Abhiskek Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Due to massive integration of information and communication technology (ICT) in electric power systems, the electric grid became more vulnerable to cyber-attack. Therefore, detection of such anomalies should be done efficiently and accurately. In this regard, a hybrid scheme to identify the False Data Injection Attacks (FDIA) in interconnected power network has been proposed in this paper. In order to emulate FDIA through an attack vector, a small sinusoidal signal is injected into frequency data. For the estimation of states, Kalman filter has been used. The proposed hybrid approach enables the deep learning technique using a convolution neural network with long short-term memory (CNN-LSTM) model. In this approach, the frequency signals are converted into spectrograms using CNN-based feature extraction. In addition to feature extraction, the temporal pattern recognition has been done through LSTM technique. The proposed hybrid models are developed in MATLAB/Simulink. In real-time deployment, the developed model is employed via a Stateful Predict block, outputting a detection flag. Furthermore, the Kalman filter and Chi-square methods are deployed in real-time using the OPAL-RT real-time simulator, in order to validate the method's accuracy and robustness for securing electric grid against cyber threats.

title: Research on Automatic Verification of Safety Risks of Secondary Operation Equipment in Smart Substation
authors: Zhang Li, Wang Dingjun, Wang Lina, Wu Jinyuan, Han Huarong, He Yijie
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of smart substation technology, numerous safety risks have emerged in these systems. This project has systematically collected and organized typical SCD virtual circuit configurations, enabling automated verification of secondary virtual circuit accuracy. By utilizing online monitoring and file download technologies to acquire dynamic equipment models, we achieved consistency checks between static and dynamic models of secondary devices. Additionally, real-time acquisition of device settings and soft-press plates was conducted for crosschecking against preset parameter lists. These comprehensive measures ensure secure and stable operation of smart substations, especially in the smart substation reconstruction and expansion project having a very important application.

title: Joint Passive Beamforming and Power Control for Full-Duplex Two-Way Wireless Systems Aided by Intelligent Reflecting Surface
authors: Xiangbai Liao, Jiagang Liu, Zhibin Pan
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: This paper investigates the adoption of an Intelligent Reflecting Surface (IRS) in a Full-Duplex Two-Way Communication (FD-TWC) system. We aim to minimize the total transmit power of the two FD sources under minimum Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) constraints by jointly optimizing the source power allocation and the IRS reflective beamforming. This joint optimization leads to a complex nonconvex problem. To tackle it, we propose two novel algorithms: a Fractional Approximation-based algorithm and a Maximum Slope Difference algorithm. Specifically, the first algorithm utilizes geometric properties and fundamental inequalities to simplify the constraints, and then employs the Lagrange method and fractional approximation to obtain the suboptimal transmit power allocation and the IRS reflecting beamforming. Alternatively, leveraging the special structure of the formulated problem, the second algorithm employs an iterative method to obtain the transmit power allocation and the IRS reflecting beamforming. In addition, We compare the proposed FD-IRS-TWC system with the traditional MIMO relay, random phase shift IRS and no-IRS schemes. Numerical results show that the proposed FD-IRS-TWC scheme can significantly reduce the power consumption over the conventional scheme.

title: Evaluating Digital Twin Visualizations for Safety and Trust in Robot-Assisted Dressing
authors: Yunus Emre Cogurcu, Mirco Bartolomei, Baslin James, James Law, James A Douthwaite, Yasmin Rafiq, Lyudmila Mihaylova, Sanja Dogramadzi
abstract: None

title: XRFlow: Visualizing Metrics and Insights for Extended Reality
authors: Johannes Günter Herforth, Fiammetta Zanetti, Karsten Schönbein, Annika P.C. Lutz, Jean Botev
publish: Proceedings of the 18th International Workshop on Immersive Mixed and Virtual Environments Systems
abstract: None

title: A Multi-modal Fusion Approach for Drug Discovery and Efficacy Prediction
authors: Agnes Chella Wanika R, A.M. Anusha Bamini, R Venkatesan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Discovery of drug behavior performed in clinical laboratories are time consuming and costly, with large percentage of drug samples are failing due to safety or efficacy issues. This work aims to propose a Machine Learning-based framework to predict whether a drug sample is likely to succeed or not using preclinical molecular and trail-related parameters. A dataset of 200 biologically safe-range samples are generated following the Lipinski’s rule of five, including the features such as molecular weight, LogP, hydrogen bond donors and acceptors, toxicity score, and trail phase. Three ML algorithms Linear Regression, Random Forest and XGBoost - were implemented and compared using performance evaluation standards such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC. The Random Forest pattern achieved the best overall performance with an accuracy of 87.5%, demonstrating strong predictive capability and interpretability. Feature importance analysis showed that toxicity score and trail phase were the most influential factors in determining trail success. The work bring forward, transparent and cost-effective approach for early prediction of drug viability, potentially reducing research costs and accelerating the drug discovery development pipeline unlike complex system

title: Explainable AI-Driven Smart Home Surveillance for Real-Time Anomaly Detection
authors: Ms.T. Saraswathi, Lakshayprethish M S, Pradeep P
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The primary research objective of this study is to develop an automated, explainable smart surveillance system that reduces human error and enhances the reliability of identifying high-risk activities in real-time. While traditional CCTV systems depend on tedious manual monitoring, this work proposes a multi-modal pipeline that integrates YOLOv8 for detecting high-risk objects like weapons and MediaPipe for human pose estimation to recognize suspicious behaviors such as climbing. A key objective is addressing the “black box” nature of AI by incorporating EigenCAM to provide visual heatmaps and the Gemini API to generate human-readable summaries, ensuring transparency for security operators. Key findings indicate that the proposed system achieves high performance, with the object detection module reaching a mean average precision of 92 % and the behavior recognition module achieving 89 % accuracy for climbing detection. Results from the confusion matrix further validate the model's stability, showing a 0.98 accuracy for identifying balaclavas and 0.93 for knives. This framework demonstrates a marked decrease in response times during simulated intrusions, providing a robust solution for home and facility security.

title: Dynamic Portfolio Allocation in Algorithmic Trading Using Sentiment Analysis
authors: R.D.M. Perera, U. A. Piumi Ishanka
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Market sentiment, as driven by news and online discussion sites is increasingly impacting algorithmic trading systems. In this work, we integrate financial news, social media sentiment gathered from Yahoo news and reddit platform to identify the sentiment related to the market of Bitcoin cryptocurrency. We trained various machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) sentiment classifiers with a dataset of 11,293 Bitcoin-related news headlines extracted from Yahoo Finance and Reddit. In baseline models, Logistic Regression achieved the best performance (accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$71.7 \%, F 1=0.704$</tex>), and among neural network-based ones, BiLSTM (Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory) does so with accuracy of 70.5%. The model FinBERT also achieved improved classification performance (accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$83.7 \%, F 1=0.835$</tex>), which was further confirmed statically by McNemar's statistical test as compared with the other models. In order to increase the interpretability, Shapley Additive explanations (SHAP) and Local Interpretable Model Agnostic Explanations (LIME) were used to examine the token level contributions to the model decisions, which revealed consistency among the patterns in which the positive and negative terms tend to influence sentiment predictions across the model architecture. This study highlights the importance of incorporating explainability and the sentiment aware modeling into the financial prediction workflows and also provide insights that would be needed for future development of transparent and reliable trading strategies.

title: Performance Analysis of Biosensor of Inverted T Stack Gate Dielectric GaAs Based FinFET
authors: Aprajita Kumari, Rajashree Das
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: This paper proposes a new FinFET based biosensor device that has an inverted t -stack based FinFET with GaAs as a fin material. Biomolecules are injected into cavities and nanocavities for sensing purposes. The electrical parameters of the device, such as transfer characteristics, switching ratio, subthreshold swing, and transconductance, have been examined using the Silvaco ATLAS device simulator tool. Using the Silvaco ATLAS device simulator tool, the electrical parameters of the device have been examined, including threshold voltage, transfer characteristics, switching ratio, subthreshold swing, and transconductance. A variety of biomolecules, including uricase, biotin, and keratin, are used to test the biosensor's sensitivity. Primarily, the performance of the proposed FinFET is compared in terms of on current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>), off current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</inf>) and the Subthreshold Swing (SS) and Sensitivity with the conventional Silicon based FinFET.

title: Research on Sports Digital Quality Monitoring Algorithm Based on Deep Learning
authors: Kexin Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a sports digital quality monitoring algorithm that integrates multi-source data and innovative deep learning models. Construct a multi-source data fusion architecture to integrate athlete action videos, physiological sensors and motion trajectory data, design an improved spatiotemporal feature extraction network model, and strengthen feature capture by introducing attention mechanism and residual structure, propose a dynamic weight adaptive optimization algorithm to improve training efficiency. Experiments were conducted on self-built data sets covering basketball, gymnastics, track and field, and the algorithm was compared with traditional CNN, LSTM and existing monitoring algorithms. The results show that the action recognition accuracy of the algorithm in this paper reached 96.8 %, which is 12.3 % higher than the traditional CNN and 15.7 % higher than the LSTM, the average error of physiological index prediction was reduced to 3.2 %, which was 4.1 % lower than the existing algorithm, the positioning error of motion trajectory monitoring was reduced to 0.15 meters, which was 28.6 % higher than the comparison algorithm. The experiment fully verified the effectiveness and superiority of the algorithm in sports digital quality monitoring, and provided an innovative technical solution for sports training and event analysis.

title: A Hybrid Spatial-Frequency Approach for Writer-Specific Handwriting Generation
authors: I.P. Anargha, M Sreeram
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Generating realistic handwriting that mimics a specific writer is a challenging task in document analysis and deep learning. This work proposes a deep learning model that generates personalized handwriting by separating content and style information. A dual-path Style Encoder processes both RGB and Laplacian images to capture a robust style representation for each writer. A Content Encoder extracts character-level features from a standard font representation. These features are fused using Adaptive Instance Normalization (AdaIN) within a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) with a multi-scale discriminator to improve realism. The model is trained and evaluated on the IAM handwriting dataset, producing handwriting images that preserve character content while reflecting individual writer styles. The paper describes the network architecture, loss functions, and training procedure, providing a complete framework for controllable handwriting generation.

title: CNN-Based Automated Diagnosis and Grading of Diabetic Retinopathy from Fundus Images
authors: Sunitha Medichalam, Anurodh Kumar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: One of the general reasons for blindness in people with diabetes is diabetic retinopathy (DR), which can only be effectively treated if caught early. However, in places where medical treatment is scarce, early detection of DR is a major challenge. The aim of this work is to train a neural network to do three things: (1) identify DR, (2) identify it early, and (3) classify its severity. We looked at 3681 fundus photos from the APTOS DR dataset, which ranges from 0 to 4 (No DR to severe DR). A three-layer convolutional neural network used to train three separate models: a binary classifier, an early detector, and a severity classifier. To keep tabs on the models' training and validation accuracy, a 5-fold cross-validation procedure was utilized. Compared to other models in the literature, the binary classifier achieved a validation accuracy of 99.99%. Although it had trouble differentiating between early and severe DR, early detection nevertheless managed a 99.41% accuracy rate. Although the severity classifier was 99.61% accurate in identifying healthy people, it failed to do so when faced with more severe cases, perhaps due to the model's incapacity to distinguish between subtle changes in later DR degrees. The suggested DR detection method outperforms multiple state-of-the-art approaches, according to extensive experimental data measuring in terms of accuracy.

title: Measurement of Electrical Conductivity of Pressurized Gaseous Helium at Cryogenic Temperature
authors: Spencer Martin, William Touza, Arup Kumar Das, Nagaraju Guvvala, Peter Cheetham, Sastry Pamidi
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This paper presents the fabrication of an experimental setup and subsequent measurements on the electrical conductivity of gaseous helium at 77 K and pressures between 0.5-2.0 MPa. The measurements demonstrate that the electrical conductivity of GHe is a function of both temperature and gas density. Measurements were also performed at room temperature to enable comparison between 77 and 300 K with equivalent helium gas density. The data collected shows that the electrical conductivity of helium gas varies up to an order of magnitude between 300 K and 77 K. This variation in turn affects the time required to establish a steady-state DC electric field, which has implications on electric field profiles of DC devices operating at cryogenic temperatures.

title: Semi-AI Approach for RSRP Prediction with Closed-Form Coverage Preservation in Cellular Networks
authors: Passawee Sombunwanich, Watid Phakphisut, Tanun Jaruvitayakovit
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Accurate Reference Signal Received Power (RSRP) prediction is essential for wireless network planning and optimization. Traditional methods rely on drive test data, which are costly and spatially limited. Several studies have presented AI-driven approaches using measurement reports (MRs) from user equipment (UE). These deep neural network (DNNs) were trained using MR and cell configuration data, outperforming the standard propagation model (SPM). However, lightweight models trained using MR data exhibit distortion in coverage due to the limited data in certain areas. To address this, we propose Semi-AI, a transfer learning-based framework that fine-tunes trained models to align predictions with real-world signals. In contrast to typical transfer learning applications, which aim to reduce training time, Semi-AI seeks to preserve the signal coverage generated by the path loss model, maintain high prediction accuracy, and offer flexible coverage planning without extensive drive testing.

title: FITS in Electric Bicycle
authors: Vaishali Katkar, Sneha Pokharkar, Shailesh Shinde
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the importance of health monitoring and preventive care. This paper introduces a Fitness Indication and Tracking System (FITS) integrated into an electric bicycle, enabling real-time monitoring of physiological parameters such as heart rate, oxygen saturation, and body temperature. Sensor data is processed through a Wi-Fi-enabled microcontroller and displayed both on the bicycle's handlebar and a web-based application accessible via mobile devices. Experimental validation confirms the feasibility of the proposed system in combining fitness and healthcare monitoring.

title: A Survey on DQN-Based MAC Protocols for Wireless Networks
authors: Junyoung Park, Woongsoo Na
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The rapid growth of wireless and mobile traffic has heightened the importance of designing efficient Medium Access Control (MAC) protocols capable of allocating limited wireless resources in dense network environments. Recently, deep reinforcement learning techniques—most notably Deep Q-Networks (DQN)—have received significant attention as a means to optimize MAC operations in highly dynamic environments without relying on prior channel models. This paper provides a systematic survey of DQN-based MAC protocol designs, classifying existing studies into three domains: WLAN & Wi-Fi, IoT & WSN, and heterogeneous wireless networks.

title: CS-UNet++: A Cell Segmentation Network Based on Attention Mechanism
authors: Changsheng Zheng, Jiani Sun, Chuanwen Ren, Lihan Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of medical images is a fundamental component in clinical diagnosis, therapeutic planning, and prognosis evaluation. To overcome the shortcomings of conventional approaches in accurately segmenting small targets and handling complex backgrounds, a novel deep learning framework named CSUNet++ is introduced in this study. Built upon the UNet++ architecture, the model introduces a Channel and Spatial Attention Module (CS Module), which dynamically adjusts the channel and spatial feature weights to enhance the model's ability to perceive key features and capture global information. The CS Module is positioned between the encoding layers of UNet++, comprising four modules that effectively improve the expression capability of features at different levels. Experimental results in three publicly available datasets show that CSUNet++ outperforms in metrics like Mean IoU(MIoU) and Validation MIoU(Val_MIoU). In particular, on the TNBC dataset, CS-UNet++ achieved an increase of 8.860 % in MIoU and 4.085 % in Val_MIoU compared to UNet.

title: Feline Emotion Recognition Using Deep Learning: A Comprehensive EfficientNet-B0 Framework for Multi-Class Sentiment Analysis in Domestic Cats
authors: Anirban Bhattacharya, Ram Krishna Verma, Debasish Swapnesh Kumar Nayak, Sanghamitra Panda, Annimesh Sasmal, Swapnajit Sahoo
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Recognizing emotional states in domestic cats presents significant challenges due to the subtle nature of feline facial expressions and behavioral cues. This research introduces a robust deep learning framework for automated feline emotion recognition across seven distinct emotional categories: angry, disgusted, happy, normal, sad, scared, and surprised. Leveraging the EfficientNet-B0 architecture with transfer learning, our model achieves exceptional classification performance with 99.20% training accuracy and 99.67% validation accuracy on a comprehensive dataset of feline facial expressions. The methodology incorporates strategic data partitioning, advanced augmentation techniques, and weighted random sampling to address class imbalance. Additionally, comprehensive statistical validation through parametric and non-parametric tests confirms the robustness and generalizability of our approach. Experimental results demonstrate near-perfect classification metrics across all emotional categories, with precision, recall, and F1-scores of 0.99 or higher. This research advances the field of animal-computer interaction by providing veterinarians, animal behaviorists, and pet owners with an accurate, automated tool for understanding feline emotional states, thereby contributing to improved animal welfare and enhanced human-feline relationships.

title: TSAFN: Temporal Spatial Attention Fusion Network of Alzheimer's Disease Classification in MRI
authors: Janarthan Vijayakumar, Vasavan Mahendrarajah, Luxshi Karunakaran
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: The objective of the proposed study is early and accurate classification of Alzheimer's, as well as Temporal-Spatial Attention Fusion Network (TSAFN) operating on magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) images. Using the publicly available Kaggle Alzheimer's MRI dataset of pictures with four levels of cognitive impairment, such as no impairment, very mild impairment, mild impairment, and moderate impairment, the work applies a group of preprocessing and enhancement strategies to improve the quality of the pictures and diversify the data. The data is divided into training (70), validation (12.5), and testing (17.5) sets. TSAFN is configured to simultaneously segregate both time and space attention features, producing interpretable attention maps that describe AD-influenced brain regions. Training was designed based on the PyTorch template, and the AdamW optimizer, mixed precision computation, and early stopping criteria were used to prevent overfitting. Measurement of performance is done through accuracy, precision, recall, macro-average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> score, confusion matrices, and receiver operating characteristic area under the curve (ROC-AUC). TSAFN produces a test accuracy of 0.9633 on average, a macro F 1 score of 0.9696, and high ROC-AUC values among the diagnostic classes. The interpretability of the attention maps generates clinically relevant data on AD related neuroanatomical alterations, and therefore, the application of the presented system in research and subsequent clinical applications.

title: Design of Fuzzy Sliding Mode Observer with Saturation Function for Sensorless PMSM
authors: Duy Thang Nguyen, Mikhail P. Belov, Duy Khanh Nguyen, Minh Duc Nguyen
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: Speed and position sensors in permanent magnet synchronous motors often pose a risk of measurement errors, leading to torque fluctuations and causing mechanical vibrations. Additionally, they can fail due to high temperatures or motor vibrations, reducing reliability, especially in motors used in aviation applications. This paper focuses on researching sensorless control of permanent magnet synchronous motors for aviation applications, based on a fuzzy sliding mode observer to enhance system flexibility. The designed Fuzzy Sliding Mode Observer is simulated in Matlab/Simulink and the results show that the fuzzy sliding mode observer performs well with smaller estimation error and better noise rejection capabilities than the conventional sliding observer.

title: Research on the Adaptive Mechanism of Ui Visual Interaction Integrating Computer Vision and User Behavior Analysis
authors: Letian Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In the context of the increasing demand for intelligent interaction, this paper focuses on integrating computer vision and user behavior analysis to construct an innovative UI visual interaction adaptive mechanism. By leveraging technologies such as object detection (e.g., YOLO series) and pose estimation (OpenPose) in the field of computer vision to real-time perceive visual information such as users' body movements and gaze focus, and applying user behavior analysis methods, data is collected through log embedding and eye-tracking. Using models such as Hidden Markov Models and Random Forests to mine user operation sequence patterns and interaction habits. Experimental results show that the proposed adaptive mechanism significantly improves the user interaction experience. In specific office software scenarios, the average task completion time is shortened by approximately 25 %, the operation error rate is reduced by about 30 %, and the efficiency of function discovery is increased by over 35 %. This mechanism demonstrates great potential in optimizing UI interaction and enhancing user experience, providing new theoretical and technical support for intelligent interface design.

title: Enhancing Occupational Safety Through Yolov12-Based Ppe and Hazard Detection
authors: Y. Ganesh, Krishnaraj Natarajan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Worker safety in construction and industrial sectors faces significant challenges due to head injuries from lack of safety helmets or poor usage. PPE compliance supervision remains time-consuming, irregular, and error-prone in dynamic environments with varying illumination and crowd density. Traditional monitoring fails to deliver real-time, scalable detection in harsh site conditions. This research develops a YOLOv12-based computer vision system for accurate helmet and head detection using the SHEL5K dataset. Enhanced augmentations including HSV adjustment, mosaic transformation, and random erasing improve feature diversity and model stability. The YOLOv12-Large architecture incorporates transformer-based neck layers and multi-scale feature fusion, enabling detection of small, occluded, and multi-oriented objects with high accuracy and real-time inference speed. Experimental results show 98.1% Precision, 97.6 % Recall, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.3 \% \text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex>, and 165 FPS, outperforming recent YOLO-based and CNN detectors. This scalable solution provides deployable real-time PPE compliance monitoring for manufacturing facilities, significantly enhancing workplace safety through automated detection and alerting.

title: Reinterpreting ECG Signals as Images: A Vision-Centric Approach to Cardiac Health Assessment
authors: Mohammed Ali Shaik, Abhisatwika Reddy Chada, Harshavardhan Thatikonda, Laxmigovini Muthuri, Nithisha Bandameedi, Sreeja Bandi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Automated ECG analysis is important in early identification of cardiac conditions, but the conventional approaches do not have the capacity to address the complexity of the signals and their interpretation. The paper presents a new vision-based deep learning architecture of cardiac health monitoring, converting raw ECG data into image features to allow the application of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to further extract features. To increase the model interpretability, the model uses explainable attention mechanism (XAttn) to emphasize the diagnostic regions in ECG signal which includes QRS complex, P-wave and T-wave. Tested on the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia dataset, where the proposed model reaches an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 2 \%}$</tex> which is better than traditional machine learning algorithms and other deep learning models. The obtained attention maps of the model can be of great use to clinicians, helping them make decisions and promote trust in AIbased diagnostics. The paper shows how advanced vision models can be used together with explainable AI to enhance automated systems of predicting cardiac diseases and clinical support services.

title: Prediction Correction Enabled Power-Angle Stability Analysis for Stochastic Excitations-Disturbing OMIB Power System
authors: Limin Ding, Yongdong Chengdu
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: The proliferation of low-carbon energy sources and inverter-interfaced devices is driving the development of power systems has transitioned towards electrification and cleanliness, resulting in larger and more intense external excitations. This paper focuses on establishing effective stochastic dynamic models and numerical algorithms for power systems under Gaussian and Poisson excitations. In this scheme, the stochastic nature of Gaussian and Poisson excitations is defined, followed by the stochastic dynamic model is formulated. The Heun numerical method, based on prediction-correction concept, is employed to iteratively solve the proposed model. The quantitative analysis of power system stability is conducted to assess the impacts stemming from varying excitation intensities. Numerical simulations are undertaken to validate the correctness and rationality of the stochastic dynamic model for power systems under Gaussian-Poisson excitations and the Heun numerical algorithm. This research endeavors to serve as a comprehensive reference for the modeling and simulation of power systems incorporating stochastic elements, thereby facilitating the secure operation and optimal control of such systems.

title: From Digital Business Strategy to Consumer Loyalty: A Bibliometric Mapping of the Engagement Landscape
authors: Elkhansa Medjedel
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: This study uses bibliometric analysis to map and visualize research on the relationship between digital business strategy and consumer loyalty. The analysis identifies technological adoption, artificial intelligence, and strategic innovation as key drivers of consumer engagement, which in turn lead to outcomes such as brand loyalty, trust, and word of mouth. The study also highlights the common use of methodologies such as PLS-SEM to validate these relationships. It offers a systematic overview for academics to identify core and emerging themes, as well as a validated framework for practitioners to design strategies that foster loyalty.

title: Automatic segmentation of temporal lobe epilepsy resection Cavity based on deep learning model
authors: Lin Yang, Fengyi Yang, Zuhong Lu, Yu Sun
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of postoperative resection cavities on magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is essential for the quantitative evaluation of surgical efficacy in patients undergoing anterior temporal lobe resection (ATLR) for drug-resistant temporal lobe epilepsy (TLE), yet conventional manual delineation remains subjective, labor-intensive, and poorly scalable, thereby limiting large-scale outcome analyses and the clinical translation of imaging-derived biomarkers. To address these limitations, this study proposes a three-dimensional attention-enhanced residual U-Net specifically designed to accommodate the anatomical complexity and signal heterogeneity of postoperative temporal lobe MRI. The model was trained on a large cohort of 297 ATLR cases from the publicly available IDEAS dataset, comprising high-resolution postoperative T1-weighted images with expert-validated resection cavity annotations, and the training pipeline incorporated standardized preprocessing, intensity harmonization, and spatially adaptive data augmentation to enhance robustness across diverse postoperative morphologies. Independent evaluation on a held-out test cohort of 45 subjects demonstrated strong segmentation performance, achieving a mean Dice similarity coefficient of 0.873 ± 0.041 and a mean Jaccard index of 0.781 ± 0.052, with precise boundary delineation reflected by a 95th-percentile Hausdorff distance of 3.62 ± 1.14 mm and an average surface distance of 1.42 ± 0.36 mm. Volumetric fidelity was maintained, yielding an absolute volume deviation of 6.8 ± 3.4%, thereby enabling reliable extraction of clinically meaningful resection metrics. Compared with baseline architectures, the proposed model improved Dice performance by 6.5% relative to a standard 3D U-Net and by 3.2% relative to a residual U-Net, while reducing boundary error by 1.5–2.0 mm, surpassing performance levels reported in prior studies based on smaller cohorts. In summary, the proposed framework provides a robust, scalable, and reproducible tool for automated cavity segmentation, which can facilitate large-scale surgical outcome research and be integrated into AI-assisted neurosurgical workflows.

title: Review of AI-Based Lane and Obstacle Detection Techniques for Embedded Automotive Systems
authors: Aarti Pawar, Rutuja Patil, Sarvesh Patil, Pradumna Bedagkar, Hrishikesh Mehta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Autonomous driving and Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) rely heavily on accurate lane detection, vehicle recognition, and obstacle avoidance to ensure safety and reliability. Traditional computer vision techniques, though widely used, often fail under poor illumination, occlusion, and degraded markings. Deep learning models, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs), attention-based architectures, and YOLO-based frameworks, have advanced detection accuracy and adaptability, while GANs improve robustness under challenging conditions. Hybrid approaches combining lightweight CNNs with classical vision methods demonstrate practical trade-offs between efficiency and robustness for embedded deployment. However, deploying computationally intensive models on automotive-grade processors remains a key challenge due to constraints on latency, power, and functional safety. This paper reviews recent advances AI-driven lane and obstacle detection methods, assesses their suitability for real-time embedded systems, and highlights emerging hardware platforms such as NVIDIA DRIVE Orin, DRIVE Thor, and Qualcomm Snapdragon Ride that aim to bridge the gap between research and real-world deployment.

title: Archives of Low-Illumination Image Enhancement Methods Based on Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Wencheng Wang, Xiaowei Lan, Guodong Zheng, Chenghao Liu, Ruiguo Wang, Weikai He, Dongfeng Li, Muwei Jian
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Images taken under poor lighting conditions typically exhibit suboptimal quality, characterized by low brightness and diminished contrast. The enhancement of such images remains a pivotal area of investigation within image processing. This paper provides a systematic review of recent progress in deep learning-based methods for low-light image enhancement. Particular emphasis is placed on categorizing and discussing the diverse approaches that utilize Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs). It delves into their core principles and model characteristics, and lists commonly used benchmark datasets in this field. Based on this, the paper summarizes the core challenges currently faced by lowillumination image enhancement technology and provides an outlook on future research trends, with the hope of providing insights for the continuous development of this field.

title: Design of Intelligent Shopping Cart Control System
authors: Haoxian Deng, Hanhong Tan, Zhihai Lin, Mujian Huang, Haiting Han, Xiaolin Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: Addressing the deficiencies of traditional supermarket shopping carts in terms of checkout efficiency, user experience, and data management, this study designs an intelligent shopping cart control system based on the STM32F103C8T6 microcontroller. The system adopts a collaborative control strategy combining distributed hardware architecture with hierarchical software design, achieving functions such as intelligent obstacle avoidance, product recognition, autonomous checkout, and human-computer interaction. The hardware architecture adopts a modular design concept, with the STM32F103C8T6 microcontroller as the control hub, achieving multi-task collaborative scheduling through hierarchical real-time operation logic, enabling real-time display of product prices, generation of shopping lists, and automated checkout. Experiments show that the system can significantly shorten user checkout waiting times, reduce supermarket operating costs, and provide data support for consumer behavior analysis and inventory optimization. The research results provide a low-cost, high-reliability solution for the intelligent transformation of the retail industry, with practical application value and market potential.

title: A Dual-Branch Network for Classifying Pulmonary Disease Using Flow-Volume Loops
authors: Jaeun Kim, Hongjun Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Although flow-volume loops from spirometry are fundamental for diagnosing pulmonary diseases, distinguishing subtle morphological patterns remains challenging—even for experienced clinicians, especially when it comes to differentiating between obstructive and restrictive types. In this paper, a deep learning framework is proposed to automatically classify lung diseases based on these loops. As its core methodology, this work presents a dual-branch architecture that decomposes the complex four-class classification task—comprising normal, obstructive, restrictive, and mixed types—into two independent binary classifiers: one for detecting obstructive types and the other for restrictive types. Additionally, to ensure robust performance despite severe class imbalance in real-world medical data, a generative data augmentation strategy operating within the learned latent space of minority classes is employed. By modeling the underlying data manifold with a Convolutional Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and synthesizing high-fidelity samples, the training distribution is effectively rebalanced. This synergistic combination of a dual-branch structure and generative data augmentation significantly enhances the classifier's generalization performance, demonstrated by an increase of 0.026 in accuracy and 0.126 in F1-score over baseline models, marking a substantial advancement towards robust and scalable automated screening for pulmonary diseases.

title: Dimensionality and randomness
authors: George Barmpalias, Xiaoyan Zhang
abstract: None
