title: Cyberattack Detection and Classification using New Custom Filter Design and Explainable-Deep Learning in Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Matthew Oinonen, Walid G. Morsi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: This paper presents a novel explainable Intrusion Detection System (IDS), for the detection and classification of attacks in cyber-physical systems (CPS). The presented approach uses only cyber data to detect attacks at the cyber layer before they reach the physical layer, thus preventing physical damage to the grid's assets. To enhance the attack's feature representation in both time and frequency simultaneously, a set of new wavelet filters are designed and integrated with the dual-tree complex wavelet transform (DT-CWT). This enables the vectorized representation of attack features instead of only the scalar representation as in the case of real transforms, which results in information loss. Furthermore, to address the lack of interpretability of existing IDS methods, this work integrates eXplainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to highlight the important regions in the time-frequency spectrograms that influence the attack classifications' decisions. This makes the model used transparent and more trustworthy. Using the XAI explanations, a new feature analysis technique, namely the XAI-Multiple Attribute Analysis Technique (XAI-MAAT), is proposed to numerically quantify the important features used for classification. The proposed approach has been tested in real-time using OPAL-RT on two IEEE test systems and the results have shown that it can achieve a detection accuracy of up to 99.54%, an increase of up to 60.93% over existing methods. The novel XAI-MAAT technique reveals that the time between sequential messages and the message status numbers are the most important features to classify the attacks at the cyber layer.

title: Deep-Q-Learning-Based Proportional-Integral Event-Triggered Fuzzy Output Control of Variable Speed AGVs Via Proportional-Integral Observer
authors: Shen Yan, Xinyu Hu, Ju H. Park
abstract: This paper focuses on the T-S fuzzy output path tracking control issue for variable speed autonomous ground vehicles (VSAGVs) by using state observer and event-triggered mechanism. The model of VSAGVs with nonlinear dynamics and parameter uncertainty is represented by the uncertain T-S fuzzy system. To enhance the estimation performance of the unknown system state, a fuzzy proportional- integral observer containing an extra integral term of estimation error is designed. To reduce the network communication burden, a novel deep-Q-learning-based proportional- integral event-triggered mechanism by adding the integration of the normal triggering rule and introducing an optimization strategy of the triggering threshold. Compared with the classical normal and dynamic event-triggered mechanisms, a larger inter-event time can be generated by the proposed proportional- integral event-triggered mechanism. In addition, the deep-Q-learning-based optimization strategy is able to dynamically regulate the triggering rate according to the historical triggering performance and control performance. Some sufficient conditions are derived to solve the fuzzy observer and controller gains and the triggering matrix such that the VSAGV is asymptotically stable with given <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> level. Finally, the superiorities of the addressed approach are confirmed through some simulation results.

title: IGDMRec: Behavior Conditioned Item Graph Diffusion for Multimodal Recommendation
authors: Ziyuan Guo, Jie Guo, Zhenghao Chen, Bin Song, Fei Richard Yu
abstract: Multimodal recommender systems (MRSs) are critical for various online platforms, offering users more accurate personalized recommendations by incorporating multimodal information of items. Structure-based MRSs have achieved state-of-the-art performance by constructing semantic item graphs, which explicitly model relationships between items based on modality feature similarity. However, such semantic item graphs are often noisy due to 1) inherent noise in multimodal information and 2) misalignment between item semantics and user-item co-occurrence relationships, which introduces false links and leads to suboptimal recommendations. To address this challenge, we propose Item Graph Diffusion for Multimodal Recommendation (IGDMRec), a novel method that leverages a diffusion model with classifier-free guidance to denoise the semantic item graph by integrating user behavioral information. Specifically, IGDMRec introduces a Behavior-conditioned Graph Diffusion (BGD) module, incorporating interaction data as conditioning information to guide the denoising of the semantic item graph. Additionally, a Conditional Denoising Network (CD-Net) is designed to implement the denoising process with manageable complexity. Finally, we propose a contrastive representation augmentation scheme that leverages both the denoised item graph and the original item graph to enhance item representations. Extensive experiments on four real-world datasets demonstrate the superiority of IGDMRec over competitive baselines, with robustness analysis validating its denoising capability and ablation studies verifying the effectiveness of its key components.

title: Chemical-Disease-Gene Association Prediction based on Pretraining-Prompt-Finetuning Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network for Drug Discovery
authors: Xi Zeng, Jing-Wen Cai, Pei-Yuan Lai, Qing-Yun Dai, Chang-Dong Wang, Min Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Chemical-Disease-Gene (CDG) association prediction-encompassing Chemical-Disease (CD), Disease-Gene (DG), and Chemical-Gene (CG) interactions-is a cornerstone of drug discovery, as it underpins target identification and drug repurposing. While these tasks are inherently synergistic, existing methods often address them in isolation, failing to capture shared heterogeneous semantics and cross-task dependencies. We hypothesize that a unified pretraining framework can learn transferable biomedical semantics across CDG tasks, with task-specific prompt tuning enabling efficient adaptive fine-tuning without full retraining. To test this hypothesis, we propose the Pretraining-Prompt-Finetuning Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network (PPF-HGNN), a two-stage framework built on heterogeneous graph neural networks (GNNs) and prompt learning. Specifically, we construct a CDG heterogeneous graph, employ parameter-free metapath-guided message passing for high-order semantic capture, and optimize generalizable representations via a dual self-supervised objective (association prediction + feature reconstruction) during pretraining. For downstream tasks, task-specific learnable prompt vectors are introduced to adapt frozen pretrained representations to CD, CG and DG association prediction tasks via additive fusion, preserving core semantics while injecting task-specific biases. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate PPF-HGNN's state-of-the-art performance: AUC of 0.9633 (CD), 0.9939 (CG), and 0.9390 (DG), with F1-scores of 0.9157, 0.9668, and 0.8955 respectively-substantially outperforming six existing baselines. This work validates the pretrain-prompt-finetune paradigm for multi-task biomedical association prediction, providing a robust AI-driven tool to accelerate translational research and decipher complex CDG relationships. The source code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/ike-zengxi/PPF-HGNN</uri>.

title: Machine Learning Models for Analyzing FBG Pressure Sensor Data in Monitoring Leak in Water Pipeline
authors: Hossam Youssef, Matthias Fabian, Mounib Khanafer, Sumsun Naher, Kenneth TV Grattan, Tong Sun
abstract: Aging water and sewage pipelines require effective monitoring as leakage and burst incidents pose environmental, economic, and public safety risks. Many existing studies rely on laboratory or simulated data from electrical sensors installed in harsh sewer environments, which require regular cleaning and calibration. The novelty of this work lies in presenting a systematic, real-world evaluation of machine learning models for pipeline leak detection using fiber Bragg grating-based pressure sensors deployed in an operational pipeline test facility for the first time. The models evaluated include supervised models, such as Random Forest and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), and unsupervised models, such as Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and LSTM Autoencoder. These models are compared under identical experimental conditions and were evaluated under both serial (single sensor) and parallel (multi-sensor) configurations, using sliding windows of varying lengths. Weighted precision and F1-scores were used to address strong class imbalance across leak sizes. Results show that XGBoost achieved the best overall performance, reaching 77% precision and 52% F1-score at a 45-point window length. The results obtained are better than those reported in previous work, most of which rely on simulations or laboratory datasets and critically do not evaluate models under real operational pipeline conditions, as is done here. The findings demonstrate the robustness of supervised XGBoost when applied to small, noisy real-world datasets and they highlight the feasibility of integrating ML-based FBG sensors into cloud-enabled pipeline monitoring systems.

title: Standalone Augmented Reality Neuronavigation System for Accurate Pulse Delivery in Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation
authors: Wally L. Niu, Christopher C. Cline, Bruce L. Daniel, Christoph Leuze
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) is a noninvasive brain stimulation technique that uses magnetic pulses to safely modulate neural activity in specific brain regions, with particular use in the treatment of major depressive disorder. However, effective treatment protocols require multiple sessions over time and accurate targeting. Current neuronavigation systems can improve pulse delivery compared to standard scalp and measuring tape methods, but are costly and have time-intensive setups for recurring sessions. Wepresent an augmented reality neuronavigation system (AR-NS) that overcomes these limitations. The AR-NS is functionally similar to other neuronavigation systems but can operate entirely within a head-mounted display. Unlike traditional 2D navigation systems that require mentally fusing screen information with real-world actions, the AR interface allows operators to perform the procedure with reduced cognitive and hand-eye coordination demands. We measured the functional targeting accuracy of the AR-NS and the Localite TMSNavigator, a commercial neuronavigation system, using a TMS phantom we developed embedded with Hall effect sensors at four different sites. We determined the coil placement for each site that maximized the sensor response to a magnetic pulse using co-registered X-ray computed tomography and structured light scanner scans. A MagVenture C-B60 coil was placed and fired at each site 30 times in a randomized order according to each neuronavigation system. We measured the resulting magnetic pulse amplitudes. A non-inferiority test with a 5 mT margin and 97.5% confidence intervals indicated that the AR-NS demonstrated similar functional targeting accuracy as the Localite TMS Navigator across all but one stimulation site, indicating that augmented reality neuronavigation systems may offer more accessible delivery of TMS stimulation without sacrificing functional accuracy compared to current systems.

title: Dictionary Multi-Modal Temporal Graph Learning
authors: Meng Liu, Ke Liang, Miaomiao Li, Xueling Zhu, Xinwang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Temporal graph learning focuses on graph deep learning in real-world dynamic scenarios, which uses interaction sequence instead of adjacency matrix to observe the graph dynamic changes more microscopically from the perspective of time evolution. However, current temporal graph methods only focus on extra dynamic information, ignoring the large amount of multi-modal information contained in the real world. These information can reflect the rich changes in the real world from different perspectives. Ignoring them means that temporal graph learning still lacks the ability to restore and mine more complex real-world data. We argue that the main challenges causing the above phenomenon in temporal graph learning are the lack of multi-modal architecture and public multi-modal datasets. To solve the above challenges, we propose ModalTGL, which enhances the computational efficiency of the model in complex dynamic scenarios by introducing the dictionary graph network, and achieves multi-modal fusion by embedding tuning. In addition, we also discuss the effects of different time encoding functions on dynamic information preservation. At the data level, we build several multi-modal temporal graph datasets from different areas, and compare with multiple SOTA methods on these datasets. The experimental results verify the effectiveness of the ModalTGL method, achieving the performance improvement of up to 18.48%. Code and data can be obtained from <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/MGitHubL/ModalTGL</uri>.

title: KICGPTv2: Large Language Model with Knowledge in Context for Knowledge Graph Completion_supp1-3674507.pdf
authors: Yu Zhang
abstract: Knowledge Graph Completion (KGC) is an essential task aimed at mitigating the issue of incompleteness in knowledge graphs, thereby enhancing their utility for various downstream applications. Existing KGC models predominantly fall into two categories: structure-based and semantic-based approaches. Structure-based methods often encounter challenges with long-tail entities due to the scarcity of structural information and imbalanced entity distributions. Conversely, semantic-based methods, while addressing those limitations, necessitate extensive training of language models and specific finetuning for each knowledge graph, thus constraining their practical efficiency. To alleviate those limitations in both approaches, in this paper, we propose KICGPTv2, an innovative framework that synergizes a large language model (LLM) with traditional KGC methods. This integration effectively mitigates the long-tail entity problem without incurring significant additional training overhead. Central to the KICGPTv2 model is a novel in-context learning strategy, termed Knowledge Prompt, which encodes structural knowledge into demonstrations to effectively guide the LLM. Comprehensive evaluations on various KGC tasks, including link prediction, relation prediction, and triple classification, underscore the efficacy of the KICGPTv2 model, highlighting its ability to achieve competitive performance with reduced training demands and without the need for finetuning

title: Enhanced Approaches for Anomaly Detection in Streaming Data: Coupling Gaussian Distributions with Space Trees and Adaptive AutoEncoders
authors: Ercan Gunbilek, Zuleyha Akusta Dagdeviren, Hasan Bulut
abstract: Processing, analyzing and continuously monitoring big data streams in real time is crucial for the security and efficiency of organizations and individuals. While machine learning and deep learning have greatly improved anomaly detection, they also have drawbacks, such as reliance on offline data and poor performance, especially with scarce or fluctuating data. To address this problem, we propose two enhanced methods for anomaly detection in streaming data: Gaussian Space Trees (GSTrees) and the Gaussian Weighted Adwin AutoEncoder (GWAAE). The effectiveness of these methods was evaluated on real datasets (ECG5000, Credit Card Fraud Detection, SMTP) processed as streaming data. For the ECG5000 dataset, GSTrees performed excellently on all metrics, consistently achieving over 94%, while GWAAE excelled with an ROC-AUC of over 89% and over 84% on all other metrics. For the SMTP dataset, the ROC-AUC of both proposed methods is above 80%. GSTrees and GWAAE successfully identified anomalies with a recall of over 82% and a ROC-AUC of over 94% and maintained this success with remarkably low false negative and false positive rates in the Credit Card Fraud Detection dataset. These results emphasize the robustness and applicability of GSTrees and GWAAE for real-time anomaly detection in dynamic environments.

title: A Lightweight GBM Based Approach for Dynamic QoS Management in Embedded Systems
authors: Rukai Liu, Cheng Li, Liang Qian, Ruifeng Liu, Rui Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Network traffic identification and classification play a crucial role in network management, security monitoring, and quality of service assurance. With the rapid development of emerging applications such as virtual reality, remote control, and embodied intelligence, network traffic characteristics have become increasingly bursty, short lived, and encrypted. Traditional statistical methods based on predefined traffic patterns exhibit limited effectiveness in identifying short flows and bursty traffic, resulting in significant statistical bias and low matching efficiency in real time QoS provisioning. To address this issue, this paper proposes a flow level traffic classification method based on an improved nDPI tool and a LightGBM model. The proposed approach performs single packet feature extraction and feature aggregation, and adopts a stratified cross validation strategy for model training and evaluation, enabling effective discrimination among multiple application types, including video streaming, instant messaging, online gaming, and web browsing. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a classification accuracy of 0.83 under encrypted traffic scenarios, while its balanced F1 score effectively enhances dynamic identification performance across different traffic categories. Furthermore, the lightweight model architecture allows efficient deployment on embedded platforms such as routers, providing a feasible solution for real time traffic awareness and fine grained network management.

title: Data-Driven Forecasting for Drug Control Through Predictive Modeling of Heroin Cases in Sri Lanka
authors: A.H.M.T.C. Bakmeedeniya, G.G.M. Wimalasena, P.A.D.L. Peramune, J.P.T.R. Wijayawickrama, P.H.P. Panapitiya, U.W.S. Premathilaka, K.A.D.C. Kodithuwakku
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Heroin use has become a major public health and social concern in Sri Lanka, with reported cases rising steadily over the past decade. The Government Analyst's Department (GAD) serves as the central authority for recording and analyzing heroin-related cases, but limited resources have resulted in backlogs, delaying timely reporting and analysis. This study analyzes ten years of heroin case data and applies time series forecasting techniques to predict the total number of heroin cases for the next two years. Classical statistical models Seasonal Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (SARIMA), deep learning approach called Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and a hybrid model by combining SARIMA and LSTM was employed to capture both linear and nonlinear patterns in the data. The hybrid model achieved the lowest forecasting error, with a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of approximately 7.92, outperforming both individual SARIMA and LSTM models. The study highlights temporal trends, changes in case distribution across weight categories, and seasonal patterns, offering actionable insights for policymakers and law enforcement agencies. The forecasts support effective resource allocation, capacity planning, and the development of data-driven strategies to prevent and reduce heroin misuse in Sri Lanka. Future work could further enhance predictive accuracy by incorporating socioeconomic, geographic, and enforcement-related variables.

title: Multi Class ECG Arrhythmia Detection Using Scalogram and Deep Learning
authors: Chaitali Asole, Samiksha Gade, Ishwari Belorkar, Aditya Turankar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Arrhythmia or heart rhythm abnormalities are terms used to define a normal heartbeat. Most of the focus of Cardiovascular health today often deals with arrhythmia [15]. The irregularities we will be checking for in the ECG are ventricular tachy-cardiac and atrial fibrillation. Other terms for normal sinus rhythm irregularities, early atrial contraction, and early ventricular contraction are Vtach, Afib, PAC, PVC and NSA, respectively [20]. In this work, we present a multi-class arrhythmia detection method using CWT, which transforms 1-D ECG visualizations into 2-D time-frequency scalograms [5] [7]. We utilize ResNet-50 deep learning models for image classification with the aim of determining the types of arrhythmias [20]. Labeled recordings from the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database were utilized for training and testing. Gradient-weighted Class activation mapping (Grad-CAM) was integrated into our system to provide visualizations of model decisions that are most salient, hence improving interpretability and making the system clinically reliable. Since the tool operates offline, it can be reliably employed in constrained resource settings or remote areas [6]. This is a reliable, sustainable, and accessible means of arrhythmia detection that enhances patient outcomes and healthcare efficiency. The proposed ResNet-50 model achieved an overall accuracy of 83%, with an average precision of 0.82, recall of 0.81, and F1-score.

title: A Purechain-Protected Ai-Based Misbehaviour Detection System for Iov
authors: Md Mehedi Hasan Somrat, Esmot Ara Tuli, Mahbuba Iasmin Sumona, Jae-Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) introduces significant security challenges, especially in detecting and mitigating misbehaviour by malicious or compromised vehicles in real-time. This paper presents a robust misbehaviour detection system safeguarded by PureChain blockchain technology. Leveraging PureChain's novel Proof-of-Authority and Association <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{PoA}^{2}$</tex>) consensus mechanism, the system achieves lowlatency, high-throughput, and tamper-proof logging of detected events. At its core, an LSTM-based deep learning model dynamically learns temporal patterns from vehicular data streams, enabling accurate identification of various malicious activities such as spoofing, message tampering, and unauthorized transmissions. The seamless integration of the blockchain layer ensures the immutable and trustworthy recording of all detection alerts, enhancing data integrity and accountability across the IoV network. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate the system's high performance, with detection accuracy exceeding 96%, a perfect logging success rate, and an average blockchain transaction latency of just 1.26 seconds at a throughput of 14.56 transactions per second. This combined approach pushes the boundaries of secure and scalable IoV security, providing a timely, efficient, and reliable solution adaptable to evolving vehicular environments.

title: A Comprehensive Authenticated Dataset of Brahmi Script Characters for Recognition
authors: Tushar B. Kute, Premanand P. Ghadekar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: The Brahmi script is among the most ancient and historically prominent systems of writing in South Asia, with applications that warrant corresponding modern Indic scripts. Digitally recognizing Brahmi inscriptions holds the key to epigraphical studies, historical research, and cultural preservation. The efforts remained always undermined for the absence of a greater and authentic dataset. To meet this requirement, a new dataset is introduced, comprising 348 different classes of characters from the Brahmi script. The dataset contains a total of 7880 character images, each such image extracted from letter carvings over stone at seven salient archaeological sites in India: Naneghat Caves, Junnar Caves, Bhaje Caves, Karle Caves, Bedse Caves, Nashik Caves, and Pitalkhora Caves. The dataset design was an iterative and exhaustive process of image acquisition, segmentation of characters, and applications of preprocessing to increase visibility and recognizability of characters therein. The usability and robustness of the dataset were tested using four different deep learning models: a custom Deep Convolutional Neural Network (Deep CNN), InceptionNet, ResNet50, and XceptionNet. The best recognition accuracy of 98.07% was attained with a loss of 0.0958 using ResNet50, which shows the adequacy and quality of the dataset for the training of advanced machine learning models. This will help researchers by providing a valuable resource and accelerating the development of Brahmi script character recognition.

title: Study and Implementation of a Partial Discharge Detection System: An Optimized Front-End Circuit for Continuous Monitoring
authors: Rafael Gomes, Mateus Giesbrecht, Fabiano Fruett
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: The reliability of rotating electric machines is critical for power generation, emphasizing the need for continuous monitoring to prevent unexpected failures. Insulation degradation, responsible for approximately 56% of failures in large generators, often results from partial discharges (PDs), which require early detection to mitigate long-term damage. This paper presents the design and implementation of an optimized front-end circuit for PD detection, tailored for continuous monitoring of electrical insulation degradation. The system employs a high-frequency transformer and coupling capacitors as PD sensors, with a signal conditioning circuit optimized for noise rejection and high-sensitivity detection. Additionally, the detection threshold can be adjusted, allowing for refined PD signal analysis and improved adaptability to different insulation conditions. The current implementation enables real-time observation of PD occurrences via an oscilloscope, while future developments will integrate a microcontroller for phase-resolved partial discharge (PRPD) analysis and remote alarm transmission. Operating within a frequency range of up to 1 GHz and powered by two 12V batteries, the system has been tested using a simulated PD circuit inside a Faraday cage. The experimental results confirm the system's ability to detect PD signals effectively. This work provides a cost-effective and optimized solution for continuous PD monitoring, contributing to improved condition-based maintenance strategies and reducing the dependency on costly, operator-dependent equipment.

title: Estimation of Metal Concentration from Hyperspectral Data Using a Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Tianhang Gao, Zongwen Bai, Meili Zhou, Zhijiang Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Hyperspectral remote sensing is a potent technique for monitoring heavy metal pollution at a regional scale. However, conventional methods based on physical models or shallow machine learning often lack accuracy and generalization due to their inability to capture the complex non-linear relationships between spectral features and metal concentrations. This paper proposes a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based method for accurate estimation of heavy metal concentrations. We design a lightweight regression model incorporating separable convolutions and skip connections to adaptively extract deep spectral features and establish a highly non-linear mapping from spectral to concentration space. In a case study on a farmland in Yan'an, the proposed model was evaluated using hyperspectral data and measured concentrations of As, Cd, Mn, Pb, and Zn. Experimental results demonstrate that our CNN significantly outperforms traditional methods, including SVR, RFR, and PLSR, achieving higher coefficients of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) for all metals. This study confirms the superiority of CNNs in hyperspectral metal concentration estimation, offering a promising approach for large-scale, high-precision environmental monitoring.

title: Research on Collaborative Optimization Scheduling of Energy Storage and New Energy Based on Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Yujun Li, Dawei Xie, Haifeng Ye, Zili Xu, Zhi Li, Dongsheng Dai
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: The purpose of this study is to solve the inherent volatility of new energy power generation in power grid, the uncertainty of load forecasting and the operational challenges brought by complex electricity price mechanism. To this end, this study proposes a collaborative optimization scheduling method for power grid energy storage and new energy based on particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm. By accurately optimizing the capacity configuration and access location of the energy storage system (ESS), the overall comprehensive operating cost of the power grid is minimized. The optimization model comprehensively considers multi-dimensional economic factors such as power purchase cost, network loss cost, new energy operation cost, ESS charge and discharge loss and ESS annual investment cost. The PSO algorithm intelligently determines the optimal ESS capacity and access node through iterative optimization, effectively copes with the uncertainty of new energy output, and uses the flexibility of ESS to realize the value of energy time shift. This study uses the IEEE 33-node distribution system for simulation verification. The results show that the proposed collaborative optimization scheduling scheme can significantly reduce the operation cost of power grid, effectively improve the new energy consumption rate and reduce carbon emissions. This study provides theoretical and practical guidance for the integrated planning and economic operation of ESS and new energy in power grid, and is of great significance to promote the green and low-carbon transformation of energy system.

title: ProDoKE: An LLM-Guided Prompt Chaining with Domain Knowledge for Depression Detection
authors: Jie Chen, Lang Chen, Shu Zhao, Chenchu Xu
abstract: With the proliferation of social media, assessing depression risk through user-generated posts has emerged as a critical and prominent research direction. However, existing methods fail to capture clinically relevant linguistic markers due to ungrounded semantic representations in non-expert models. Therefore, this paper proposes a model, ProDoKE, which leverages prompt chaining integrated with domain knowledge to facilitate in-depth extraction and interpretation of clinically significant linguistic and semantic cues from social media posts. ProDoKE comprises two key modules, consisting of a prompt chaining enhancing module and a capsule fusion module. Following the collection of risky posts, the prompt chaining module systematically integrates domain-specific knowledge with Large Language Models (LLMs). This structured reasoning process guides the LLM to identify relevant clinical symptoms and then infer their underlying emotional states, resulting in a richer and more accurate interpretation. Furthermore, the capsule fusion module incorporates contrastive learning to optimize the alignment of emotional and symptom semantics, while using capsule networks to fortify the model's robustness. Evaluations on the eRisk2017 and eRisk2018 datasets show that ProDoKE achieves state-of-the-art performance, significantly outperforming previous baseline models in F1 scores.

title: Evolutionary Optimization for Low-Voltage, Wide-Temperature Range SRAM Cell Designs
authors: Rafael Knust, Thiago Brito, Fernanda D.V.R. Oliveira, Fabián Olivera
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Variability in nanometer CMOS manufacturing processes, wide operating temperature ranges, and continuous supply voltage scaling in low-power applications present significant challenges for static random-access memory (SRAM) designs, as they substantially degrade the stability of read and write operations. In this paper, we present a variability-aware evolutionary algorithm (EA)-based approach for low-voltage, widetemperature range SRAM cell designs. The proposed framework integrates Monte Carlo simulations into the EA fitness evaluation to enhance robustness against manufacturing process variations. To validate the effectiveness of the methodology, design examples are implemented in IHP 130 nm and TSMC 65 nm CMOS technologies. Extensive Monte Carlo simulations of the resulting SRAM cells are performed, covering both traditional performance margins and the cell operation in a 8 kb SRAM macro, using supply voltage of 0.6 V, temperature range from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> and operation frequency of 40 MHz.

title: SocraTeach: AI Powered Personalized Mentor for Socratic Learnings
authors: Junaidali Shaikh, Varad Vaidya, Ayush Munot, Isha Ghorpade, Anuradha Yenkikar, Nilesh Sable
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: SocraTeach is a novel AI-driven educational platform aimed at altering user approaches towards learning through Socratic pedagogy. SocraTeach guides learners through interaction rather than simply providing information or knowledge. The learner progresses through a uniquely crafted puzzling sequence of questions. A custom cyclone of user answer evaluation, utilizing NLP and Machine Learning, sits at the center. Advanced Answer Checking Algorithms (ACA) measure the thoughtfulness, scope, and originality of the provided answers. Each user assessment analysis is optimized in such a way that further prompts and clarifications encourage deeper thinking and stretching recursive elaboration to refine previous answers. What marks SocraTeach as different is its approach to education, focused on dialogue, simulating a student-educator interaction where the learner takes center stage as an active participant in the process. SocraTeach allows learners from schools and self-learners at any formal education stage to foster curiosity, intellect, and engagement. The use of dynamic knowledge graphs to provide real-time feedback holds tremendous potential to revolutionize SocraTeach.

title: A Lightweight Vehicle Detection Model for Complex Traffic Scenarios
authors: Feifan Liu, Yuhu Shi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the development of autonomous driving, the detection of pedestrians and vehicles in complex scenes has been challenged by the problems of misdetection, omission and high model complexity. In order to overcome these problems, a novel lightweight target detection algorithm PCA-YOLO is proposed based on YOLO11 in this study. Firstly, partial convolution is integrated into the backbone network, and the C3k2 module is improved into the P_C3k2 structure, which can efficiently extract spatial features while reducing the number of model parameters and computational overhead. Secondly, a lightweight upsampling operator are added to the neck structure to better capture target information; meanwhile, the adaptive spatial feature fusion module is embedded into the object detector head, which effectively filters out conflicting information. Finally, experimental outcomes on the KITTI dataset show that the model PCA-YOLO can attain higher detection accuracy when compared against YOLO11n and other baseline algorithms, with varying degrees of model size, parameter count, and complexity reduction.

title: Spiking neural P system with voltage-gated ion channels
authors: Jiaqi Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: The spiking neural P (SN P ) system is a third-generation spiking neural network that enables distributed and parallel computing and possesses strong biological interpretability. Although various SN P variants have been developed and applied in different fields, relying solely on synaptic connections to transmit spikes remains biologically inadequate. Therefore, drawing inspiration from the mechanism by which voltagegated ion channels in neurons can switch between different channels based on the positive or negative charges received, this study proposes an SN P system equipped with voltage-gated ion channels, referred to as the SNVI P system.

title: TuckerDiff: Fusing Tucker Decompositi on in Diffusion Model for High-Fidelity Radar Point Cloud Generation
authors: Shuang Sun, Tingkai Hu, Zixuan Lu, Zhen Luo, Hailing Xiong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) radar provides robust all-weather sensing capabilities, but often produces sparse and noisy point clouds. Raw radar signals are high-dimensional and challenging to embed effectively, posing difficulties for learning-based approaches. To address this, we propose TuckerDiff, a framework that directly utilizes raw radar data to guide a Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM). The method integrates two complementary features: a core tensor based on Tucker decomposition for efficient dimensionality reduction, and a 2D Direction-of-Arrival (2D-DOA) map incorporating physical priors. By directly leveraging raw radar data that retains richer information, TuckerDiff generates dense, LiDAR-like 3D point clouds, achieving state-of-the-art fidelity and demonstrating strong generalization in real-world scenarios.

title: Multi Variant Deep Transfer Learning Analytics Model for Improved Health Care Solution Using Big Data
authors: P. Malathi, R. Parameswari
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: The recent surge in healthcare big data has presented new possibilities of intelligent disease prediction, but currently used analytics models fail to handle heterogeneous data, feature imbalance, and are not generalized across clinical domains. This paper tries to resolve these issues with a new Multi Variant Deep Transfer Analytics Model (DVDTAM) of better healthcare decision support. The aim is to improve the accuracy of disease prediction and reduce the rates of false classification. DVDTAM has a Deep Variant Feature Normalizer that uses the Feature Covariance Value to stabilize heterogeneous distributions of features after which a Multi Variant Deep Transformer with transfer learning is used to model complex clinical dependencies. Final disease classification is done using a Multi Factor Disease Absorption Weight mechanism. As it is experimentally assessed on the MIMIC-III dataset, DVDTAM reaches a high accuracy of 96.8 % and is better than state-of-the-art models by up to 2.5 percent, and the false rate is decreased to 0.05. These findings support the usefulness of the suggested structure of scalable and dependable healthcare analytics.

title: A Modified RRT-Connect-Based Approach to Motion Planning for Manipulators
authors: Ying Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: Aimed at resolving the problems of low operational efficiency, unsatisfactory path quality, and poor adaptability exhibited by conventional robotic arm path planning methods when applied in obstacle-dense complex scenarios, this paper proposes a hybrid value point constraint robotic arm path planning algorithm (CA-RRT-Connect) based on a bidirectional adaptive RRT-Connect algorithm. First, via an analysis of environmental topological features, a target-biased sampling strategy is employed in free space to minimize redundant exploration. Combined with B-spline curve interpolation technology, the proposed method ensures that the path accurately traverses predefined value points to enhance task accuracy. Subsequently, path length, smoothness, and joint motion constraints are incorporated into a multi-objective optimization framework. Finally, both the precision of the proposed algorithm and the practicality of the established model are confirmed. This paper employs multiple experiments, and the outcomes reveal that the suggested approach achieves performance levels comparable to or exceeding those of multiple conventional algorithms.

title: Complexity vs. Prevalence Assessment for Prediction Steps for VVenC Encoding Profiles
authors: Rodrigo Feldens, Sergio Bampi, Felipe Sampaio
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of the prediction tools implemented in the open-source VVenC encoder, focusing on their computational cost, prevalence, and overall efficiency. The experiments target both intra- and inter-frame prediction modules across the five VVenC coding profiles (faster to slower), aiming to capture the encoder's behavior across different scenarios. An efficiency metric was defined relating the prevalence to the measured computational complexity of each prediction tool within the video encoding process. Our results show that inter-frame prediction tools achieve significantly higher efficiency (up to 4.47) compared to intra-frame tools (up to 0.92), primarily due to their higher prevalence and similar computational costs. Thus, VVC novel intra-frame prediction tools present promising opportunities for optimization schemes. From another perspective, the inter-frame prediction's efficiency drops in more complex profiles and at higher resolutions, as heavier implementations of internal prediction tools are enabled, indicating a trade-off between complexity and performance. Looking at inter-frame prediction steps, Integer Motion Estimation (ME) plays an important role and benefits from hardware acceleration, while Fractional ME presents opportunities for adaptive optimizations. Affine ME, despite its computational cost, shows limited prevalence and emerges as the most promising candidate for complexity reduction and hardware optimizations.

title: A Predictive Framework for Fault Vulnerabilities in Edge Hardware Accelerators
authors: Ernesto Cristopher Villegas Castillo, Josie E. Rodriguez Condia, Juan-David Guerrero-Balaguera, Felipe Augusto da Silva, Michael Glaß
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Modern Hardware accelerators, such as Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), are increasingly being deployed in edge computing safety-critical applications, including autonomous and semi-autonomous automotive systems. However, evaluating fault vulnerabilities and reliability remains highly resource-intensive and time-consuming when using simulation-based fault injection (FI) campaigns. This challenge is amplified by the architectural complexity of modern devices and the huge number of workload scenarios, making traditional FI approaches impractical. This work introduces and evaluates a high-level modeling framework for the early fault propagation analysis and reliability estimation of hardware accelerators for edge computing. Our framework uses Colored Generalized Stochastic Petri Nets (CGSPNs) to map the most crucial features of a design into a model that allows the prediction of fault vulnerabilities in a design. The framework aims to support designers in estimating key reliability metrics, such as fault propagation rates, before low-level implementation (e.g., gate-level), thereby facilitating informed architectural decisions and the development of more robust systems.

title: Anomaly Detection Methods in Social Networks
authors: Rutika Arun Khedkar, Deepti Manoj Singh, Chhaya Gosavi, Aparna Hajare
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Social networks are widely developed and extensively used platforms that facilitate global communication and interaction. Popular sites like Twitter, Facebook, and others have revolutionized the way people connect and share information. However, these networks are often susceptible to misuse by malicious actors who exploit them to breach user privacy, spread spam, or cause harm through various sophisticated techniques. This review examines two advanced approaches to detecting anomalies in social networks: Dual Variational Autoencoder (VAE) with Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) and graphbased anomaly detection using structural graph metrics. The Dual VAE-GAN method combines deep learning techniques to capture nonlinear patterns in network structures and node attributes, ensuring robust anomaly detection with high accuracy on benchmark datasets. In contrast, the graph-based approach uses egonet analysis and metrics like Betweenness Centrality and Brokerage Value to identify nodes deviating from expected structural patterns. Both methods are evaluated for their statistical foundations, detection capabilities, and performance results, showcasing their effectiveness in addressing social network threats while highlighting their respective strengths and limitations.

title: Edge-Accelerated Monocular Depth Estimation: A Quantized U-Net on Kria KV260 for Real-Time Interior Scenarios
authors: Nicolás Urbano Pintos, Emanuel Trabes, Carlos Valderrama, Alaeddine Aajouj, Héctor Lacomi
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Real-time monocular depth estimation (MDE) is pivotal for edge applications like autonomous indoor navigation and AR/VR, yet achieving it on power-constrained devices remains a significant challenge. This work presents an optimized, INT8-quantized U-Net model specifically designed for the Xilinx Kria KV260 FPGA. Trained on the NYUDepthV2 dataset and compiled using the Vitis AI toolchain, our implementation achieves a throughput of 41.03 FPS with a latency of 24.3 ms and an RMSE of 0.9553 m. This represents a 2.27× speedup over a desktop-grade Intel i7-11700K CPU while maintaining comparable precision. Our contributions include: (i) a FPGAtailored U-Net (ii) an efficient quantization-to-DPU workflow, and (iii) a demonstration of the real-time MDE indoors on the KV260.

title: Nonlinear Control of a Rigid Multi-Degree-of-Freedom Electromechanical Plant
authors: Anton A. Kuznetsov, Nguyen Duy Khanh, Taniana L. Rusyaeva, Ekaterina Ilatovskaya
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: This paper discusses the synthesis of a control system for a four-DOF (degree-of-freedom) electromechanical manipulation arm that takes into account the electromagnetic dynamics of the manipulator's electric drives and three-circuit subordinate control. It is shown that traditional PI or PID controllers in three-circuit subordinate control do not provide the required control quality for the complex electromechanical system under consideration. To solve this problem, a hybrid algorithm is proposed in which nonlinear PID control is combined with a two-step integrator bypass procedure, a modification of the classic three–step integrator bypass (backstepping) method. To overcome the difficulties of analytical calculation of the virtual control derivative, it is proposed to use a first-order low-pass filter at the first step of synthesis. The simulation demonstrates the advantages of the synthesized nonlinear controller in comparison with the traditional subordinate control.

title: A Short Review of Buffer Overflow Attack and its Prevention
authors: Ahmed Faraz
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: The unallocated random access memory locations provides loop holes to gain the access control of the system to the attacker. This loop hole in the software of computer system provides a big vulnerability to the attacker who gains the access of the system by exploiting Buffer Overflow Vulnerability. The Buffer Overflow attack is hybrid in nature, both the hardware and software are compromised by the attacker by gaining the access control of the system. As Internet of Things technology has been evolved and computer communication networks are more linked with it, the Buffer Overflow attack has become more security concern. In all the types of Buffer Overflow attack launched on the machines, the operating system level control is gained by the attacker which can harm the security of all the machines and devices connected to the compromised machines through Internet of Things. Securing Stack Pointer, Program Counter, Processor Status Word registers in safe locations in memory at the hardware level can prevent Buffer Overflow attack.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of a Compact Dual-Band Patch Antenna for IoT-Enabled Smart Wireless Applications
authors: Rajavel V, Mahaprajin M, Akash T, Mohana Muthu Bala Kumar D
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) smart wireless applications that need seamless multi-standard communication, a small dual-band microstrip patch antenna is introduced. The antenna uses a benzene-shaped slot with a partial ground plane on a FR-4 substrate (relative permittivity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=4.4$</tex>, thickness <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=1.6$</tex> mm) for miniaturization and multi-band resonance. Supporting WLAN (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.4 / 5 \text{GHz}$</tex>), WiMAX, LTE (Bands 3/7/40), and sub-6 GHz 5G services, the suggested architecture distributes two wide working bands spanning 1.79-3.00 GHz (43.37%) and 3.64-5.28 GHz(36.77%). The radiation characteristics of the antenna remain consistent across both bands, and it displays a peak gain of 4.8 dB. The design evolution, parametric evaluation, and performance comparison with state-of-the-art antennas confirm the superiority with respect to the proposed antenna's compactness, bandwidth, and ease of fabrication. Its small form factor, consistent gain, and ability to operate in two bands make the suggested antenna an excellent choice for incorporation into mobile IoT and smart wireless communication devices.

title: The Future of Cloud Data Engineering: Multi-Tenant, Multi-Region Pipelines Leveraging LLM-Powered Data Governance
authors: Balaji Krishnan, Anjali Thaneeru, Rajesh Lingam, Sashi Kiran Kaata
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Cloud data engineering is evolving rapidly toward architectures that unify multi-tenant scalability, cross-region data replication, and intelligent governance. Large Language Models (LLMs) are now being integrated into data governance frameworks to automate policy enforcement, lineage validation, and compliance management at scale. This paper provides a detailed study of the emerging paradigm of LLM-powered data governance within multi-tenant, multi-region pipelines. It surveys current frameworks, analyzes architectural strategies, compares implementation models, and presents practical insights into performance, compliance, and future research directions. The findings highlight how AI-augmented governance mechanisms are redefining the future of resilient, scalable, and ethically aligned cloud data ecosystems.

title: Hybrid Quantum-Attention Fusion Network for Stroke Lesion Segmentation and Explainable Prognosis Using CT–MRI Integration
authors: M.C. Revathi, T.Saju Raj
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Early detection, precise segmentation and reliable prognosis prediction are plays a vital importance in acute stroke management. Traditional unimodal systems often fail to obtain the intrinsic relationship between structural and pathologic cues from different imaging modalities. This paper presents a Hybrid Quantum-Attention Multimodal Fusion Network for (HQAMFN) the synergistic integration of CT and MRI data toward comprehensive stroke lesion analysis. The HQAMFN architecture is proposed in this work, consisting of a Hybrid Attention-Driven Multimodal Fusion Module (HAFM) integrated with a Quantum-Enhanced Vision Transformer (QEVT) for end-to-end stroke segmentation, classification and prognosis. The HAFM utilizes modality-specific encoders and cross-attention fusion to capture rich spatial-contextual representations while QEVT leverages quantum-inspired embedding to model sophisticated nonlinear dependencies and global feature correlations. Furthermore, a Dual-Stage Refinement Mechanism enhances the accuracy of lesion boundaries, and an Explainable Prognostic Module fuses imaging biomarkers with clinical features to make interpretable outcome predictions. Experimental evaluations on the benchmark datasets confirm that HQAMFN attains the highest performance, with a Dice coefficient of 0.958, IoU of 0.924, an accuracy of 98.9%, and an AUC of 0.992, which outperforms other state-of-the-art multimodal fusion approaches.

title: Cooperative Multi-LiDAR Deployment Using Decentralized Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hyeong Jun Park, Chang Mook Kang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Optimal placement of multiple LiDAR sensors is critical for autonomous driving, robotics, and smart city applications. This study compares three multi-agent coordination strategies—a local-search Greedy baseline, Independent Q-Learning (IQL), and Deep Q-Network (DQN)—for maximizing coverage in discrete, obstacle-rich environments. Experiments with 1-5 LiDAR sensors across varying obstacle densities show that IQL achieves the highest average coverage (89.5%) with low variance, while DQN exhibits irregular scaling and high sensitivity to environment changes. Our reward shaping strategy targets 30–40% sensor overlap to support inter-sensor coordination requirements. We additionally include a zero-shot evaluation for DQN as a transfer probe; the results suggest that generalization remains challenging in this discrete coordination setting. Results indicate that computationally efficient algorithms such as IQL can outperform complex deep learning approaches in robustness, scalability, and computational efficiency. These findings provide practical guidelines for designing multi-sensor systems in dynamic environments such as autonomous vehicles and urban infrastructure.

title: Research on Excitation Waveform Classification and Core Loss Prediction Based on Random Forest Model
authors: Yaoteng Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: To address the issues of excitation waveform classification and core loss prediction in electronic devices, this paper first extracts image features from magnetic flux density data, including mean value, standard deviation, zero-crossing rate, and peak count, and employs a random forest model for waveform classification training. Subsequently, by comparing Gaussian process regression, convolutional neural networks, BP neural networks, and random forest models, the random forest model is selected for data feature analysis and model construction. The model's performance is further enhanced through parameter optimization and out-of-bag (OOB) error validation. The results show that the random forest-based waveform classification model achieves a prediction accuracy of 100 %. In core loss prediction, the coefficients of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{R}^{2}\right)$</tex> for the training and testing sets are 0.9858 and 0.9557, respectively, demonstrating excellent performance. The study indicates that the random forest model excels in excitation waveform classification and core loss prediction, offering high reliability and generalization capabilities, thereby providing strong support for the optimized design of electronic devices.

title: Comparative Analysis of Silkworm Incubator Based on Arduino UNO and Spartan-3E
authors: R Kamaladevi, A Varshini, Elakkiya, K.R Sathish Kumar, D Senthoor Seruvan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: In sericulture field, farmers are constantly struggling to maintain proper environment condition for silkworms growing to produce efficient quantity of silks. In this research work we propose microcontroller and Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) based approaches to maintain proper environment condition to cultivate healthy silkworm. The ideal temperature of the silkworm incubator is $27^{\circ} \mathrm{c}-30^{\circ} \mathrm{c}$, the light intensity has to be less than 312lux and the humidity has to be between $65 \%$ to $85 \%$. In both the systems, sensors such as temperature, humidity, light and actuators such as cooling fan, Light Emitting Diode (LED) light have been interfaced to maintain the ideal environmental conditions inside the silkworm incubators. A comparative analysis of these two approaches also carried out using Arduino UNO microcontroller and Spartan3E Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) in terms of speed of execution and hardware complexity. Image processing based healthy silkworm classification is also implemented to improve the yield of sericulture field.

title: Method for Determining the Polarization of LFM Probing Pulses of Airborne Radars in an Autocorrelation Receiver of Information and Control Systems
authors: The Quan Trong, Nguyen Trong Nhan, Tran Huu Nghi
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: The article presents a method for determining the polarization of LFM probing pulses emitted by airborne radars using an autocorrelation receiver of information and control systems. An analysis of the features of autocorrelation reception of LFM probing pulses from airborne radars is carried. The necessity of additional procedures at the autocorrelator output to enable the determination of polarization of these LFM pulses is substantiated. A functional diagram of an autocorrelation receiver of information and control systems is developed, which facilitates the identification of the polarization of LFM probing pulses from airborne radars.

title: Rate Maximization for RIS-Aided System Based on Step Size Prediction Networks
authors: Zhen Luo, Jingyu Huang, Wei Dong, Menggang Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Joint optimization of beamforming and RIS phase shifts is a key to improving the rate of wireless communications. Addressing the gradient ascent algorithm widely adopted for this problem, this paper proposes a core improvement: converting its fixed step-size parameter into a dynamically controlled variable driven by a neural network. We design and train a deep neural network (DNN) that can intelligently determine the optimal step size for each iteration based on real-time states during the optimization process (e.g., gradient information). This paradigm of leveraging machine learning to "control" traditional optimization algorithms effectively resolves the challenges of difficult step-size selection and slow convergence in the gradient ascent method. We apply this dynamic step-size control strategy to both the beamforming and phase shift optimization subproblems. Simulation results demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms conventional approaches significantly in terms of convergence speed and final performance.

title: Uncovering Hidden Biases in Hydropower: Why Detailed Inflow Data is Crucial for Energy System Optimization Models
authors: Felix C. A. Auer, Robert Gaugl, Thomas Klatzer, Diego A. Tejada-Arango, Sonja Wogrin
publish: 2026 Open Source Modelling and Simulation of Energy Systems (OSMSES)
abstract: Hydropower plays a critical role in balancing highly renewable power systems, yet its representation in energy system optimization models is often constrained by limited availability of high-resolution inflow data. In practice, aggregated inflow time series are frequently used to reduce data requirements, potentially introducing hidden modeling biases. This paper analyzes the impact of inflow aggregation on generation expansion and operation decisions in energy system optimization models. Using an adapted NREL-118 bus test system and the open-source Low-carbon Expansion Generation Optimization (LEGO) model, we compare planning outcomes based on hourly inflow data against daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly aggregations. By computing expost regret with respect to hourly inflow operation, we quantify the cost and investment distortions caused by aggregation.Our results show that inflow aggregation leads to substantial cost increases and misallocation of generation capacity investments once hydropower becomes a significant share of the energy mix, as coarse aggregations systematically underestimate inflow variability. These findings demonstrate that high-resolution hydropower inflow data is essential for robust energy system planning and highlight the need for openly available, standardized inflow time series to support planning of future energy systems.

title: Agentic AI in Network Slicing
authors: Anshul Anilkumar Mundakatil, Anjan Phani Raj Narla, Akshil Anilkumar Mundakatil, Sai Srinivas Muriki, Ahmed Hambaba
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)
abstract: Network slicing has emerged as a pivotal innovation in modern telecommunications, enabling the creation of tailored virtual networks within shared physical infrastructures. By dynamically allocating resources to meet the unique demands of various applications, network slicing fosters both flexibility and efficiency. However, traditional methods for slice orchestration and resource allocation struggle with the complexity of multi-domain environments and rapidly evolving service requirements. To address these challenges, we propose a novel framework leveraging Large Language Models and multi-agent systems for automated slice management within Open Radio Access Network architectures. The system translates user intent into precise technical requirements through LLMs, ensuring seamless alignment between application needs and O-RAN configurations. These requirements are deployed in an Open RAN environment, where real-time performance monitoring and adaptive resource management occur through collaborative learning, enabling continuous system improvement by sharing insights between different components. Our approach incorporates Graph RAG for slice prediction, enhancing the system's ability to model complex relationships and dependencies within the network. We also present a standardscompliant O-RAN testing environment to validate the system's effectiveness in addressing the complexities of modern network slicing. This work underscores the transformative potential of combining LLMs with multi-agent systems to redefine resource allocation and orchestration in next-generation networks.

title: An LLM-Assisted Fuzz Testing Framework for Unmanned System Software
authors: Yupeng Zhang, Qing Wei, Shaoxun Liu, Yankun Xu, Haoyang Chen, Jinfeng Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The booming application of unmanned system has led the software vulnerabilities of these systems becoming increasingly prominent. Fuzz testing is an effective approach to detecting software vulnerabilities particularly during the software development phase. However, the requirement for manually written fuzz drivers renders it a resource-intensive and expert-dependent task, which hampers the widespread application of fuzzing. To address these issues, we proposed an automated fuzz testing framework based on large language models (LLMs). First, we employ an intelligent code parsing module to circumvent the context length limitations of LLMs, enabling efficient processing of target project code. Second, we engineer a structured prompt template to guide LLMs in generating high-quality fuzz driver code. Subsequently, to mitigate the inherent propensity of LLMs to produce flawed code, we integrate a feedback-based correction mechanism. Finally, we have deployed an automated fuzzing platform and applied it to several typical software components for unmanned systems. The system performs notably well on relatively simple fuzz testing targets and alleviates the manual workload required for fuzz testing complex targets. Ablation experiments confirm that the feedback-based correction mechanism effectively enhances the performance of LLMs in fuzz driver generation. This framework provides a viable approach to automated fuzz testing and is expected to contribute to vulnerability analysis in unmanned systems.

title: IoT-Based Real-Time Gesture Talk System for Hand Sign Recognition in Speech and Hearing Impairments Using Cloud Computing
authors: Divyaa N, Boopathi Raja G, Devi Priya J, Dharani S K, Dharshan K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: People with speech or hearing impairments often face challenges by a lack of realtime, cost effective and dependable systems for converting sign language to speech. Most existing methods of communicating with the non-speaking require either wearable devices with expensive hardware or processing done offline. All of these make communicating in real time in actual situations very difficult. It significantly limits the types of communication users can achieve therefore reducing how and if users will continue to be able to access these types of assistance and other related services. To solve these issues, the proposed system creates an intelligent assistance communications framework which interprets human hand motion, and translates them into meaningful audio messages with respect to hand motion detection and Hand Gesture Classification (HGC). The system includes the use of advanced computer vision techniques including MediaPipe and OpenCV. It is used to accurately identify and classify each of the 45 hand gestures; enhanced accuracy in the classification process will lead to capable and robust real time application under changing conditions of lighting, background, and hand orientation. It then sends the recognised hand gestures to an ESP32 microcontroller through the MQTT protocol, which will in turn trigger a DFPlayer Mini module to play the relevant pre-recorded voice response to each of the recognised hand gestures. The integration of cloud computing technology provides secure storage of data, as well as monitoring, analytic and continuous optimization, via remote accessibility. The quantitative evaluation results show that the gesture recognition average accuracy is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$95 \%-97 \%$</tex>. System latency from end to end is below 300 ms, therefore providing the framework with real-time assistive communication capabilities. The result shows that how AI based IoT architecture significantly enhances assistive technologies and improves the interaction among humans and machines.

title: The Use of Virtual Reality for Training Automated Control System Operators on Digital Twins
authors: Margarita V. Inozemtseva, Mikhail Y. Serebryakov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: Traditional training for Industrial Control System operators, often based on 2D SCADA interfaces, is insufficient for preparing personnel for high-stakes emergency scenarios. These methods fail to replicate the necessary psychological immersion and stress, leading to critical human errors during real-world incidents. This paper analyzes the application of immersive Virtual Reality as an advanced Human-Machine Interface for operator training, coupled with a high-fidelity Digital Twin of the technological process. This «Operator-in-the-Loop» architecture enables the simulation to dynamically and accurately respond to operator actions in real-time. The evaluation focuses on metrics such as reaction time and error rates during simulated abnormal conditions. The analysis demonstrates that VR training on Digital Twins significantly enhances operator readiness. By creating a safe, high-fidelity environment that simulates both process physics and psychological stress, this method improves situational awareness, resulting in faster and more accurate operator responses to rare but critical events.

title: Compressor-Based Adaptive Approximate Multiplier with MFA and Clock Gating for Reduced Latency in Neural Network Applications
authors: SMD.Fayaz Basha, K. Riyazuddin, Vadla Ram Charan, Kalva Sucharitha, Pilli Vasantha Lakshmi, G.V Jeevan Kumar Reddy
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The field of approximate computing is rapidly evolving, particularly in neural network applications that can tolerate some error. I'm presenting a novel multiplier design in this letter. To reduce errors in each partial product column, it employs an algorithm that selects the best approximate compressors from both old and new sets. In order to speed up the MFA, I used pipelining instead of the typical full adders. A simple error-correcting module is built into the design to correct any errors resulting from this approximation. When I tested this 8-bit multiplier, it outperformed other recent designs and exact multipliers in terms of power consumption and power-delay product (PDP). Additionally, I tested it on image processing and neural network tasks, and it performed well, demonstrating its effectiveness and dependability.

title: Algorithmic Midwifery: Benchmarking Machine Learning Models for Intelligent Fetal State Classification
authors: Niranjani K, Senthil K, Ayyapan G
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The fetal condition is of great significance in the prenatal care and minimizing the negative birth complications. This paper presents a baseline model, which is known as Algorithmic Midwifery, that implements classical machine learning algorithms to classify the intelligent fetus state through the Cardiotocography (CTG) signals. To classify fetal conditions into the normal, suspect, and pathological classes, three popular models, including K-Nearest Neighbours (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Random Forest (RF), were used. Very few preprocessing steps such as median-based imputation of missing values, scaling of the features, and label encoding were used so that the original data integrity was not lost. Grids of hyperparameters were optimized with grid search and 5-fold cross-validation, and the performance of the model was evaluated with the accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score and the analysis of the confusion matrix. SVM had the best performance and reported an overall accuracy of 92.4 which exhibited balanced accuracy-recall behavior. RF offered an important amount of feature importance scores, which are easier to interpret, whereas KNN was used as a simple and efficient baseline reference. Misclassifications that are present between Suspect and Pathological classifications underline the necessity to refine them further. All in all, this framework provides a solid classical-machine-learning baseline of fetal state classification and is a solid reference point of future deep learning methods, with practical implications of creating clinically-reliable decision-support systems in practice.

title: INTELLI-BDC: Intelligent Bidirectional DC—DC Converter for Optimized EV Battery Management
authors: J. Surendiran, S. Gomathi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper introduces INTELLI-BDC, a smart bidirectional DC to DC converter that will be used to operate the advanced electric vehicle (EV) battery management systems. The architecture that is proposed will combine adaptive Fuzzy PID control, parameter optimization through Genetic Algorithm (GA) and fault-tolerant switching to achieve a high level of efficiency, fast transient response, and operational resilience. INTELLI-BDC is able to dynamically modulate PWM duty cycles based on load changes, battery state-of-charge and changes in temperature, unlike traditional converters, which operate on a fixed control set, and the converters also reduce conduction and switching losses by using a synchronous buck-boost topology with SiC MOSFETs. The optimal parameters controlled by the GA are the switching frequency, the duty cycle and the control gains in order to produce a low level of ripple, less power losses, and the shortest settling time possible. A machine-learning unit inside it estimates the malfunction of a MOSFET or sensor and recovers itself automatically by redundancy switching, so that it can continue to operate with minimal disruption. Simulations of MATLAB/Simulink and hardware checks on DSPs show 97.8 per cent conversion efficiency, 63 per cent transient overshoot, 66 per cent output ripple and less than 10 ms fault recovery. These findings confirm that INTELLI-BDC is a strong, efficient, and intelligent product to manage the next generation EV battery energy in a more intelligent way.

title: Advanced Feature Engineering and Machine Learning for Simultaneous Cyber Attack Detection in Smart Digital Substations
authors: Aybüke Şenel, Mohammed Al-Hubaishi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper introduces a machine learning solution for detecting simultaneous cyber attacks in smart digital substations, and we developed more than 50 features from GOOSE heartbeat and MAC address data. We evaluated 14 classifiers, including Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, and deep learning models such as CNN, LSTM, and a hybrid CNN-LSTM, because we utilized the IEEE DataPort Smart Digital Substation Simultaneous Attack Detection Dataset. Ensemble methods achieved an accuracy of over 99% with F1-scores exceeding 0.98 for multi-class attack detection, and thus the hybrid CNN-LSTM achieved an accuracy of 99.72% and performed best in detecting replay, masquerade, and denial-of-service attacks. Feature importance analysis revealed that GOOSE heartbeat patterns, MAC source sequence patterns, and temporal anomalies were the most important features, therefore the proposed approach combines rich, protocol-aware feature engineering with powerful ensemble and deep learning models to provide a practical, real-time framework for securing critical infrastructure.

title: GraphDistill-TI: A Temporal-Structural-Aware Data Distillation Framework for Fine-tuning Threat Intelligence LLMs
authors: Kaihua Zheng, Jianan Huang, Jinsheng Sun, Weiwei Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The application of Large Language Models (LLMs) to Cyber Threat Intelligence (TI) is promising in automating and scaling threat analysis. However, their performance is often constrained by the immense volume and complex dynamics of TI data. Current data distillation techniques for fine-tuning, which primarily focus on local sample quality, largely ignore the global structural relationships and temporal evolution inherent in threat campaigns. This oversight leads to inefficient data utilization with an insufficient grasp of the macro-context, particularly the time-sensitive nature of threats. In this context, we propose GraphDistill-TI, a novel data distillation framework, which preserves the temporal and structural integrity of TI data for efficient LLM fine-tuning. GraphDistill-TI first constructs a temporal-structural threat graph, integrating semantic associations, explicit timestamps, and key entity labels from raw intelligence reports. Then, it employs a Graph Attention Autoencoder (GAT-GAE) to learn structural representations for each report. These representations facilitate a K-Means clustering that divides the data into structurally coherent groups, and in turn, guide a novel weighted greedy selection algorithm. This algorithm strategically selects a data subset by optimizing intra-cluster representativeness, inter-cluster diversity, and fidelity to a predefined Expected Temporal Distribution (ETD). Experiments on a real-world dataset containing 16,493 TI reports demonstrate that fine-tuning GPT-2 on a 10% subset distilled by GraphDistill-TI not only surpasses a full-dataset baseline in a threat intelligence question-answering task but also outperforms random 10% sampling by approximately 6.0%. Furthermore, ablation studies reveal and quantify a critical trade-off between structural purity measured by clustering metrics and temporal fidelity measured by Kullback-Leibler divergence. By strategically prioritizing temporal fidelity, GraphDistill-TI enhances model performance on dynamic and time-sensitive tasks. Through delicately balancing the priorities, GraphDistill-TI establishes a new paradigm for domain-aware data distillation and paves the way for more efficient and context-aware LLMs in cybersecurity applications.

title: Review of Cyber Security Challenges & Solutions in Cloud Networks
authors: Hiba A. Tarish, Mohammed Q. Mohammed
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: With the development of modern communications and the increasing spread of telecommunications and internet networks, as well as the exchange of information and data transfer, the urgent need arose to meet the requirements of cybersecurity against intrusive attacks and various breaches of cloud networks. Cloud networks serve as a communication area between users and all units of telecommunications networks, providing a suitable communication environment to facilitate data transfer.
