Therefore, achieving security in cloud networks has become one of the essential priorities to ensure the continuity of data flow and the privacy of information among users. In this study, a comprehensive scientific review will be conducted on the latest types of cyber attacks targeting cloud networks, along with their analysis and classification. This review also includes an overview of the most important and efficient techniques for detecting, repelling, analyzing, and classifying cyber-attacks according to the type of attacks and their targeting of cloud network layers. This review includes various statistics about the most prominent and recent articles that addressed the key methods and techniques for detecting and repelling attacks, along with the types of these breaches and their targeting levels. Finally, the intended benefit of this review is to generate a comprehensive idea about the information security requirements for cloud networks and to identify the most prominent types of cyber-attacks and the corresponding efficient and accurate detection and response techniques, in addition to understanding the targeted areas and layers in cloud networks.

title: CRESS 2025 Contributor Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)

title: Resilient Reinforcement of CPSs against Sparse Sensor Attacks by Prior Information Decomposition
authors: Zheyi Zhao, Xuqiang Lei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The system resilience against sensor attacks is critical for secure state estimation of Cyber-Physical Systems (CPSs). Specifically, the upper limit on the number of compromised sensors that can be tolerated is a critical factor in determining the CPSs defensive performance. This paper proposes a method to enhance system resilience by leveraging double congruent information. A unique vector decomposition is introduced that equivalently transforms an initial state with dual congruent characteristics into two more sparse vectors. Based on this decomposition, an optimization model for secure state estimation is proposed, along with its feasible necessary and sufficient conditions. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that, under specific observability conditions, the proposed approach enables efficient state estimation through convex optimization. Numerical simulations on a diffusion process validate the effectiveness of dual congruent information in resilience enhancement. Results show significantly higher estimation success rates compared to traditional sparse optimization methods under identical attack conditions.

title: MindMorph: An Integrated Framework for Myers-Briggs Personality Classification Through Multi-Source Data Fusion and Advanced Ensemble Techniques
authors: Aishwarya A, Anisha Mehta Jain, Belde Sowjanya Laxmi, Suma L
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The Myers-Briggs Type Indicator framework serves as a prominent instrument for psychological profiling across various professional and academic contexts. Contemporary self-assessment methodologies face challenges in authentically representing behavioral manifestations while remaining vulnerable to systematic reporting biases. The advent of ubiquitous digital platforms has transformed textual discourse into a rich substrate for computational personality analysis. We introduce MindMorph, a novel classification architecture that harmonizes three heterogeneous information streams: structured scenario responses, unrestricted written narratives, and online social discourse. Our framework implements dimension-wise binary discrimination coupled with sophisticated ensemble algorithms to maximize inferential reliability. Through strategic amalgamation of diverse input channels with rigorous ensemble methodologies, our system demonstrates substantial advancement in computational personality profiling, registering an aggregate classification accuracy of 80.36 % across the complete MBTI dimensional space.

title: TEE-Based Privacy-Preserving Arbitration for Dynamic Heterogeneous Redundancy in Industrial Control Systems
authors: Yukun Niu, Lei He, Xiaopeng Han, Yu Xie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Dynamic heterogeneous redundancy (DHR) enhances industrial control system security through multiple structurally diverse executors achieving consensus via arbitration, but exposes executor outputs to confidentiality attacks in cloud-edge architectures. Existing homomorphic encryption-based schemes achieve privacy protection at the cost of substantial computational overhead (31–99 ms arbitration latency), limiting applicability to time-critical scenarios, while adaptive approaches sacrifice privacy for efficiency. This paper presents a trusted execution environment (TEE) based architecture leveraging Intel SGX to eliminate the latency-privacy trade-off by processing outputs in plaintext within hardware-protected enclaves. We design a mutual authentication protocol based on SGX remote attestation and ECDH key exchange, and implement credibility-weighted arbitration algorithms supporting exact and inexact matching with autonomous edge-local scheduling. Experimental evaluation demonstrates 151× speedup compared to best-case homomorphic encryption schemes (0.21 ms vs. 31.86 ms) while maintaining strong privacy protection and unconditional arbitration autonomy, enabling deployment for sub-10 millisecond real-time industrial control loops.

title: PhysiKKT-Net: A KKT-based Neural Network with Guaranteed Compliance for Real-Time Power Flow Analysis
authors: Qingbin Jiang, Jianglin Feng, Zijun Wang, Haichuan Zhang, Siqi Meng, Ziyue Jia, Yujie Yang, Yang Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The large-scale integration of renewable energy sources introduces unprecedented volatility and expands the attack surface for cyber-attacks, creating an urgent need for massive-scale, near real-time power flow simulations to safeguard grid security. To counter this and address the broader computational and trustworthiness challenges, this paper proposes the Physics-KKT Network (PhysiKKT-Net), a novel method that embeds linear physical constraints directly into the neural network architecture. PhysiKKT-Net accelerates power flow analysis while guaranteeing the physical consistency of its predictions, serving as a resilient tool for attack verification. Specifically, a softplus activation function enforces non-negativity, and a projection layer derived from the Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions ensures strict compliance with Kirchhoff's laws. Validated in the Machine Learning for Physical Simulation Challenge, our method achieves significant acceleration while reducing violation rates of these fundamental physical laws to zero. By providing a trustworthy, physics-grounded baseline, PhysiKKT-Net empowers operators to rapidly distinguish between legitimate grid states and malicious data injections, providing a robust solution for secure grid operation, particularly under the out-of-distribution (OOD) conditions inherent to volatile, renewable-heavy power systems.

title: Section 2. Information Security
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)

title: Minimizing Total Cost for Power Grid Energy Distribution
authors: Hitasha Singh, Nishant Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This document discusses the need for optimization of Power Grid in terms of the total cost and how we achieve the goal, what factors determine the total power generation and power transmission. The problem is formulated as a constrained data optimization model, closely related to optimal power flow, which could be further solved by various optimization techniques. The operation of the modern power grid faces the challenge of managing the growing demand for power, economical operation, and technical restrictions on the equipment used in the power grid. Through the application of these optimization techniques, it is possible for grid managers and analysts to analyze the impact of demand and boundary constraints on both the security of the system and the efficiency of the power system. Today, with the increasing demand of energy, it has become crucial to think about being energy efficient such that everyone and the future generations could benefit from utilizing power correctly.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Decentralised Architecture for Biomedical Data Governance
authors: Devendra Singh, Shiv Ashish Dhondiyal, Ritik Bisht
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The increasing utilization of digital health systems that utilize EMR and EHR systems, computerized imaging, telemedicine, and Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) technologies, has, in turn, created a near-continuous stream of biomedical data. Although this data stream enables more effective and efficient clinical service utilization, and supports more data-driven clinical decisions, it has created challenges related to data governance, ease of integration, and other clinical service accessibility. Integrative data systems, such as EHR systems, must store sensitive biomedical data on centralized servers that, in turn, can easily be subject to unauthorized access and theft through a cyber attack on a singular, exposed access point. Further, biomedical data are residually dispersed in systems and/or data silos, such as hospitals, clinical laboratories, and diagnostic imaging centers, with no safeguards to protect from the loss of data verifiability, authenticity, and auditability. No blockchain technologies are currently integrated into existing data sharing systems. This study explicitly seeks to determine if incorporating data blocks into a persistent interventional data sharing structure mitigates the risk of loss of data integrity and access, as minimal access to data blocks is synchronously given to the entire peer network. This ongoing research seeks to determine if a hybrid blockchain-InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) structure, integrated with smart contract-based access control, enables confident computation of data, within the emergent health systems, as demonstrated in the compilation of validated datasets from the biomedical field.

title: MsGA: Gestational Age Estimation with Multi-plane Unified Measurements Driven by Anatomic Segmentation
authors: Mingjun Huang, Junbo Zhang, Wei Hu, Chao Sun, Xiantao Cai, Bo Du
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: An accurate estimation of gestational age is critical for prenatal care and clinical decision-making. Existing ultrasound-based gestational age estimation methods are limited by the insufficient information representation capacity of conventional medical segmentation models, noise interference in ultrasound images, and inter-observer variability in traditional geometry-based measurement methods. To address these challenges, we propose the MsGA model to estimate gestational age with multi-plane unified measurements driven by anatomic segmentation. In the anatomic segmentation stage, a lightweight and high-performance LGF-UNet module is proposed, which utilizes the Deep Patch Embedding module to expand the receptive field, the Local-Global Fusion Transformer block to enhance local-global feature fusion, and the Focusing Attention Bottleneck module to suppress ultrasound noise via an adaptive threshold. In the measurement stage, a Point Regression module is introduced to refine biometric landmark localization. Furthermore, we create a fully annotated ultrasound plane dataset for the estimation of gestational age across various gestational stages. Extensive experiments on the dataset have demonstrated the effectiveness of the whole model and each module. Our MsGA model is superior to existing models with fewer parameters and achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Gestational Age Estimation task.

title: The use of artificial intelligence requires increasing criticality
authors: Moira Prates

title: GMPPO: Guided Model Predictive Path Optimization for Mobile Robots in Obstacle-Constrained Environments
authors: Lin Zhang, Wenhao Zhang, Runjiao Bao, Tianwei Niu, Shoukun Wang, Junzheng Wang
abstract: Autonomous navigation of industrial mobile robots in obstacle-constrained environment, such as manufacturing floors, warehouse storage systems, and factory inspection scenarios, poses significant challenges in ensuring path feasibility, safety, and real-time performance. This article proposes a guided model predictive path optimization (GMPPO) framework to address these issues. First, an enhanced convex feasible set algorithm is employed to generate robust initial paths by integrating decoupled dimensionality reduction and a dual-layer fault-tolerant mechanism. Second, based on this reference path, robot geometry, and real-time obstacle information, dynamic safety corridors are constructed to characterize local feasible spaces. Finally, these reference paths and corresponding safety corridors guide a high-fidelity model predictive optimization process, yielding smooth, dynamically feasible, and collision-free trajectories under nonholonomic constraints. Theoretical analysis establishes the resolution completeness and feasibility guarantee of the proposed method. Extensive simulations, real-world robotic experiments, and engineering deployments demonstrate that GMPPO significantly improves planning success rates, trajectory smoothness, and operational safety, while maintaining high computational efficiency and broad applicability.

title: RippleVision: Unobtrusive Gaze-Dependent Guidance via Directed Wave Motion in Virtual Reality
authors: Jérôme Kudnick, Daniel T. Mayer, Colin Groth, Bipul Mohanto, Ralf Dörner, Martin Weier
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: The inherent freedom of exploration in virtual reality poses a challenge for directing user attention toward relevant points or objects of interest, which are potentially located outside the user's field of view or are temporarily obscured. Accordingly, guidance must sustain users' perceptual focus while preserving the sense of presence. This paper presents RippleVision, a subtle gaze guidance technique using wave-like ripples that modulate brightness with inverted polarity between the eyes and appear only within a cone from the point of interest to the gaze location. A calibration study defined detectability and acceptability thresholds for the cues, followed by a comparative user study against four state-of-the-art techniques. Results show RippleVision achieves similar search times to clearly visible techniques while significantly reducing visual dominance. Moreover, its effectiveness scales with cue visibility, improving search performance with minimal impact on perceived visual obstruction and presence.

title: Perceive, Adapt, and Interact: An Intelligent Virtual Agent for Education in Mixed Reality
authors: Naveed Ahmed, Zulaiha Afrah Sadakathullah Shaduly, Mohammed Lataifeh, Imad Afyouni
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Mixed Reality (MR) environments are reshaping education by fostering engagement and motivation. Within such environments, Intelligent Virtual Agents (IVAs) can serve as personalized guides, tutors, or collaborative partners. In this research, we present a novel IVA for education that perceives the learner's environment, adapts its guidance to their progress, and interacts with both the learner and the real world. Our multimodal IVA integrates speech capabilities powered by large language models (LLMs) with computer vision for dynamic scene understanding, allowing it to converse naturally with learners and provide adaptive support based on their progress. To evaluate our IVA, we implemented a Scratch programming scenario using the Magic Leap 2 headset. We conducted a user study with 24 university students (12 male, and 12 female) who completed the same tasks using three systems: basic AR cues, a chatbot with AR cues, and our IVA. Quantitative data were obtained using standardized questionnaires such as the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), the User Engagement Scale (UES), and the System Usability Scale (SUS), alongside qualitative feedback. The results revealed that although the IVA system took a longer time compared to other systems, it fostered greater motivation and engagement, achieved the highest SUS score, and showed the strongest TAM correlations. Participants perceived IVA as the most valuable system, highlighting the potential of multimodal embodied agents as virtual tutors in MR.

title: Robust Visual Servo Control with Visibility Constraints for High-Dynamic Landing of Quadrotors
authors: Xin Ding, Tao Jiang, Xiaojie Su, Chao Chen, Hao Liang, Hengrui Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: High-dynamic and high-precision landing of visual servoing quadrotors poses significant challenges, primarily attributed to the low-frequency feedback characteristics of visual sensors and the physical coupling between the field of view (FOV) and quadrotor attitude variations. This paper presents a composite nonlinear feedback control framework based on visual servoing (PBVS-CNF) for the autonomous landing of quadrotors on rapidly moving platforms. First, an adaptive interpolation-compensated extended Kalman filter (AICEKF) is designed to compensate the measurement delays and obtain high-frequency updates of visual positioning information. Subsequently, a composite nonlinear feed back performance-optimized controller based on a pixel repulsion function (PRF) is developed to maintain continuous target visibility during high-dynamic landing tasks, while enhancing the vehicle's response speed and terminal landing accuracy. A compensation function observer (CFO) is designed to estimate full-course landing uncertainties via multi-source state fusion, exhibiting higher accuracy and faster response speed than conventional observers. Finally, simulations and experiments validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Energy-Conserving Event-Triggered Intermittent Control for Multi-Spacecraft Attitude Coordination
authors: Wenyu Qin, Pengyu Wang, Yueyong Lyu, Bohao Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The reliance on continuous communication and uninterrupted control actions in conventional spacecraft formation control imposes a heavy burden on communication bandwidth and energy resources. To address these challenges, an energy-conserving event-triggered intermittent control strategy is proposed for multi-spacecraft attitude coordination. First, a cooperative observation problem is formulated. Second, a distributed dual-loop neural network event-triggered observer is developed. The observer employs a neural network to approximate the unknown nonlinear dynamics of the leader spacecraft, incorporates a dual-loop observer and a Zeno-free event-triggered mechanism to eliminate the need for continuous communication, and achieves accurate state estimation using only discrete-time information. These features enable short-term state prediction by the observer even when inter-spacecraft communication is unavailable. Third, a prescribed performance intermittent controller is designed based on the estimated states. The controller deactivates the actuators when the estimation becomes unreliable and reactivates them when the tracking error exceeds the prescribed performance bounds, thus reducing energy consumption without compromising tracking accuracy. Finally, numerical simulations are conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy and its advantages in energy efficiency, attitude coordination accuracy, and short-term prediction.

title: Facial Expression Recognition for Chinese Elderly Using Edge and Semantic Features Dual Path Network With Two-Step Transfer Learning
authors: Keke Shi, Hongbin Chen, Keshu Cai, Jinhui Wu, Wei Wang, Jie Li, Bin Liu, Liangcheng Qu, Kuiying Yin, Pasquale Molinaro, Giancarlo Fortino, Jianqing Li, Wentao Xiang
abstract: Mild cognitive impairment (MCI), an early stage of Alzheimer's disease, often impairs cognitive abilities and emotional expression. However, accurate facial expression recognition (FER) in MCI patients remains challenging due to age-related facial textural interferences (i.e. wrinkles) and structural deformations (i.e. skin laxity). To address this issue, we propose ES-DPNet, a Dual-Path network that fuses Edge and Semantic features for robust FER in elderly subjects. Specifically, the Structure-preserved Edge Path leverages the Histogram of Oriented Gradients algorithm to preserve emotion-related facial structure and suppress local wrinkle interference; Meanwhile, the Region-aware Semantic Path employs large-kernel residual blocks with efficient channel attention to capture synergistic changes in facial regions and dynamically enhance discriminative cues. We have constructed the MCI-FED dataset, a high-confidence FER dataset with seven emotions from 25 Chinese MCI patients using a multi-model voting scheme to facilitate evaluation. We have introduced a two-step transfer learning strategy to adapt to age-related physiological and MCI-related pathological expression patterns. Experiments have shown that ES-DPNet demonstrates superior performance compared to existing deep learning methods, achieving 92.72%/85.22% accuracy and 92.77%/85.88% weighted precision on public youth and elderly datasets, 94.22% accuracy and 94.21% weighted precision in a subject-dependent validation on MCI-FED dataset, respectively. Our code is available at https://github.com/Codenewwer/feresdpnet.

title: Anomaly Detection Algorithm for Hyperspectral Images Based on Gabor Constraint Collaborative Representation
authors: Zhiyuan Xu, Xiaofei Wang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: To address issues such as the insufficient utilization of spatial information in hyperspectral imagery (HSI), this paper proposes a novel hyperspectral anomaly detection algorithm based on Gabor constraint collaborative representation (GCCRD), building on the traditional collaborative representation detector (CRD) algorithm. First, spatial texture cues and spectral discrepancies between the test pixel and its surrounding circular window are jointly incorporated into a weighting model to enhance background representation. Second, a local consistency constraint is introduced to identify reliable background samples and suppress isolated noise by examining the spatial–spectral consistency among neighboring background pixels. Third, to avoid excessive suppression of anomalous targets, a saliency refinement strategy is adopted by combining the original saliency map with its Gaussian-smoothed counterpart. Experimental results on five real datasets demonstrate that the proposed GCCRD algorithm outperforms six state-of-the-art methods.

title: Cross-Frequency Implicit Neural Representation with Self-Evolving Parameters
authors: Chang Yu, Yisi Luo, Kai Ye, Xile Zhao, Deyu Meng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Implicit neural representation (INR) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for visual data representation. However, classical INR methods represent data in the original space mixed with different frequency components, and several feature encoding parameters (e.g., the frequency parameter <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\omega$</tex-math></inline-formula> or the rank <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R$</tex-math></inline-formula>) need manual configurations. In this work, we propose a self-evolving cross-frequency INR using the Haar wavelet transform (termed CF-INR), which decouples data into four frequency components and employs INRs in the wavelet space. CF-INR allows the characterization of different frequency components separately, thus enabling higher accuracy for data representation. To more precisely characterize cross-frequency components, we propose a cross-frequency tensor decomposition paradigm for CF-INR with self-evolving parameters, which automatically updates the rank parameter <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R$</tex-math></inline-formula> and the frequency parameter <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\omega$</tex-math></inline-formula> for each frequency component through self-evolving optimization. This self-evolution paradigm eliminates the laborious manual tuning of these parameters, and learns a customized cross-frequency feature encoding configuration for each dataset. We evaluate CF-INR on a variety of visual data representation and inverse imaging problems, including image regression, inpainting, denoising, and cloud removal. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CF-INR outperforms state-of-the-art methods in each case.

title: Intrinsic Reward-Driven SAC-IRCNet: A Novel Energy-Saving Control Method for HVAC Systems
authors: Jian Cen, Linzhe Zeng, Xi Liu, Xinyao Li, Yongkang Li, Jianming Yang, Chengming Huang, Feiqi Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: With the rising global energy consumption, the energy use of heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems has become a critical concern. Existing deep reinforcement learning control methods for HVAC systems often exhibit slow convergence and poor adaptability to dynamic environments, resulting in significant indoor temperature fluctuations, inefficient temperature control strategies, and consequently, energy waste and failure to meet thermal comfort requirements. To address these challenges, this study proposes a novel HVAC control method based on the Soft Actor-Critic with Intrinsic Reward and Correlation-Aware CNN (SAC-IRCNet) model. The model incorporates cooling load prediction as a constraint to enable on-demand cooling supply. It integrates an Elliptical Dynamics Exploration intrinsic reward mechanism, which accelerates SAC convergence through elliptical exploration rewards and inverse dynamics models, thereby reducing energy waste. Additionally, the Correlation-Aware CNN enhances SAC’s feature extraction capability by leveraging state correlations to better understand contextual information, enabling more accurate responses to environmental changes and improved thermal comfort. Experimental results show that SAC-IRCNet achieves a 3.94% faster reward convergence and a 5.78% higher maximum cumulative reward within five episodes compared to SAC. It reduces energy consumption by up to 17.25% (21,837.58 kW) and lowers thermal discomfort violations by up to 6.44%, demonstrating excellent generalization ability across two datasets.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Two-Dimensional Model-Free Off-Policy Optimal Iterative Learning Control for Time-Varying Batch Systems
authors: Jianan Liu, Zike Zhou, Jinglin Huang, Wenjing Hong, Jia Shi
abstract: Although iterative learning control (ILC) has been widely used in batch processes, designing an optimal iterative learning control scheme for batch systems with unknown dynamics and time-varying parameters remains an open problem. In this paper, we propose a novel two-dimensional model-free off-policy optimal iterative learning control to achieve optimal control performance for linear time-varying batch systems. First, the one-dimensional state space is expanded to the two-dimensional state space by integrating time and batch information. Then, based on dynamic programming and a recursive algorithm, the framework of two-dimensional model-based optimal iterative learning control is established. Based on this framework, two-dimensional model-free optimal iterative learning control is further developed using model-free Q-learning reinforcement learning. The optimal iterative learning control policy is obtained through online off-policy iteration using historical and online operation data. Meanwhile, a rigorous convergence proof of the model-free optimal iterative learning control law is presented. Finally, the simulation results in the injection molding batch process demonstrate the proposed control scheme's effectiveness, feasibility, and significant improvement in control performance.

title: Photonic Sampler for mmWave Signals up to 70 GHz Using a 1-GHz MLL and a Silicon PIC
authors: Lucía Amaro-Losada, Cédric Bruynsteen, Shengpu Niu, Joris Van Kerrebrouck, Simone Cammarata, Kasper Van Gasse, Benjamin Rudin, Florian Emaury, Colm Browning, Nishant Singh, Xin Yin
abstract: Modern RF communications require the digitization of narrow bandwidth signals modulated on very high frequency carriers which can be a challenge requiring high-frequency oscillators and mixers for downconversion which can add jitter and distortion while being costly to implement. This work presents a photonic bandpass RF-sampling system using a silicon-photonic PIC and a 1-GHz 10.5-fs-jitter mode-locked laser where the MLL is modulated with mmWave complex modulated signals using a silicon photonics Mach Zehnder modulator and received using a low bandwidth photodiode. The usage of a MLL plus a low bandwidth PD demonstrates photonic downconversion of complex QAM signals at millimeter-wave frequencies up to 70 GHz, without the use of a mixer and local oscillator.

title: A Multi-Scale Perimeter Control and Route Guidance System for Large-Scale Road Networks
authors: Xianyue Peng, Hao Wang, Shenyang Chen, H. Michael Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Perimeter control and route guidance are effective ways to reduce traffic congestion and improve traffic efficiency by controlling the spatial and temporal traffic distribution on the network. This paper presents a multi-scale joint perimeter control and route guidance (MSJC) framework for controlling traffic in large-scale networks. The network is first partitioned into several subnetworks (regions) with traffic in each region governed by its macroscopic fundamental diagram (MFD), which forms the macroscale network (upper level). Each subnetwork, comprised of actual road links and signalized intersections, forms the microscale network (lower level). At the upper level, a joint perimeter control and route guidance model solves the region-based inflow rate and hyper-path flows to control the accumulation of each region and thus maximize the throughput of each region. At the lower level, a perimeter control strategy integrated with a backpressure policy determines the optimal signal phases of the intersections at the regional boundary. At the same time, a route choice model for vehicles is constructed to meet hyper-path flows and ensure the intra-region homogeneity of traffic density. The case study results demonstrate that the proposed MSJC outperforms other benchmarks in regulating regional accumulation, thereby improving throughput.

title: Performance Enhancement of the DMS Filter on SiC Substrate Using Intermediate Reflecting Grating
authors: Wei Guo, Pengliang Jia, Furong Li, Feihong Bao, Yedi Zhou, Qiaozhen Zhang, Jie Zou, Xianqi Lin
abstract: This paper presents a performance-enhanced, longitudinally coupled double mode surface acoustic wave (DMS) filter employing three interdigital transducers (3-IDT) on a 42° YX-LiTaO3 (LT)/SiO2/ $\alpha $ -Si/SiC multilayer substrate. By employing pitch modulation, the filter effectively suppresses bulk wave radiation, resulting in low loss and a flat passband. Furthermore, adding intermediate reflector grating between the 3-IDT of the DMS filter can significantly enhances out-of-band suppression, which is validated by comparison of finite element analysis (FEA) and experiment. The fabricated DMS filter with the intermediate reflector grating achieved a center frequency ( $f_{\mathrm {c}}$ ) of 1.83 GHz, a 3 dB fractional bandwidth (FBW) of 6.25%, and a minimum insertion loss (IL ${}_{\min }$ ) of merely 0.94 dB, closely agreeing with FEA results. In addition, the filter exhibited a power tolerance of 26.7 dBm, with lower and upper sideband temperature coefficient of frequency (TCF) values of +18.63ppm/°C and +7.23ppm/°C, respectively, indicating excellent temperature stability. Consequently, the proposed DMS filter with enhanced performance shows great potential for applications in radio frequency communication and radar systems.[2025-0226]

title: CCTformer: Calibrated Context-aware Transformer for Correspondence Pruning
authors: Weiwei Zhou, Shengjie Zhao, Yizhang Liu, Yanping Li
abstract: Correspondence pruning is a core problem in computer vision, aiming to distinguish correct matches from a large set of initial correspondences. Recent Transformer-based methods, such as VSFormer, have shown remarkable performance by jointly modeling visual appearance and spatial relationships. Nevertheless, these methods often depend on abstract, scene-level visual representations, which are insufficiently precise for validating individual correspondences. In addition, their context aggregation modules often struggle to capture multi-granularity corresponding features, particularly under complex geometric variations. To overcome these limitations, we propose the Calibrated Context-aware Transformer with two key components. The Calibrated Visual Cues Extractor samples features directly at putative match locations to provide well-aligned, correspondence-specific visual cues. The Dual-Perspective Graph Transformer replaces conventional graph attention to better model both node-level and edge-level interactions. Together, these modules enable more robust local-global context fusion. Extensive experiments on YFCC100 M and SUN3D show that CCTformer consistently outperforms the baseline and other state-of-the-art methods.

title: Comparing the Humanness of Machine-Generated and Human-Authored Text
authors: Autumn Toney, Leticia Bode, Tiago Ventura, Ethan Wilcox, Lisa Singh
abstract: None

title: Redefining Machine Learning Models Performance: What matters?

title: Characterization of FDSOI Technology for Energy-Efficient Cryo-CMOS Circuits
authors: Arpit Rao, David R. Daughton, Benjamin Chacko, Jason Chonko, Yukang Kuang, Sarah Guenther, Subhanshu Gupta
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: High-performance cryo-CMOS circuits are the foundation for quantum computing hardware. The energy efficiency of cryo-CMOS circuits is limited by the changing operating point of the device as the temperature drops. Operating point calibration becomes imperative to achieve energy-efficient designs, yet accurate threshold-voltage calibration at deep-cryogenic temperatures proves challenging. At cryogenic temperatures, conventional threshold-voltage extraction methods fail as the device operating point exhibits a nonlinear shift with temperature, rendering standard threshold voltage extraction methods unreliable, particularly in the low-VGS regime where cryo-CMOS circuits are typically biased for energy efficiency. This work characterizes flip-well 22 nm FDSOI technology and demonstrates a robust methodology for operating point calibration using back-gate control across a variety of VDS. Established extraction methods, including constant current, maximum transconductance, second-derivative, third-derivative, linear extraction, and transconductance-to-current ratio, are performed to assess their applicability under cryogenic conditions. In doing so, we demonstrate that back-gate tuning provides an effective and scalable mechanism for compensating operating-point shifts without emphasis on the absolute value of the threshold voltage. Calibrated threshold voltage results across temperature are presented for a 22 nm FDSOI super-low-VT (SLVT) transistor using body bias sweeps from 0 to +2 V. To enable circuit-level calibration in the low-VGS regime, this work identifies the body-coefficient η as a relevant metric for biasing, capturing the effective sensitivity of gate threshold voltage to back-gate bias. The extracted η exhibits nonlinearity with respect to both temperature and bias conditions, providing a physically meaningful and design-relevant parameter for energy-efficient cryo-CMOS calibration.

title: Novel Switching Frequency Injection Sensorless Control for SPMSM in Finite-Control-Set Deadbeat Predictive Current Control
authors: Yonglin Cui, Siqi Wang, Shanmei Cheng
abstract: When switching frequency injection (SFI) is implemented in finite-control-set (FCS) model predictive control (MPC) frame, position error signal will deteriorate due to inaccurate voltage injection. This paper establishes novel switching frequency injection sensorless control with proposed FCS deadbeat predictive current control (DPCC) based on novel angular domain iterative optimization method with extended control set (ECS). The inherent limitations of the FCS method inevitably introduce injection errors, which degrade current control performance. Compensating for these errors requires significantly extended execution time to achieve acceptable results. To address these issues, this paper further proposes a novel injection-time based SFI method, realizing precise injection with greatly reduced execution time. With these contributions, the proposed SFI sensorless control strategy based on d-axis current offset is successfully implemented on a target surface-mounted PMSM (SPMSM). Moreover, a simple but effective initial position detection method is proposed. And speed oscillation induced by current offset is studied. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed methods and validate the theoretical analysis.

title: A Three-layer AI-driven Image Filtering for Efficient LEO Satellite Remote Sensing
authors: Nourhen Sboui, Hakim Ghazzai, Sameh Najeh, Lokman Sboui
abstract: Low-earth orbit (LEO) satellites are considered essential tools for remote sensing (RS) and Earth observation (EO) applications thanks to their high spatial resolution images.However, limited onboard resources (storage, downlink throughput, and bandwidth) make it challenging to manage the large volume of data collected. Real-time data management is needed to avoid wasting on-board compute and downlink budget, especially when dealing with a significant influx of anomalous data images resulting from factors such as redundancy, seasonal changes, sensor properties, and satellite orbit dynamics. This paper introduces a novel three-layer filtering solution designed to optimize satellite image transmission for EO. Incorporating a fusion of machine learning and deep learning methodologies, our data management solution mitigates critical challenges associated with LEO satellite resource constraints. The proposed solution includes a no-reference deep image quality assessment (NoR-DIQA) model using a convolutional neural network architecture to identify and filter out distorted images. Then, it employs a perceptual hashing redundancy detection approach to eliminate duplicated images, and finally, it employs a classification model that categorizes suitable RS and EO applications for each image set before transmission. This framework effectively maximizes data utility and optimizes resource allocation for RS and EO satellites. The proposed framework was tested on different RS and EO datasets, where each layer was benchmarked against established models and consistently achieved superior results (e.g., higher correlation and lower error in image quality assessment, improved redundancy detection, and classification accuracy up to 99.4%), demonstrating the robustness and reliability of the three-layer filtering solution.

title: Purpose-Driven Data Sanitization for Secure AI/ML Applications in Next Generation Mobile Networks
authors: Chaitanya Aggarwal, Divya G. Nair, Huanzhuo Wu, Christian Mannweiler, Emrah Tomur, Jörg Ott
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AI/ML) into 5G and forthcoming 6G networks critically relies upon User Equipment (UE) collected data. However, this dependency creates a vulnerability to data poisoning, as current defenses on UEs lack contextual understanding of the collected data’s intended use by data consumers. This paper proposes SANITGUARD-UE, a novel data consumer purpose-driven solution designed to prevent the ingress of poisoned data. It seamlessly integrates with the 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) architecture by enabling UE sanitization capability signaling, Data Collection Configuration Function (DCCF)-configured purpose-driven data sanitization and an enhanced L2 header. Leveraging the use case of cell load estimation, empirical experiments demonstrated that SANITGUARD-UE significantly improves AI/ML model accuracy, outperforming purpose-agnostic sanitization by a factor of 3.73 to 7.66. This research establishes the essential role for purpose-driven data sanitization to build the trustworthiness and to optimize the performance of AI/ML services in next-generation mobile networks.

title: Data-Driven Motion Planning for Uncertain Nonlinear Systems
authors: Babak Esmaeili, Hamidreza Modares, Stefano Di Cairano
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: This article presents a data-driven motion-planning framework for nonlinear systems based on constructing a sequence of overlapping invariant polytopes. The planner randomly generates waypoints, and around each sampled waypoint, it learns a locally invariant polytopic safe set together with a corresponding local state-feedback gain. Each invariant polytope is obtained as the convex hull of a collection of invariant ellipsoids induced by a piecewise-affine controller. Safe and smooth transitions between waypoints are ensured by introducing an intermediate waypoint. Control gains are interpolated in real time via simplex-based interpolation, keeping the state inside the invariant polytopes throughout the motion. Unlike traditional approaches that rely on system dynamics models, our method requires only data to compute safe regions and design state-feedback controllers. The approach is validated through simulations by comparing against a containment-based invariant-ellipsoid baseline and reporting planning time, sampling effort, and trajectory quality, demonstrating safe and dynamically feasible navigation in complex environments.

title: Multifocal metalens for photodetector arrays based on material library fitting
authors: Junjing Huang, Kai Wang
abstract: In this paper we proposed a metalens design scheme based on material library fitting, which utilizes mathematical models, functional relationships, and a small amount of database resources to quickly predict the properties of the materials without the need to scan each sample one by one for simulation. The calculation time is about 3/13 of the traditional method. This greatly reduces the simulation time and improves the efficiency. In addition, we used this method to design a high performance quadfocal metalens with a focusing efficiency of 64% at 1310nm, an average transmittance of 70% and a maximum transmittance of 82% at 1000nm-2000nm. This is a significant advantage over the work of other researchers. The proposed work will significantly improve the design efficiency of metalens, thereby facilitating the advancement of the technology.

title: Quantifying Computational Reliability of Task Assignment in Extreme Edge Computing
authors: MHD Saria Allahham, Hossam S. Hassanein
abstract: This paper presents a reliability analysis framework for distributed computing in extreme edge computing (XEC) with limited information availability. XEC pushes computation to the outermost boundaries of networks by leveraging consumer-owned devices, known as Extreme Edge Devices (XEDs). Unlike traditional distributed systems with defined computational resource states, XEC operates under uncertainty due to consumer device usage patterns, varying computational capacities, and local scheduling algorithms. In this work, we address discrete task assignment particularly. The framework analyzes scenarios for computational reliability assessments with minimal knowledge of XED capabilities and service requirements. The framework adapts to different levels of available information, from operational limits to historical performance data, providing refined reliability estimates. The aim of this work is to provide generalized reliability models for distributed computing in XEC that allow decision-makers (e.g. service orchestrators) to make informed decisions about task allocation, service placement, and resource allocation under uncertainty in XEC. Simulations and experimental analysis demonstrate the framework's effectiveness in estimating reliability under various system conditions.

title: PointRL: Reinforcement Learning-Based Approach for Air-Ground Communications Using Multi-Dimensional Target Sensing Point Cloud
authors: Leyan Chen, Kai Liu, Peng Yang, Zehui Xiong, Tony Q. S. Quek, Jisi Fang, Zhibo Zhang
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly used in smart city communications for air-ground communications due to their flexibility, low cost, and independence from ground conditions, enabling high data rates for future networks. This paper explores UAV-to-vehicle (U2V) mmWave integrated sensing and communication (ISAC), where vehicles are represented as rigid shapes in a 3D radar point cloud. Considering maximizing channel capacity with multi-user interference and radar performance, two adaptive optimization problems are proposed, incorporating vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) communication for interference mitigation. The radar point cloud-driven reinforcement learning (PointRL) algorithm is designed to solve these problems. It includes a point cloud-based deep neural network (PDNN) for extracting action spaces from 3D radar data and a decision network that reduces network complexity through segmentation and connection. A linear weighted sliding window reward mechanism is also designed to enhance decision-making in dynamic environments. Simulation results show that the proposed PointRL outperforms benchmark methods.

title: High-Resolution Spatial Downscaling of Multi-Frequency Brightness Temperature in Global Mare Basalts on the Moon
authors: Shuangshuang Wu, Jianping Chen
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Research on lunar maria offers key constraints on the Moon’s endogenic evolution, yet thermal characterization of mare basalts remains limited by the low spatial resolution of Chang’e-2 microwave radiometer (MRM) data. To address this issue, we develop a SHAP-assisted downscaling framework for lunar difference brightness temperature (dTB). Using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), we quantify the contributions of candidate kernel-driven factors and identify FeO, TiO₂, and the digital elevation model (DEM) as the most influential kernels. Two regression models (Ordinary Least Squares, OLS; Geographically Weighted Regression, GWR) combined with two kernel sets (full and SHAP-selected) are compared to evaluate the effects of regression strategy and feature configuration. The SHAP-screened GWR model (GWR-3) delivers the best performance, attaining R² values of 0.954, 0.956, 0.951, and 0.950 at 3.0, 7.8, 19.35, and 37.0 GHz, respectively. The resulting ~2 km dTB products represent the highest-resolution multi-frequency dTB datasets to date. This SHAP-assisted framework offers an effective tool for enhancing lunar thermal analyses and supports refined investigations into the thermal heterogeneity and geological evolution of mare basalts.

title: Period-Two Dynamics of an Optically Injected Semiconductor Laser for Range-Sensitive Resonance Detection
authors: Wen-Hao Jiang, Sze-Chun Chan
abstract: Period-doubling nonlinear dynamics of a laser is experimentally found to be highly sensitive for ranging the distance of an optoelectronic feedback loop. The semiconductor laser is first optically injected for period-one (P1) dynamics at a fundamental frequency <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The optoelectronic feedback then induces period-doubling bifurcation in yielding period-two (P2) dynamics at the subharmonic frequency <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0}/2$</tex-math></inline-formula>. By adopting current modulation, the periodic dynamics adiabatically exhibits frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) behavior in general, but a resonance is unveiled when the modulation frequency <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{\mathrm{{m}}}$</tex-math></inline-formula> matches the reciprocal of the feedback delay <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Such a resonance is manifested through broadening the FMCW comb around the fundamental and concurrently suppressing the P2 dynamics at the subharmonic. It can be detected by simply monitoring the subharmonic power without frequency mixing. It allows for measuring <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau$</tex-math></inline-formula> to determine the effective absolute distance of the feedback loop with a microwave path included. It is also highly sensitive to fine changes in distance without being limited by the FMCW microwave coherence length. For the laser operating at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0} = 15$</tex-math></inline-formula> GHz with feedback of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1/\tau = 17$</tex-math></inline-formula> MHz, the resonance is sensitive to fractional changes of the order of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-4}$</tex-math></inline-formula> in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Ranging at 8.8 m is demonstrated with the capability to resolve down to 1 mm, where the required bandwidth is no more than 3 GHz. The novel form of resonance as detected through the P2 suppression enables the applications of range-sensitive nonlinear dynamics.

authors: N/A

title: Enhanced Dynamic Representation via Gradient-Motion Modeling for Micro-Expression Recognition
authors: Tong Zhang, Shiting Xu, C. L. Philip Chen
abstract: Micro-expressions (MEs) are brief, involuntary facial expressions revealing inner emotions. The unique occurrence mechanism of ME brings challenges for micro-expression recognition (MER). However, existing methods often suffer from task-irrelevant and overly localized representations, which lead to the loss of crucial discriminative information and thus limit recognition performance. In this paper, an enhanced dynamic representation via gradient-motion modeling (EDR) is proposed to obtain a complete and discriminative representation of the dynamic variations in ME. We provide theoretical support for EDR based on the complementarity of gradient and motion information in the computational rules of traditional optical flow. Specifically, a self-attentive gradient module (SAGM) is designed to enhance the sensitivity and localization of subtle motions. Inspired by the analytical solution of optical flow, we design a cross-attentive motion module (CAMM). Besides, dual-stage training is proposed to enhance fine-grained perception and generalization by first learning local-global correlations through a mask modeling strategy, followed by optimization under a standard recognition objective. Extensive experiments show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods. Ablation studies also demonstrate the effectiveness of each component.

title: A Vibration Suppression Method Based on Anisotropic Wedge for FSCW-PMSMs
authors: Siyao Huang, Wei Wang, Zheng Xu, Wenxiang Zhao, Ming Cheng
abstract: Fractional-slot concentrated-winding (FSCW) permanent magnet (PM) synchronous machines (PMSMs) exhibit numerous low-spatial-order radial force waves (RFWs), which are a dominant source of vibration and noise. This article investigates the influence of slotting structures on flux density harmonics, RFWs, and vibration and proposes a new vibration suppression method tailored for FSCW-PMSMs. First, analytical and numerical studies reveal that the closed-slot structure can effectively mitigate the non-integer-pole-pair vibrations induced by slotting effects, but it increases flux leakage, leading to torque loss. To address the torque deficiency of the closed-slot structure design, an anisotropic wedge-based vibration suppression method was proposed to achieve simultaneous torque retention and vibration attenuation. Next, the effectiveness of the proposed method for the FSCW-PMSMs with different pole–slot combinations is validated via finite element analysis (FEA) on two groups of FSCW-PMSMs, where each group comprises three machines—employing open slot, anisotropic wedge, and closed slot, respectively—while maintaining identical geometries. Finally, experimental verification using three prototyped machines confirms that the proposed method can significantly reduce the vibration acceleration without compromising electromagnetic torque for FSCW-PMSMs.

title: Voltage-dependent Capacitance as a Measure of Pore Accessibility of Supercapacitor Electrodes_supp1-3677937.pdf
authors: Nandini Gupta
abstract: Despite extensive research over the past two decades, achieving simultaneous high energy and power density in supercapacitors (SCs) remains a challenge. The key lies in designing nanoporous networks that offer a very high surface area while also maximizing electrolyte-ion accessibility of the pores. However, experimentally measuring pore accessibility in supercapacitor electrodes is challenging. This study employs a Particle-In-Cell-based computational approach to simulate charge transport, governed by the Poisson-Nernst-Planck equation, from the bulk electrolyte into porous electrodes. The influence of applied potential and pore morphology on charge dynamics of the SC is investigated. Results reveal that highly tortuous pores, along with low porosity, significantly reduce the charging rate. However, this adverse effect is mitigated by applying higher voltages, making initially inaccessible pores accessible, and thereby enhancing capacitance. Hence, the extent of voltage-dependence of capacitance is introduced as a measurable index for quantifying the pore accessibility in SC electrodes. Experimental validation confirms that this metric effectively distinguishes pore networks of various SC electrodes in terms of pore accessibility, using simple capacitance measurements. The proposed metric thus serves as a practical tool for designing high-performance SC electrodes for energy storage in electric vehicles and renewable energy systems.

title: Message from the General and Program Chairs: CW 2025
authors: N/A
abstract: Welcome to the 24th International Conference on Cyberworlds (CW 2025), hosted by Taiyuan University of Technology (TYUT) in Jinzhong, Shanxi Province, China, from October 14 to 16, 2025. As one of China's century-old and prestigious universities, TYUT takes great pride in organizing this international event. Shanxi, the venue of CW 2025, is renowned as a “treasure house of cultural relics,” offering a unique blend of rich historical heritage and vibrant modern development, which provides an inspiring backdrop for academic exchange and innovation.

title: Blockchain-Based Auditable Power Data Sharing Scheme
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: To facilitate the intelligent and digital transformation of the power grid, Grid Corporations have undertaken the construction of a data center-oriented infrastructure. This architecture enables the real-time acquisition, analysis, and sharing of massive volumes of electricity data, thereby significantly enhancing the efficiency of grid dispatching and the utilization of energy resources. However, the existing power data-sharing systems suffer from a lack of comprehensive auditing mechanisms, giving rise to several critical risks—including difficulty in tracking access behaviors, opacity in data transmission processes, and challenges in tracing the source of data leakage. These issues collectively compromise the trustworthiness and operational stability of the overall system. In response to these concerns, this paper proposes a blockchain-based auditable data-sharing scheme, which integrates smart contracts and cryptographic primitives to implement decentralized access control and behavioral auditing. The proposed solution ensures not only secure and traceable data interactions but also maintains favorable performance and scalability, as validated through experimental evaluation.

title: Bias-Compensated Maximum Quaternion Correntropy for Adaptive Filtering
authors: Minyong Wei, Qiangqiang Zhang, Zhengwen Tu, Xueqin Yang, Jianfeng Liu, Dongyuan Lin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Quaternion adaptive filters have been widely used in processing three-dimensional (3-D) and 4-D signals. To improve the filtering performance and estimation accuracy in the quaternion errors-in-variables (QEIV) model where the output signal is disturbed by non-Gaussian impulsive noises, this paper addresses the issue of quaternion parameter estimation. First, a novel bias-compensated maximum quaternion correntropy (BCMQC) criterion is proposed to resist impulsive outliers and mitigate the bias induced by noisy inputs, simultaneously. Then, based on an alternating optimization strategy involving explicit input compensation, the BCMQC algorithm is derived using the quaternion generalized Hamilton-real (GHR) calculus. Moreover, the Sherman-Morrison formula is employed to derive a computationally efficient update rule. Finally, simulations demonstrate the improved filtering performance and robustness of the proposed algorithm in the QEIV model.

title: ADODroid: A Static Detection Model for Android Malware Based on GCN and BiLSTM
authors: Shuoyuan Zhang, Xinyou Zhang, Zonghai Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This article proposes a new Android malware detection model ADODroid. The model extracts three types of software features through static analysis: API call graph, data flow, and opcode sequence. The API call graph provides spatial semantic information of the software, the opcode sequence provides contextual semantic information of the software, and the data flow can enhance the integrity of spatial semantic features. Abstracting the API call graph to enhance the model's anti-obfuscation ability and reduce computational complexity, then embedding the data flow into the graph to enrich the spatial semantics of features, while converting the opcode sequence into embedding vectors. Finally, inject the graph data and embedding vectors into a fusion neural network composed of GCN and BiLSTM, and output the classification results. The experimental results on the AndroZoo and VirusShare datasets show that the detection precision of the trained ADODroid can reach 96%, while having stronger robustness than other similar methods.

title: E-GPT: A Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Intelligent Educational Assistance
authors: Tabassum Ara, Shreyansu Panda, Jason Samuel Das, Amit Ranjan Das, Sidharth Vivek Prabhugoankar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Recent developments in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) have significantly influenced the education sector, mainly through Large Language Models (LLMs) and Multi-Agent Systems (MAS). Educational chatbots such as GPTutor use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and transformer-based models to offer more intelligent tutoring and automated assessment support. Still, most existing systems depend on centralized APIs and monolithic designs, which restrict scalability, adaptability to different contexts, and data privacy.In this paper, we introduce E-GPT, a locally deployable multi-agent educational chatbot framework designed to divide learning tasks among independent, specialized agents. These include a PDF-to-Quiz Generator, PDF-to-Technical Article Writer, RAG-based Chat Assistant, Question Paper Generator, and an OCR-enabled PDF Analyzer. The system integrates fine-tuned versions of LLaMA3 and Mistral models with a MongoDB-FAISS vector database, allowing efficient context retrieval and smooth collaboration between agents.Experimental results show that E-GPT improves contextual accuracy, modular scalability, and response time while maintaining user data privacy. By distributing different cognitive functions across coordinated agents, the system takes a step toward building a more adaptive, transparent, and scalable AI-driven learning environment.

title: AI-based Emergency Help Service
authors: Afraim Uzzaman Romeo, Maisha Rahman Diya, Ataur Rahman, Mahady Hasan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: In today's world, timely response during emergencies is a matter of life and death. This paper highlights the critical need for a fast and reliable emergency help system that is capable of alerting emergency contacts, authorities, and first aid services immediately. The goal of this system is to build a safe and efficient platform capable of sending emergency notifications, providing real-time location, establishing secure communication channels, offline communication, and assisting users with AIdriven first aid assistance. In this paper, we conducted a survey and an interview. In our survey, we wanted to find out if people actually needed the mobile app in emergency situations. After developing a user interface design, we conducted an interview for user satisfaction to understand how well it worked and what could be improved. Based on their feedback, we made some changes. Their insights helped us refine the design further, were reliable, and easy to use. The system was created following Agile methodology, with iterative design and regular user feedback. In short, the Emergency Help Service system adequately improves emergency response. Future improvements will aim to integrate IoT devices, predictive AI, and multiple communication channels to make the platform stronger and smarter.

title: Electricity Price Forecasting Using BiLSTM Model with Carbon Market Correlation
authors: Kheleshwar Sahu, Ashish Prajesh, Prerna Jain
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Carbon emissions are mostly caused by the energy industry, particularly by the production of power. Carbon price has become a key tool to deter excessive emissions among the numerous mitigation strategies. With an emphasis on hourly energy price forecasting and carbon cost pass-through, this work examines the connection between electricity market prices and carbon pricing in the Spanish power system. A deep learning framework built on Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks is employed to capture temporal dependencies in both directions and model long-range relationships in electricity price formation. Correlation research confirms the effectiveness of the model by revealing strong correlations between carbon pricing and actual and predicted electricity costs across Pearson, Spearman, and Kendall Tau metrics. Ordinary Least Squares regression also suggests a carbon cost pass-through of about 2.86 for expected electricity prices and about 2.04 for actual market prices. These findings show how the BiLSTM framework can identify and amplify carbon market signals, providing regulators, policymakers, and market participants with useful information in the context of the ongoing decarbonisation of the power sector.

title: PPDFP: Privacy-Preserving Data Filtering Protocol for Large-Scale ID Alignment of Vertical Federated Learning
authors: Zhengtao Jiang, Hanyu Zhou, Zhengyang Wu, Shujia Li, Yiyang Peng, Chenqing Cai
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables collaborative model training without raw-data sharing, but its performance hinges on efficient alignment of vertically partitioned samples. Private set intersection (PSI) is the standard tool for this task; however, when sets reach millions or billions of records, the computational and communication burden of PSI becomes prohibitive, especially if the true intersection is sparse. We present the Privacy-Preserving Data Filtering Protocol (PPDFP), a Paillier-homomorphic preprocessing layer that identifies and discards non-overlapping intervals before the heavyweight PSI phase. PPDFP privately determines interval-overlap relations and computes candidate intersection ranges without revealing plaintext endpoints. Experiments on datasets of size 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">20</sup>-2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">24</sup> elements demonstrated that equipping PSI with PPDFP reduced the runtime by 19%-79% and trimmed communication to 0.07%-1.5% of the baseline. These gains empirically demonstrate that PPDFP provides a practical and scalable privacy-preserving preprocessing framework for largescale private set intersection.

title: Day-ahead Scheduling and Reserve Optimization Strategy for Renewable Energy
authors: Peng Yuan, Junjie Sun, Xinwei Li, Dai Cui, Fei Xu, Ling Hao
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: The escalating penetration of renewable energy (RE) sources, notably photovoltaic (PV) and wind power, introduces substantial operational complexities for day-ahead scheduling and reserve management in modern power systems, owing to the inherent unpredictability of $\mathbf{R E}$ generation. To address the above challenges, this study proposes a collaborative optimization strategy for day-ahead scheduling and reserve configuration that explicitly accounts for RE uncertainty. The methodology is structured into three sequential stages: first, the probability distribution of RE forecast errors is derived through statistical analysis of historical data, providing a quantitative basis for characterizing uncertainty; second, reserve requirements are systematically determined based on the obtained error distribution to mitigate potential imbalances between supply and demand; and finally, a comprehensive cost-minimization model is developed, incorporating risk costs to hedge against uncertainties. The proposed model rigorously enforces constraints related to power balance, operational limits of thermal units, energy storage systems (ESSs), and grid interactions, while integrating reserve constraints to ensure system stability. Results of the simulations indicate that the proposed day-ahead scheduling framework enables an optimal allocation of reserve capacity, effectively balancing operational security and economic efficiency under uncertain RE conditions.

title: Detecting Test Case Smells Using Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study in Industry
authors: Lennon Chaves, Flávia Oliveira, Leonardo Tiago
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)
abstract: In the software testing process, test cases are developed to verify whether the software functions as intended. Consequently, test cases must be comprehensible to those who will execute them, and to ensure that they are written comprehensively and accurately, Large Language Models (LLMs) can be employed to assist with this task. With the aim of recommending issues within test cases, this study developed a prompt with 12 smells that should be avoided in test cases to facilitate comprehension. To evaluate the prompt, an empirical study was conducted with 5 participants from a software test team, employing the prompt on test cases from the requirements validation scope of a real industrial scenario. During the study, for each test case, the participants recorded the number of smells recommended by the LLM and subsequently indicated whether they agreed, partially agreed, or disagreed with the response obtained for each test case. Among the results of this experiment, the detection of “Misplaced verification” was observed in 90% of the utilized test cases, while no test case exhibited any “Spell check” smells. Another finding was that approximately 63% of responses from the LLM were classified as “Agree” or “Partially agree” by the participants regarding the smells detected in the test cases, indicating that improvements are necessary for these test cases. Thus, this empirical study presents the results of applying a prompt with 12 test case smells to recommend issues in test cases that validate the requirements of carriers.

title: A Multi-Modal AI Framework for Exotic Fruit Classification and Soil Suitability Mapping Using Deep Learning and Agro-Environmental Data
authors: Rini PR, Kavitha R, Dalvin Vinoth Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Rising demand for exotic fruit is challenging accurate identification of crops and evaluation of soil suitability as there are currently no integrated Decision Support Systems (DSS) that incorporate visual and agronomic-environmental information. Methods currently being used for assessing plant potential & determining soil suitability typically address these concerns separately, thereby limiting decision-making accuracy in constructing detailed cultivation plans. This research provides a multi-modal Artificial Intelligence (AI) platform that merges Deep Learning for Images with Machine Learning for soils for the production of exotic fruits. In this framework, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) are employed to classify images representing both fruit and soil. The Random Forest (RF) algorithm evaluates the potential of a site by utilizing several agronomic and chemical properties of the soil such as pH, Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium (N-P-K) values. This multi-pathway design allows for fruit recommendations or soil recommendations based on either method. With respect to Experimental validation using a self-developed dataset, EfficientNetB0 achieved an accuracy of 93% while MobileNetV2 demonstrated comparable performance (91 %) with the added benefit of having less CPU utilization. The proposed system is a practical, easy-to-use solution for providing guidance to an individual with respect to environmentally friendly exotic fruit farming practices.

title: Motion-Robust Vital Signs Detection Using Millimeter-Wave FMCW Radar Based on DCT-Wavelet Compressed Sensing*
authors: Xin Xiang, Bo Yang, Zehua Nie, Fu Zhang, Kunming Zhou, Ling Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the increasing application of millimeter-wave radar technology in non-contact health monitoring, motion interference has become a core challenge limiting its accuracy. This paper proposes an adaptive motion perception and signal reconstruction method based on Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW) radar, aiming to achieve robust extraction of respiration and heartbeat signals in dynamic scenarios. The method first utilizes range-dimension Fast Fourier Transform (FFT), Moving Target Indication (MTI), and Constant False Alarm Rate (CA-CFAR) detection to precisely locate the target's chest range bin, effectively overcoming range migration caused by motion. Subsequently, the Extended Differentiate and Cross-Multiply (DACM) algorithm is employed for phase demodulation to avoid phase wrapping distortion. The core contribution lies in the design of a motion detection mechanism based on phase variance. For identified motion interference signals, a Compressed Sensing (CS) reconstruction algorithm combining Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) and Wavelet bases is proposed. This algorithm effectively suppresses motion noise while accurately reconstructing vital sign signals. Experiments conducted on a platform equipped with a TI IWR6843 radar show that in various motion scenarios, such as walking, the average detection accuracy of respiratory rate and heart rate reached 95.0% and 95.9%, respectively, with Mean Absolute Errors (MAE) of 2.48 BPM and 2.74 BPM. These results verify the robustness and accuracy of the proposed method in dynamic environments, providing reliable technical support for continuous health monitoring.

title: Self - Organizing LoRa Network Based Ocean Observation Platform
authors: Shiqiang Wang, Xuan Geng, Yanli Xu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: At present, the ocean observation platform has the problems of low real-time performance, weak data interaction and poor ad hoc network ability, which cannot meet the observation needs of the marine environment. To adapt to the marine environment, this paper proposes an ocean observation platform system based on STM32 and LoRa network. The marine data collected by the LoRa terminal is transmitted to the gateway, and then transferred to the local core network through the cloud for data processing. The star network topology of multiple LoRa terminals and gateways is realized, which gets rid of using LoRaWAN gateway in the traditional LoRa ad hoc network system. Our solution provides a new concept for offshore observation platforms, which is low-cost, has strong self-organizing network capabilities and offers a wide communication coverage area. Under conditions of low power, short antenna, and close range without obstruction, the overall packet loss rate is only 1.6 %. When there is obstruction, the overall packet loss rate is 5.4%,which has a good performance.

title: EfficientNetB0-based Feature Extraction for Single and Double-Hand Mudra Recognition
authors: Prathviraj Chavan, Pooja Choudhary, Shubhi Upadhyay, Saniya Devarshi, Sumitra Sureliya, Awanit Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Bharatanatyam is an ancient classical dance form of India that depicts mudras as a systemic language representing emotions, storytelling, and spiritual expression. Modern learners find it challenging to interpret these correctly due to their visual complexity and minute variations of the hands forming different mudras. We propose a deep-learning-based single-hand and double-hand Bharatanatyam mudra recognition framework using a dataset collected for the purpose. We adopt EfficientNetB0 for feature extraction and classification purposes and implement the deep network using PyTorch. Further, the system is deployed with a modern FastAPI backend and React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS frontend. Our dataset consists of 51 mudras (28 single-hand, 23 double-hand) with 300 images in each class, which our team has fully collected and annotated. The model obtains an accuracy of 99.71 % and 99.89 % for single- and double-hand mudra recognitions, respectively, which is exceptionally good. This work represents one of the pioneering attempts to develop an automated recognition framework of Bharatanatyam mudras using deep learning, hence contributing to the preservation of culture, digital learning, and performing-arts education.
