title: A Transient Stability Assessment Model Based on the Broad Learning System
authors: Shenghong Liu, Bo Wei, Jinghao Zhang, Zhengdan Wang, Kuiping Ding, Xiyuan Zhang, Junyi Bian, Jun Liu
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: Accurate online assessment of transient stability remains a key challenge in power system analysis. To overcome the limitations of time-domain simulation and direct methods in practical use, this paper proposes a data-driven model based on the Broad Learning System (BLS). A data-driven framework for online transient stability assessment is established, and feature augmentation is achieved using the algorithmic principles of BLS. An online incremental learning model is developed for transient stability assessment. Simulation studies on the IEEE 39-bus system demonstrate that the proposed BLS-based model achieves superior accuracy and shorter training time compared to several benchmark models, highlighting its potential for practical engineering applications.

title: A Hybrid LSTM-VGG16 and Fuzzy Logic Framework for Illegal Land Occupation and Flood Damage Assessment from Satellite Imagery
authors: S. Madhavan, V. Logeshwar, G. Sivagurunathan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Under this work, a combined scheme of tracking down cases of illegal land occupation and measuring flood damages through satellite images are also presented. The method of analyzing the pattern of space and time land use uses a deep learning and fuzzy logic method. High-level spatial features are extracted in images using a pre-trained VGG16 convolutional neural network. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network is used to sequence and analyze these features to display dynamic variations and discover illegal land occupation. In flood damage assessment, fuzzy logic system is applied to data uncertainties occurring with pre and post event imagery to facilitate subtle damage assessment. The framework creates real time notifications to the authorities automatically to aid quick response to disasters and sound decision making in land management. The system, written in Python and powered by geospatial libraries, shows potential to improve the urban planning and environmental agency in terms of providing accurate and timely understanding of land use dynamics and the effects of disasters.

title: MultiMotion: A Scalable Multi-Person Interactive Motion Generation Framework
authors: Shuaibo Li, Ke Lv, Kun Dong, Jian Xue
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Multi-person motion generation is a fundamental problem in computer vision, with broad applications in gaming, animation, and virtual environments. This task entails the joint modeling of multi-person motion synthesis and interactive dynamics—an inherently challenging problem due to the complex spatiotemporal dependencies among participants. Existing approaches are largely confined to single-person or dyadic (two-person) settings and struggle to generate coherent, scalable multi-person motion sequences. In this paper, we rethinks the formulation of multi-person motion generation and proposes MultiMotion, a conditional collaborative motion generation framework. MultiMotion comprises two key components: a generation module and an extension module. The generation module synthesizes realistic and diverse single-person motion sequences. The extension module coordinates and guides the motion generation process during diffusion by conditioning on the motion sequences of other agents, thereby enabling seamless scalability from individual motions to multi-person collaborative and interactive behaviors. furthermore, our framework utilizes a collaborative diffusion mechanism that exchanges noisy motion representations among participants at every diffusion step. By leveraging these shared intermediate signals, the extension module dynamically extends single-person actions into multi-person collaborative interactions throughout the denoising process. Comprehensive evaluations confirm that our approach is effective and capable of simultaneously inferring motions involving either individual or multiple persons within a unified generative framework.

title: Stock Trading Prediction Based on the DQN Series Algorithm
authors: Yichuan Lu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Stock trading decisions hold significant importance in financial trading. Traditional stock trading decisions rely on fundamental analysis, technical analysis, or expert experience, which have shortcomings such as strong subjectivity. As technology advances, reinforcement learning techniques are also being applied to stock trading decisions. Based on the reinforcement learning theory, agents are allowed to autonomously learn stock trading strategies in the stock market environment to achieve maximum trading returns. To overcome the limitations of traditional methods, this paper uses three reinforcement learning algorithms: DQN, Double DQN (DDQN), and Dueling Double DQN (DDDQN) for stock trading decisions, and carry out tests and comparative analysis. The experimental results show that all three methods can achieve certain returns in the training set and the test set. Among them, the Dueling Double DQN algorithm performs the best overall. The Double DQN algorithm performs well, and the stability of the DQN algorithm needs to be improved. The effectiveness of the algorithm is verified, and the reproducible solution of stock trading decision is provided in this study.

title: Explainable Hybrid PSO-SVM Framework for EEG-Based Cognitive Confusion Detection
authors: Ravichander Janapati, Vasupalli Manoj, Usha Desai, Viswaprakash Babu, Ghousia Sanober Sabreen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Cognitive confusion is an urgent affective condition that affects learning effectiveness and decision-making performance. Identification of such states with the help of electroencephalography (EEG) helps in human cognitive processes and allow the adaptation of educational or neurofeedback systems. This study proposes an explainable hybrid approach to Particle Swarm Optimization-Support Vector Machine (PSO-SVM) to detect the presence of confusion states in a dataset of publicly available Wanghaohan Confused Student EEG Brainwave Dataset with sustainable accuracy and interpretability. In this study, EEG data recorded through a single-channel NeuroSky MindSet device, which has power spectral features, such as delta, theta, alpha, beta, and gamma, as well as the indices of attention and meditation during the e-learning activities. PSO is used in the proposed framework to optimize the SVM hyperparameters and subsets of features, which guarantees better generalization and less overfitting. The model attained high classification rates in identifying the confounded and non-confounded cognitive states compared to baseline machine learning classifiers. Moreover, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) models, such as SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and feature importance analysis, were combined to understand the value of each EEG feature in the model decision. The findings prove that high-frequency gamma and attention scores are the prevailing signs of confusion. The proposed PSO-SVM-XAI system is accurate as well as transparent, providing scalability of intelligent human-computer interaction and adaptive learning systems based on monitoring the cognitive state.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Method for Separating Overlapping ADS-B Signals in Continuous Single-Antenna Reception
authors: Chenchen Wang, Wenyi Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast (ADS-B) signals are prone to in-air overlapping, which leads to decoding failures. Most existing deep learning-based methods are trained and evaluated on pre-segmented overlapping signals, which differs from real reception scenarios. In practice, continuous signals received by a single antenna are dominated by noise and interference, with only a few randomly occurring ADS-B signal segments. To address this issue, an end-to-end time-domain ADS-B signal separation method is proposed to process continuous observation signals directly. This work is the first to introduce the overlap-add (OLA) strategy into this task, enabling direct recovery of overlapping signals. Considering the low proportion of effective signals in continuous data and the insufficient constraints of conventional scale-invariant source-to-noise ratio (SI-SNR) loss, a log-envelope region-weighted SI-SNR (LERW-SNR) loss is designed. The proposed loss enhances the emphasis on effective signal regions during training while suppressing the influence of background noise. Experimental results on real-world continuous observation signals show that, compared with the standard SI-SNR loss, the proposed method improves cyclic redundancy check (CRC) pass rates by 3.27% and 11.24% on Dual-Path Recurrent Neural Network (DPRNN) and Separation Transformer (Sepformer) networks, respectively. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method for continuous signals received by a single antenna.

title: Bengali Speech Based Medical Assistant Using Retrieval Augmented Generation for Symptom-Based Diagnosis and Treatment
authors: Dhruba Chowdhury, Saklain Abdullah, Riad Hossain, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin, M. M. Musharaf Hussain
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Thousands of rural and marginalized people in Bangladesh still face serious barriers to accessing basic healthcare due to language limitations, low literacy, and social prejudice. Women and adolescent girls are especially affected, as they often lack a safe and private way to ask health-related questions in their native language. Since most digital health systems are designed primarily in English, a large number of Bengali speakers remain excluded from these services. To address this challenge, this paper presents a Bengali voice-based medical assistant developed using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The system understands spoken Bengali, retrieves trusted medical information from a verified knowledge base, and generates clear and reliable healthcare advice. The proposed framework integrates Speech-to-Text, hybrid information retrieval (fuzzy matching for direct disease queries and semantic search using FAISS for symptombased queries), and a large language model to provide an end-to-end speech-to-speech healthcare assistant. The system was evaluated on a doctor-verified dataset of 100 diseases and symptom profiles, achieving a macro F1-score of 0.93. This performance is significantly higher than approaches relying only on keyword search, semantic retrieval, or a language model without retrieval support. The results demonstrate that combining knowledge-based retrieval with generative AI can deliver safe, accurate, and understandable medical guidance in Bengali.

title: Numerical Modeling of Radiosonde-Assisted Compact Ranges for Electromagnetic Propagation under Atmospheric Variability
authors: Sujit Kumar Chakravarty, Amul Batra, Mohd Imran Ansari, Vijay Kumar Soni
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Conventional compact-range electromagnetic (EM) characterization assumes homogeneous atmospheric conditions and ignores thermodynamic gradients that distort propagation. In this paper, we propose a RadiosondeAssisted Compact Range (RACR) framework to integrate real-time temperature, pressure, and humidity profiles into Maxwell-based full-wave simulations using continuous altitude-dependent refractivity mapping. RACR preserves vertical dielectric gradients while enabling closed-loop validation through S-parameter analysis, which is not possible with discrete-layer or ITU-based models. The proposed method reduces transmission prediction RMSE by 61 % across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-5 \text{GHz}$</tex>, enhances correlation with measurements to 0.97, and limits phase-delay deviation to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 1.2 \%$</tex>. The results demonstrate enhanced beam coherence and transmission fidelity in stratified and humid atmospheres, paving the way for weather-adaptive electromagnetic calibration.

title: Strengthening Password Security in Virtual Learning Environments: An Analysis of Protocols, Compliance, and User Behavior
authors: Bin Hu, Ifrah Malik, Fanyu Zeng, Noman Sohail
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: This study evaluates password security protocols in virtual learning environments (VLEs), addressing critical gaps in current practices and proposing enhancements to improve security and user satisfaction. Using hypothetical four platforms (A, B, C, and D), the study examines the effectiveness of various security measures, including minimum password length, special character requirements, multi-factor authentication (MFA), and password expiry periods. The findings reveal significant disparities in security practices across platforms, with Platform D, which implements stringent password policies and MFA, demonstrating the highest user compliance, fewest security incidents, and greatest user satisfaction. Conversely, Platform B, with the least stringent measures and no MFA, shows the highest incidence of security breaches. The study highlights the crucial role of MFA in enhancing security, as platforms with MFA report significantly fewer incidents. A strong inverse correlation between password strength and security incidents underscores the need for robust password policies. User behavior, such as password reuse and poor password hygiene, remains a challenge, emphasizing the importance of user education programs. Additionally, the study advocates for standardized security practices across VLEs to ensure consistent protection. Implications of this research suggest that educational institutions must adopt comprehensive security measures, including stringent password policies, MFA, and user education, to safeguard sensitive information and improve user satisfaction. The findings provide a framework for developing more secure VLEs and highlight the critical role of cybersecurity in supporting the future of digital education. This study contributes to the broader effort of enhancing VLE security and fostering a safer online learning environment.

title: Blue Finance Policy for Sustainable Development Goals: An Evidence-Based Approach
authors: Naglaa Fathy El Dessouky
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: Blue finance has emerged as an instrumental policy tool for directing funds to associated ocean activities. It supports the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), specifically SDG 14. Maritime ecologies face increasing challenges, ranging from climate change to risky pollution and unsustainable resource utilization, which underscores the need for rational financial mechanisms to support sustainable ocean policies. Given the scarcity of research on this topic and its novelty, the paper aimed to deepen understanding and address that gap. The research methodology is framed in a qualitative, evidence-based approach that utilizes critical case study analysis to investigate marine sustainability and blue finance initiatives across selected MENA and GCC countries. The paper examined the conceptual foundations of the blue finance notion, its policy instruments and tools, its governance and networking framework, and its policy elaboration within international contexts. Through the analysis of these leading initiatives, the study examined the intervention approach, policy instruments, stakeholder engagement, and governance modes of the blue finance policy design. The study emphasized that, despite contextual differences, countries consistently aligned their initiatives with international standards for the Sustainable Development Goals. The paper concluded with practical recommendations to strengthen public policy design, enhance policymakers' interventions in policy formulation and implementation, improve stakeholders' governance, and inform impact strategies.

title: An Exploratory Study of User Perceptions of Information Across Social Media Platforms

title: Parameter-Efficient Federated Multimodal Alignment Tuning for Heterogeneous Clients
authors: Xu Tan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Large-scale vision-language models like CLIP achieve breakthrough performance in zero-shot classification and cross-modal retrieval by learning aligned representations from massive image-text pairs. However, their centralized training paradigm faces data sharing challenges in privacy-sensitive domains. Although federated learning enables collaborative training without sharing raw data, it encounters the cross-modal alignment challenge with heterogeneous client modalities. To address this challenge, a parameter-efficient federated multimodal alignment tuning framework Fed-MoAlign is proposed in this paper. In Fed-MoAlign, the model is decomposed into vision encoder, text encoder, and alignment layer. The backbone is frozen and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) layers are inserted into encoders based on pretrained CLIP. Unimodal clients train corresponding encoder LoRA parameters independently, while multimodal clients additionally update the alignment layer. To enable unimodal clients alignment training, a dynamic prototype-based pseudo-modality generation algorithm is proposed where prototypes are maintained at the server from multimodal clients, and pseudo features are generated by unimodal clients via similarity-weighted prototype retrieval. Furthermore, a hierarchical selective aggregation mechanism based on cross-modal mutual information is introduced, designing differentiated strategies for different network layers according to their roles in multimodal alignment. Experiments on MS-COCO and Flickr30K demonstrate that Fed-MoAlign achieves 55.1% and 35.6% I2T R@1 respectively, outperforming state-of-the-art federated multimodal methods CreamFL by 3.0% and 3.3%. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of our proposed components.

title: Automated Brain Tumor Detection and Segmentation using Rob flow annotation & YOLOv11 Model
authors: Nivedita Gudigenavar, Manjunath Managuli, Nikhil Inamdar, Ramesh Koti, Jagadish S Jakati, Sharanappa P H
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: A brain tumor, which can be either benign or malignant, is an abnormal development of brain cells. For better treatment outcomes and increased survival rates, early identification is essential. Traditional analytical techniques such as CT and MRI provide valuable structural and locational information but rely heavily on manual interpretation. Biopsy remains a gold standard but is invasive and time-consuming. In this work, we employ Artificial Intelligence (AI) to enhance brain tumor detection using MRI scans. The dataset was annotated and preprocessed using Rob flow, incorporating data augmentation to improve model generalization. A YOLO-based deep learning framework was trained in Google Colab with GPU support, enabling efficient and instantaneous discovery of tumor regions. The proposed structure integrates Rob flow’s dataset management with YOLO’s high-speed object detection, adapted for tumor segmentation. Performance evaluation was carried out expending metrics such as mean Average Precision (mAP), Correctness, F1-score, Recall, and confusion matrix. Investigational results demonstrate the capability of the approach to support radiologists in early diagnosis, offering a reliable, automated, and non-invasive solution for brain tumor detection.

title: Problem Formalization and Benchmarking of Multimodal Learning for Text, Image, Audio, and Video Understanding to Promote Inclusive and Sustainable Knowledge Access
authors: K. Ramu Dev Burman, Moorthy Agoramoorthy
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The Multimodal learning, being a combination of text, image, audio and video information has come up as the essential paradigm of creating intelligent and context sensitive information system. They are also being used more in education, healthcare, governance, and media, where the successful combination of various types of data can be used to improve knowledge, decision-making, and accessibility. Although there has been much progress, there have been a number of challenges such as the absence of standard definitions of the problem, disparities between benchmarking protocols and the problem of equitable and inclusive knowledge access. The current paper will provide a detailed system toward formalizing and benchmarking of multimodal learning models, especially in terms of interpretability, fairness, and resource efficiency. Representative architectures are analyzed in a systematic manner, in a variety of modalities, to measure performance, robustness as well as computation requirements trade-offs. The results offer viable lessons to researchers and practitioners in order to create not only accurate and scalable multimodal systems but also socially accountable and able to promote inclusive knowledge sharing. Besides, the research points out the most influential areas where research should focus in the future to develop effective, accessible, and sustainable multimodal models.

title: Perceptual Quality Assessment of Compressed Vibrotactile Signals Based on Weber–Fechner Threshold Adjustment
authors: Yixuan Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Vibrotactile signal compression is essential for efficient transmission in the Tactile Internet. However, distortions introduced during compression often degrade perceptual quality, while conventional objective metrics based on signal-domain errors fail to accurately reflect human tactile perception. To address this issue, this paper proposes a biomimetic multi-channel vibrotactile perceptual quality assessment framework based on Weber–Fechner threshold adjustment. Inspired by the frequency selectivity of Fast-Adapting Type I (FA-I) and Fast-Adapting Type II (FA-II) mechanoreceptors, the proposed method decomposes vibrotactile signals into multiple perceptual channels and incorporates Just Noticeable Difference (JND) calibration within each physiological channel. Grounded in standardized psychophysical thresholds, the proposed model establishes a perception-consistent evaluation baseline in the signal domain. Experiments conducted on diverse subsets of the HapticGen dataset demonstrate that the proposed method maintains stable accuracy under low compression ratios, while achieving an average improvement of approximately 0.2–0.3 in Pearson Linear Correlation Coefficient (PLCC) compared to existing mainstream metrics in high-compression scenarios.

title: PC-CDS: Real-Time Secure Authentication and Intelligent Steering for Hybrid TN-NTN Networks
authors: Abdul Samim, Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The integration of Terrestrial Networks (TN) and Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) represents a cornerstone for 6G wireless systems, promising ubiquitous connectivity and enhanced quality of service. However, hybrid TN-NTN architectures introduce critical security vulnerabilities and complex network steering challenges. This paper proposes PureChainsecured Cognitive Dual-Steering (PC-CDS), integrating a custom blockchain authentication with Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)-based intelligent network steering. PureChain achieves a mean authentication delay of 15 ms with a 99.8% success rate, compared to 100 ms and 98.5% for traditional blockchain. The PPO-based cognitive agent dynamically orchestrates user connections between terrestrial gNBs and Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) satellites. Extensive simulations with 80 users, 25 terrestrial gNBs, and 3 VLEO satellites over 200 episodes demonstrate superior performance: 87.5% VLEO connectivity, network switching latency under 20 ms versus 100 ms for traditional blockchain, stable throughput at 90 Mbps versus 85 Mbps degradation, and controlled packet loss at 11% versus 17%. PPO outperforms Soft Actor-Critic, achieving a median reward of 160 compared to 140. PC-CDS lays the foundation for secure, intelligent 6G hybrid networks.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning-Based Remaining Useful Life Prediction for Industrial Motors
authors: Debasis Jana, Suprakash Gupta, Sukomal Pal, Sandip Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Precise Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction of industrial machinery and its components is important for ensuring high operational availability, reducing unplanned downtime, and optimizing maintenance costs. Industrial machinery are operates in dynamic and harsh environmental condition leading to a complex, non-linear and non-stationary degradation. It is challenging to model such degradation pattern using the traditional statistical models and classical machine learning techniques. To overcome this issue, a deep learning based hybrid model comprises of a Bi-directional Long Short Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) network and a Transformer architecture, has been developed for predicting the RUL of industrial machinery in this study. The developed model is trained and tested using the multivariate time-series sensor data collected from a heavy duty industrial DC motor operated in a mining machinery. The developed model exhibits superior performance over the standalone model of Bi-LSTM and Transformer, achieving a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 231.77 and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.94. the results demonstrate strong predictive capability of the hybrid model. The study highlights the interpretability through attention mechanisms, providing insights into critical degradation patterns of industrial motor for inform maintenance decisions.

title: Design and Implementation of a Bifacial Solar-Powered Water Pumping System Using Boost Converter for Off-Grid Homes
authors: Kiruthiga Devi V, Veena P, Jeyabharath R, Prabhakaran K, Loganath D, Pradeep M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The aim of this research project is to design and implement a Gallium Nitride FET (GaN-FET) boost converter in conjunction with a bi-facial photovoltaic panel-based water pump to enhance the reliability of energy usage in off-grid homes and to improve the overall efficiency of the energy usage. In this research, there are two groups. Group 1 refers to the conventional monofacial solar panel + silicon-MOSFET converter configuration, and Group 2 refers to the proposed bifacial solar panel + GaN-FET converter configuration. The system performance was evaluated under 6 different irradiance levels using MATLAB/Simulink R2025a simulation and hardware prototype testing. Using the GaN-FET based converter, the system consistently generated a stable <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$48-\mathrm{V}$</tex> output with less than 0.5 V peak-to-peak ripple across all irradiances from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 ~\text{kW} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 ~\mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>. With respect to the irradiance level, the bi-facial configuration produced between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}-\mathbf{2 5} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> more power than the monofacial module. The average efficiency of the complete system was approximately 96.5 %. With regard to the battery backup, the transition from bright light conditions to dark light conditions resulted in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 8 - 3 2 \%}$</tex> more average power delivery and approximately 30 % more total daily volume of water production compared to the monofacial + Si-MOSFET system. Bifacial Solar-Powered Water Pump with a GaN-FET Converter Offers Improved Reliability and Efficiency of Energy Usage in Comparison to Monofacial Systems and is therefore suitable for residential off-grid water supplies.

title: Intelligent Resume Analysis and Skill Matching Using NLP
authors: Dontamsetti Nageswarrao, M Sai Kiran, V Sai Krishna, V Sree Hansitha, Bunga Sekhar, Sultana Saba
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Resume screening that is performed manually is also tedious, expensive, and can be biased and does not usually result in the rightful matching of job descriptions with the candidate. The proposed solution to these dilemmas is described in the paper that offers an intelligent resume analysis and skill-matching system based on the application of Natural Language Processing (NLP) and machine learning methods. The system uses SpaCy and PyResparser to get structured information like skills, education, and experience using unstructured resumes. The features extracted are then converted into a form of vectors and the cosine similarity is used to generate the relevance scores between resumes and job descriptions. On these scores, the candidates are ranked so as to help the recruiters in short listing the most appropriate candidates. They are experimentally tested and prove that the offered system is characterized by the high accuracy of resume parsing, generating rankings which are similar to the expectations of recruiters, causing a significant shortening of the screening process, as opposed to the traditional ones. Moreover, real-time rejection and selection messages are used to increase the transparency of the recruitment process. The suggested solution is a fair, efficient, and scalable method of automated hiring and enhances the efficiency of the contemporary recruitment system appropriately.

title: An ML Model to detect Cyber-attacks for Wireless Sensor Networks in Microgrids
authors: Harshitha H B, Pavithra A C, Manjunath K, Juslin F
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Microgrids' operating efficiency has been completely transformed by the development of smart energy systems and the incorporation of Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs). The stability, security, and dependability of microgrid infrastructures are seriously threatened by these technical advancements, which also increase susceptibility to cyberattacks. WSNs are especially vulnerable to a range of cyberattacks, such as Denial of Service (DoS), data integrity breaches, reconnaissance assaults, and more sophisticated persistent threats, because of their resource-constrained design, wireless communication protocols, and widespread deployment A thorough machine learning-based approach for identifying cyberattacks in WSNs installed in microgrid systems is presented in this study. The suggested system is intended to use intelligent classification algorithms to effectively categorize hostile actions, learn from past patterns, and proactively monitor network traffic and sensor data. To find the best method for real-time intrusion detection under microgrid-specific limitations, a number of machine learning approaches were assessed, including decision trees, random forests, support vector machines, and ensemble learning. Data collection and preprocessing were the first steps of a multi-phase methodology that included feature selection, model training, and a thorough performance evaluation. Both publicly accessible benchmark datasets designed for network intrusion detection and simulated cyberattack scenarios were used to train and evaluate the model. To guarantee a fair trade- off between detection efficacy and computing efficiency, key performance parameters including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and detection latency were examined The study's findings show that the suggested model may effectively generalize to novel and changing assault patterns while achieving high detection accuracy and a low false positive rate. Additionally, the system is flexible and scalable, which allows it to be integrated into actual microgrid designs without sacrificing their functionality. To sum up, this work offers a fresh and useful approach to the increasing cybersecurity issues that contemporary energy systems face. The framework improves microgrid resilience and lays the groundwork for further study of safe, sustainable, and self-sufficient energy systems by utilizing machine learning for intelligent threat identification.

title: Research on Ceramic Product Classification and Localization Algorithm Based on Deep Learning
authors: Yihong Yuan, Yanke Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Due to uncertainties in the manufacturing process and interference from human factors, various anomalies can occur in ceramic products during production. These anomalies not only affect the quality of the products but may also pose a threat to user safety. Therefore, using anomaly detection methods is crucial for improving the quality and safety of ceramic products. In light of this, this paper proposes a target detection algorithm based on YOLOv8 for the classification and localization of ceramic products, leveraging computer vision detection technology. The algorithm employs a lightweight model and incorporates an attention mechanism, ensuring high accuracy while simultaneously enhancing the inference speed. This makes the algorithm more accurate and efficient in collecting object category information and key point location information. The improved algorithm is then tested, and experimental results demonstrate a significant improvement in both accuracy and speed, achieving an average precision of 97.8% on the test set. Compared to manual inspection, this method improves efficiency and accuracy and addresses the long-tailed distribution problem of positive and negative samples encountered in actual industrial production.

title: MMS-DETR: Fusing State Space Model and Mixed Aggregation for Small Object Detection on USVs
authors: Jizhang Luo, Feng Liu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: Unmanned Surface Vehicles(USVs) are playing an increasingly crucial role in key areas such as marine monitoring and maritime search and rescue. The accurate and realtime detection of small water-surface objects, such as persons overboard and small obstacles, is a core technical bottleneck for achieving autonomous navigation. Currently, this task faces severe challenges, including minute target scales, sparse features, and complex dynamic background interference from waves and reflections. To address these issues, this paper proposes MMS-DETR, an end-to-end network specifically designed for small object detection on the water surface. First, we designed the MambaVision Cross Stage Partial(M-CSP) module in the backbone network, which integrates the powerful long-range dependency modeling capability of the Vision State Space Model (MambaVision) with the efficient feature reuse concept of the CSP architecture. While maintaining high computational efficiency, this integration significantly enhances the model's perception of global context, thereby more effectively distinguishing minuscule targets from complex backgrounds. Second, we introduced a Mixed Aggregation RepC (MA-RepC) module to optimize the multi-scale feature fusion in the neck. This module deeply integrates a mixed aggregation network, Faster-Block, and a convolutional gated unit, improving the robustness of feature representation while effectively preventing the loss of small target information. Experimental results on the Flow-Image dataset show that, compared to the original RT-DETR-r18, the proposed model improves mAP @ 0.5 by 3.7% and mAP @ 0.5: 0.95 by 3.1%, while reducing parameters and computational cost by 19% and 5. 6%, respectively. It also demonstrates superior performance in small object detection on water surfaces compared to other mainstream algorithms.

title: An Efficient Anaphora Resolution Framework for Text Summarization
authors: Farjana Farvin Sahapudeen, D. Vaishnavi, N. Moratanch, M. Anuradha, R. Ramya
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Anaphora resolution is a challenging discourse-level task that involves identifying the correct antecedents of referential expressions in text. This work presents a novel rule-based approach for resolving pronominal and nominal anaphora, including references to verb phrase antecedents. Unlike conventional methods that rely mainly on gender and number agreement, the proposed system incorporates high-level syntactic and semantic rules to improve resolution accuracy. These rules are integrated with fine-grained linguistic constraints reported in earlier studies, enhancing overall efficiency and robustness. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms standard anaphora resolution systems by effectively utilizing rich linguistic information for antecedent identification. Anaphora resolution plays a vital role in natural language processing tasks such as term extraction, question answering, and text summarization. In this study, the proposed pronominal anaphora resolution method is applied within a cluster-based summarization framework to improve summary coherence and reduce ambiguity caused by unresolved references and achieved 4.7 ROUGE Score.

title: ATPG-Compliant Digital Modeling of MRAM for Cell-Aware Testing
authors: Arshid Nisar, Lorena Anghel, Gregory di Pendina
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: As memory complexity increases, traditional fault models are falling short in ensuring test quality. Cell-Aware (CA) testing, using post-layout transistor-level analysis, has proven effective for static random access memory (SRAM). The methodology uses detailed CA models which are further processed by Automatic Test Pattern Generators (ATPG) to create targeted test patterns that ensure high fault coverage. Despite its success in digital circuits, the application of ATPG-based CA testing to emerging non-volatile memory technologies such as Magnetic Random Access Memory (MRAM) remains largely unexplored. In this work, we propose a methodology dedicated to MRAM testing by developing a Verilog model derived from an analog SPICE netlist. This digital model accurately captures the architectural and behavioral traits of the underlying MRAM cell, ensuring compatibility with standard digital test tools such as Automatic Test Pattern Generators (ATPG). The proposed framework enables the generation of ATPG-based test patterns and supports fault simulation, paving the way for realistic, robust and accurate test strategies for MRAM devices.

title: Saliency-Guided YOLO11 for Robust Player Detection in Counterstrike 2 Game Scenes
authors: Chenxi Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Detecting players in first-person shooting (FPS) games such as Counterstrike 2 (CS2) is a challenge mission due to rapid movements, complex lighting, and varied character poses. To solve these difficulties, this paper introduces a Saliency-Guided YOLO11 framework that augments standard detection by integrating a visual saliency attention branch. This extended module enables the network to learn both object localization and saliency prediction simultaneously, guiding the detector to emphasize crucial visual cues like player contours and weapon edges. The model is trained on a custom CS2 dataset with diverse data augmentation strategies-such as Mosaic, Mix-up, and random perspective-to better mimic dynamic in-game environments. Experimental evaluations shows that our method achieves an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{mAP} @ 0.5$</tex> of 0.784, outperforming the baseline YOLO11 by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+2.6 \%$</tex>, and showing improved resilience against occlusion, low contrast, and fastmotion conditions. Further analysis through Precision-Recall and F1-Confidence curves indicates steadier detection confidence and fewer false positives. The results suggest that giving detection models a sense of visual saliency helps them see and recognize game scenes more accurately in real time.

title: Benchmarking CNN Components in EZKL: A Layer-Level Analysis for EVM-Compatible Deployment
authors: George Chidera Akor, Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Zero-knowledge machine learning (ZKML) enables verifiable inference on private data, but deploying convolutional neural networks (CNNs) in production remains constrained by a multi-dimensional tradeoff between proof-generation latency, bandwidth consumption, and computational complexity. Existing ZKML frameworks and engineering blogs provide qualitative heuristics, yet practitioners lack systematic, layer-level measurements to guide architecture design under these constraints. This work presents the first systematic, layer-level characterization of CNN component costs in EZKL, a Halo2-based ZKML framework targeting EVM-compatible blockchains. We profile 8 feasible layer types (activations, pooling, normalization, and linear) across two EZKL precision settings (scale 7 and 10), measuring proof-generation time, proof size, circuit complexity, and peak memory in 26 experiments. We reveal critical infrastructure requirements by documenting 10 additional experiments that exceeded hardware limits (Conv2d operations, LayerNorm, and ReLU-based composite CNNs requiring <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>125\ \text{GB}$</tex> RAM). Contrary to conventional wisdom, we find that precision configuration has a negligible performance impact (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.002 \times$</tex> ratio), and that system RAM, not GPU VRAM, is the primary bottleneck. We release an open-source profiling toolkit and a public dataset that enable practitioners to query expected costs for their architectures and constraints.

title: Study on the Armour Loss of Three-Core Submarine Cables
authors: Shihu Yu, Yongbin Liu, Ji Wu, Nan Cao, Xin Yu
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: In recent years, the installed capacity of offshore wind power has increased significantly, leading to a substantial rise in the number of submarine cables used for power transmission. A thorough understanding of the armor losses in submarine cables is a prerequisite for the economical design of power cables. This paper uses the COMSOL simulation platform to build a multi-physical field coupling model of electromagnetic current for three-core submarine cables, calculates the armor losses and current-carrying capacity of submarine cables using the finite element method, and corrects the IEC calculation results based on the simulation results. The results show that for the three-core submarine cables studied in this paper, the IEC standard overestimates the cable losses and underestimates the cable's current-carrying capacity. The corrected current-carrying capacity is about 7% higher than the value calculated by the IEC formula. This study can provide ideas for increasing the currentcarrying capacity of submarine cables and reducing economic costs.

title: Study of Fault Location in Distribution Network with Inverter-Based Distributed Power Supply
authors: Xia Wang, Qingxue Liu, Zhe Zhang, Xinzhi Li, Yun Su, Jianghong Wang
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: The Distribution network is the most frequent fault link in the power system, accurate and fast fault location algorithm plays a crucial role in the safe operation of the distribution network. A large number of IIDG (Inverter Interfaced Distributed Generation) connections not only change the power flow distribution of the distribution network under the normal state, but also change the current characteristics under the fault state the traditional fault location methods cannot be directly applied to the distribution network with IIDG. Therefore, according to the national requirements of Low Voltage Ride Through (LVRT) control strategy for IIDG, this paper proposes a method of rapid fault identification using the difference of the feeder terminal unit (Feeder Terminal Unit, FTU); finally, the method is verified using MATLAB / Simulink software.

title: Dispatch Algorithm for Power Service Repair Resources Based on Multi-Source Data Fusion
authors: Jianguang Yao, Yanfang Mao, Zhonghao Qian, Xiaoyi Xu, Xiaoxiang Lv
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: This paper proposes a multi-time-interval fault repair strategy considering coordinated restoration between transportation network operation and regional distribution networks. A system implementation recovery index is introduced to quantify the current restoration level of the distribution network, with corresponding adjustments made to the transportation network operation based on this indicator. The transportation network transmits the solved minimum movement time matrix of repair crews to the distribution network for fault recovery operations, scheduling both the repair plan for maintenance teams and the access plan for mobile energy storage devices. Leveraging feeder telemetry data from the distribution network automation system, a multiple regression analysis algorithm is employed for efficient identification of potential fault locations and types. An intelligent analysis system performs comparative assessment of distribution transformer health conditions and historical operational data to precisely locate fault sources. Case studies ultimately validate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: Human-Centric Digital Leadership and e-HRM Effectiveness in Emerging Economies: A Post-Digital Transformation Perspective
authors: Ansa Savad Salim, Karim Ben Yahia
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: The post-digital transformation era necessitates a reevaluation of leadership paradigms, shifting focus from technological adoption toward human-technology synergy. This study introduces and empirically validates the construct of Human-Centric Digital Leadership (HCDL), integrating ethical awareness, digital empathy, employee vitality, and community orientation within digitalized work environments. Using a quantitative, cross-sectional survey design, the research draws on the Digital Humanism framework and the Information Systems Success Model to examine how HCDL influences Digital Human Resource Management Effectiveness (DHRE). A total of 410 employees from 85 SMEs in Tunisia, Bahrain, and India participated in the study, selected because these firms had undergone significant digital transition and had used a formal e-HRM system for at least two years-ensuring respondents had relevant experience with digital HR practices. Data were analyzed using correlation and regression techniques. Findings show that HCDL significantly predicts DHRE, with ethical awareness and digital empathy emerging as the strongest drivers of system quality, user satisfaction, and perceived net benefits. This study advances humancentric digital leadership research in emerging economy contexts and offers a validated framework for fostering sustainable, human-led digital transformation.

title: Improved Droop Control Strategy for Islanded Operation of Energy Storage Converter for Underground Local Ventilation Fan
authors: Fangjun Han
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: To make sure the continuous and reliable power supply of local ventilation fan in underground coal mine, for the problem of traditional inductive sag control failure due to the high resistance-inductance ratio of underground low-voltage distribution network, this paper puts forward a three-loop P-U sag power control strategy based on the calibration of the low-voltage distribution network, and builds a two-stage storage converter emergency power supply model in Matlab/Simulink for simulation and validation. A two-stage energy storage converter emergency power supply model is constructed in Matlab/Simulink for simulation verification. The results reveal that the proposed control strategy can effectively maintain the voltage and frequency stability of the islanded system, realize the precise distribution and fast regulation of power, with a low total harmonic distortion rate of the output current and a good dynamic response, which ensures the stable operation of the energy storage converter in the emergency power supply mode, so as to provide uninterrupted electric power support for the local ventilator, and can effectively improve the level of safe production in coal mines.

title: Comparative Analysis of Classical and Genetic Algorithm Based Portfolio Optimization Using Historical Stock Data
authors: Vinod Jain
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: A key issue in financial analytics is portfolio optimization, which aims to optimize asset allocation to maximize returns while minimizing risk. Due to their simplicity and theoretical rigor, traditional methods like Markowitz meanvariance optimization have been used extensively. However, nonlinearity, dynamic asset behavior, numerous objectives, and market limits present difficulties for these traditional approaches in real-world financial markets. By imitating natural selection and evolution, evolutionary algorithms, like as Genetic Algorithms (GA), offer a viable substitute for portfolio optimization in complicated situations. Using historical stock price data from Microsoft (MSFT), Apple (AAPL), Amazon (AMZN), and Google (GOOG) from February 2022 to February 2023, this article gives a thorough comparison between GA-based optimization and classical minimum-risk portfolio optimization. The paper describes the dataset's structure, portfolio generation methods, assessment measures, and visualization strategies. The results show that the GA strategy delivers a more diversified allocation (maximum weight of 0.3030) with somewhat higher volatility <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\sigma p=0.3571)$</tex>, whereas the conventional approach produces a lower-risk, highly concentrated portfolio (minimum variance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sigma p=0.3372$</tex> and a Sharpe ratio of - 0.4146). This comparative analysis shows that the GA's flexibility and search capabilities produce a robustly diversified strategy, a crucial benefit in complex and uncertain financial markets, whereas traditional optimization excels at minimum-variance allocation.

title: Field Oriented Control of PMSM Drive: A Comparative Study of Sinusoidal and Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation Techniques
authors: Manish Kumar, Vinod Kumar Bussa, Tarakanath Kobaku, R Jeyasenthil
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSMs) are multipurpose motors as they can be applied in electric vehicles, robotics, and factory automation due to a broad range of operations as highly efficient and compact inside and a high extent of torque feature. The control of PMSMs implies a high level of precision as well to allow the maximum level of performance to be preferred under various operational circumstances. This paper will place a comparison of two popular modulation techniques: Sinusoidal Pulse Width Modulation (SPWM) and Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation (SVPWM) in a Field-Oriented Control (FOC) model. The test is applied on dynamic response, torque ripple, harmonic distortion and overall efficiency. The simplest of these SPWM generates the comparison between sinusoidal references and a high frequency (HF) triangular carrier to generate switching pulses, but has the poorest overall harmonic distortion (THD) and poorest DC bus usage. On the other hand, SVPWM calculates the optimum voltage vectors, and this is used to offer a superior voltage utilization and low THD. Simulation proves that the SVPWM can be better in the case of a lower torque ripple as well as the quality of harmonics hence can be utilized in high-performance applications. However, in less complex systems where the performance requirements are moderate, it is possible to employ SPWM. The current research can be useful in selecting the modulation techniques that are expected to be employed in the PMSM-based controllers.

title: Developer Sentiment in Open Source: Effects of Commit Frequency, Time, and Issue Characteristics
authors: Mahmudur Rahman, Mohammad Kawsar Ahamed Siam, Moudud Hassan, Shohel Ahmed
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Developer emotions in open-source projects strongly influence productivity, code quality, creativity, and team dynamics. Sentiment analysis of commit messages provides insights into these emotional states and their impact on project outcomes. In this study, we analyzed 630,164 commit messages from the 20 MAD dataset using a fine-tuned seBERT model. Contributors were grouped by commit frequency to examine how sentiment varies with activity levels. We also studied correlations with commit time, issue type (bug vs. non-bug), and priority. Our findings show that low and medium-frequency contributors express more negative sentiment. Commits made in the afternoon are generally more negative than morning or late-night commits. High-priority bug-related tasks trigger stronger negative emotions. We validated our findings using multiple parametric tests for statistical significance. Our findings can help project managers intervene early with support, adjust schedules, manage workloads, and provide emotional guidance during stressful periods.

title: Design of a Rehabilitation Device for Hand Grip and Wrist Mobility with an Interactive Interface and IoT Capabilities
authors: Castalia Leiva-Cordero, Arys Carrasquilla-Batista, Gabriela Ortiz-León
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: This article presents a prototype of a rehabilitation system for grip strength and wrist mobility. It is part of the REHAB-GYM project in the Mechatronics and Automation Research Laboratory, whose main objective is to address the challenges related to the accessibility of rehabilitation devices in Costa Rica and to improve user progress tracking through technology. The outcomes allow for the construction of a rehabilitation device that measures the grip strength applied by the user and the angles of wrist movements using potentiometers as sensors and a Teensy microcontroller. The system includes a playful and interactive graphical interface, which is an Android application that allows the user to set goals and transmit information via IoT.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Antenna Metamaterial Structures for Wireless IoT Applications
authors: Sakthevel. T. C, Sugumar. D, Sherlin Leela Princy. S, Allwin Devaraj. S
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Artificially created structures known as metamaterials have distinct electromagnetic properties not present in natural materials. Split Ring Resonators (SRR), Complementary SRR (CSRR), Omega structures, Mushroom structures, Frequency Selective Surfaces (FSS), High-Impedance Surfaces (HIS), Chiral metamaterials (CMM), and Fractal geometries (FG) are just a few of the metamaterial structures that are thoroughly examined in this paper. Each structure is examined according to its operating principle, equivalent LC circuit, resonance mechanism, and possible uses in electromagnetic manipulation, filtering, and antenna miniaturization. Researchers working on metamaterial-based antennas and RF components can refer to this review.

title: Design and Implementation of Pulse Signal Analyzer Function on Spectrum Analyzer
authors: Xinghai Liu, Xiangdong Cui, Yuan Ou, Ying Zhao, Peipei Fang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Complex pulse modulated signals are widely used in radar, electronic countermeasure and other weapons and equipment. It is necessary to measure and analyze the pulse modulated signals efficiently and comprehensively in order to accurately evaluate the performance of radar systems and other equipment. This paper designs and realizes the pulse signal analysis function based on the spectrum analyzer. Through the research on the measurement principle, pulse data processing and display processing flow of the pulse signal analysis function, the overall implementation scheme of the pulse analysis function is designed. This scheme adopts an independent plug-in structure, through the combination of Server service process and UI display process, and through shared memory to read parameter status, transfer and obtain display results, to achieve the separation of business logic and display interface, and successfully applies the pulse signal analysis function to the spectrum analyzer software platform, achieving good results.

title: Dynamic Spectrum Allocation via Quantum-Driven Learning for Cognitive Radio Systems
authors: G. Amjad Khan, Rajganesh A, P. Thenmozhi, V. Anitha Lakshmi, R. Krishnamoorthy, R. Thiagarajan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: The sharp increase in the number of wireless devices has increased the need of dynamic and smart spectrum management within Cognitive Radio Networks (CRNs). The traditional methods of static allocation are not efficient towards responding to real-time spectrum scarcity and dynamic channel environment. In this paper, a new Quantum-Driven Reinforcement Learning (QRL) paradigm is proposed as an efficient and safe method of allocating dynamically the spectrum in CRNs. The suggested model uses the principles of quantum amplitude encoding and Grover-inspired quantum search to allow the Secondary Users (SUs) to acquire the knowledge of spectrum availability at a higher rate of convergence and reduced collision risk with the Primary Users (PUs). The CRN simulation environment has real world limitations, that are multipath fading, user mobility and adversarial interference. The suggested QRL model was tested in comparison with the classical Q-learning, Deep Q-Networks (DQN), and Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifiers. Findings indicate that the QRL model attains 95.0 % efficiency in spectrum utilization, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 7 \%} \text{PU}$</tex> interference and a faster convergence rate (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 \%}$</tex>) than DQN. In addition, the QRL architecture has less inference latency and scales to multiple SU agents, and it is also computationally efficient. These advancements confirm the possibilities of quantum-enhanced learning to real-time spectrum decision-making of next-generation 6 G and IoTdriven wireless networks. The paper draws a conclusion that quantum-based decision agents will be capable of offering adaptive, energy efficient and interference aware spectrum allocation even in highly dynamic settings. Further development of this project will be to execute quantum circuit optimization and to test the model on quantum processors hardware to determine the viability of real-world implementation in distributed spectrum systems.

title: Research on Vehicle Multi-Working-Condition Braking Force Detection Platform Based on Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Ziyi Sun, Jiapeng Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of accurately evaluating vehicle braking performance under complex road and thermal environments by proposing a multi-workingcondition braking force detection platform integrated with the Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithm. The platform simulates road conditions with varying friction coefficients <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.15 \sim 0.85$</tex>, a span of 467 %) through replaceable modular friction plates, including dry asphalt, wet slippery surfaces, and snow/ice-covered roads. A built-in air-guided thermal control system in the support rollers enables active regulation of friction surface temperature. The PSO algorithm is introduced to identify key parameters of the LuGre dynamic friction model, while a one-dimensional transient heat conduction-PID feedback control model based on Fourier's law is established to achieve dynamic collaborative regulation of friction-thermal coupling factors. Simulation results show that the braking distance error under typical working conditions is less than 5%, and the temperature field uniformity is less than 1.5° C, significantly improving the efficiency and accuracy of braking performance testing in complex scenarios. The platform can be applied to braking system development, friction material screening, and vehicle multi-condition verification. Future research may integrate intelligent condition recognition and CAN bus communication to expand testing capabilities for advanced braking strategies such as ABS/ESC.

title: HHM-UNet: Hierarchical Heterogeneous State Space Modeling for Residential Floor Plan Semantic Segmentation
authors: Liangliang Su, Haoran Jiang, Jiamu Sheng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Semantic segmentation of residential floor plans is a fundamental task for building information modeling and indoor space parsing, yet it remains challenging due to complex spatial structures, multi-scale components, and long-range semantic dependencies. To address these issues, we propose Hierarchical Heterogeneous Mamba-UNet (HHM-UNet), a novel semantic segmentation framework based on hierarchical state space modeling. Built upon the U-Net architecture, HHM-UNet employs a dual-branch convolutional encoder and selectively integrates state space modules across different semantic levels. Specifically, boundary-aware state space modeling enhances local structural details at shallow layers, directional scanning modules capture oriented architectural patterns at intermediate layers, and cross-layer state space modeling encodes global semantic relationships at deeper layers. FloorStruct1025 dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves substantial performance improvements, outperforming existing state-of-the-art approaches by 7.6% in mean Intersection over Union (mIoU). In addition, HHM-UNet exhibits strong robustness and high accuracy in segmenting room regions, structural components, and fine-grained objects.

title: Efficient Spectrum Utilization in Cognitive Radio Networks Using Advanced Cluster-Assisted Spectrum Sensing Techniques
authors: Savita, Aarif Ahamed S, Keerti Rai
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: Cognitive Radio Networks (CRNs) have emerged as a promising solution to address spectrum scarcity and inefficient spectrum utilization in modern wireless communication systems. However, an essential challenge in CRNs is achieving accurate and efficient spectrum sensing, which ensures reliable detection of available spectrum opportunities while minimizing false alarms and missed detections. This research proposes an advanced Cluster-Assisted Spectrum Sensing (CASS) technique that leverages a Machine Learning (ML)-based approach to enhance detection performance while reducing energy consumption. An Adaptive Tunicate Swarm-optimized Scalable Elman neural network (ATS-SENN) is introduced to improve spectrum gap identification, ensuring reliable detection of Primary User (PU) activity. By aggregating data at Cluster Heads (CHs), the suggested clustering mechanism reduces the burden on the fusion center (FC) by grouping Secondary Users (SUs) according to similarity criteria. Adaptive Tunicate Swarm Optimization (ATSO) is used to fine-tune the network's parameters dynamically, improving its ability to detect available spectrum gaps accurately. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the Hybrid Support Vector Machine-Red Deer Algorithm (SVM-RDA) achieves a high probability of finding solutions and reduces the likelihood of error to a low level, outperforming conventional clustering techniques in both accuracy and computational efficiency. These findings highlight the effectiveness of the proposed CASS framework in enhancing spectrum, maximizing Energy Efficiency (EE), and minimizing probability error, combined with a Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR) of 0.9548, and maximizing the throughput and optimizing spectrum sensing in CRNs.

title: Real-Time Multilingual Chat Translator
authors: Goutham R, Hamjath S, Sabeer Ahamed N, B. Mathiarasi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Rapidly growing global digital communication increases the demand for real-time language translation systems that are secure and privacy-preserving. Most currently available multilingual chat applications utilize centralized servers to route messages and translate their contents, hence exposing sensitive user data and metadata to security risks and unauthorized access. To meet these needs and challenges, this paper describes a Real-Time Multilingual Chat Translator, which is a decentralized peer-to-peer communication platform that integrates end-to-end encryption with artificial intelligence-based language translation. The model proposes WebRTC for establishing direct device-to-device connections, thereby eliminating the use of centralized messaging servers. To provide confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity of user communications, a hybrid cryptographic framework using RSA-OAEP for secure key exchange and AES-GCM for message encryption has been implemented. Google Gemini AI has been integrated for offering accurate, context-aware, real-time translation support in multiple languages. Regarding privacy, real-time translation is performed only on demand, initiated solely by the user. The application has been implemented using React and TypeScript, allowing for a responsive and intuitive user interface while enabling users to work comfortably with desktop and mobile platforms. Experimental evaluation proves that the proposed system enables low-latency communication while ensuring robust security and reliable accuracy of translation. Thereafter, the Real-Time Multilingual Chat Translator offers effective crossing of language barriers without violating user privacy, thereby making the scalable solution viable for global real-time communication.

title: Hierarchical Session-Aware Recommendation Model Based on Temporal Reasoning
authors: Li QinGhai, Li Yanhong, Xu Renyi, Li Yan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Session-Based Recommendation Systems (SRS) aim to predict users' next behaviors via anonymous sessions, with the core challenge of simulating the temporal relationships of user behaviors and depicting user interests using limited sessions. Existing methods mostly model behavior patterns based on the temporal relationships of adjacent items in sessions and selectively aggregate item information as user interests. This paper proposes a Temporal Reasoning-based Hierarchical Session-Aware Recommendation Model (TRHSR): on one hand, it breaks the assumption of “adjacent items being relevant”, infers dependencies between items to learn more flexible temporal relationships; on the other hand, it aggregates information at both the item and item feature levels to achieve fine-grained interest inference. Experiments on two public datasets (Diginetica and Retailrocket) show that the model outperforms other benchmark models, verifying its effectiveness.

title: Research on Multi-Dimensional Prediction Methods Based on Random Forest and Xgboost Algorithms
authors: Huiwan Wang, Bingyan Li, Keyi Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: This study established a multi-dimensional prediction framework centred on random forest and XGBoost. Firstly, baseline prediction values are generated using a theoretical model, combined with time series decomposition and K-means clustering to extract multi-scale features, thereby completing system characteristic diagnosis. Secondly, three core models are constructed for different scenarios: when relying solely on historical data, the ‘Fourier basis function’ baseline is combined with an LSTM/TCN recurrent collaborative framework, integrating lagged time series features and sine/cosine periodic signals, and achieving multi-step prediction through the collaborative use of SAR linear and non-linear models. In multi-source data scenarios, an XGBoost residual correction framework is constructed to learn the mapping relationship between external parameters and prediction residuals, combined with quantile mapping algorithms to suppress systematic biases. For spatial scale adaptation, a dualpath downscaling framework combining ‘random forest + convolutional super-resolution network’ is proposed, using static factors to downscale coarse-grid fields, followed by Kriging interpolation and linear response coefficients to correct the results. This framework achieves high accuracy and strong robustness in complex scenario predictions through the deep integration of mechanistic models and data-driven algorithms, validated by significance tests, providing a transferable technical paradigm for related fields.

title: A Spatial-Temporal Learning Framework for Robust Deepfake Video Detection
authors: G. Shanmukhi Rama, Swathi Edem, Isha Padhy
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Deepfakes have become increasingly popular due to the rapid growth of generative artificial intelligence (AI) technology. These convincingly modified media typically contain invented material that is difficult to differentiate from reality. In the digital age, identifying these deepfakes is a crucial task for guaranteeing the authenticity and originality of information. Existing solutions primarily focus on spatial consistency, often neglecting temporal inconsistencies that occur between frames in deepfake videos. To address this, we present a deepfake detection model that leverages the Swin Transformer for spatial feature extraction and 3D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for temporal consistency learning. The extracted feature representations are fused using a feature concatenation strategy to enhance classification performance. The model was trained on the FaceForensics++ dataset and evaluated for generalization on Celeb-DF(v2), achieving an accuracy of 89%. These results demonstrate its robustness in detecting deepfakes across different datasets, highlighting its effectiveness in diverse and challenging evaluation settings.

title: Technology Trend Forecasting and Idea Support Incorporating Generative AI Using Patent Information
authors: Yurie Okuhara, António Oliveira Nzinga René
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The rapid accumulation of patent documents presents challenges for traditional analysis methods due to the high dimensionality and volume of data. This study proposes an integrated framework that combines Transformer-based Sentence-BERT embeddings, UMAP dimensionality reduction, and k-medoids clustering to extract and visualize meaningful patent clusters. Selected clusters are analyzed through 2D and 3D visualizations to reveal core and peripheral technological concepts, semantic densities, and inter-cluster relationships. Furthermore, generative AI is employed to provide descriptive summaries and predictive insights for each cluster, enabling the identification of emerging technological trends and potential applications. The proposed approach enhances interpretability, supports strategic decision-making in research and development, and demonstrates a scalable method for AI-assisted patent landscape analysis.

title: Visualization and Search Method for Secondary Full Circuit of Substation Based on SDD and Forward-Probing and Backward-Tracking Search Algorithm
authors: Jin Li, Weiguo Yu, Yuansheng Chen, Hongjun Liu
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problem that existing SCD analysis software and methods cannot achieve complete visualization of the secondary circuit of substations, a visualization and search method for the secondary full circuit of substations based on SDD and forward-probing and backward-tracking search algorithm is proposed. Firstly, a secondary circuit digital description file (SDD) for the secondary circuit is defined, which includes the secondary system grid identification system code and secondary circuit cable information based on the overall fiber optic physical circuit configuration file. Then, combining the SSD model and the grid information model (GIM), a visualization system for the secondary full circuit of the substation is constructed, which includes geometric element intersection calculation, model cutting, model fitting, and other steps. Finally, the forward-probing and backward-tracking search algorithm is used to search for the secondary circuit of the substation, which involves searching for the target point from the source point through forward search and obtaining the connected path between the two points through backtracking, ensuring fast and accurate search for the secondary circuit. Experimental tests are conducted on the proposed method based on the secondary circuit of a 220 kV substation. The results shows that when the number of circuits is 120, the visualization and search accuracies are about 88% and 93%, respectively. The search time does not exceed 10s, which can meet the needs of intelligent development of substations.

title: A Multidimensional Data Warehouse Approach to Exploring the Poverty's Role in Child Abuse and Teenage Pregnancy in Sri Lanka
authors: Nishadi Jayasooriya, Kavinda Gihan Bandaranayake, Diyorina Fernando, Tharushika Silva, Dinujaya Liyanaarachchi, PPG Dinesh Asanka
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Child abuse and teenage pregnancy remain a critical social challenge in developing countries like Sri Lanka where poverty constitutes a main social determinant influencing child welfare. Most of such vulnerabilities continue to be influenced by complex interactions between economic, social, and demographic factors. As a major limitation in existing research, absence of an integrated analytical framework to study the interactions between such factors across populations could be identified. This study addresses that gap by applying a multidimensional data warehouse and OLAP-based analytical methodology to explore the relationship of poverty with child abuse and teenage pregnancy in the country. A heterogeneous dataset covering 31000 records from 16 districts of the year 2024-2025 was integrated through a structured ETL process and modeled using a galaxy schema data warehouse. Results indicate only a weak positive correlation between poverty and child abuse and poverty and teenage pregnancy challenging the poverty centric interventions on such problems in the country. The findings suggest that economic deprivation alone does not sufficiently explain these outcomes and demonstrate the need for further studying into other determinants on such social issues. The significance of this study lies in both its empirical and methodological contributions. Empirically it challenges the emphasis on poverty as a singular determinant of child and teenage vulnerability and suggests the need for holistic interventions. Methodologically it emphasizes the application of multi- dimensional data warehousing approach as an evidence based decision support tool for social surveys.

title: Asymmetric Impact of Oil Price Shock on Main Islamic Stock Indices: An MTNARDL Analysis
authors: Somar Al-Mohamad, Amamr Jreisat, Mohamed Sraieb, Audil Rashid Khaki, Nasser El-Kanj, Imad Chehade
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: The aim of this paper is to measure the financial connectedness and extreme tail effect among the oil, bitcoin and gold prices, and returns on the major global Islamic Stock Indices between 2020 and 2025. We employ the Nonlinear Autoregressive Distributed Lag (NARDL) and Multiple Threshold NARDL (MTNARDL) models. The study focuses on capturing the effects of extreme shocks in global oil market along on the main Islamic stock indices by introducing MTNARDL adjusted to reflect extreme shock effects. The outcomes reveal that Islamic indices are generally resilient to extreme shocks in global oil demand and fluctuations in the bitcoin market. However, oil supply shocks demonstrate significant long-run effects on the Dow Jones Islamic World Emerging Index, suggesting that emerging Islamic markets are more vulnerable to supply-side disturbances.
