Moreover, gold price shocks exhibit strong short- and long-run connectedness with all Islamic indices, indicating possible contagion effects and increased portfolio risk when combined with Islamic stocks. This study contributes to the existing literature on the dynamic interactions and connectedness among cryptocurrency and precious metal prices and Islamic financial instruments and offers noticeable insights into their hedging potential and risk exposures, particularly under extreme market conditions.

title: Research on Chinese News Text Classification Based on Improved TF-IDF and MNB
authors: Ting Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: With the rapid development of Internet technology, news data has experienced explosive growth. How to classify massive news quickly and accurately has become an important research topic in the field of information processing. As Chinese news texts are diverse and complex in content, the traditional TF-IDF algorithm merely weights word frequency by inverse document frequency when extracting thematic feature words, ignoring the influence of factors such as part of speech, word length, word position, and word span on word information content, as well as the impact of word distribution in different documents on the importance of keywords. This paper proposes a Chinese text classification model based on improved TF-IDF and MNB. On the basis of the traditional TF-IDF algorithm, the model integrates combined feature factors and dispersion to weight feature words, and applies the MNB classification algorithm to Chinese news text data classification. Experimental results show that the model can effectively improve the automatic classification performance of Chinese news texts, providing strong support for practical news classification scenarios.

title: Phishdet: an Online Phishing Detection System Based on Cnn Model
authors: Minxing Kuang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: This work firstly presents a functional, integrated prototype system that bridges the gap between offline deep learning models and online network security applications. Secondly, through a comprehensive empirical study, it validates the practical suitability of a character-level CNN (PhishDet) as a stable and accurate core for online inference, despite known computational constraints. Our system demonstrates a viable pathway towards shifting the protection paradigm from passive analysis to active warning, providing a foundational framework for future low-latency phishing defense systems.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Secure Data Sharing in Cyber-Physical Systems: A Comparative Study
authors: P. Arthi, Swapna S, Sangeetha K, Chandrika G, Moorthy Agoramoorthy, K. Malathy
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) are computer-based systems that combine computer-related intelligence and realworld phenomena and are actively used in such spheres as intelligent transportation, smart healthcare, industrial control, energy systems, and others. Reliable and safe information exchange between heterogeneous CPS components has been a significant obstacle because of the problem of data integrity, access control, scalability, and management of trust. The promising element to mitigate these challenges has been the blockchain technology that offers decentralized, immutable and auditable data exchange mechanisms. The paper provides a comparative analysis of data sharing solutions in CPS environments, based on blockchain-enabled secure data sharing. The paper compares various blockchain designs, consensus algorithms and data management plans with regard to security, latency, scalability and system reliability. By making comparative assessment, the paper shows the advantages and shortcomings of the currently available blockchain-based CPS solutions and makes significant design trade-offs to deploy CPS in the real world. The results have proved that blockchain can greatly contribute to transparency and trust in sharing CPS data, but lightweight and hybrid designs are needed to address the real-time and resources requirements of cyber-physical settings. The research will inform the researchers and practitioners to develop sustainable, secure, and resilient CPS data-sharing models.

title: A Multi-Model Deep Learning Technique for Detecting Malicious Network Traffic
authors: Ogechukwu Okonor, Fakhreldin Saeed, Obinwa Ozonze
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)
abstract: Digital transformation and network transactions have brought about more complex network connectivity and complexity in controlling network traffic, leading to a significant rise in network vulnerabilities. Hence, it opens a gap in networking control strategy, which needs to be equated to rapid digital advancement and the impending numerous malicious attacks. Therefore, adopting proactive, intelligent mechanisms to monitor and manage network communication is imminent. In this paper, deep learning techniques are embedded in our chosen intrusion detection system to monitor and track malicious network traffic proactively. Hence, based on the network behaviour and certain set thresholds, an alert will be triggered while gathering transactions and managing the network activities by event and time. This proposed model then collects the dataset and trains the network to detect transactional traffic flow toward alertness and an efficient detection strategy for network anomalies. This technique will be used on different datasets with a defined malicious traffic suspicion perimeter to evaluate the impact of our proposed technique in mitigating vulnerabilities that led to network anomaly behaviour.

title: Deep Learning-Powered Probing Architecture for Intelligent Anomalous Event Tracking
authors: S. Munirullah, Krishnaraj Natarajan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: The expansion of digital ecosystems has intensified the frequency and complexity of anomalous behaviors across networks, applications, and human interactions. Traditional rule-based detection systems struggle to adapt to evolving threats, resulting in delayed incident response and reduced reliability. This study proposes ProbeAIT, an AI-driven framework for real-time anomalous event identification, explanation, and tracking. ProbeAIT integrates multi-stage feature probing for granular behavioral analysis, temporal pattern extraction via dual-stream processing, and adaptive learning modules combining statistical baselines, deep learning classifiers, and contextual correlation engines to distinguish normal from malicious deviations while remaining interpretable. Empirical evaluation across diverse datasets demonstrates ProbeAIT's superior performance: 96.4% detection accuracy, 97.1% recall, 15% reduction in false positives, and 38 FPS real-time processing compared to CNNand transformer-based baselines. Its self-evolving architecture enables dynamic pattern adaptation. These results establish ProbeAIT as a scalable, intelligent solution for modern anomaly monitoring, bridging gaps in adaptability, interpretability, and operational efficiency.

title: Quantum Computing Approaches for Option Pricing Models
authors: Chandrashekhar Ramtirthkar, Sharyu Ikhar, Ananta Narayana, Jaganathan D, Sodiq Ziyodullayev, Adilbaev Ismayıl Bekbaulievich
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: The article provides a literature review and analysis of quantum computing methods in the context of option pricing, comparing them against a strict classical benchmark and recommending a viable way forward to quantum computing implementation. We assess the capabilities and the weaknesses of Windows-choice models closed-form legacy formulae of vanillas, lattice/PDE true hockeygoal models of early exercise, and Monte Carlo models of flexibleness and point out where classical run attracted and precision restraints are shown to apply in the new generation(s): complexity of variables, in this path dependence, in a portfolio composing. We proceed to describe quantum algorithms that are based on quantum Monte Carlo which is centered on amplitude-estimation-based algorithms to cut down on contact with sampling; avoiding the scale of classical Monte Carlo and are particularly appealing to complex derivatives; we describe circuit components to prepare states, encode payoffs, and approximate expectations. The study establishes research priorities that achieve scale implies: depth frequently used to design loaders in, and variants of payoff circuits, workflow standardizations that exchange the exchange of error-resilient quantum modules and technological risk engines in the NISQ age. In general, the paper offers a single source of information on classical-quantum option pricing, clean insertion architecture to deploy hybrids and a roadmap between demonstrations and reliable production.

title: Neural Machine Translation in English to Tamil Using Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning
authors: Arsath S.M, Tharukshan P., J. Jananie
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Neural Machine Translation (NMT) faces challenges with low-resource language pairs like English-Tamil due to data scarcity and linguistic complexity. This paper investigates enhancing English-to-Tamil translation by efficiently fine-tuning Google's Gemma 3 4B instruction-tuned model. We employed Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), optimized via the Unsloth library, on a 4-bit quantized model. Training was conducted for two epochs on a synthetic English-Tamil dataset (1836 pairs) using the SFTTrainer. Evaluating on a 204-pair test set, our fine-tuned model achieved a BLEU score of 36.12 and a chrF++ score of 65.25. This significantly surpasses the base Gemma 3 4B (BLEU 28.84, chrF++ 59.95) and Llama 3 8B Instruct (BLEU 2.94, chrF++ 21.35) baselines. The results confirm that LoRA-based PEFT, even with synthetic data, provides a resource-efficient and effective method for adapting Large Language Models (LLMs) to improve NMT quality for low-resource languages like Tamil. The fine-tuned model is publicly available.

title: Optimizing the Accuracy of Spam Filtration Techniques Through Q-Learning
authors: Mrunali Bharadwaj D S, Ruhika Sai S, T. V. Smitha
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: This paper puts forward an adaptive reinforcement learning-based approach for spam detection in Online Social Networks. The framework of this approach allows it to effectively handle the rampant problem of concept drift by classifying the spam detection problem as a sequential decision-making problem using the Markov Decision Process. This paper proposes the usage of a Q-learning-based agent to dynamically learn the most optimal policy through continuous interactions between the agent and the environment. The proposed model penalizes false positives and false negatives while rewarding correct classifications, thereby refining the policy over time. The environmental feedback allows the agent to learn continuously, ensuring sustained performance even with evolving spam patterns. This work highlights the promising framework of reinforcement learning models in spam detection platforms while also enabling the development of a long-term spam detection model facilitating improved spam detection in Online Social Networks.

title: Data-Driven Classification and Recognition Algorithm for Traditional Chinese Architectural Styles
authors: Sun Luyao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Aiming at the needs of automatic classification and recognition of traditional Chinese architectural styles, this paper proposes an original algorithm based on multi-scale attention fusion. The algorithm is data-driven. First, a dataset of 10,000 images covering 10 styles such as official buildings and Jiangnan folk houses is constructed. The data quality is improved through preprocessing and enhancement operations such as rotation, scaling, and brightness adjustment. Then, ResNet50 is used as the basic network to extract multi-scale features from conv2_x to conv5_x layers, which include both architectural detail features and global features. Then, channel and spatial attention mechanisms are designed to weight feature channels and spatial regions respectively, enhance the expression of key style features, and then fuse the processed multi-scale features into a comprehensive feature vector. Finally, the classification is completed through the fully connected layer combined with the softmax function. Experimental simulation shows that the algorithm has an accuracy of 92.5 %, a precision of 91.8 %, a recall of 92.1 %, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> value of 91.9 % on the test set. Compared with the traditional SIFT+SVM method (accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 2. 3 \%}$</tex>), it is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0. 2 \%}$</tex> higher, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6. 9 \%}$</tex> higher than ResNet50 (85.6 %), and 10.4 % higher than VGG16 (82.1 %). The ablation experiment verifies the effectiveness of multi-scale features and attention mechanisms. At the same time, the algorithm takes 8.5 hours to train 100 rounds, and the reasoning of a single image is only 0.025 seconds. It takes both accuracy and efficiency to provide an efficient and feasible technical solution for the classification of traditional Chinese architectural styles.

title: Dual-Lane Voice-Preserving Real-Time Speech Translation: A System Architecture for Cross-Lingual Speaker Identity Retention
authors: Aryuemaan Chowdhury, Marpini Himabindu, Madhumithaa S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: In real-time multilingual systems, maintaining speaker identity is still a major technical difficulty. The majority of pipelines now in use handle voice synthesis and translation as separate operations, producing output that eliminates the vocal qualities of the original speaker. In order to preserve vocal identity during cross-language transmission, this research suggests a parallelized architecture. Through the use of a dual-lane framework with synchronized speaker embedding, we are able to maintain a voice similarity score greater than 0.85 in English, Spanish, and French while achieving end-to-end latency below 3.2 seconds. The suggested system incorporates ECAPA-TDNN embeddings feeding into NLLB translation, a hybrid ASR technique with fallback logic, and adaptive speech activity detection. YourTTS, which has been adjusted for cross-lingual adaptability, is used for synthesis. The architecture effectively maintains speaker identification without sacrificing the real-time limitations necessary for conversational usability, according to the results.

title: Thermal Consequences of the 5GHz Wi-Fi Frequency Electromagnetic Field on Humans
authors: Tamar Nozadze, Giorgi Ghvedashvili, Vera Jeladze
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)
abstract: The objective of this study is to investigate the thermal effects caused by human exposure to 5 GHz WI-FI electromagnetic fields (EMF) based on electromagnetic and thermal modeling using the finite difference time domain-FDTD method based software - FDTDLab. We analyzed the human whole body EMF exposure by calculating the Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) and the temperature increase due to the absorption of EMF energy by body tissues. We calculated values for 1g and 10g averaged SAR, whole body averaged SAR, and subsequent tissue temperature increase. The resulting SAR and temperature increase values were evaluated for compliance with international standards and regulations.

title: Research on Interactive Optimization Model of Educational Robot Based on Speech Recognition in Oral English Teaching
authors: Zhuoying Zhou, Junlin Zhai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: This study improves the teaching effect of educational robots by optimizing speech recognition technology, interactive process and providing personalized learning support. In the optimization of speech recognition technology, advanced noise filtering algorithm and highsensitivity microphone array design are studied to reduce the influence of background noise and improve the quality of speech signal acquisition. Aiming at the problem of accent recognition, a multi-accent speech recognition model is constructed, and the user adaptive mechanism is introduced to dynamically adjust the model parameters according to the pronunciation characteristics of students, which significantly improves the recognition accuracy. In the optimization of interaction process, the wake-up mechanism and semantic understanding module of voice interaction are improved, and the natural language generation technology is adopted to make the robot reply more natural and smooth. At the same time, dialogue management module and parallel processing technology are introduced to shorten the response time and improve the naturalness of interaction. In terms of personalized learning support, by analyzing students' pronunciation characteristics, speech speed, intonation and other information, we can understand their oral English level and learning style, offering customized learning materials and feedback accordingly. Teaching content and degree of hardship are dynamically adapted with respect to the learning process and achievements of students, so as to inspire their interest and motivation for study. 100 student in the second grade of a middle school were chosen as experimental and controlled group respectively. Results show the experimental group has a significantly higher score than control group in speech recognition accuracy, interactive response time and overall improvement of oral expression ability. It shows that the optimized educational robot can recognize students' speech input more accurately, respond to instructions faster, and effectively improve students' oral expression ability, which provides an intelligent, efficient and personalized solution for oral English teaching.

title: Motion Trajectory Prediction and Multi-View Synthesis Based on CycleGAN
authors: Jun Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Aiming at the shortcomings of traditional models in dynamic scene adaptability and multi-view consistency, this paper proposes a synthesis method based on CycleGAN. By improving the generator structure of CycleGAN and introducing a temporal attention mechanism to capture the temporal dependencies of trajectories, a dual-discriminator system is designed to constrain trajectory realism and view consistency respectively. A composite loss function integrating adversarial loss, cycle consistency loss, and temporal smoothness loss is constructed to ensure the rationality of predicted trajectories and the accuracy of multi-view synthesis. Experiments on the ETH, UCY datasets, and a custom multiview trajectory dataset show that the proposed method reduces the ADE and FDE metrics by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 \% - 2 2 \%}$</tex> compared to traditional methods. The PSNR and SSIM of the multi-view synthesis results reach 28.6 dB and 0.89, respectively, demonstrating the method's effectiveness and superiority in complex dynamic scenes.

title: A Hybrid BiLSTM–Transformer–Attention Model for Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: S. M. Ahanaf Tahmid, Ling Lu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) remains challenging under speaker variability, recording conditions, and multi-dataset evaluation. To capture both local and global dependencies in emotional speech, this paper proposes a hybrid BiLSTM–Transformer–Attention model that integrates bidirectional sequence modeling with multi-head self-attention. Robustness is enhanced through 40-dimensional MFCC features and waveform-level data augmentation (noise injection, time shifting, and time stretching) applied exclusively during training. Experiments on the TESS and RAVDESS datasets use a fixed 80/10/10 train-validation-test split with a random seed of 42. The proposed model achieves 99.91% accuracy on TESS and 92.88% accuracy on RAVDESS, demonstrating consistent performance across datasets with differing levels of variability.

title: Explainable Heart-Disease Risk Prediction Through Boosting-Based Classification and Shap-Guided Clinical Feature Interpretation
authors: S. Vairachilai, Markala Karthik, Sandeep Madarapu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Heart disease is one of the major causes of death in the world and it is important that it is predicted at an early stage to enable medical attention. The proposed study suggests an idealized clinical data-informed model, which is comprised of the preprocessing, SMOTE-assisted class balancing, features characteristics selection, and comparative modelling in improving prediction accuracy. Among the entire range of clinical variables, six strongly discriminating features were identified and employed to train Machine Learning and Ensemble Learning models. The findings indicate that the Ensemble Learning performed better than the other methods as AdaBoost had the greatest overall performance of 0.8833 with precision of 1.0000 and ROC-AUC of 0.9621. To guarantee interpretability, SHAP was integrated to make visual the contribution of each feature on the AdaBoost prediction at the individual and global level that would give clinical transparency as opposed to a black-box result. The results prove that the combination of optimized feature subsets, boosting-based learning and SHAP-enabled explainability can provide high predictive power and medical interpretability. The given framework has a high potential of becoming a valid and reliable decision-support tool in terms of early heart-disease risk assessment.

title: Exploring Spatial Redundancy for Boosting the Performance of Stochastic Adcs in Deep Nanometer Cmos
authors: Rodrigo N. Wuerdig, Georges Gielen, Sergio Bampi
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Employing spatial redundancy allows stochastic ADCs to statistically mitigate non-idealities such as mismatch and noise without relying on precise analog components or calibration. This paper introduces the key principles of stochastic averaging, exploring the boundaries in scaled CMOS processes. We demonstrate that the commonly assumed SNDR improvement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \cdot \log_{10}(N) dB$</tex> reaches its limits when the quantization noise overwhelms the thermal noise. We highlight that stochastic yields meaningful improvements in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F o M_{S}$</tex> only when the power increases sub-linearly with the number of sampling channels. We identify architectural strategies that mitigate the power overhead. Finally, this work outlines key design trade-offs and guides when and how to deploy stochastic principles effectively in deepnanometer ADC architectures.

title: Research on Decision-Level Fusion Algorithm for Unmanned Surface Vehicle Object Detection
authors: Jianjun Guo, Jinchao Xiao, Zhijia Zhang, Jihai Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the increasing use of unmanned surface vehicles (USVs) in waterborne inspection, channel monitoring, and port security, higher demands are placed on the accuracy and robustness of environmental perception. However, in complex water surface environments, single sensors suffer from inherent limitations and cannot provide reliable perception. In this work, YOLOv5s is adopted as the image-based baseline detector, and four lightweight backbone networks are evaluated. The results show that EfficientViT with a 20% channel reduction achieves a good balance between inference speed and model size. For point cloud object detection, SECOND, PointPillars, and CenterPoint are trained and compared. CenterPoint achieves the best performance, reaching a BEV AP of 87.7% and a 3D AP of 78.6% at an IoU threshold of 0.5, and a lightweight version of CenterPoint is further designed to reduce computational cost. Finally, a decision-level fusion algorithm is proposed to integrate image and point cloud detections. For each 3D detection, the point cloud inside the bounding box is projected onto the image plane, and a 2D bounding box is generated from the projected points. The IoU between these boxes and image detections is used to construct a cost matrix together with confidence scores, which is solved by the Hungarian algorithm. The final fused results are obtained through confidence-weighted fusion.

title: Optimized Arithmetic Architecture in Cryptographic Processor for IoT Embedded System
authors: Sivanandam K, Sugan N, Vijay P, Yasvonth V L
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The arithmetic architecture of a cryptographic processor with a low-power and high-throughput model with Internet-of-things (IoT) nodes using minimal resources. Resourceconstrained Internet of Things (IoT) devices demand cryptographic solutions that offer strong security with minimal power and hardware overhead. In this work, an optimized arithmetic architecture for a cryptographic processor is presented, targeting low-power IoT embedded systems. The proposed design focuses on improving modular arithmetic operations by replacing conventional adder–multiplexer structures with a LUT-based arithmetic unit optimized for FPGA implementation. This architecture reduces critical path delay, power consumption, and logic utilization while supporting essential cryptographic operations such as modular addition and subtraction. The design is implemented and verified on a Xilinx FPGA using Vivado, where performance is evaluated in terms of power, delay, and power–delay product (PDP). Experimental results demonstrate a significant reduction in power consumption (up to 50%) and improved PDP compared to the existing architecture, validating the suitability of the proposed design for energy-efficient and secure IoT applications.

title: Transformer-Based NLP Models for Predictive Consumer Behavior Analysis
authors: Venkatesh Ankarla Sri Ramuloo, Lokesh Vegi, Sumanth Banakar, Awanit Kumar, Rohit Maheshwari, Ravinder
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: The social media has transformed to be one of the top drivers of consumer perception and purchase intention especially in new luxury markets like India. In this paper, transformer-based Natural Language Processing techniques (NLP) will be suggested as predictive, AI-based methodology to model consumer behavior. Unlike the other past descriptive studies, we use a BERT based classifier of sentiment and intent to predict purchase forecast in social media content. It was further extended to 5,100 Instagram captions and 1,200 online product reviews each having the positive, neutral or negative sentiment labels and the binary purchase-intent indicators annotated on them individually. The models that were analyzed include Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and BERT. According to the experimental outcomes, it has been established that BERT is always superior to the traditional machine learning and deep learning methods with an accuracy of 91.6, F1-score of 0.92 and an area under the ROC curve (AUC) of 0.94 in the prediction of purchase intent. Such results demonstrate that contextual language representations provide a better ability to capture the subtle emotional and aspiration signals in the luxury consumption environment compared to rule-based or association-mining systems. The study provides a predictive low scale alternative to Apriori based market basket analysis and has explicit applications to personalized marketing campaigns, recommendation systems and real-time social listening.

title: Comparison of YOLOv8 and RT-DETR for Road Damage Detection
authors: Chenghuan Jia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: To solve the road damage detection problem, various object detection models have been applied to this task due to the rapid development of deep learning, yet their relative strengths under consistent settings remain underexplored. This study presents a comparative analysis of YOLOv8-m and RT-DETR-I on the subset of the Road Damage Detection (RDD) 2022 dataset using identical training configurations. Both models achieved similar detection accuracy, with minor differences across standard metrics. Whereas, there were also differences in the results. First, Mosaic augmentation yielded model-dependent effects, slightly improving YOLOv8-m while reducing RT-DETR-1 performance. Furthermore, differences in category sensitivity and detection behavior were revealed by per-class heatmaps. RT-DETR performed better on particular categories according to the heatmaps. The findings explain the differences in the aspect of working principles of convolutional and transformer-based detectors.

title: Optimization Model of AIGC Mainstream Ideology Communication Strategy Based on Intent Deconstruction
authors: Na Zhao, Mutong Sun, Hongfang Xu, Xuanjin Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: In the context of the current Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC) technology reshaping the ideological communication pattern, this research aims to construct a mainstream ideological communication strategy optimization model based on intention deconstruction to cope with the opportunities and risks brought by technology. The research adopts a multi-disciplinary approach, combined with deep learning, natural language processing and technology, and designs an algorithm framework including multi-level intention analysis algorithms and geo-cultural adaptation factors. The experimental results show that the model can increase the response time of the system by 33.15 %, the processing throughput by 44.68 %, and the cultural adaptation accuracy by 21.90 %. Moreover, the model's audience ideological recognition increased by 16.89%-19.64%, and the model maintained 76.5 % performance under heavy data poisoning attacks (18.2% better than traditional methods). The research conclusion confirms that the model effectively solves the adaptability problem of cross-cultural communication. It performs well in the ideological management scenario of universities and can help educational institutions achieve precise and intelligent ideological education and communication, but it has some shortcomings such as limited processing ability of non-text content. Therefore, follow-up research will expand multi-modal fusion and extreme scenario adaptability.

title: CSDSM: A Multi-Layered Cybersecurity Framework for Threat Detection and Mitigation in Social Media
authors: Ajit Baral, Bijay Kumar Paikaray, Sumati Baral
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Social media platforms have revolutionized human communication and information sharing, and simultaneously created unprecedented cybersecurity challenges. This focuses on the significant role of cybersecurity in solving the dark side of social media, including malicious activities such as cyberbullying, misinformation campaigns, identity theft, privacy violations and coordinated attacks. Through comprehensive analysis of current threats, existing security frameworks and emerging protective technologies, this study highlights the multifaceted nature of social media security challenges and proposes integrated cybersecurity strategies. The research emphasizes the effective cybersecurity measures are important not only for protecting individual users but also for maintaining societal trust in digital communication platforms and upholding democratic discourse in the digital era.

title: Hybrid Blockchain-Assisted Federated Learning Intrusion Detection System for DoS Attacks in UAV Sensor Network
authors: Odinachi U. Nwankwo, Simeon Okechukwu Ajakwe, Dong-Seong Kim, Jae Min Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly deployed in mission-critical domains, raising significant security concerns. This paper presents a Federated Learning-based Intrusion Detection System (PureChain-FL) for UAV clients, focusing on Denial-of-Service (DoS) attack detection. To enhance trust and auditability, the proposed architecture integrates PureChain, a lightweight blockchain framework based on the Proof of Authority Association (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PoA}^{2}$</tex>) consensus algorithm for secure logging of detected threats. The system features client-based localized model training using the WSN-DS dataset and a global FL server for model aggregation. A smart contract governs audit logging, enabling verifiable and tamper-resistant record-keeping. Evaluation of multilayer perceptron (MLP), one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D CNN), and CNN combined with long-short-term memory (CNN-LSTM) models over 20 FL rounds with five clients using the Flower framework and TensorFlow in a Google collaborative environment revealed CNN-LSTM as the best-performing model with 99.3 % accuracy. Figures and diagrams illustrating system architecture, communication flow, and model training are integrated into the discussion for clarity. The results show promise in the system's effectiveness in privacypreserving and trustworthy intrusion detection for unmanned aerial wireless sensor networks.

title: An Adaptive Genetic Algorithm-Based Energy Storage Optimization Method for Microgrids with Dynamic Electricity Pricing and Reliability Awareness
authors: Ye Xiaohui, Li Ziang, Tan Fucheng
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: Existing microgrid energy storage scheduling strategies often neglect the two-way interaction between storage operation and locational marginal prices, while reliability evaluations relying on static scenarios may underestimate realtime system risks. This paper proposes a game-theoretic model that integrates dynamic LMP feedback and reliability assessment based on Monte Carlo Markov Chain. A novel dynamic state-ofcharge penalty coefficient is introduced to adjust multi-objective weights in the genetic algorithm. Experimental results from a 5node microgrid system demonstrate that, compared to static models, the proposed method achieves a 10.5 % improvement in comprehensive reliability with an income loss of only 6.51 %, thereby realizing better risk-reward balance.

title: Novel and Efficient Plant Disease Prediction Using ViT and YOLOv8 Deep Learning Models
authors: S. Vetriselvi, A. Balamurali, Shakhboz Meylikulov, Yadgarova Nazokat Saparbayevna, Anorgul Ashirova, B. Venkataramanaiah
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Plant health is affected by several abnormal conditions which indicate virus, bacteria and fungus caused by pathogens. Environment conditions cause plant disease such as water stress, pollution and nutrient lack. These diseases cause several symptoms on plants such as spots, stunted growth and wilting. Sanitation is used for curing plants from plant diseases. Plant diseases need to be monitored frequently and early disease prediction can save plant health. Image processing techniques used earlier for plant disease prediction and machine learning techniques replace image processing techniques in disease prediction. Deep learning models are introduced and show accurate results over machine learning. Proposed research uses YOLOv8, Convolution Neural Network(CNN) and Vision Transformer(ViT) deep learning models to predict plant diseases. YOLOv8 deep learning model predicts plant diseases with accuracy 98% over lower accuracy results from CNN and ViT. Proposed research is useful for agriculture field where plant diseases can be predicted more accurate than other existing works.

title: High-Accuracy Facial Emotion Recognition for Visually Impaired Using Multi-Stage Transfer Learning and Attention-Based CNN
authors: P. Kalyanasundaram, P. Meenakshi Devi, N Saravanan, R. Bharanitharan, B. Bharath, Hariprasath M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Aim: To design an accurate facial emotion recognition system that assists visually impaired users by identifying emotions during conversation using an attentionbased CNN and multi-stage transfer learning approach. Materials and Methods: Group 1 is a lightweight CNN model based on partial transfer learning. Group 2 is a multi-stage transfer learning model with an attention-based CNN. A sample size of 2000 images from the FER+ dataset is used for each group, and the models are evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, class-wise recognition rate, and confusionmatrix performance. Result: The propose–d emotion recognition model identified facial emotions with the overall accuracy of 82.80%. The performance was reliable across different emotion classes. Conclusion: The developed facial emotion recognition model has achieved multiple evaluation matrix helps assist visually impaired users to understand people emotions during social interactions.

title: Energy Communities as the Next Step in Smart Grids: A Decision-Making Approach for Decentralized Energy Exchange
authors: David González-Asenjo, Aarón Rodríguez-Marrero, Álvaro Galerón-Saiz, Javier Sedano
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Power Engineering (AIPE)
abstract: Digitalization of electricity infrastructures has accelerated the evolution of Smart Grids, with Energy Communities representing a pivotal advancement in decentralized energy management. This study introduces a decision-making framework that optimizes energy exchanges through a dynamic evaluation process, adapting in real time to participants' preferences on energy usage, considered as User Policies (UPs). To achieve optimal energy coordination, the proposed methodology incorporates demand response strategies, enabling participants to regulate consumption through controllable loads and storage assets. A structured multi-objective optimization approach formalizes decision-making, embedding the five key User Policies (i.e. economic efficiency, peak power mitigation, sustainability, comfort, and energy efficiency) into an evaluation function that quantifies prosumer preferences. Fairness and arbitrage mechanisms are employed to maintain system equilibrium and prevent inefficiencies in energy trading to achieve the goal of long-term sustainability. The framework dynamically reacts to fluctuations, optimizing energy distribution while maintaining system stability and long-term adaptability. Computationally, the framework leverages genetic optimization techniques to enhance prediction accuracy and decision-making efficiency. The results demonstrate significant improvements in energy allocation, peak demand reduction, and financial optimization, validating the framework's effectiveness. By systematically aligning individual and collective energy objectives, this methodology provides a scalable and adaptive approach to decentralized energy management, reinforcing the resilience and intelligence of next-generation Smart Grids.

title: A Hyperparameter Exploration of LSTM Models for U.S. Airline Tweet Sentiment Analysis
authors: Krish Pillai, Atharva Kawtikwar, Masooda Modak
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This paper examines how deep learning can be used to analyze the reviews of United States airlines on Twitter in terms of sentiment analysis. Although Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks are highly applicable in sequence-based tasks such as text classification, their execution highly relies on the choice of hyperparameters. The paper itself is a systematic experimental research, which aims to measure the influence of major hyperparameters on the accuracy of the model. We search a grid of 18 different model configurations, including an experimentation of three optimizers (Adam, RMSProp, SGD), three dropout rates (0.2, 0.4, 0.6), and two LSTM activation functions (tanh, relu). The models are trained and tested on a dataset consisting of more than 14000 labelled tweets. We find that the choice of optimizer and activation function have a considerable effect on test accuracy, with the Adam and RMSProp optimization functions always better than the SGD and the tanh activation function more effective. The current study offers empirical results that can be used to select the best hyperparameters to use in sentiment analysis of the airline industry.

title: Closed-form High-order Parameter Estimation for Polarimetric Arrays of Arbitrary Configurations
authors: Ruiqian Liao, Fuqian Nie, Hang Zhou, Yaxing Yue
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: High-order parameter estimation using a polarimetric array benefits from enhanced effective aperture thanks to the exploitation of high-order statistics (HOS). However, when dealing with polarimetric arrays of arbitrary configurations, the estimation procedure faces high computational complexity because the involved parameters, namely direction-of-arrival and polarization, are coupled and require multi-dimensional spectral searching. To address this, we study the closed-form high-order parameter estimation problem for polarimetric arrays by leveraging the polarimetric element space technique. In our proposed approach, we present a general framework to derive the polynomial coefficients involved in the estimation procedure. Thanks to the proposed approach that combines closed-form estimation with HOS, our method achieves low computational complexity while maintaining high effective aperture. Simulation results validate the performance advantages of the proposed approach.

title: Neural Signal Compression using RAMAN tinyML Accelerator for BCI Applications_supp1-3674434.pdf
authors: chetan thakur
abstract: High-quality, multi-channel neural recording is indispensable for neuroscience research and clinical applications. Large-scale brain recordings often produce vast amounts of data that must be wirelessly transmitted for subsequent offline analysis and decoding, especially in brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) utilizing high-density intracortical recordings with hundreds or thousands of electrodes. However, transmitting raw neural data presents significant challenges due to limited communication bandwidth and resultant excessive heating. To address this challenge, we propose a neural signal compression scheme utilizing Convolutional Autoencoders (CAEs), which achieves a compression ratio of up to 150 for compressing local field potentials (LFPs). The CAE encoder section is implemented on RAMAN, an energy-efficient tinyML accelerator designed for edge computing. RAMAN leverages sparsity in activation and weights through zero skipping, gating, and weight compression techniques. Additionally, we employ hardware-software cooptimization by pruning the CAE encoder model parameters using a hardware-aware balanced stochastic pruning strategy, resolving workload imbalance issues and eliminating indexing overhead to reduce parameter storage requirements by up to 32.4%. Post layout simulation shows that the RAMAN encoder can be implemented in a TSMC 65-nm CMOS process, occupying a core area of 0.0187 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> per channel. Operating at a clock frequency of 2 MHz and a supply voltage of 1.2 V, the estimated power consumption is 15.1 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$μ$</tex-math></inline-formula> W per channel for the proposed DS-CAE1 model. For functional validation, the RAMAN encoder was also deployed on an Efinix Ti60 FPGA, utilizing 37.3k LUTs and 8.6k flip-flops. The compressed neural data from RAMAN is reconstructed offline with signal-to-noise and distortion ratios (SNDR) of 22.6 dB and 27.4 dB, along with R2 scores of 0.81 and 0.94, respectively, evaluated on two monkey neural recordings.

title: Rethinking Frequency Modeling: Tail-Aware Dynamic Adversarial Training for Long-Tailed Robustness_supp1-3679189.pdf
authors: Jie Gui
abstract: Adversarial training (AT) is among the most effective defenses against adversarial attacks on deep neural networks. However, in real-world scenarios where data often follow long-tailed distributions, conventional AT methods struggle to handle such imbalance, resulting in severe robustness disparities across classes and limited overall robustness. Although recent efforts attempt to improve robustness through class frequency-aware weighting or distribution adjustments, our empirical analysis reveals that class frequency alone is an insufficient indicator of adversarial vulnerability, as robust accuracy does not correlate with the number of examples per class. Furthermore, AT under long-tailed distributions exhibits optimization instability, particularly for tail classes with limited data. To address these challenges, we present Tail-Aware Dynamic Adversarial Training (TAD-AT), which integrates three complementary components targeting the training loss, attack strategy, and weight average. TAD-AT captures data imbalance and performance disparity, improving adversarial robustness under long-tailed distributions. First, our training loss incorporates frequency- and accuracy-aware regularization to emphasize learning for vulnerable classes. Second, our attack adjusts perturbations based on class-wise vulnerability, encouraging robust feature learning around vulnerable regions, thereby mitigating robustness overfitting and improving clean accuracy. Third, our weight average improves robust generalization and training stability by adaptively controlling the decay rate across classes. Experiments on long-tailed benchmarks demonstrate that our TAD-AT significantly improves adversarial robustness, offering a systematic and practical solution to robustness challenges under long-tail distributions. Our code is publicly available on https://github.com/bookman233/TADAT.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Brain Tumor Using Deep Learning
authors: Anshiya, Smily Jasrotia, Nikita
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The ability to identify brain tumors using magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) has become an important aspect in enhancing the accuracy of detection procedures as well as the treatment planning. Conventional machine learning algorithms can usually be quite sensitive to changes in the shape, intensity, and texture of tumors; hence, deep learning has developed into a groundbreaking remedy. As recent research, such as points out, much more impressive results on tumor localization and classification are provided by convolutional neural networks (CNNs), U-Net versions, variants equipped with GAN, and transformer-based models. Embodied within larger models, U-Net-based GAN models and the DacFormer generative adversarial network shown to be better at preserving boundaries and resisting noise, and multimodal Transformerbased models such as TransBTS are shown to be less prone to boundary failures. In sum, recent developments in deep learning innovations in the sphere of brain tumor diagnosis and segmentation that can be identified between 2018 and 2025 will be reviewed through (1) highlighting progress in the fields of CNN, U-Net, GAN, and Transformer as well as ensemblebased methods; (2) evaluating the dynamics of AI performance in terms of accuracy and sensitivity across datasets, architectures, and optimization algorithms; (3) describing the contributions to the discussion using explainable and clinically deployable AI solutions; and (4) revealing the limitations of this research.

title: Structured Light-Assisted Optical Biosensor Using Airy–Bessel Excitation for Early Oral Cancer Diagnosis
authors: Bodem Indraja, Arijit Datta
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Early detection of oral cancer is crucial for improving patient survival, yet conventional diagnostic techniques remain slow and heavily dependent on chemical reagents. To overcome these limitations, this work proposes a high-sensitivity fiber-optic biosensor illuminated by an Airy-Bessel beam for reliable identification of oral cancerous cells. The sensor is constructed using a cladding-removed multimode fiber, allowing the surrounding analyte to act as the cladding region. This design increases the overlap between the guided light and the analyte, thereby strengthening the evanescent-wave interaction. The use of an Airy-Bessel beam further enhances sensing performance by combining the self-bending characteristics of Airy beams with the non-diffracting nature of Bessel beams, resulting in improved field confinement and coupling efficiency. The sensing mechanism operates on multimodal interference (MMI), where changes in transmitted power reflect the refractive index (RI) contrast between INOK normal cells and YD-10B oral cancer cells. The propagation behavior and sensing response were analyzed using the Beam Propagation Method (BPM) in OptiBPM (v13.1.3). Our Simulations confirm that a 25 cm sensing length achieves a sensitivity of 322.45 dB/RIU, providing a 3.29 -fold improvement compared to Gaussian-beam excitation. With its compact structure, low cost, and fabrication-friendly design, the proposed sensor shows strong potential for early oral cancer detection and broader biomedical sensing applications.

title: Architecture and Power-Balancing Effects on Side-Channel Leakage Protection in AES Hardware Implementations
authors: Valery D. Alekseev, Alexander S. Tishin, Victor Y. Mikhailov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: This paper investigates the impact of architectural decisions and power-balancing methods on protection against power side-channel leaks in hardware implementations of the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) cryptographic block. We analyze AES implementations with bit widths of 8, 16, 32, 64, and 128 bits, including a baseline version and three power-balancing side-channel protection techniques: XOR-based inversion, S-box inversion, and enhanced S-box inversion. The study evaluates architectures combining XOR and S-box operations in a single cycle, as well as a pipelined implementation with parallel XOR and S-box operations. Security assessment is conducted using correlation power analysis (CPA) and the Test Vector Leakage Assessment (TVLA) methodology, based on 10,000 traces for each configuration, to identify optimal solutions for minimizing side-channel leakage.

title: FPGA Implementation of a Fault-Tolerant Low-Latency Reversible ALU Using Hybrid Parity-Preserving IG–NFT Logic Gates in Verilog HDL
authors: Kattubadi Abdul Raseed, S. Rambabu, Manubolu Venugopal Naidu, C. Gangaiah Yadav, Bikkili Alekya Himabindu
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper presents a fault-tolerant, low-latency reversible Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) designed using hybrid parity-preserving IG and NFT gates in Verilog HDL. Reversible computing minimizes energy dissipation and enables quantumcompatible designs, while parity preservation ensures on-chip error detection. The proposed ALU performs addition, subtraction, AND, and XOR operations with reduced quantum cost, fewer garbage outputs, and lower logic depth compared to conventional and earlier reversible ALUs, such as the hybrid Fredkin–Feynman ALU, Peres–HNG ALU. Synthesized on an Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA board with the VIVADO simulation tool, the design exhibits notable improvements in power efficiency, logic utilisation, and fault tolerance. Scalability for multi-bit operations and compatibility with quantum frameworks make the architecture suitable for low-power embedded systems, IoT processors, and cryptographic applications.

title: Combating Social Bots: Advances in Detection and Prevention
authors: Pathan.Hussain Basha, Swathi Buragadda, Syed Muqthadar Ali, V T Ram Pavan Kumar, Sriharsha Vikruthi, V S R K Raju Dandu
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The rapid evolution of social bots, which mimic human behavior to spread misinformation, demands innovative detection frameworks that combine structural and linguistic analysis. Existing methods often rely on isolated features-such as network anomalies or language patterns-leading to limited generalizability and high false positives in real-world scenarios. This study introduces a hybrid detection framework that synergizes Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and transformer models, augmented with self-supervised contrastive learning, to address these limitations. By integrating graph-based structural analysis of user interactions with transformer-driven linguistic scrutiny, the framework captures multi-modal behavioral signatures of bots while minimizing reliance on labeled data. Evaluated on a comprehensive social media dataset, the model achieves an F1-score of 0.93, outperforming standalone GNN (0.83) and transformer-only (0.90) approaches. Key innovations include adversarial robustness techniques and computational optimizations for real-time deployment. Results demonstrate a 5–8% improvement over state-of-the-art methods, highlighting the efficacy of combining structural and linguistic features. This work advances proactive misinformation mitigation and offers a scalable solution for social media platforms combating evolving bot strategies.

title: AI-Driven Optimization of Sustainable Hydrogen Transportation Systems in the Global Energy Transition
authors: Palanichamy Manikandan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This study examines the transformative role of artificial intelligence (AI) in optimizing sustainable hydrogen transportation systems during the global energy transition. AI technologies demonstrate significant potential for enhancing operational efficiency and safety across various hydrogen delivery modes through advanced applications including machine learning (ML)-based route optimization, predictive maintenance algorithms, and real-time monitoring systems. Regional analysis across Europe, Asia Pacific, and the United States reveals diverse approaches to infrastructure development and regulatory frameworks adapted to local energy policies and market conditions. While AI implementation has successfully improved operational efficiency and risk management capabilities, significant challenges persist in infrastructure standardization and cross-network technology integration. This paper highlights critical technical barriers and policy implications influencing the scalability of AI-enhanced hydrogen transportation systems. These insights contribute to the growing knowledge base essential for developing robust, and sustainable hydrogen transport infrastructure while supporting the global clean energy transition. The research underscores the necessity of coordinated technological and policy approaches.

title: Semantic fusion of dual perspectives on genomic sequences and quorum sensing for bacteriophage lifestyle prediction
authors: Zhen Xiao, Ankang Wei, Yi Jia, Weizhong Zhao, Xingpeng Jiang
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: As the most ubiquitous and abundant viral community, bacteriophages (phages for short) play a vital role in regulating the ecological balance by infecting bacteria and archaea. Phages can be classified into two types based on their lifestyles: virulent phages and temperate phages, which are closely related to their functional characteristics and influence their interaction patterns with hosts. Therefore, identifying phage lifestyle is critical for understanding the mechanisms by which phages infect bacteria and represents a key step in mastering their functions and potential applications. In this paper, we propose a novel method for phage lifestyle identification by considering two perspectives. One perspective is based on the genomic sequences of phages, in which both local and global semantic features are integrated. The other perspective focuses on the host quorum sensing phenomena that influence phage lysogen-lysis decisions. Specifically, we first capture local sequence variation patterns by extracting the relative positional information of nucleotide fragments at different intervals, which enables robust representation of local genomic semantics. Secondly, the pretrained nucleotide language model DNABERT is applied to capture the semantics of genome sequences by considering the global contextual information. Finally, combined with quorum sensing signals from the bacterial host, a final fusion representation is obtained, which is fed into a predictive model to identify the phage's lifestyle. Experimental results show that our method has excellent and stable performance in both phage complete genome and short contigs from metagenomic data. We also investigate early-life viral colonization in the human gut metagenome, further validating the model's generalizability and real-world applicability.

title: AI-Driven Feature-Enhanced Stacking Ensemble with Global-Context Vision Transformers for Breast Cancer Classification in Ultrasound Images
authors: Nghia Trong Vo, Hoang Phi Yen Duong, Tuan Thanh Nguyen, Nhan Duc Le, Trung Q. Duong
abstract: Breast cancer remains a leading cause of death among women worldwide. Early detection of breast cancer is a crucial step towards improving survival rates for patients affected by the disease and is typically performed with the help of ultrasound imaging. Current rapid advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) research have produced a plethora of machine learning methods that aid in building automated diagnostic assistance systems for early cancer detection, including breast cancer detection. While deep learning has shown promise in medical image analysis, most existing approaches rely on single models or simple ensemble methods that fail to fully exploit complementary feature representations across architectures. This paper introduces a novel feature-enhanced stacking ensemble framework that combines state-of-the-art global context vision transformer (GCViT) with well-established convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures (ResNet-50V2, ConvNeXt-Tiny, and EfficientNetV2-B3) for automated breast cancer classification from ultrasound images. Unlike conventional ensembles that aggregate only prediction probabilities, our approach extracts deep feature embeddings from a dedicated CNN branch and concatenates them with base model predictions as input to a meta-learner, a multi-layer perceptron (MLP), enabling the ensemble to leverage both decision-level and feature-level information. When incorporating a meta model with feature representations from a CNN-based feature extractor, we are able to produce superior performance across multiple metrics compared to prior works. We accomplish top performance of 94.23% accuracy, 95.47% AUC-ROC. To further evaluate the robustness and generalizability of our approach, we conduct additional experiments on the melanoma cancer image dataset and achieve 95.4% accuracy. We provide comprehensive explainability analysis through shapley additive explanations (SHAP) values for feature attribution, permutation importance for model contribution quantification, and saliency maps for visual interpretation from base models and the end-to-end ensemble model to explain their contributions to final predictions.

title: Closing the UV-Induced Photodegradation Gap Through Global Scale Modeling of Fixed Tilt and Tracking Photovoltaic Systems
authors: S. Poddar, S. Liu, P. Hamer, M. Kay, B. Hoex
abstract: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation accelerates the degradation of photovoltaic (PV) modules, both at the cell and module levels, leading to reduced efficiency and a shorter lifespan. While reliability testing has advanced, current standards often fail to reflect real-world UV exposure, especially across diverse climates. Global UV irradiance varies widely, from <30 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in high-latitude regions to >80 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in arid zones, while standard tests such as IEC-61215 only test up to a dose of 15 kWh /m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> that can be reached in less than 50 days in some locations. This study develops a high-precision model to estimate UV radiation on tilted surfaces and assesses the influence on UV photodegradation. The model shows minimum bias (<±3.87 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, i.e., <±4.28%) when compared with observations. Results show notable global variation in UV irradiance on tilted surfaces between single-axis tracking (SAT) and fixed-tilt systems, hence affecting degradation rates. SAT systems experience approximately twice the degradation in arid and semi-arid climates compared to fixed-tilt systems, where UV exposure is most intense. These findings highlight the critical need for climate-specific degradation assessments even for identical module technologies. To ensure outdoor durability and long-term performance, advanced regionally adaptive reliability testing thresholds should be adapted for better-informed material selection and manufacturing.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Electromagnetic Analysis of SPM Using Sub-domain Model Based on the Magnetic Scalar Potential Field
authors: Anning Niu, Jiaqiang Yang, Rafik Ighil Nessouk, Zhihan Wu
abstract: This paper presents an improved analytical sub-domain model based on the magnetic scalar potential (MSP) for analyzing the no-load and on-load magnetic fields and performance of the surface-mounted permanent magnet motor (SPM). In this approach, the magnet is treated as a polarised magnetic source, while the armature current is represented by the MSP boundary condition. Analytical expressions for each sub-domain are derived using Poisson’s and Laplace’s equations. The proposed analytical model is validated through finite element simulations and compared with the classical sub-domain model based on the magnetic vector potential (MVP). The results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high accuracy for key performance indicators, including the magnetic field in the air-gap, torque ripple, and induced voltage. Compared with the MVP-based sub-domain model, the proposed sub-domain model treats the magnetic field as a scalar field, similar to that of an electric or thermal field, which enhances the understanding of magnetic field behavior. Furthermore, this model has the potential for improved coupling analysis with magnetic circuit models, making it suitable for complicated magnetic field analysis problems.

title: Follow the Signs or the Crowd? Effects of Environmental Load and Crowd Dynamics in VR Evacuation
authors: Zheng Wei, Jingchen Gao, Zhe Yan, Yihan Liu, Linjie Qiu, Xian Xu, Yun Huang, Huamin Qu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Emergency evacuation in VR must balance realism with clear guidance. However, most prior studies strengthen either sensory or social factors in isolation, leaving equal-geometry causal estimates of load versus crowd still lacking. We present SAFE-VR, a controlled testbed that orthogonally varies Environmental Load (low vs. high) and Crowd Dynamics (orderly vs. chaotic) while keeping layout, signage, and spawn constant. In a preregistered 2×2 between-subjects experiment (N = 80), we analyzed time-to-exit, frame-coded behavior, presence, and workload to distangle sensory from social effects. Both factors impaired egress, with the High×Chaotic condition performing worst overall. For time-to-exit, effects were additive (no reliable Load×Crowd interaction); in contrast, Temporal demand showed a crossed interaction. High load increased effort, frustration, and object contacts; while chaotic f low increased route deviations, human contacts, and slowed exits. These patterns align with reliability-weighted cueing: as guidance becomes harder to perceive, participants may shift toward crowd-following, especially when flow is unstable. SAFE-VR thus delineates how load and crowd structure jointly shape route fidelity, collisions, and evacuation time, and highlights conditions where subjective time pressure diverges from objective delay.

title: Digital Twin-Based Motion Planning and Virtual-Physical Mapping Control for an OMR Through Narrow Corners
authors: Hongjiu Yang, Guowei Zhao, Yuanqing Xia, Peng Li
abstract: In this paper, a digital twin system is presented to enable static motion planning, reliable digital simulation and dynamic state monitoring for an omnidirectional mobile robot (OMR) through narrow corners. A virtual robot is constructed to realize precise collision detection and real-time motion visualization for the OMR which is taken for a physical robot. A precise configuration space is obtained based on the virtual robot in a virtual environment which is used to map the real environment. A six-neighborhood three-dimensional <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$A^{*}$</tex-math></inline-formula> algorithm is designed to find the optimal path from an initial configuration to a target configuration. A virtual-physical mapping control strategy is designed to realize trajectory tracking and dynamic monitoring of the physical robot and the virtual robot. Experimental results show effectiveness of the proposed motion planning and virtual-physical mapping control for the OMR through narrow corners.

title: The Standards Landscape in the IEEE Computer Society
authors: Darren Galpin
abstract: Here, we highlight the work done by IEEE Computer Society’s various standards committees by showcasing the standards and project authorization requests approved in the December 2025 New Standards Committee (NesCom), Standards Review Committee (RevCom), and IEEE Standards Association Standards Board (SASB) meeting series.

title: IEEE Trial-Use Guide for Testing Methods for Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell Systems in Automotive Applications Using Compressed Hydrogen as Fuel
authors: N/A
abstract: The methodology for the test of proton exchange membrane fuel cell systems for automotive applications using compressed hydrogen as fuel is provided as a guidance in this document, including the recommendations on the accuracy of the test instruments, preparation before the test, test conditions, and calculation processes.

title: Standardized Approach for Assessing Wireless IoT Devices in Millimeter-Wave Industrial Work-Cell Channels
authors: Mohamed Kashef, Kate A. Remley, Paritosh Manurkar, Joshua M. Kast, Iyemeh E. Uchendu, Robert D. Horansky, Dylan Williams, Matthew Simons, Michael Frey, Lucas Koepke, Richard Candell
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: We present a standardized, repeatable test approach that replicates key features of measured industrial environments in a fully-characterized hybrid test chamber. The hybrid chamber consists of an anechoic chamber along with movable metallic spheres that provide discrete reconfigurable multipath components. The chamber is characterized by a synthetic-aperture system providing a reference measurement that captures the non-idealities of the chamber along with the intended spatial multipath, completing characterization of the test environment. A key feature of our approach is the propagation of correlated uncertainties through to the final system response – including uncertainties specific to synthetic apertures such as cable bending due to scanning and inexact positioner locations. The uncertainty analysis provides statistically accurate knowledge of the environment, allowing separation of measurement errors from errors related to the device or system under test. We have also extracted three exemplar channels that capture key features of a work-cell-sized industrial environment from measurements made with the same synthetic aperture. Each exemplar channel presents different, realistic angular-diverse multipath challenges to a millimeter-wave internet-of-things device, subsystem, or active antenna under test. This allows engineers to evaluate their hardware designs in a physical, repeatable test environment and also facilitates future standardization of test methods for these and other millimeter-wave wireless devices.

title: Construction of Binary Sequence Having PMEPR Bounded by 4
authors: Piyush Priyanshu, Sudhan Majhi, Subhabrata Paul
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) is widely employed for multi-carrier communication systems due to its spectral efficiency and resilience to multi-path fading. However, the high peak-to-mean envelope power ratio (PMEPR) remains of significant challenge in OFDM. Golay complementary sets (GCS) have been used to mitigate this issue, but their applicability is limited due to strict constraints on sequence lengths and flock size. To address this limitation, Xin et al. (2004) introduced the concept of multiple-shift complementary sets (MSCS), which extend the GCS framework. An MSCS consists of sequences whose summed aperiodic autocorrelation function (AACF) vanishes at specific integer multiples of a given time shift. In this paper, we propose a new construction of a binary 4 -shift complementary sequence of length <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2^{m-1}+4$</tex>, where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m \geq 3$</tex>, using generalized Boolean functions (GBF). Furthermore, the PMEPR of the resulting sequence is bounded above by 4.

title: Modeling of Control and Monitoring Systems for Positioning Workers in a Coal Mine
authors: Ludmila G. Musta, Yuriy K. Kozhubaev, Valeria V. Gerasimova, Yin Changwen, Hassan Shbib
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: This article provides a review and analysis of existing multifunctional communication and positioning systems for mining companies. It also models an automated lamp station operator workstation using CoDeSys, MySql, and SimpleScada. A model for generating reports on workers who have received warning devices and returned them at the end of their shift is developed to monitor the exit of all workers from the mine. The report is generated for a specified time interval. A window model for registering and monitoring warning devices is developed. These automated lamp station operator workstations (AWS) for employee accounting are integrated into the control and monitoring system for worker positioning in a coal mine. Recommendations for the development of an automated lamp station operator workstation are provided.

title: Analysis of Approaches to Executing Fuzzy Queries to Relational Databases
authors: Tran Duc Manh
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: Relational databases are currently widely used in many fields, such as business, banking, and population management. However, this type of database only supports exact queries and cannot directly process fuzzy concepts in natural language. Therefore, one of the current areas of relational database development is the development of fuzzy query methods for them. This article presents the description and results of experiments evaluating the effectiveness of two approaches to executing fuzzy queries: 1) an approach based on vectorization technology, and 2) an approach based on fuzzy logic. To compare the accuracy and speed of fuzzy query execution using these approaches, a series of experiments was conducted on databases with different structures and different query forms. The results show that each approach has its own advantages and limitations, which should be considered when selecting and developing algorithms for executing fuzzy queries in the future.

title: Section 1. Computing & Processing (Hardware/Software)
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)

title: Adaptive Model-Based Transfer Learning for Dynamic HVAC Control
authors: Quang-Thang Le, Kevin Wijaya, Hsin-Yi Lai, Che-Kai Liu, Ching-Chun Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: In this paper, we aim to automate the adjustment of air handling unit (AHU) setpoints within heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems to maintain indoor temperatures at user-specified levels. A key challenge lies in obtaining sufficient high-quality sensor data from real buildings. To address this, we explore transfer learning and leverage simulation software to generate training data. We propose an adaptive model-based transfer learning approach for dynamic HVAC control, where the agent directly controls the source domain under conditions identical to the target domain. This eliminates the need for extensive target-specific knowledge to define data generation schedules and reduces the risk of collecting irrelevant samples, while also providing greater flexibility during learning. At the control level, we enhance performance through physics rule embedding, which ensures physical consistency, and long-term-aware setpoint selection strategy, which mitigates abrupt setpoint changes. Finally, to accelerate and stabilize deployment in new buildings, we enable knowledge transfer directly between similar real-world buildings, reducing the need to construct virtual source domains repeatedly.

title: An Up to 10-MHz 6.8% Minimum Duty Ratio GaN Driver With Adaptive Short-Pulse High-CMTI Level Shifter and Dual-Switch Bootstrap Techniques_supp1-3676911.pdf
authors: Xin Ming
abstract: Level shifter and floating power supply for high-side channel are major challenges in high-voltage high-frequency DC-DC converters. This paper presents an up to 10 MHz GaN driver suitable for high-power-density automotive electronics. An adaptive short-pulse (ASP) high d<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V_{\text {sw}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/d<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$t$</tex-math> </inline-formula> noise immunity level shifter (LS) with efficient analog noise blanker (ANB) is proposed, achieving 6.8 ns minimum on-time at 10 MHz <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{\text {sw}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. A dual-MOS-switches bootstrap (DMSB) circuit is proposed to ensure that the bootstrap (BST) rail achieves saturation (>80%) at 10 MHz frequency by sensing <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V_{\text {sw}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and the low-side gate drive signal. To validate this work, a half-bridge GaN driver has been fabricated in a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.5~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m 80 V HV CMOS process and the active area is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.44\times 1.42$</tex-math> </inline-formula> mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The d<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/d<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$t$</tex-math> </inline-formula> immunity has been verified both in Buck converter and double pulse conditions, which is up to 46 V/ns at 60V Buck converter and 56 V/ns at 48 V double pulse topology. The proposed DMSB technique achieves a peak efficiency of 86.7% at 10 MHz for 12-to-5V conversion, representing a 6.7% improvement over the conventional approach.

title: A Reuse-Rate-Driven and Gain-Scheduled MINCO Trajectory Optimization Method for UAV Formations
authors: Muyang Niu, Pengfei Zhang, Yawen Li, Zhongliu Wang, Huiyan Han
abstract: Addressing the limitations of MINCO trajectory optimization in multi-density obstacle environments-namely fixed parameters and poor environmental adaptability-this paper proposes a reuse rate-driven dual-mode gain scheduling framework. By constructing a dual-mode gain scheduling optimization framework, the spatial density distribution of obstacles is incorporated as prior information.
