Under the spatiotemporal integrated modeling of MINCO, adaptive scheduling of trajectory timescale and cost function weight parameters is achieved. Furthermore, a velocity-aware repulsive potential field is designed to dynamically adjust the ratio of attractive and repulsive forces, effectively mitigating local spikes and oscillations during high-speed turns while enhancing trajectory quality during maneuvers. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method enhances passage efficiency and trajectory smoothness while maintaining formation consensus in both sparse and dense obstacle scenarios. This provides theoretical reference for efficient collaborative planning of UAV formations in complex environments.

title: Neurodynamic Optimization Approaches with Fixed-Time Convergence for Nash Equilibrium Seeking: Theory and Hardware Experiment
authors: Xingxing Ju, Xinsong Yang, Chuandong Li, Gang Feng, Daniel W. C. Ho
abstract: The convergence rate is one of the key performance measures for Nash equilibrium (NE) seeking strategies. In this work, we present several novel fast decoupled/coupled time-varying neurodynamic optimization approaches with fixed-time (FT) convergence to Nash equilibrium seeking in non-cooperative games. The dynamics trajectories are demonstrated to converge to the NE solution within a fixed time from any initial states. The proposed neurodynamic networks exhibit a faster convergence rate with appropriately selected time-varying coefficients. Additionally, the upper bounds of the convergence time of the proposed NE seeking networks are smaller than those for strategies with constant coefficients. The robustness of the proposed NE seeking neurodynamic approaches under bounded perturbations is further studied. The efficacy and practicality of the proposed NE seeking approaches are validated through simulations and field-programmable gate array (FPGA) experiments on duopoly market games.

title: LACO-OFDM-Based Optical ISAC System Design and Performance Analysis
authors: Benben Li, Jiale Wang, Yan Gao, Jie Lian, Baowang Lian
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) in free-space optical (FSO) systems must contend with the intensity-modulation non-negativity constraint and atmospheric turbulence. This paper develops an LACO-OFDM-based ISAC framework that rigorously models the hierarchical signal structure and clipping-induced distortion via Bussgang decomposition and Price’s theorem. First, we derive closed-form expressions for the clipping-noise power spectral density, the ergodic communication capacity, and the ranging Fisher information under Gamma-Gamma turbulence fading. Then, based on these analytical results, a joint power-allocation problem is formulated to optimize the communication-sensing trade-off under average optical and electrical power constraints. The resulting nonconvex problem is efficiently solved via an alternating optimization algorithm, where the communication and sensing subproblems are addressed through successive convex approximation (SCA) and the quadratic transform, respectively. Finally, numerical results characterize the communication-sensing Pareto frontier and demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed scheme across a wide range of turbulence conditions and transmission distances.

title: Optimisation of TES design for the CRESST experiment
authors: G. Angloher, S. Banik, A. Bento, A. Bertolini, R. Breier, C. Bucci, J. Burkhart, L. Burmeister, L. Canonica, E. Cipelli, S. Di Lorenzo, J. Dohm, F. Dominsky, A. Erb, E. Fascione, F. v. Feilitzsch, S. Fichtinger, D. Fuchs, V.M. Ghete, P. Gorla, P.V. Guillaumon, S. Gupta, D. Hauff, M. Ješkovský, J. Jochum, M. Kaznacheeva, H. Kluck, H. Kraus, B. von Krosigk, A. Langenkämper, M. Mancuso, B. Mauri, V. Mokina, C. Moore, P. Murali, M. Olmi, T. Ortmann, C. Pagliarone, L. Pattavina, F. Petricca, W. Potzel, P. Povinec, F. Pröbst, F. Pucci, F. Reindl, J. Rothe, K. Schäffner, J. Schieck, S. Schönert, C. Schwertner, M. Stahlberg, L. Stodolsky, C. Strandhagen, R. Strauss, I. Usherov, M. Zanirato, V. Zema
abstract: The CRESST experiment aims at the direct detection of sub-GeV dark matter particles via elastic scattering off nuclei in different target crystals at cryogenic temperatures. The advancement in W-TES sensors allowed the CRESST detectors to reach energy thresholds of 10<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\,$</tex-math></inline-formula>eV and lower, opening the way to the exploration of dark matter masses as low as <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim 70\,$</tex-math></inline-formula>MeV/c². This work presents optimisation studies of W-TESs aimed at further improving the signal-to-noise ratio and overall detector performance. In particular, we investigate the thickness, dimensions and material composition of phonon collectors and assess their impact on detector response. The results demonstrate a significant performance enhancement and establish new benchmarks for the sensors used within CRESST.

title: Dual-Branch CNN-MLP-Dropout Network for Multi-Class Scene Recognition: Fusing Multi-Sensor Time-Domain Inputs and Time-Frequency Representations
authors: Jiale Wang, Yue Mei, Ming Xia, Chuang Shi, Wu Chen
abstract: In the current digital age, scene recognition technology is increasingly applied in the Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystem, including transportation, smart homes, elderly care, and terrain surveying. Existing technologies have two limitations: sensors are often worn on different body parts, causing inconvenience; and limited sensor types with inadequate data processing restrict recognition accuracy. Consumer-grade smartphones, with high portability, low cost, lightweight design, and diverse sensors, offer an ideal solution and serve as crucial intelligent agents in IoT scenarios. To leverage multi-sensor information for effective, efficient, and trustworthy IoT scene recognition, we propose a CNN-MLP-dropout method using time-domain and time–frequency-domain features. This method synchronizes data from eight built-in heterogeneous sensors, extracts effective features via filtering and Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT), then feeds them into the CNN-MLP-Dropout model. The model first extracts local patterns, captures global representations, and achieves multi-class scene recognition. Test results on 10 indoor/outdoor scenarios showed multi-sensor data improved accuracy by 21.98%, 36.67%, and 6.09% vs. limited-sensor groups. The proposed model significantly outperforms five state-of-the-art networks, with dropout avoiding overfitting, enabling high-precision, robust, user-friendly multi-class activity scene recognition in IoT.

title: Federated Learning Framework for Privacy-Preserving Explainable AI-Driven Clinical Decision-Making
authors: Emad-ul-Haq Qazi, Waleed Khalid AL-Ghanem, Muhammad Hamza Faheem, Hanif Ullah
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: The application of artificial intelligence (AI) in clinical diagnostics has shown substantial potential; however, conventional centralized learning frameworks often encounter critical limitations related to patient data privacy, data heterogeneity, and limited generalizability. To address these challenges, we propose a novel Federated Deep Learning (FDL) framework tailored for privacy-preserving, AI-driven clinical decision support. The proposed architecture integrates Vision Transformers (ViT) with DINOv2-based self-supervised learning to enable effective representation learning in the absence of extensive labeled datasets. Furthermore, personalized model updates are facilitated using Federated Self-Supervised Learning (FedSSL) in conjunction with FedProx, ensuring client-specific adaptation in non-identically distributed (non-IID) data environments. Privacy preservation is ensured through the application of differential privacy mechanisms at the model update level, coupled with Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) for secure communication. To enhance clinical transparency and interpretability, the framework incorporates Grad-CAM and LIME for sample-level explainability. The proposed system is evaluated on three publicly available medical imaging datasets encompassing Tuberculosis (TB) detection from chest X-rays, Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) from fundus images, and Brain Tumor (BT) classification from MRI scans. The federated model achieved an accuracy and F1-score of 99.80% for Tuberculosis, 89.0% for Diabetic Retinopathy (DR), and 97.1% for Brain Tumors (BTs), reflecting high overall diagnostic performance across all tasks. These findings validate the efficacy, scalability, and privacy-resilience of the proposed method, positioning it as a robust candidate for real-world clinical deployment in distributed healthcare environments.

title: Conference Committee: ICCSM 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)

title: Wearable sensing technology for knee joint monitoring and functional assessment: a systematic review
authors: Meernah Mohammed Alabdullah, Zhané N. Murrell-Smith, Sheng Quan Xie, Aiqin Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Traditional knee joint monitoring and assessment methods rely heavily on subjective evaluations, leading to an increasing interest in wearable sensing technology for objectively monitoring knee joint function. This review evaluates current research on wearable devices for knee monitoring and functional assessment, highlighting current approaches, limitations, technological advancements and identifying knowledge gaps to guide future research. Following PRISMA guidelines, a systematic search of PubMed, Scopus, IEEE Xplore, Web of Science, Cochrane Library, and Google Scholar identified 77 eligible studies, with methodological quality assessed using the COSMIN Risk of Bias tool. Findings were categorized into four domains: kinetics, kinematics, muscle activity, and integrated domains based on the measured biomechanical parameter. Knee angles were the most frequently measured parameter; however, significant methodological variability was identified, particularly inconsistent sensor placement protocols, calibration methods, and limited real-life applicability. The COSMIN assessment revealed few studies achieved high overall reliability ratings, with kinematics showing the highest consistency and muscle activity the lowest. Kinetic assessments primarily measured knee adduction moments with generally acceptable accuracy but were constrained by indirect measurement methods. Muscle activity assessments predominantly employed surface electromyography, yet exhibited substantial inconsistencies, particularly in electrode placement and analysis techniques. Integrated biomechanical assessments showed significant potential but were constrained by the lack of simultaneous multi-parameter measurement capabilities. Notably, ML/DL integration demonstrated promise, though inconsistencies in performance metrics and sensor configurations complicate direct comparisons, emphasizing the need for standardized evaluation criteria for broader clinical applicability.

title: A Scenario-Driven AI+VR Language Practice Platform: A Technical Feasibility and Formative Usability Study
authors: Zeynep Cipiloglu Yildiz, Furkan Baytak, Furkan Ozkaya
abstract: We present a scenario-driven AI+VR language practice platform designed to support immersive role-play and open-ended spoken interaction for L2 speaking practice. The system combines (i) interactive VR scenarios, (ii) LLM-driven real-time conversations, (iii) a block-based scenario authoring tool for customizing practice content, (iv) reflective analytics derived from conversation logs, and (v) a privacy-aware social layer for peer support. The platform integrates the OpenAI GPT-4 Realtime API within the Unity 3D engine to enable low-latency conversational interaction in immersive environments. We report a technical feasibility assessment and a formative usability evaluation. In a study with 30 learners, the platform achieved a mean System Usability Scale (SUS) score of 73.25, a p95 frame time of 10.69 ms, and an average AI response latency of 0.77 s. These results provide initial evidence that the proposed design can deliver responsive AI-mediated dialogue and a usable end-to-end experience for scenario-based practice. We conclude with design trade-offs, key limitations of the current short-term evaluation, and directions for learning-oriented and longitudinal studies.

title: TriCloud: Drug-Target-Disease Ternary Network for Drug Repositioning Research Based on Point Cloud Modeling_supp1-3675301.docx
authors: Wenjun Li
abstract: In recent years, the “drug-target-disease” association has become increasingly complex and data-scarce. Existing methods are limited by information loss caused by explicit graph construction when modeling triplet relationships, making it difficult to effectively capture geometric structures and long-range dependencies, thereby affecting prediction performance. To address this, this paper proposes a new method based on point cloud modeling—TriCloud. This method represents each triad as a spatial point cloud, encoding semantic and topological relationships through geometric coordinates, and performs feature learning directly on an unordered point set, avoiding reliance on predefined graph structures. Based on the PointNet architecture, it introduces a multi-view feature extraction and fusion mechanism to enhance the modeling capability of global structures and complex spatial patterns. Experimental results show that TriCloud significantly outperforms existing methods on multiple benchmark datasets, achieving an AUC of 0.9995 and an AUPR of 0.9996, with all metrics ranking first. External validation demonstrates its excellent generalization ability. Feature analysis reveals that the geometric-semantic joint features of positive samples play a dominant role in classification. This study provides an efficient and reliable computational framework for drug repurposing, contributing to the development of precision medicine.

title:
VS-ReLEnT:
                    <u>V</u>
                    oltage
                    <u>S</u>
                    caling by
                    <u>R</u>
                    einforcement
                    <u>L</u>
                    earning to Balance
                    <u>E</u>
                    nergy/Reliability
                    <u>T</u>
                    rade-off in STT-MRAM Caches
authors: Alireza Banejad, Amir Mahdi Hosseini Monazzah
abstract: With the advancement of computing systems, the demand for energy-efficient NVMs has increased due to the recent challenges of traditional memory technologies like SRAMs and DRAMs. Thus, alternative NVMs like STT-MRAM have gained significance. However, one of the significant challenges of STT-MRAMs, is their stochastic/unreliable write operations. To address this, a noticeable amount of energy should be applied to the STT-MRAM cell to perform a reliable write operation. While reliable write operation in STT-MRAMs matter, there are many situations in which the applied write energy is more than enough for the required reliability level. In this research, we proposed VS-ReLEnT to balance the reliability and energy consumption of STT-MRAMs cache write operations. In VS-ReLEnT the write voltage actuation knob of the STT-MRAM-based cache is controlled by a Reinforcement Learning (RL) algorithm. Accordingly, based on the contents that will be written on the STT-MRAM cache block, the suitable voltage is chosen by VS-ReLEnT and applied to the cells. This allows the adjustment of each level of VS-Relent to the most suitable reliability level. The simulation results demonstrate an average energy improvement of 16% compared to the golden case, where all data is written with the highest reliability level while preserving reliability in the system. Conversely, the area overhead and imposed leakage energy of VS-ReLEnT show only a marginal increase of 0.24% and 0.21%, respectively.

title: IEEE Computing Edge
authors: N/A

title: Velocity Estimation in ISAC Systems with Non-uniform Sensing Resource based on 5G NR Frame Structure
authors: Boshi Wang, Songtao Gao, Cunhua Pan, Hong Ren, Zhiyuan Yu, Yiming Yu, Jiangzhou Wang
abstract: Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) is a promising technology that improves resource utilization efficiency and reduces costs by sharing physical hardware and spectrum. However, in practical fifth-generation (5G) New Radio (NR) frame structures, different time slots may include various auxiliary signals for synchronization, switching, or calibration, resulting in non-uniform intervals between sensing signals. In this paper, we firstly consider a Time Division (TD)-ISAC system model and examine the Cram´ er-Rao Lower Bound (CRLB) for velocity estimation with arbitrary resource spacing. Interestingly, the derived CRLB reveals that non-uniformly distributed sensing resources are not only necessary but are also beneficial. Nonetheless, velocity estimation using non-uniformly distributed sensing resources often leads to high Doppler sidelobes when employing traditional Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT)-based methods. To address this issue, we propose a velocity estimation algorithm that incorporates a dictionary-based coarse estimation and phase unwrapping for refinement. By further adopting the RELAX (Relaxation) framework, the method can not only identify multiple targets but also mitigate the impact of static clutter. Finally, simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm, which achieves higher estimation precision and lower computational complexity than the existing methods, even with a limited number of grid points.

title: A Tutorial on Secure and Efficient Firmware Delivery
authors: Dakshina Tharindu, Aruna Jayasena, Prabhat Mishra
abstract: This tutorial analyzes various aspects of firmware delivery and how they are affected by the introduction of post-quantum safe cryptographic algorithms. Specifically, we describe various firmware delivery stages from both the manufacturer (packaging) and device (deployment) perspectives, including compression (decompression), encryption (decryption), and signing (verification). Extensive experimental evaluation using both real and synthetic benchmarks explores the trade-off between firmware delivery effort (time) and security requirements. Interestingly, our results show that adopting advanced post-quantum digital signature algorithms does not significantly increase firmware deployment time, highlighting their practical viability in next-generation secure firmware delivery pipelines.

title: Battery State-of-Health Estimation across Diverse Aging Conditions with Intra-Inter Cycle Fusion Perception
authors: Yuan Cao, Xinzhi Zhang, Yinqin Liao, Bingchuan Wang, Chunsheng Wang
abstract: Accurate State-of-Health (SOH) estimation is paramount for the safe and reliable operation of lithium-ion batteries. However, conventional methods often overlook inter-cycle degradation dynamics. Furthermore, existing predictive models, primarily developed and validated within a restricted set of aging conditions and battery types, raise doubts regarding their extensive applicability. Therefore, the I²C-transformer is proposed—a deep learning framework specifically designed for battery SOH estimation across diverse aging conditions. Its feature extraction component comprises a unique I²C-convolutional module. Based on an analysis of how inter-cycle relaxation voltage (RV) variations impact SOH, this module employs fast fourier transform (FFT) to adaptively identify critical historical cycles. Subsequently, it fuses intra- and inter-cycle RV data via a 1-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) to extract comprehensive aging characteristics. These features are fed into a lightweight transformer to establish a mapping relationship with SOH, an architecture that innovatively dispenses with the traditional positional encoding module. The model achieved a mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 0.75% across four large-scale datasets encompassing various aging conditions, battery chemistries, and initial SOCs of relaxation. Additionally, transfer experiments demonstrated its strong generalization capability and practical applicability in diverse real-world scenarios. Finally, we deployed our model on an embedded platform, where each inference takes only 0.65 milliseconds to complete. We expect this study to promote exploration of cross-cycle insights for SOH estimation and facilitate battery research across comprehensive aging factors.

title: Video Respiratory Rate Measurement in Walking Scenarios Using Multi-strategy Adaptive Denoising
authors: Gan Pei, Junhao Ning, Chenrui Niu, Siqiong Yao, Menghan Hu, Guangtao Zhai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: For non-contact respiratory rate (RR) measurement, effectively addressing the interference from continuous motion artifacts remains a significant challenge. Most existing research focuses on the removal of weak motion artifacts in a two-dimensional plane, and the fixed spatial scale of the scenes limits the generalization of these methods to real-world scenarios, especially in real walking scenarios. To tackle this issue, we propose an RR measurement framework based on a multi-strategy fusion motion artifact suppression algorithm and have constructed a real-world walking dataset. Specifically, the framework consists of three core modules: an ROI automatic selection and adaptive enhancement module to guide the selection of high-quality corner points; a signal quality evaluation module that adaptively assesses whether the signal is noisy, preventing blind denoising; and a multi-strategy fusion motion artifact removal module that dynamically selects the appropriate strategy to suppress motion interference. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first study to investigate the task of video-based RR measurement in real walking scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that the method achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple datasets, with a mean absolute error (MAE) of 1.04 breaths per minute (bpm) on the COHFACE, 3.17 bpm on the OVRM-Walking dataset, and an average MAE of just 2.41 bpm on the in-house real-world walking dataset, which includes both indoor and outdoor scenarios. This study broadens the applicability of camera-based non-contact RR detection technology. The dataset and code will be publicly available in this project.

title: Adaptive High-Frequency and Temporal Multi-Scale Network for Radar-Based Human Activity Recognition
authors: Peiquan Tian, Hongji Xu, Zhikai Xu, Fei Gao, Hao Zheng, Zihan Ruan, Dongyu Li, Yipeng Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Human activity recognition (HAR) leveraging millimeter-wave (mmWave) radar is a key research area in Internet of Things perception. A key challenge is that existing methodologies often struggle to dynamically identify and preserve high-frequency details in micro-Doppler (MD) spectrograms, which are vital for distinguishing fine-grained activities. Moreover, these approaches lack the ability to capture multi-scale temporal information efficiently and adaptively. To overcome these limitations, the adaptive high-frequency and temporal multi-scale (AHF-TMS) network is proposed. AHF-TMS features a dual-branch parallel architecture that synergistically extracts comprehensive and discriminative features from MD spectrograms. Its spatial branch incorporates the spatial adaptive high-frequency enhancement (SAHFE) module to adaptively enhance critical boundary details. Concurrently, its temporal branch utilizes a multi-scale weighted convolution (MSWConv) module to capture and fuse information from different temporal scales through a weighted strategy. Through comparative experiments on the public datasets IMG848, Ci4R, and RadarMD, the AHF-TMS network achieves accuracies of 96.83%, 93.00%, and 97.84%. Ablation experiments supported by visualizations of convolutional kernels and effective receptive fields validate the effectiveness of the proposed modules. The results confirm that AHF-TMS achieves high recognition accuracy, demonstrates excellent generalization, and is highly effective under data-limited conditions.

title: Lightweight Feature Watermark for Robust Image Attribution in Latent Diffusion Models
authors: Zhangyi Shen, Yuzhong Zhuang, Yani Zhu, Ye Yao
abstract: Latent diffusion models (LDMs) have become the dominant paradigm for AI image synthesis, while also posing unprecedented challenges to AI image attribution. To mitigate these risks, digital watermarking has emerged as a crucial technique for embedding traceable identity information into generated content. However, existing watermarking techniques for LDMs often suffer from degraded image quality, limited robustness, and substantial computational and storage overhead. To address these limitations, we propose Feature Watermarking, a novel watermarking paradigm in which pre-trained, randomly sampled feature vectors are leveraged to enable robust attribution capabilities. Subsequently, we employ a dedicated encoder-decoder architecture to generate initial noise and reconstruct features, respectively. Image attribution is performed by measuring the similarity between the original and reconstructed features. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method matches or outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches in image quality and attribution accuracy, while significantly reducing resource consumption.

title: PrivacyGuard: Differentially Private Machine Learning Against AI-Driven Background Knowledge Attacks
authors: Shoaib Ullah, Razaullah Khan, Madiha Haider Syed, Adeel Anjum
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Preserving an individual privacy in the Artificial Intelligence (AI) era is a pressing challenge, as traditional anonymization techniques often fall short against AI-driven threats. While AI has enabled transformative advancements across sectors such as healthcare, finance, aviation, smart cities, and the Internet of Things (IoT), it also introduces new privacy challenges. In particular, AI-driven background knowledge attacks occur when adversaries combine auxiliary information from external sources such as public records, social media, or previously leaked datasets with advanced AI techniques to re-identify individuals or infer sensitive details from anonymized datasets, rendering conventional defenses less effective. In this study, we present PrivacyGuard, an automated framework that integrates machine learning with traditional anonymization to address these risks. PrivacyGuard employs K-Nearest Neighbor and Random Forest classifiers to identify and evaluate privacy risk of each data tuple in AI-driven attack scenarios. Following risk assessment, Laplace-based differential privacy is applied to mitigate identified risks, while fuzzy logic forms equivalence groups that reflect varying risk levels. Experimental validation on the 1:M Adult dataset (an individual having multiple records) demonstrates that PrivacyGuard achieves 96.47% accuracy and mitigates approximately 42.21% of the identified risks, confirming its effectiveness in balancing privacy and data utility against emerging AI-driven threats.

title: Hybrid CNN-Transformer Models for Automated Brain Tumor Detection in MRI Scans
authors: Swati Killikatt, Sanjay B. Ankali
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: One of the most accurate imaging techniques for identifying and diagnosing brain tumors is magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). Automated, precise, and scalable solutions are required since manual MRI scan interpretation is laborious and subject to inter-observer variability. This study introduces a hybrid deep learning system for automated brain tumor analysis that combines Transformer encoders and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). While the Transformer encoder represents long-range relationships and global contextual information, the CNN component efficiently extracts local spatial characteristics. When tested using publicly accessible brain tumor MRI datasets (BraTS), the suggested hybrid architecture outperformed traditional CNN and Transformer-only methods. The CNN layers are employed to capture fine-grained spatial features, while the Transformer component models long-range contextual dependencies across volumetric MRI data. The framework was evaluated on publicly available BraTS datasets and achieved superior results compared to CNN-only and Transformer-only baselines. Experimental findings demonstrate consistent improvements in accuracy, Dice coefficient, sensitivity, and specificity, confirming the robustness of the approach. These outcomes suggest that CNN–Transformer hybrids can serve as a strong foundation for computer-aided diagnosis systems in clinical practice.

title: Early-Stage Turmeric Crop Disease Identification Using Multi-Scale Convolution and Transformer Attention
authors: Anshita Jain, Shruti Gugilwar, Tanmay Bhatnagar, Vinukonda Emanuel Raju, B.N. Jagadesh, Anugya Tomar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Early detection of diseases in turmeric plants is important because infections like leaf spot and rhizome rot spread fast and can lead to major losses in yield. While computer-vision techniques have improved plant disease diagnosis, older deep-learning models like ResNet and Inception still struggle with real field images and often miss the small, early-stage patterns that matter the most. To overcome this, the study uses MSCVT, a hybrid model that blends CNN layers for capturing fine textures with Vision Transformer attention for understanding global context and long-range relationships. Its multi-scale structure helps the model analyze symptoms at different resolutions, making it more effective at identifying faint spots, unusual textures, or early infection marks. Results show that MSCVT delivers clearer feature representation, more stable predictions, and higher accuracy than standard CNN-based models, making it a stronger option for real-world turmeric disease detection. This improvement can help farmers catch infections earlier, reduce crop loss, and make disease monitoring more efficient in practical agricultural settings.

title: Deployment of SAR Target Recognition Model Based on Embedded Devices
authors: Jinxin Xia, Qian Luo, Zaiping Lin, Qian Yin, Miao Li, Weidong Sheng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: With the continuous development of computer-related technologies, deep learning-based technologies are playing an increasingly important role in the field of Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) target recognition. The current deep learning algorithms have a large number of parameter models and rely on powerful computer equipment to achieve high-precision object detection results. However, due to the limited computing power of the radar terminal equipment, it is difficult for the model to run on the radar terminal equipment. In response to the above issues, this paper implements model deployment on domestic embedded devices by adopting lightweight neural networks while ensuring recognition accuracy. This paper choose domestic embedded development board Atlas200IDKA2 developer suite. Convert the pth file obtained under the PyTorch framework into a dedicated om model, and perform inference on the Pruned-ResNet18-SE and Pruned-MobileNetV3 models by calling the model inference interface provided by the developer suite. The experiment found that the converted model could ensure accuracy, and the average reasoning speed per SAR image was 2.28ms and 2.49ms respectively. Meanwhile, by comparing the ResNet18-SE and MobileNetV3 models before pruning, the experiment found that Pruned-ResNet18-SE increased the accuracy rate by $1.48 \%$ and the reasoning speed by $32.14 \%$ on the basis of a $95.69 \%$ reduction in the number of parameters. Pruned-MobileNetV3 has increased the accuracy by $2.24 \%$ and the inference speed by $8.46 \%$ on the basis of reducing the number of parameters by $36.79\%$

title: Prioritizing Factors Influencing Cloud ERP Adoption in Sri Lankan Organizations: An Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) Approach
authors: F. Mariyam, R. Wickramarachchi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: The rapid evolution of enterprise systems has positioned Cloud-based Enterprise Resource Planning (Cloud ERP) as a strategic solution for improving operational efficiency and scalability. However, enterprises, particularly in developing economies such as Sri Lanka, face challenges in identifying which factors most critically influence successful adoption. This study aims to prioritize the key factors affecting Cloud ERP adoption in Sri Lankan enterprises by applying the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) within the framework of the Technology-Organization-Environment (TOE) model. A comprehensive literature review identified fifteen critical factors across the three TOE dimensions. Ten domain experts representing academia, ERP vendors, and user organizations participated in structured pairwise comparisons to evaluate the relative importance of these factors. Their judgments were aggregated using the geometric mean method, and consistency was verified through the AHP consistency ratio. The resulting prioritization revealed that technological factors hold the greatest overall influence, with data security, system reliability, and integration capability emerging as top-ranked determinants. Top management support and organizational readiness were identified as dominant organizational factors, while vendor support and regulatory compliance led the environmental dimension. The findings provide empirical guidance for Sri Lankan enterprises and policymakers by highlighting which dimensions require strategic emphasis for effective Cloud ERP adoption. Moreover, the study demonstrates the applicability of AHP as a systematic decision support tool for prioritizing complex multi-criteria issues in enterprise technology adoption contexts.

title: AI-Driven Beamforming Optimization in Massive MIMO-Based 5G Networks
authors: Vijayalakshmi S, A.Narendrakumar, Dhanasekhar D, R. Arul, T V Ananda Babu, P. Vinod Kumar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This paper presents an AI-driven deep-reinforcement-learning (DRL) framework for beamforming optimization in massive-MIMO 5G new-radio (NR) systems. Traditional linear precoders Zero-Forcing (ZF) and Minimum-Mean-Square-Error (MMSE) degrade under fast-fading and imperfect channel state information (CSI). To address these issues, a Deep-Deterministic-Policy-Gradient (DDPG) agent is designed to learn optimal beam directions and power allocation that jointly maximize system sum-rate and minimize transmit power. Simulations in MATLAB R2024b for a 64-antenna base station serving 16 users (Rayleigh fading, 20 MHz, 3.5 GHz carrier) show that the proposed AI-beamformer achieves a 23.5 % gain in spectral efficiency (12.9 bps/Hz vs 10.4 bps/Hz for MMSE), a 30.8 % increase in average SINR (26.6 dB vs 20.3 dB), and an 18 % reduction in total power (16.4 W vs 20 W). The DRL model converges within 150 episodes and maintains stable performance across time-varying channels, confirming its suitability for real-time 5G base-station deployment.

title: A YOLO-Based Stereo Vision System for Torsional Stiffness Measurement of Helicopter Blades
authors: Yuxin Yu, Yong Tang, Bangshu Xiong, Jiujiu Chen, Fan Wu, Jianping Huang, Zhidong Zhu, Qiaofeng Ou
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: The structural dynamic characteristics of the rotor system are influenced by the torsional stiffness of each blade section. Traditional methods for measuring blade torsional stiffness involve extensive wiring, slow measurement speed, and high costs. This paper proposes a stereo vision-based system for torsional stiffness measurement of helicopter blades using YOLOv8. First, the YOLOv8 algorithm is used to detect circular markers attached to different sections of the helicopter blade. Second, the stereo vision system captures displacement images of the markers under applied loads and performs three-dimensional coordinate reconstruction based on pre-calibrated camera parameters. Finally, the relative displacement changes are utilized to calculate the torsional angle of each blade section, from which the torsional stiffness is derived. Experimental results show that within a measurement range of 0.5 meters, the system achieves a three-dimensional coordinate measurement error of less than 0.05 millimeters and a relative error in torsional stiffness calculation of $2.7 \%$. The system enables noncontact, high-precision measurement of torsional stiffness for helicopter blades.

title: Sign-Speak
authors: Dania Ann Babu, Deliza Anil, Dona Lonappan, Dreamy K Johnson, A Elsa Raju, K Anusree
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: People who use American Sign Language (ASL) often rely on it as their primary way of communicating with each other. When they find it hard to express themselves verbally, it can sometimes be challenging for them to share their feelings or emotions in a way that others easily understand.. And since the vast majority of the population does not use American Sign Language (ASL), there is a significant communication barrier between hearing impaired and hearing communities. The purpose of our SIGN-SPEAK application is to provide a web application that will translate sign language into text from video uploaded and back into sign language from uploaded video in real time. Computer vision via Media Pipe is used to detect hand movements, along with a Random Forest Classifier to provide validation of accurately recognizing specific signs (or gestures). Media Pipe captures 21 unique key points on both hands to render an accurate representation of the gestures made; additionally, Random Forest uses the captured data to identify the patterns of signs. This helps the system recognize letters,numbers, and words in real time. The prototype achieves 96 percent accuracy on four static gestures. Processes at 30 frames per second with less than one second of latency. Our application connects users with disorders to the outside community,making communication easier and more inclusive. Testing shows that the system detects and translates signs effectively, making it useful for schools, hospitals, and public services.

title: Feature Selection for Human Activity Recognition Using Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Sanika Rithe, Adnan Rouf Patel, Shaurya Saxena, Vinit Tribhuvan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Feature selection is a crucial step in machine learning, aimed at improving model performance by reducing data dimensionality and enhancing interpretability. This study investigates the use of PSO techniques for selection of features on the UCI Human Activity Recognition (HAR) dataset. We explore and evaluate three PSO-based methods: PySwarms Binary PSO, DEAP GA-PSO Hybrid, and Custom Binary PSO. Unfeatured baseline model have been compared with our techniques. Our results show that by efficiently choosing the optimal feature subsets, the PSO-FS based method yields optimal result than the baseline, resulting in better classification metrics. The study emphasizes how crucial feature selection is for activity detection tasks and shows that Custom Binary PSO is a viable method for obtaining accurate and efficient classification.

title: AI-Augmented Legal Analytics for Assessing the Public Health Consequences of Corporate Carbon Emissions
authors: Shalini. R, Anju Mohan, U.Marimuthu
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The increasing contribution of corporate carbon emissions to climate change and public health degradation necessitates a robust, interdisciplinary framework for legal accountability and impact assessment. The study introduces an AI-augmented legal analytics system designed to evaluate the public health consequences of corporate carbon emissions by integrating environmental, legal, and epidemiological datasets. The proposed model employs spatio-temporal alignment of satellite-based pollutant concentration data (Sentinel-5P, GHGsat) with public health metrics (hospitalization, mortality, morbidity), and incorporates legal compliance data extracted from databases like LexisNexis and Westlaw. Natural language processing techniques—such as Named Entity Recognition and transformer-based models (Legal-BERT, CaseLaw-BART)— enable extraction of obligations, violations, and litigation precedents. Graph Neural Networks and Double Machine Learning models are utilized to quantify the causal relationship between emissions and health outcomes, while a cross-domain knowledge graph links corporate entities, pollutants, legal norms, and medical conditions. Results show that the LSTM-Attention network achieved a predictive accuracy of 91.6% in estimating respiratory hospital admissions, while Bayesian Neural Networks reached 88.5% accuracy with uncertainty estimation. The Legal Environmental Non-Compliance Index (LENCI) accurately ranked corporations based on violation history and associated health impact. Knowledge graph inferences exposed latent legal-pollution-health associations, enhancing regulatory visibility. The AI-augmented pipeline offers a comprehensive, interpretable framework that facilitates policy-making, regulatory enforcement, and early health risk detection. By combining legal reasoning with machine learning and geospatial analytics, the study demonstrates a new frontier in environmental governance and public health protection.

title: Self-Supervised Learning Approach for Reconstruction and Quality Assessment of Noisy CT Images in Lung Disease Diagnosis
authors: Sarumathi T, M. Jasmin, J.R. Arunkumar, Dinesh. D. P
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Higher noise levels in computed tomography (CT) pictures make it hard to effectively identify lung disease because they mask important clinical indicators and sensitive anatomical details. This paper presents a self-supervised learning approach for precise reconstruction and objective quality assessment of noisy lung CT images, obviating the necessity for explicit ground-truth references. The suggested method effectively removes noise while keeping important diagnostic information by exploiting internal data consistency to directly recover intrinsic picture representations from corrupted data. A quantitative analysis of the input and processed CT images indicates significant enhancements in statistical characteristics and visual quality. The histogram spread goes from 172 to 200, which means that the contrast is better spread out. The entropy goes from 6.12 to 6.57, which means that the information is richer. The average brightness went up from 63.27 to 78.27, which means that the light was better. A PSNR of 21.81 dB and an SSIM of 0.759 show that the anatomical parts have been kept, which shows that the reconstruction quality is constant. Heatmaps that show pixel-by-pixel absolute changes can help to find areas of improvement more clearly without causing structural problems. The suggested self-supervised architecture provides a scalable and dependable way to improve CT scans in clinical settings when it comes to diagnosing lung diseases, where data availability and annotation problems still exist. This technique makes it easier to make decisions and analyze radiological data, and it works well with automation.

title: Automated Bone Fracture Detection using X-Ray Images with CNN
authors: M. Jagadish, M. Akash, N. Kathirvel
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Major cause of worldwide emergency visits is as a result of bone fractures. Radiographs interpreted by hand are tedious and subject to errors, especially minor fractures. The hypothesis is the hybrid deep learning framework based on the unique detection system, with the option of automation. This model combines two or more sophisticated overarches to preserve the global context and tight-scale fracture structure and does not require any feature engineering. The system has been evaluated on a multi-body part, thoroughly annotated Kaggle dataset of radiographs, and uses multi-stage ductus avoidance through thorough preprocessing and augmentation under adverse conditions of imaging variation. It has a classification accuracy of 99.8 that is much higher than traditional and standard deep learning methods. This shows a high level of accuracy of the framework and the capability of scaffolding the radiologists as well as speed up diagnosis and efficiency of clinical workflow.

title: Optimized Architecture and Strategies for High Performance Computing in Cloud and Hybrid Environments
authors: S. Shanthi, R. Karthik, R. Madhuramya, Vimit Varghese, G.M.Banu Priya, S. Ramasamy
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: High-performance computing has traditionally been based on large on-site supercomputers that provide powerful but costly and rigid performance. HPC workloads may now benefit from flexible on-demand access to computing resources because to the expansion of cloud computing. Yet issues like inconsistent performance slow networks and complex data that processing persist when HPC is used to cloud or hybrid environments. A practical method for enhancing the performance of HPC applications in the cloud and hybrid settings is presented in this study. The framework leverages hardware that the acceleration and intelligent scheduling for containerized programs to balance the cost of performance and resource use. To lower communication latency, it also uses the software-defined networking adaptive task transfer and data-aware scheduling. When compared to conventional cloud-based HPC systems that experimental testing using the scientific and industrial workloads demonstrates up to 17% quicker performance. An average 10- 14% reduction in cloud compute cost, primarily due to reduced billed execution time on cloud resources. The results demonstrate that while retaining scalability to security and sustainability the suggested hybrid HPC architecture can manage the demanding applications such as genetic research, climate modeling, and AI simulations.

title: An Efficient Deepfake Image Detection Framework Based on Facial Landmark Localization and Deep Feature Extraction
authors: Aryan Upadhyay, Sujay Pandey, Utkarsh Kumar Pandey, Priyanshu Varshney, Shelly Gupta, Mukesh Kumar Tripathi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Recent developments in Deep Learning have made it frighteningly easy to create artificially realistic content, resulting in an increasingly depressing reality crisis on all digital media. These are systems that have been extracted from the lab and that have acquired social, political and other public uses in which they often are being misused, as in the case of fraud, disinformation and identity theft. The increasing risk of this threat further underscores the need for effective and automatic deepfake detection systems. To counter this threat, author herein present Deep Shield as a generic framework to recognise the AI-generated visual content. The system employs an MTCNN for precise face detection and alignment, and the classifier is trained to focus on facial regions of interest where artefacts are likely to occur. The extracted facial features are processed by a Meso-Net-based architecture designed to capture minor mesoscopic distortions and pixel-level warping artefacts arising from GAN-based synthesis. Unlike typical forensics techniques that get nullified under heavy compression, Deep Shield employs special preprocessing to preserve information at high frequencies. With training on a cross-dataset set based on Face Forensics++, the method shows strong generalisation to various resolutions and compression factors. Experimental results suggest that Deep Shield is capable of achieving better accuracy and efficiency than traditional classifiers, indicating the practicability and scalability of Deep Shield to real-time content verification and digital forensics.

title: PC-MAL: Enhancing Log Labeling Consistency via Multi-Agent Collaboration and Reflective Feedback
authors: Jiarui Liu, Yang Ji
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: In the operation and maintenance of complex software systems, log analysis is crucial for anomaly detection and fault diagnosis. However, manual log labeling is labor-intensive and error-prone due to diverse log formats and complex semantics. While Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise for automated labeling, single-agent approaches are often sensitive to prompt design and may suffer from semantic drift (e.g., confusing recovery messages with failures), leading to unstable results. To address these challenges, this paper proposes PC-MAL, a multi-agent labeling mechanism that combines Prompt Collaboration with Reflective Feedback. PC-MAL adopts a closed-loop process to improve consistency and accuracy under a comparable token budget. The framework consists of four agents: Task Decomposer, Annotator Agents, Reviewer, and Prompt Optimizer. Experiments on the public LogHub dataset show that PC-MAL achieves 91.0% accuracy and a Fleiss’ Kappa of 0.87 on the Zookeeper test set, outperforming strong baselines such as self-consistency and debate-style multi-agent methods. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of PC-MAL for structured log labeling tasks.

title: Research on the Construction and Application of Forecasting Model of China's Trade Flow and Export Potential Based on Trade Big Data
authors: Taosheng Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: With the liberalization of trade and investment and the deepening of economic globalization, the prediction of trade flow and export potential has once again become a hot issue in the international community. It is difficult for the traditional linear model to explain the nonlinear phenomenon in export trade, which leads to a large prediction error. However, for linear models with piecewise and time-dependent variables, the modified results are not ideal. Therefore, it is necessary to find a nonlinear method that can predict and model foreign trade exports. Based on big trade data, this paper applies FNN (Fuzzy neural network) method to build a forecasting model of China's trade flow and export potential. By optimizing the model, the influence of parameters on the model during FNN training is discussed. According to the availability of data and the need of research, this paper selects the relevant trade data of 20 major trading countries in China from 2010 to 2019 to analyze the FNN trade flow and export potential prediction model. The research results show that the prediction results of training samples are of high accuracy and the error is within 5%, indicating that the FNN prediction model of China's import and export trade volume is reasonable and available. Compared with other forecasting methods, the MSE and MAPE of the proposed scheme are reduced to a great extent, and the MSE and MAPE of the proposed scheme are 8.107 and 5.944, respectively, which shows that this method is effective and feasible for forecasting China's trade flow and export potential.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Model for ECG Arrhythmia Detection: Integrating CNN, Quantum Computing and LSTM
authors: Sreya Mynampati, Premanand V, Girishkanna D
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) signals are very important in the diagnosis of cardiovascular diseases like arrhythmias. Current deep learning models would either concentrate on spatial or temporal feature extraction and thus may fail to grasp the intricate dynamics of ECG data effectively. This work proposes a hybrid deep model that combines the CNNs, QNNs, and LSTM networks for enhanced ECG arrhythmia classification. The model proposed begins by preprocessing PTB-XL 12-lead ECG signals via resampling, noise removal, normalization, and segmentation. CNN layers learn spatial features like QRS morphology, and LSTM layers learn temporal dependencies in the sequence of heartbeats. A quantum layer-parameterized quantum circuits modeled-is introduced between CNN and LSTM phases to facilitate more powerful feature transformation via quantum entanglement and superposition, allowing richer representations for classification. Fully connected layers in the end do multi-class arrhythmia predictions. The hybrid model is compared on the basis of accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC across various diagnostic classes like myocardial infarction, conduction disturbances, and ST/T abnormalities. The model proposed in the paper exhibits more classification accuracy and stability than in the case of conventional CNN, LSTM, or Transformer-based models. The point to note here is that by adding QNN, performance improves in edge cases with minimal variations in waveform arrhythmias. This model presents an innovative architectural contribution using quantum feature embedding coupled with classical deep learning to perform biomedical signal classification. It is scalable to low-power hardware implementation, and therefore, it is appropriate for use in wearable health monitoring system.

title: A Seamless Workflow from Open-Source Planning Models to Industry-Standard Stability Simulations
authors: Daniel Jung, Saikrishna Vallabhaneni, Frank Schuldt, Karsten Von Maydell
publish: 2026 Open Source Modelling and Simulation of Energy Systems (OSMSES)
abstract: The ongoing transition toward decentralized generation and inverter-based resources has heightened the need for robust simulation methodologies capable of evaluating system stability under a wide range of operating conditions. Nevertheless, restricted access to real world grid data and prevailing confidentiality constraints hinder the dissemination of detailed models, thereby limiting collaborative research and reproducibility. Although open frameworks such as Python for Power System Analysis (PyPSA) offer transparent and flexible tools for system optimization and scenario development, they currently lack advanced analytical capabilities, particularly for dynamic simulation. To address this deficiency, we present pypsa2pf, a Python-based converter that translates PyPSA network representations into DIgSILENT PowerFactory projects. The converter automatically maps network topology, equipment specifications, control schemes, and time-series data to PowerFactory, requiring minimal subsequent manual intervention. The utility of pypsa2pf is illustrated through its application to a multi-sectoral 2045 reference network for Northwest Germany. Validation of the conversion is performed by comparing direct-current (DC) load-flow results of the PowerFactory model with those obtained from the original PyPSA model. The resulting model reproduces the original network parameters and operating set points with high fidelity, requiring only minimal adjustments to enable AC load-flow and dynamic power-system analyses. pypsa2pf markedly streamlines the workflow from open-source scenario development with PyPSA to industry-grade stability assessment in PowerFactory, facilitating more efficient and collaborative power system studies.

title: Object Detection for In-Store Product Identification: A Camera Point of Sale (POS)–Centered Approach
authors: Ralph Edcel R. Fabian, Louis Raphael Q. Lagare, Peter Miles Anthony Laporre, John A. Bacus
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Power Engineering (AIPE)
abstract: This paper explores the development of an AI-powered object detection system for retail Point of Sale (POS) applications, specifically targeting small businesses. Addressing the limitations of traditional barcode-based and manual POS systems, the research proposes a cost-effective, camera-based solution leveraging the Roboflow YOLOv11 Accurate model for real-time product identification. The methodology involved dataset creation using Roboflow, model training on a custom dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 1}$</tex> grocery product classes, and performance evaluation using precision, recall, and mean Average Precision (mAP). The results demonstrate the model's strong performance, achieving high accuracy in product detection and tracking, with mAP reaching 96.1 %. The study concludes that the AI-enhanced system offers a significant improvement over existing POS solutions for small retailers, providing efficient sales tracking, reduced pricing errors, and enhanced customer experiences. Future work recommends expanding the dataset and exploring custom neural network architectures to further improve the system's robustness and accuracy.

title: Inhale Insight: A Data-Driven Approach to Breathing Pattern Analysis
authors: Sandhya G, Krishna Paresh Raichura, Sreejha J, Srimathi B, Sivanithishkumar D, Sumathi S
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Respiratory illnesses continue to be a serious global health concern, making early and reliable diagnosis increasingly important. This work investigates how machine learning techniques can assist in identifying and classifying respiratory conditions through the analysis of breathing sounds. The proposed framework operates in three main stages: collecting and preprocessing respiratory audio signals, extracting important sound characteristics such as wheezes and crackles, and classifying diseases using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model. The system was developed and tested using 920 annotated respiratory recordings obtained from 126 patients diagnosed with conditions such as COPD, lower respiratory tract infections (LRTI), and upper respiratory tract infections (URTI). The results show strong classification performance, suggesting that automated respiratory sound analysis can support early detection and continuous monitoring. Such a system has the potential to be used in real-time clinical settings, enabling faster treatment decisions while reducing healthcare costs. Overall, this approach demonstrates how machine learning can contribute to more accurate diagnoses and improved patient care.

title: Deep learning-based Epileptic Seizure Stage Classification from EEG using GDCC features
authors: T. S Anishma, Rajeev Rajan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Epilepsy is recognized as one of the most prevalent neurological conditions in global statistics and its consequences are reflected as seizures. In epileptic patients, the brain may certainly be affected by prolonged and unprovoked seizures.The best method for detecting the most prevalent brain disorders, particularly epilepsy, is an Electroencephalogram(EEG) technique. It takes a lot of time to diagnose brain disorders manually using EEG. The study suggests an EEG analysis system to analyze signals and examines distinctive EEG patterns in an epileptic patient. The main objective of this study is to classify the various stages of epilepsy using the Group Delay Cepstral Coefficient(GDCC) features and deep learning algorithms. The LSTM model proposed for this study allow us to obtain an accuracy of $\mathbf{9 3 . 3 3 \%}$. For practical implication, the user interface framework is deployed to highlight the epilepsy stage and to provide appropriate warning about seizure.

title: Automated Waste Sorting Based on MobileNetV3 with Transfer Learning
authors: Chenan Wu, Yanning Yang, Xingxing Yang, Jingyi Shi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: Traditional waste sorting methods primarily rely on manual operations, presenting limitations of high costs and low efficiency. In contrast, automated waste sorting algorithms based on deep learning can significantly reduce manual labor intensity. The MobileNetV3 model adopted in this study, combined with transfer learning, significantly reduces the number of parameters while markedly improving computational efficiency. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, comparative experiments were conducted with mainstream models including MobileNetV2, DenseNet121, ResNet18, and GoogleNet after confirming the feasibility of transfer learning. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed MobileNetV3 model achieves the optimal classification accuracy of 0.9123.

title: Transfer Learning for Remaining Useful Life Prediction with Irregular Time-Series under Small-Sample and Right-Censored Conditions: An Application to Avionics Systems
authors: Jinhe Yu, Jianyin Zhao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Accurate remaining useful life (RUL) prediction is crucial for the health management of avionics systems, yet it is often hindered by irregularly sampled time-series data, limited labeled samples, and right-censored degradation trajectories. These challenges substantially reduce the effectiveness of conventional data-driven prognostic methods. This paper proposes a transfer learning–based framework for RUL prediction under small-sample and right-censored conditions. Knowledge from data-rich source domains is transferred to target avionics systems with scarce failure data to enhance model generalization. A time-aware representation learning strategy is employed to model irregular temporal intervals without uniform resampling. In addition, a censoring-aware learning mechanism is incorporated to effectively utilize right-censored observations during training. Experimental results on avionics system datasets show that the proposed approach outperforms representative baseline methods in prediction accuracy and robustness, particularly in small-sample scenarios. The results demonstrate the practicality of transfer learning for RUL prediction with irregular and censored time-series data in avionics applications.

title: Spatially-suppressed Reliability-gated Memory for Transformer Tracking
authors: Aoshen Hao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Single-stream Transformer trackers have achieved remarkable success by unifying feature extraction and relation modeling. However, many existing methods still suffer from model drift in long-term scenarios, largely due to blind online updates where unreliable observations (e.g., occlusions and dis-tractors) are inadvertently written into historical representations. In this work, we propose SRMTrack, a reliability-gated one-stream tracking framework that maintains a compact set of high-quality Appearance Tokens as historical cues. Unlike complex multi-branch designs, SRMTrack integrates historical information through a unified Appearance Decoder with lightweight cross-attention. Crucially, we introduce a Reliability Assessment module that is executed after generating the current prediction (score map and bounding box) to decide whether the current frame should be used for updating the Appearance Tokens. This assessment employs two rigorous gates: (1) a Low-Confidence Filter that rejects updates when the target is likely lost and heavily occluded; and (2) a Multi-Peak Filter with Spatial Suppression that masks the primary peak region to accurately detect potential distractors and skip ambiguous updates. During inference, the curated Appearance Tokens are fused with the current search features via the Appearance Decoder, improving robustness while avoiding error accumulation. Experiments on standard benchmarks demonstrate that SRMTrack effectively mitigates memory corruption and consistently improves robustness in complex scenarios.

title: Enhancing Organizational Security through Specialized Deep Transfer Learning for Threat Identification
authors: Vijayakumar G, Nelson S, Mohan Dhass G, Mohamed Ashraf M, Logesh M, Madhankumar M
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Organizations should run advanced security methods to proactively detect and counter combating against cyber threats. Deep Transfer Learning (DTL) has emerged as a powerful technique for leveraging the application of pre-trained deep learning models against the domain of cyber security. This study introduces a DTL framework that integrates domain knowledge and adaptive feature learning to enhance threat detection accuracy, meaning this model will transfer its knowledge from large-scale security datasets to detect newer threats with almost zero labelled data. Unlike conventional machine learning, fine-tuned deep neural networks capture attack signatures across vectors. The experiments reported in this paper prove that DTL has certainly given a better scenario in hunting down malware, phishing, and intrusions, as it reduced false alarms while offering quite a reliable defensive measure. Because of its ultimate ability to learn adaptively, the framework behaves on its own by means of self-improvement and is more scalable against new threats. Here is a clear emphasis on DTL's ability to pave the way for the emergence of intelligent, automated, and adaptive security systems, hastening the pace to fight against cyber dangers while buttressing enterprise safety.

title: AI Driven Cultural Preservation Multimodal Language Models for Intangible Heritage Image Editing
authors: Taorui Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This study looks into new ways to edit images and videos of intangible cultural heritage using simple language models. The goal is to help save tangible cultural heritage with AI. The researchers combined computer vision, natural language processing, and deep learning to create an AI system. This system can understand cultural backgrounds and make smart image edits. It can spot and protect cultural details in images, while also letting users make creative changes using plain language. Tests showed that the new method, called CARE-MLLM, makes editing intangible cultural heritage images faster and better, while keeping the culture true. The study also talks about ethical issues and what could happen next in AI-based cultural preservation. Overall, this research offers a fresh tech approach to protecting intangible cultural heritage in the digital age, which is of great significance for promoting cultural diversity and heritage preservation in human society.

title: Advanced AI Systems for Noise Suppression in Wireless Signal Transmission Using Deep Learning
authors: M. Jyothi, R. Senthamarai, Narasimhulu K, Gotte Ranjith Kumar, Gangu Rama Naidu, M. Nathiya
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Modern wireless communication systems encounter significant performance deterioration due to noise sources that decrease their reliability criteria. The document shows how deep learning algorithms enabled an improved AI-controlled system for reducing wireless signal transmission noise. The study analyses the combining power of CNNs and LSTM networks to remove time-series wireless signal noise. Both CNNs and LSTMs operate differently in signal processing through feature extraction because they detect spatial patterns in noisy data, but LSTM specifically retains long temporal dependencies to support dynamic noise suppression mechanisms. The proposed architectural structure selects CNNs to obtain local features along with LSTM networks which detect sequence patterns because noise manifests temporally. The CNN-LSTM model produces outstanding results for noise reduction applications by achieving better SNR levels and BER reduction based on experimental testing. Proof from this research indicates deep learning methods demonstrate robust potential to develop signal noise reduction solutions and deploy them directly in communication system noise cancellation.

title: Survey of malware classification Models for Multi-source data
authors: Lu Hou, Chao Li, Ruliang Lin, Xintong Lin, Mengyu Xia, Yiming Shi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the continuous evolution of network security threats, the detection and classification of malicious code has become a core topic in the field of information security. The malicious code classification model based on multi-source data can effectively improve the detection accuracy and model robustness by fusing multi-modal information such as static features, dynamic behavior features and network traffic. This paper systematically reviews the research status of malware classification models driven by multi-source data, deeply analyzes the main pain points faced by current models, and summarizes the key technologies such as feature extraction methods, data preprocessing processes and model architecture design. On this basis, this paper further discusses possible future research directions, including the development of multimodal deep fusion methods, the improvement of model robustness and interpretability, and the construction of high-quality malicious code data resources. This paper aims to provide a reference for the research of malicious code classification and promote the application of related technologies in the field of network security.

title: DMedic: Design and Development of a Health Monitoring Platform for Diabetic Patients
authors: Md. Obidul Huq Sagor, A. H. M. Imtiaj, Md Reduanur Rahman, Nasrin Akter Tohfa, Md Shakhawat Hossen, Md. Ataur Rahman
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Diabetes is an escalating health problem across the world, and it is particularly hurting nations such as Bangladesh where there are no adequate resources. Although digital health tools have taken huge steps globally, they are not always structured to meet the specific demands of such destinations as Bangladesh. Adoption is challenging due to language Barriers or lack of access and lack of localized aspects. Their effective diabetes management is analyzed by their patients. Health insurance that is provided by the government and is applied through the platform. To address this, we have come up with DMedic A diabetes management platform that is userfriendly and designed to close this gap. DMedic contains a variety of tools which are aimed at assisting diabetic patients, healthcare workers, and administrators. These characteristics will involve real-time glucose monitoring notifications in case of appointments, customized meal plans, as well as data storage that is safe. The only difference is that it completely supports the Bangli language to make it available to individuals in rural and underserved regions. It is user friendly where it does not require one to be techno savvy and has high data security to ensure privacy of the users. The platform was developed through the contribution of major stakeholders and was developed based on a flexible and scalable framework. When testing it with the users, 85 percent of people found it simple to use and 90 percent were particularly pleased with the appointment scheduling feature. Moving forward, there is the possibility of further development of DMedic (with telemedicine functionality, incorporation of wearables, and more personalized care).

title: Enhanced DNA-Based Image Encryption Framework for Secure Healthcare Data Transmission
authors: K. Bhavani, Ganivada Venkata Poornika Devi, Tammana Vijaya Manikanta, Gandi Calvin Pradeep Kumar, Mandapalli Teja
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Image transmission in healthcare networks requires encryption techniques that provide strong security while maintaining low computational overhead. Conventional cryptographic algorithms such as AES and RSA offer high levels of security; however, their computational and energy demands often make them unsuitable for resource-constrained medical systems. To address this challenge, this paper presents an Enhanced DNA-Based Image Encryption Framework that integrates chaotic logistic map-based keystream generation with DNA-inspired binary nibble substitution to achieve lightweight and efficient medical image encryption. The proposed approach employs nucleotide encoding of pixel data combined with XORbased diffusion to enhance confusion and diffusion at minimal computational cost. Experimental evaluation on medical images demonstrates that the encrypted images achieve an average Shannon entropy of 7.99 bits, indicating a near-ideal level of randomness and strong resistance to statistical attacks.

title: A Study on Design Requirements for Ensuring Cross-Compatibility in Korean Public-Sector AI Systems
authors: NunSol Park, Eunju Kim, DongHee Kim, HanKyoung Choi, JinKyu Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies and the diversification of commercial models, the demand for AI adoption in the public sector has surged dramatically. Governments and public institutions are actively leveraging AI technologies to enhance administrative efficiency, deliver personalized services, and enable data-driven policy-making. However, in the actual implementation process, numerous limitations and challenges have emerged. In particular, when AI systems are designed in a way that is dependent on specific cloud platforms or proprietary models, organizations are often burdened with high costs and technical constraints during system transitions, expansions, and maintenance in response to evolving technologies. In such a fast-changing AI ecosystem, ensuring the stability and scalability of AI services in the public sector requires establishing cross-compatibility across AI infrastructure, cloud platforms, and AI models. Cross-compatibility refers to the ability for services, data, and models to be seamlessly interoperable and reusable across different AI environments. This enables public institutions to avoid vendor lock-in and adopt a diverse range of suppliers and solutions. Moreover, securing cross-compatibility acts as a strategic means to reduce long-term maintenance costs while strengthening the sustainability and technological autonomy of public AI services. Nevertheless, many current AI projects in the Korean public sector are being implemented based on heterogeneous standards, model formats, and platform environments. As a result, compatibility issues frequently arise during model replacement or technology migration processes. To effectively introduce and manage diverse AI technologies, it is imperative for public institutions to adopt cross-compatibility strategies grounded in platform independence and open standards. Therefore, this paper proposes design-level requirements aimed at securing cross-compatibility in AI services for the public sector.

title: To Avoid Unauthorized Authentication by Identification Through Behavioural Analysis
authors: Lokesh Khedekar, Chinmay Mahesh Yadav, Satyam Kumar, Nishikant Waghmare, Vaishnavi Patil, Radha Yeginwar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Keystroke dynamics represent a promising form of behavioural biometrics that are capable of strengthening password-based authentication, traditionally applied without the need for further hardware or intrusive procedures. This research develops and evaluates a machine-learning-powered authentication framework that analyzes users' typing behaviour to detect unauthorized access attempts-even when an attacker is in possession of valid credentials. The system captures the following keystroke timing features during a controlled typing task: hold times, flight times, inter-key latencies, digraph/trigraph patterns, and error metrics. By leveraging these features, multiple machine-learning models-termed as Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, One-Class SVM, Isolation Forest, and a lightweight LSTM-are trained to compute a Behaviour Confidence Score in the range <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0 - 1 0 0 \%}$</tex>, determining the access policy, which may be allow, challenge with OTP, or deny. The proposed approach provides a complete end-to-end pipeline that includes data-collection mechanisms, feature engineering, model training, drift-handling strategies, and real-world deployment using a Flask backend, JavaScriptbased keystroke capture, and MongoDB storage. Prominent experimental plans aimed at the quantitative evaluation of accuracy, robustness against imitation and credential-only attacks, performance variation across devices, and long-term drifts are proposed. Due to the absence of real data, placeholder results are presented; empirical design, however, anticipates AUC values higher than 0.90 for the Random Forest classifiers, while Equal Error Rates should be significantly lower when using per-user one-class models given sufficient amounts of training samples. Ethical and privacy considerations are integrated into the proposed system by storing only anonymized timing vectors, by participant consent, and by mitigating replayattack risks. This will contribute to a deployable behaviouralbiometric authentication framework, a replicable methodology, and insight into the usability-security trade-off. Findings support the feasibility of integrating keystroke dynamics into multi-layered authentication systems, showing future research opportunities in deep behavioural modeling, adaptive learning, and cross-device generalization.

title: Real-Time Remote Monitoring of Toxic Environments in Mining Sites using ESP and LoRa
authors: Tadela Vasundharadevi, Ramesh Jayaraman, Kalipatnapu Mahima, Menti Yugandhar, Pondugula Sahithi, Shaik Fathima
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Miners working either at ground or below ground mining sites are exposed to potentially dangerous gas and unstable conditions as part of their work.
