Real time monitoring of potential hazards is challenging due to high cost, limited reach, and fragility of cable-based systems and poor range performance of most commercially available short distance wireless alternatives for use in long tunnel mining environments. A low cost, real time environmental monitoring system has been developed by this researcher using a LoRa (long range) transmitter/receiver interface to an ESP8266 microcontroller with a variety of sensors that measure methane <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{C H}_{\mathbf{4}}$</tex>), carbon monoxide (CO), hydrogen sulfide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{H}_{2} ~\mathrm{S}$</tex>), and carbon dioxide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{CO}_{2}\right)$</tex>. In addition to measuring levels of these four hazardous gases, temperature and humidity were also measured. All sensor data was sent via a wireless link to a gateway unit that displayed readings locally, logged them to the cloud, and allowed remote access to all readings using an Internet of Things (IoT) platform. Both on site alerts, and off-site monitoring of mine environment was possible. An experimental evaluation demonstrated reliable long-range communication, low power consumption, and reliable detection of hazardous gases. The use of LoRa to enable a wireless sensor network for mining is scalable and will be used to improve the safety of the environment and support proactive risk management for the mining industry.

title: MRBiLSTM: Integrating Relative Position-Aware Multi-Head Attention with Bidirectional LSTMs for Fine-Grained Sentiment Analysis
authors: Battu Chiranjeevi, R. Vijaya Prakash
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Sentiment analysis remains a critical challenge in natural language processing due to the complexity of capturing contextual dependencies and sentiment-bearing linguistic patterns. This paper presents MRBiLSTM (Multi-Head Relative Attention with Bidirectional LSTM), a novel hybrid architecture that synergistically integrates bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory networks with multi-head relative position-aware attention mechanisms for fine-grained sentiment classification. Unlike conventional approaches using absolute positional encodings, our model explicitly captures pairwise token distances through relative position embeddings, enabling translation-invariant pattern recognition across variable-length sequences. Evaluated on the IMDB movie review dateset containing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> samples, the proposed architecture achieves 88.56% accuracy, 88.80% F1-score, 0.9518 AUC-ROC, and exceptionally low calibration error as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E C E} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 1 0 0 8}$</tex>. Comprehensive ablation studies demonstrate the effectiveness of our regularization strategies including dropout, layer normalization, gradient clipping, and early stopping. Attention weight visualizations reveal interpretable decision-making processes, with the model successfully identifying sentiment-critical tokens. The results validate that combining recurrent sequential modeling with relative attention mechanisms produces robust, well-calibrated, and interpretable sentiment classifiers suitable for production deployment in sentiment-driven applications.

title: FPGA Architecture of Reliable High-Throughput Error Correction for UAV FSO Communication System
authors: Irzal Zaini, Ida Bagus Krisna Yoga Utama, Fadhila Ahmad, Muhammad Fairuz Mummtaz, Yeong Min Jang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) Free-Space Optical (FSO) is the next generation technology in wireless communication systems. The high bandwidth potential of FSO communication is not without flaw, the optical wave is easily distorted by channel conditions and must have accurate tracking. To improve reliability, error correction must be applied. In this paper, FPGAbased error correction architecture is proposed to comply with the UAV FSO system. The design is based on Reed-Solomon (RS) code and Line Product Code (LPC). The proposed system utilizes the parallel processing and pipelining of Euclidean Processor in decoding unit, which increases data throughput significantly. Our design also offers low resource usage on the FPGA board, effectively reducing power consumption. Performance test shows BER of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup>, data rate at 8.921 Gbps, and power consumption at 1.6 W.

title: Smart Mental Health Band for Students using AIoT and Predictive Algorithms
authors: M. Misba, B. Yashwanth, S. Nikhil, K. Nandi Vardhan
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Mental health concerns such as stress, anxiety, and sleep disorders are increasingly observed among students, yet they often remain undetected due to the reactive nature of traditional assessment methods. Existing approaches mainly rely on questionnaires and periodic evaluations, which are unsuitable for continuous monitoring. Recent advancements in wearable sensing, Internet of Things (IoT), and artificial intelligence provide opportunities for real-time and proactive mental health monitoring. This paper proposes an AIoT-enabled smart wearable band for continuous monitoring of student stress using physiological and activity-based parameters such as heart rate, body temperature, and physical activity. The collected sensor data are transmitted to a cloud platform through an ESP/NodeMCU module and analyzed using machine learning algorithms to identify stress patterns. The system provides real-time visualization, alerts, and personalized recommendations through a web-based interface. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed system achieves a maximum classification accuracy of 94.4 using deep learning-based models for stress detection.

title: Sequential Dual-Loop Digital and Analog PLL Synthesizer for Fast-Locking IoT Applications in 18nm FD-SOI CMOS
authors: Virgile Colrat, David Gaidioz, Andres Asprilla, Frederic Paillardet, Andreia Cathelin, Yann Deval
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: This paper introduces a dual-loop frequency synthesizer that synergizes the scalability of an all-digital phaselocked loop (AD-PLL) with the high precision of an analog PLL, targeting the demanding specifications of Internet of Things (IoT) applications. Traditional AD-PLLs suffer from quantization noise due to limited time-to-digital converter (TDC) resolution, whereas analog PLLs offer low jitter but compromise on reconfigurability and silicon area. In the proposed design, the ADPLL achieves a coarse frequency lock within approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 0} \boldsymbol{\mu} \mathbf{s}$</tex>, followed by fine frequency refinement via the analog loop, reaching full lock in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.3 \mu$</tex> s. For frequency hopping scenarios, this dual-loop architecture supports rapid relocking in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.6 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex>, satisfying Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) channel-switching timing requirements. Implemented in 18 nm FD-SOI CMOS technology, the synthesizer attains jitter below 2 ps while consuming power in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{W}$</tex> to mW range. Compared to existing hybrid approaches, the sequential dual-loop strategy effectively eliminates loop interference, minimizes loop filter area through a low-Kvco design, and achieves an optimized balance among speed, noise, and power consumption, positioning it as a compelling solution for next-generation low-power wireless transceivers.

title: PGPNet-X: A Multi-modal and Explainable Pill Detection System
authors: N Balahariraj, M Subiksha, R Dharshini, T Arunmozhidevan, P Kalpana
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: Despite its accuracy, pill identification in healthcare has always been a challenge due to similar-looking drugs, actual imaging variations, or “look-alike/sound-alike” (LASA) similarity between medications, causing thousands of cases of adverse events each year. In this work, we present PGPNet-X, an explainable multi-modal model that combines YOLOv5-based visual recognition, robust imprint recognition OCR, logo identification from packages, imprint recognition, and verification from electronic prescriptions through gated fusion with graph reasoning for context-constrained explanations. Tested on an expanded international dataset of 18,400 images for 1,120 classes, PGPNet-X records 91.3% mAP@ 0.5 and 63.1% mAP@ 0.5: 0.95 performance-a 5.7-point improvement for mAP@ 0.5 and 7.9 points for mAP@ 0.5: 0.95 over previous PGPNet model.

title: Dynamic Optimization Model of Financial Transaction Key Management Based on Quantum Random Number Generator
authors: Ting Zhu, Lingfei Peng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: In view of the background that financial transactions are facing the threat of quantum computing, this paper proposes a dynamic optimization model of key management based on quantum random number generator (QRNG), aiming to solve the contradiction between security and efficiency of traditional encryption systems through quantum enhancement technology. Methodologically, this paper integrates a four-layer architecture, the quantum physics layer generates true random numbers, the algorithm layer adopts LWE lattice cryptography to resist quantum attacks, the system layer implements quantum key distribution through BB84 protocol, and the application layer dynamically optimizes the key refresh mechanism. The test results show that (1) the key leakage rate of anti-quantum brute force cracking is only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.02 \%(18.45 \%$</tex> in the control group), (2) the leakage rate of man-in-the-middle eavesdropping is 0 %, (3) the high concurrent transaction delay is as low as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.82 ~\text{ms}(5.74 ~\text{ms}$</tex> in the static QRNG group), (4)the success rate of key generation under quantum noise is 99.91 %, (5) dynamic optimization increases the key entropy value by 38.7 % and the anti-cracking time is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>1000$</tex> hours. The conclusion proves that the model can significantly improve the security level of financial transactions through the triple collaboration of true randomness of hardware, quantum resistance of algorithm and dynamic optimization, but it is necessary to further reduce the cost of quantum hardware and establish cross-border QKD certification standards to achieve large-scale deployment.

title: Individual Tree Crown Segmentation in UAV Images Based on Improving Mask R-CNN
authors: Yuxin Wang, Yibo Song, Gang Yang
abstract: The detection and segmentation of tree crowns based on UAV images have important application value. Although deep learning methods have been widely used in target detection and segmentation of UAV images, there are still a series of problems in tree crown segmentation. Annotating data for individual tree crown segmentation in forest images incur high costs, and currently, large-scale open-source datasets are lacking. However, when performing segmentation on small-scale datasets, overfitting is likely to occur, resulting in over segmentation of tree crowns. In addition, current tree crown segmentation methods commonly suffer from issues such as missing segmentation of small tree crowns, blurring and truncation of tree crown edges. To address these problems, we propose an improved Mask RCNN model to achieve effective tree crown segmentation on smallscale datasets. We introduce a novel transfer learning strategy with dynamic learning rates to rectify the problem of overfitting on small-scale datasets. The PointRend module is integrated into the mask head to refine the segmentation result of crown boundaries. In addition, we enhance the FPN structure with a context module to facilitate more effective feature extraction for small tree crowns. Compared with the baseline Mask RCNN, our model improves the detection rate increased by 16.216

title: Exploring Deep Learning Models for Multi-Label Sentiment Analysis in Pandemic Reviews
authors: Nurlieda Ellyanna Munirrah Razali, Ramesh Kumar Ayyasamy, Suthashini Subramaniam
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: The term ‘pandemic' has become widely familiar following the lived experience of the global COVID-19 crisis. The pandemic resulted in widespread discussions in the digital world, especially on social media platforms, where people expressed a wide range of thoughts and emotions. Therefore, multi-label sentiment analysis is functional as it captures multiple sentiment classifications in social media data for richer insights. However, there is a need to know the best-suited deep learning models for multi-label sentiment analysis in the pandemic reviews domain. Thus, this study aims to explore, compare, and analyse existing deep learning models applied in this domain by using the PRISMA guidelines. The study identified CNN, LSTM/BiLSTM, and Transformer models (BERT, RoBERTa) as the most common for multi-label sentiment analysis in pandemic reviews. The hybrid CNN + BiLSTM model is utilised on the existing COVID-19 Twitter dataset as a pilot experiment. This model is used to gain preliminary insights into deep learning models' behaviour for pandemic reviews. The result shows that the model can handle multi-label sentiment analysis well in pandemic-related content.

title: A Metric for Evaluating Face Image Deep Learning Classification Models
authors: Fernando Quiroz, Vladimir Y. Mariano
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This study proposes a multidimensional metric for evaluating deep learning models in face image classification, addressing long-standing limitations of accuracy-centered evaluation. While modern architectures such as convolutional neural networks and Vision Transformers demonstrate strong predictive performance, conventional metrics fail to capture deeper structural behaviors, including fairness, representational quality, interpretability, and computational viability. To address this gap, the proposed framework integrates six components: validation F 1 score, linear-probe embedding separability, fairness via skin-tone gap reduction, compute efficiency, anatomical interpretability, and embedding stability, yielding a holistic assessment of model performance. Applied across six benchmark architectures and four Baumann Skin Type tasks, the metric reveals consistent superiority of transformer-based models, which exhibit stronger fairness, stability, and interpretability compared to CNNs. Findings demonstrate that responsible facial model evaluation requires multidimensional criteria that move beyond accuracy to ensure equitable, transparent, and deployment-ready systems. This work contributes toward trustworthy and fairness-aware facial AI.

title: Integration of AI Chatbots in MSMEs: Factors Affecting Adoption and Their Effects on Organizational Performance
authors: Gabriella Kristiani Sandhya Dewanto, Okky Rizkia Yustian
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: AI Chatbot is currently growing rapidly to be implemented by various large companies, but many MSMEs still do not have the readiness and feel the impact of using AI Chatbot. To support digital transformation in Indonesia, it is important to know, how the impact of AI chatbot on organizational performance. However, research that discusses AI chatbot factors and their impact on organizational performance is still limited. This study aims to identify Organizational readiness and Competitive pressure factors on AI Chatbot Adoption and evaluate the impact of the chatbot on Organization Performance with a quantitative approach. Data was collected from 250 MSME owners/managers in Bandung City through a survey and analyzed using PLS-SEM. The results of the analysis show that AI Chatbot Adoption has a negative impact on organizational performance. Then Organizational Readiness has a significant effect on AI Chatbot Adoption, but the direction of the effect is negative and Organizational Readiness and Competitive Pressure have no significant effect on Organization Performance. Additional variables such as digital literacy, organizational culture, AI governance, and maturity implementation, as well as the use of mixed method for deeper understanding can be used for future research.

title: An SNR-Adaptive Deep Joint Source-Channel Coding Scheme for UAV Semantic Image Transmission
authors: Quang Tuan Do, Tung Son Do, Thanh Phung Truong, Dongwook Won, Sungrae Cho
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Deep learning-based joint source-channel coding (Deep JSCC) has demonstrated substantial promise for robust image transmission in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) systems. However, conventional Deep JSCC models operate exclusively at fixed Signal-to-Noise Ratios (SNRs) and exhibit severe performance degradation when channel conditions deviate from their design parameters. Furthermore, deploying multiple specialist models to accommodate varying channel qualities becomes impractical for resource-constrained UAVs due to storage and computational limitations. To address this challenge, we adopt the Attention DL-based JSCC (ADJSCC) mechanism, which conditions channel-wise soft attention on SNR to enable a single model to maintain high performance across diverse channel conditions, and tailor it for UAV semantic image transmission. In particular, we integrate ADJSCC's Attention Feature (AF) modules throughout both encoder and decoder to modulate features based on instantaneous SNR, and provide a UAV-focused training and evaluation protocol. Our comprehensive evaluation on the CIFAR-10 dataset demonstrates that this ADJSCC-based model achieves robust performance across all tested SNRs, consistently matching or surpassing specialist models within their optimal operating regions while significantly outperforming them under mismatched conditions, validating the practical viability of ADJSCC for reliable UAV image transmission in dynamic wireless environments.

title: An Explainable and Computationally Efficient Deepfake Detection Framework Employing XGBoost for Image and Video Media
authors: Dhivya K, Neeraj Kumar M P, Jitesh Gowda G R, Naveen B
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The rapid growth of generative models has led to the creation of convincing fake media. This raises concerns about the spread of misinformation and the loss of trust in digital content. As a result, developing effective and accurate methods for detecting deepfakes is essential in digital forensics. This paper presents a lightweight and clear deepfake detection framework that uses the extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) algorithm. Instead of depending on complex deep convolutional network representations, this system focuses on learning from specific features. It extracts important facial and texture-based descriptors from the FaceForensics++ dataset to classify media as real or fake. By modeling the complex, non-linear relationships between these features, XGBoost can detect subtle, systematic inconsistencies that occur during the forgery process. One significant benefit of this model is its natural interpretability. Through feature importance analysis, it clearly shows how each feature affects the final classification. Experimental results demonstrate that this framework achieves high accuracy in detection with a peak Accuracy of 94.8% and an F1-score of 94.7% while keeping a low computational cost, making it well-suited for real-world media authentication systems. Overall, the XGBoost-based approach provides a reliable balance between performance, clarity, and efficiency in detecting deepfake images and videos.

title: Reliable Hallucination Detection in Large Language Models using Multi-Source Evidence and Natural Language Inference
authors: Lakshmana Kumar T, Yasir Abdullah R, Vijayakumar M, Sanjai Prakash M, Gowtham A, Vignesh K
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The rise of Large Language Model developments in re-shaping various Natural Language Processing (NLP) functional developments, however, despite their ability to produce plausible responses the generated responses will frequently fail to reflect factual accuracy, this misconception is referred to as Hallucination. This critical limitation undermines trust in AI systems and poses significant risks in domains requiring factual accuracy such as healthcare, legal analysis and educational content generation. Existing hallucination detection methods rely primarily on modelinternal consistency checks or simplistic keyword matching against limited reference databases, failing to capture semantic nuances and cross-domain factual verification requirements. This paper presents the Verity AI framework: a Multi-Source Verification System using Artificial Intelligence (MVS-AI) that addresses these limitations through semantic embeddings, natural language inference (NLI) and AI-powered source alignment. The proposed framework decomposes LLMgenerated text into verifiable claims, retrieves evidence from diverse trusted sources including Wikipedia, PubMed, arXiv and web search results, then employs transformer-based models (sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 for embeddings, Facebook/bart-largemnli for NLI, and Groq's llama-3.3-70b-versatile for final verification) to determine claim veracity. Experimental validation demonstrates 87.3% accuracy in detecting hallucinated content, 34 % improvement over baseline keyword-matching approaches and robust performance across medical, scientific and general knowledge domains with an average confidence score of 78.2 %. This study introduces MVS-AI is a multi-source and domainadaptive solution to hallucination detection that employs semantic embeddings, natural language inference and artificial intelligence verification to provide greater precision, fewer false positives and trustworthy confidence levels across a multitude of fields.

title: User Selection Strategy for Covert Communication Systems with Dual-Relay Assistance*
authors: Xiaofei Zhai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: A user selection scheme is proposed for a dual-relay-assisted wireless communication scenario with mutually untrusted relays. A power superposition method is employed by the two relays to combine the forwarded public signal with their own covert signals. Covert communication is achieved through a probabilistic time-divisional transmission method. In a Rayleigh fading environment, the outage probability and interception probability are derived. Furthermore, performance bounds at high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) are provided. Finally, Monte Carlo simulations are conducted to verify the correctness of the theoretical derivations. Compared with a single-relay forwarding system, the proposed scheme not only ensures the efficient and reliable transmission of public signals, but also achieves secure data transmission between mutually untrusted relay users.

title: Line Parameter Identification Method Based on Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolutionary Algorithm
authors: Xian Zheng, Chenyu Zhang, Wenqiang Xie, Jianyu Yu, Xiaolong Xiao
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Distribution networks often have imperfect structures and complex power - supply line distribution, leading to missing line parameters and incomplete topology information. This paper presents a distribution network line parameter identification method using the covariance matrix adaptation evolution strategy (CMA - ES). The method builds an optimization model from historical measurement data to identify line parameters by minimizing the voltage deviation between calculated and actual measured values. Then, the CMA - ES algorithm solves this non - convex optimization problem by iteratively searching the parameter space to optimize the line parameters, enhancing the accuracy and speed of power flow calculations. Finally, simulations on the IEEE 33 - bus system demonstrate the method’s effectiveness in multi - dimensional nonlinear problems and highlight CMA - ES’s good performance in optimizing line parameter identification.

title: Beamforming Design for RIS-Assisted Mobile ISAC Systems Based on OTFS Modulation
authors: Ke Li
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: This paper addresses a Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS)-assisted Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) system based on Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation. In this system, a multi-antenna Base Station (BS) simultaneously performs Multi-User Multiple-Input SingleOutput (MU-MISO) communication and radar sensing with the assistance of an RIS. The target detection performance is evaluated in terms of Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR). Under the constraints of SNR requirements for target detection, transmit power budget, and unit-modulus constraints of RIS reflection coefficients, an optimization problem is formulated to maximize the achievable sum rate of multi-user communication. To tackle this complex non-convex problem, the proposed approach integrates the system model with OTFS modulation, employing a standard OTFS modulation matrix to avoid the complexity of joint optimization. An effective optimization algorithm is proposed to jointly optimize the transmit beamforming matrix W, the receive filter u, and the RIS reflection coefficients. The original problem is decomposed into several tractable subproblems, which are solved iteratively. Simulation results demonstrate the fast convergence and effectiveness of the proposed algorithm. Compared to scenarios without RIS, the joint design of radar and communication beamforming achieves significant performance improvements. Moreover, under mobile channels, the Bit Error Rate (BER) of the proposed OTFS-based scheme gradually decreases, indicating enhanced transmission reliability and a reduction in the proportion of erroneous bits.

title: Integrating Uncertainty Quantification in AI-Driven Predictive Maintenance for Industrial Systems
authors: Lasandi Randini, Lahiru Ubesingha, Dinesh Asanka, Thilini Mahanama, Chathura Rajapakse
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: This research combines Uncertainty Quantification (UQ) with AI for predictive maintenance (PdM), aiming to produce dependable Remaining Useful Life (RUL) predictions and inform risk-managed choices. Recent studies have shown a growing interest in advanced techniques, such as Bayesian deep learning, Monte Carlo (MC) Dropout, ensembles, and heteroscedastic regression, across various fields, including aerospace, manufacturing, and energy. Based on these trends, a Bayesian CNN-LSTM model with a dual-head heteroscedastic output is developed using NASA C-MAPSS data to simulate both epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty. An operational decision rule converts the probabilistic results into maintenance scheduling recommendations. The evaluations demonstrate that the proposed model matches or improves upon the RMSE and MAE of the traditional standard. It also exhibits better calibration, with a lower NLL and a near-nominal PICP, resulting in fewer false alarms and missed detections. These benefits support its use in industrial applications.

title: A MPLS VPN Network Model Based on SDN
authors: Jun Tao, LiPing Zhang, Lei Yue, Hao Xu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: MPLS VPN networks effectively overcome the limitations of NAT, but their management relies heavily on Internet Service Providers, resulting in high costs that are often prohibitive for ordinary users. Moreover, the traditional MPLS VPN architecture suffers from certain performance constraints. To address these issues, a novel MPLS VPN model based on GRE tunnels and CHBF mode is proposed, which offers high speed, security, and cost efficiency. By incorporating SDN which separates control and forwarding functions to enhance automation and programmability, the proposed model achieves improved network efficiency through SDNbased switching. Finally, simulation results confirm the superiority of the new model.

title: An Optimized Machine Learning Framework for Heart Disease Detection Using the Jellyfish Algorithm
authors: Babby E, K. Rajakumari, Karthik V
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: We are seeing millions of people dying from heart problems every year only, making it one of the most common and dangerous diseases in the world. Early and correct identification actually helps reduce death rates and definitely improves patient outcomes. Machine learning itself provides strong tools for disease prediction due to its speed, costeffectiveness, and accuracy. This study further proposes a hybrid approach combining Jellyfish Optimization Algorithm with Artificial Neural Network and Decision Tree classifiers for heart disease prediction. Basically, JOA was used to reduce the dimensions of the Cleveland Heart Disease dataset to solve the curse of dimensionality problem and improve generalization. The same JOA method quickly found the best feature subsets and greatly improved model performance. As per the improved dataset, the ANN and DT classifiers were trained regarding the classification process. The ANN model achieved 97% accuracy, which was slightly better than the DT model itself that showed 96% accuracy. Further analysis revealed that ANN performed marginally superior to DT. Research shows that JOA actually works well to make predictions better and select good features. When doctors use Jellyfish Optimization with simple machine learning models like ANN and DT, they can definitely build reliable systems that help detect heart disease early and make better treatment decisions.

title: Revolutionizing Microcontroller Virtualization: Exploring Micro-Controller as a Service (MaaS) on AWS
authors: Vijay Savani, Palak Kapuriya, Tapan Patel, Jitendra Bhatia, Malaram Kumhar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Microcontroller-as-a-Service (MaaS) is an emerging model that applies cloud principles such as scalability, resource pooling, and remote access to physical embedded hardware. Microcontrollers are increasingly becoming central components of IoT and embedded systems education; due to their limited availability and cost, though, logistical concerns impede practical learning processes. MaaS allows students to remotely execute programs on real hardware that resides in the cloud. This paper describes the proof-of-concept implementation of MaaS with Amazon Web Services. It explain the architecture, system components, and automation workflow that enable uploading MicroPython programs by users for execution on a Raspberry Pi Pico board and fetching outputs through cloud services. We show real interaction logs (SCP/SSH/EC2) that demonstrate the operational feasibility of MaaS. It further discuss issues of scalability, security implications, comparison with state-of-theart remote lab tools, and directions for future expansion and empirical evaluation.

title: Nature Inspired Hierarchical Optimization for Predictive Load Balancing in Multi-Cloud Environments
authors: Shalini A, Mridulla S, R Mohandas, Kandavel N, M Vinodhini, Karthikeyan P
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Multi-cloud computing helps companies to use services from multiple cloud providers, improving reliability and cost optimization, but efficiently balancing workloads across different clouds is still a major problem. This paper introduces a simple and effective hierarchical optimization approach for load balancing in multi cloud environments by combining three nature inspired algorithms Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), and Artificial Bee Colony (ABC) with the LSTM prediction model. In this method, PSO is used to select the best cloud provider, ACO allocates tasks to suitable data centers and ABC distributes tasks among virtual machines. In order to support proactive resources allocation, the future workload is predicted by the LSTM model. Experiments conducted using the Google Cluster Trace shows that the proposed Hierarchical Optimization Algorithm (HOA) accomplishes 96% improvement in multi objective optimization compared to traditional methods. This ensures a robust solution for heterogeneous multi cloud load balancing and a smooth failover between cloud providers during service disruptions.

title: AURA- Autism Understanding and Rehabilitation Assistant
authors: Renju Rachel Varghese, Anagha Devi P, Hima Rose George, Aleesha Mariam Sabu, Ann Mariya Jose K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Early detection of neurodevelopmental disorders is critical for ensuring timely interventions and improving long-term outcomes. Among these, Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) presents unique diagnostic challenges, as traditional assessments are subjective, resource-intensive, and often delayed. To address this, we explore machine learning (ML) techniques for automated ASD prediction using behavioral and clinical datasets. Three models were implemented and compared: Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), Linear Discriminant Analysis with AdaBoost, and a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) neural network. Although XGBoost achieved 100% accuracy on training data, it showed limited generalization, highlighting overfitting. The MLP demonstrated superior reliability, achieving 99.53% accuracy with a 0.9966 F1-score, making it the most effective model. The LDA+AdaBoost pipeline also produced competitive results with 94.31% accuracy. These findings emphasize the robustness of deep learning, particularly MLP, in ASD detection. The proposed framework can assist clinicians in reliable early screening, thereby supporting timely diagnosis and effective management.

title: Hybrid Computational Techniques for Evaluating Biofuel Blends: Impact of Nano Additives on Internal Combustion Engines
authors: Ankita Thakur, Malepati Chandra Sekhar, Prem Nath Suman
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: The increasing demand for sustainable energy sources has prompted extensive research into biofuels as viable alternatives to traditional petroleum-based fuels. This research investigates the impact of nano-additives on the emissions and performance of biofuel blends in Internal Combustion Engines (ICEs). Specifically, the research focuses on evaluating biofuels derived from Eichhornia crassipes (water hyacinth) and their blends with nano-additives, such as metal oxides, to enhance engine performance. Mamdani Fuzzy Inference Adaptive Gravitational Search Optimization (MFI-AGSO) is employed to predict the exhaust emissions and performance characteristics of these fuel blends. Adaptive Gravitational Search Optimization (AGSO) is used to fine-tune its parameters and select the best operating conditions for lower emissions and improved performance. Mamdani Fuzzy Inference system (MFIS) is applied to achieve more accurate predictions. Key input parameters such as Engine Load (EL), fuel blend percentage, nanoparticle concentration, and Injection Pressure (IP) are analyzed to determine their effects on outputs such as Brake Thermal Efficiency (BTE), Brake Specific Fuel Consumption (BSFC), and exhaust emissions. The system is implemented using Python software, and the MFI-AGSO model outperformed conventional methods in predicting emissions characteristics and engine performance of biofuel blends. With the B25Ce50 blend, the BTE is 33.4%, the BSFC is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.255 \text{kg} / \text{kWh}$</tex>, the Carbon Monoxide (CO) emissions achieve 0.234%, the Nitrogen-oxides (NOx) emissions are 595 parts per million (ppm), and the HC emissions are 36 ppm. The results indicate that the optimal biofuel blend with nano additives improves performance and also reduces harmful emissions compared to conventional fuels. This research provides valuable insights into the potential of biofuels with nano additives as an eco-friendly and efficient alternative for ICEs.

title: Multi-Function Radar Work Mode Recognition Based on Extended Long Short-Term Memory Net-
authors: Zihan Ni, Mou Wang, Xianxiang Yu, Guolong Cui, Lingjiang Kong
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: In modern battlefield scenarios, the rapid and accurate identification of the work modes of enemy multi-functional radars is of great significance for achieving victory. However, in complex electromagnetic environments, signal overlap and residual interference from signal sorting can lead to impure Pulse Description Word (PDW) parameter sets, which affects the reliability of subsequent processing. To address these issues, this paper proposes a multi-functional radar (MFR) work mode recognition method based on the Extended Long Short-Term Memory (xLSTM) network. First, the waveform characteristics of radar work modes are analyzed, and a sample dataset of PDWs for MFR is constructed. Then, leveraging the temporal feature extraction capability of the xLSTM network model, a high-efficiency and high-accuracy recognition algorithm for MFR work modes is designed. Finally, simulation tests are conducted under non-ideal conditions, including measurement noise, pulse loss, and false pulses. The experimental results show that the proposed method achieves a radar work mode recognition accuracy of 95.25%, which is 8.25% higher than that of the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model.

title: Distributionally Robust Day-Ahead Dispatch of Distribution Grid With Renewable Energy Uncertainty
authors: Dongyang Cai, Lv Xiaopeng
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: This paper introduces a distributionally robust dayahead dispatch model for active distribution grids. The model accounts for renewable energy uncertainty through an ambiguity set that integrates expected value information and distance metrics. Additionally, a time-decoupling technique is developed for energy storage, incorporating allowed bounds for charging and discharging power. To effectively solve the model, the probability distribution uncertainty is first converted to scenario uncertainty using the duality theory of infinite programming. Then, the Big-M method is introduced to address nonlinear terms within the model. The transformed model is finally tackled using the column and constraint generation (C&CG) method, where the sub and master problems are iteratively optimized to derive optimal dispatch decisions. Numerical simulations conducted in the IEEE 33-node system validate advantages of the proposed model and methods.

title: Design of Sports Tourism Demand Prediction System Based on Big Data and Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Jun Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: There is a remarkable growth in the application and use of machine learning in tourism. This allows for an organized proposal of a predictive system. The research work in this paper focuses on the development of a sports tourism demand prediction system using big data and machine learning. It is aimed at processing the big data about sports tourism, such as historical tourism data, demographic data, climate conditions, and calendar of events to forecast future demand. Using such techniques as decision trees, random forests, and neural networks, one is able to identify various factors that affect tourism demand. Big data integration improves the system's predictive capability since it captures detailed information about different factors influencing tourism behaviours. The results of the experiment prove that the system is effective in predicting demand and can be useful for tourism operators. This work will help to allocate resources more effectively and plan further development in the sphere of sports tourism, which will enhance its efficiency and sustainability.

title: Design of Performance Centric Multiplier Architecture with Dynamic Adder Selection for Digital Signal Processing Applications
authors: V. Sanjay, S.Kavi Shaghar, C.K. Balasundari, S.Sankara Kuttralam
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: In Digital Signal Processing (DSP), Computer Arithmetic, and VLSI System Design, fast multiplication is a key requirement. Traditional array multipliers typically have delay, area or power issues related to the many partial products being created, and the complexity of the addition. In order to reduce the number of partial products, this work proposes a modification to an 8-bit multiplier based on Radix-4 Booth encoding method which will condense the original eight partial products down to four by grouping the multiplier bits into segments of three-bits. To help eliminate the partial product matrix some exact 3: 2 compressors are integrated into the partial product reduction. For the last accumulation stage, we also utilize a configurable adder, to combine the sum row and the shifted carry row, to address the row addition. Each specific design for the adder architecture, Ripple Carry Adder (RCA), Conditional Sum Adder (CSA), Carry Save Adder (CSA), and Kogge-Stone Adder (KSA), can be independently enabled using the existing decoding control logic architecture to create the structure suitable for the parameters of the design. The results of the study showed that the Radix-4 Booth-based multiplier contributes to improved results in speed, power efficiency, and area effectiveness than traditional designs. The flexibility of this architecture makes it very suitable for real-time mathematical and signal processing, where high performance is required, but needs low power consumption. From FPGA device family Virtex Ultrascale+: xcu250-figd2104-2L-e, the obtained implementation results shows that the design's efficient performance utilizes 15 CLBs and 102 LUTs with 24.139 W of dynamic power and 3.541 W of static power, totaling an on-chip power of 27.68 W. The design can achieve a data path delay of 7.37 ns with 10 logic levels for a PDP of 204.002. The findings illustrate that the architecture has a very high performance-topower ratio, which would be ideal for high-speed and low-power VLSI applications.

title: Data-Driven Crop Yield Forecasting in Tamil Nadu Using Advanced Deep Learning Models
authors: A. Philip C Jacob, B. D. Pamela, C. P. KingstonStanley, D. D. Bini
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: The investigation presents the development and evaluates a forecasting framework which uses deep learning to make agricultural predictions through a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network. The proposed framework addresses the limitations of conventional statistical forecasting approaches which struggle to represent the complex and nonlinear characteristics of agricultural time-series data during their projections. The data shows strong susceptibility to changes brought about by climatic variations and seasonal agricultural practices and environmental transformations. The model achieves accurate agricultural forecasting results because it uses LSTM networks to learn both long-term dependencies and short-term pattern changes. The agricultural datasets from each district underwent complete pre-processing procedures which improved model performance through subsequent tuning of critical hyper parameters. The proposed frame achieved strong predictive performance because its $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ values went beyond 0.90 while it maintained low RMSE and MAE results throughout the training and testing periods. The residual diagnostics showed minimal bias because the mean errors stayed close to zero and the skewness remained low which confirmed the predictions had stable and trustworthy results. The validation metrics maintained their stability throughout the training process which proved the model could generalize well without showing any signs of overfitting. The model demonstrates its potential as a dependable decision-support system for Tamil Nadu agricultural planning based on resource management and supply chain optimization and evidence-based policy development. The research uses LSTM to model residual analysis which includes agro-climatic factors as its main focus.

title: mosaiks are made of tesserae: GUI design for a co-simulation framework
authors: Eike Schulte, Jan Sören Schwarz, Malte Stomberg, Sharaf Alsharif, Danila Valko, Jirapa Kamsamsong
publish: 2026 Open Source Modelling and Simulation of Energy Systems (OSMSES)
abstract: In a mosaic, a tessera is a single stone. We introduce tesserae for the co-simulation framework mosaik, where they are sets of entities. They allow for a visual, intuitive, and yet systematic description of simulation scenarios by allowing their entities to be created together and the entities of two tesserae to be connected simultaneously, while ensuring that multidirectional data-flow between tesserae remains consistent without further manual synchronization. We further present an extension of mosaik by a graphical user interface (GUI) based on these tesserae, enabling the drag-and-drop creation of co-simulation setups and their execution. The GUI aims to make mosaik more accessible to users previously excluded by its script-based nature. At the same time, it preserves mosaik’s flexibility, extensibility, and modular architecture.

title: Two-Stage Beam Index Estimation Method Using Spectrum Sensing in Local 5G
authors: Hayato Mitsuhashi, Osamu Takyu, Kohei Akimoto
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: With the proliferation of local 5G systems, we consider expanding coverage by allowing a certain level of interference to solve the coverage reduction problem. At this time, identical frequency interference occurs between adjacent systems. As a method to suppress the resulting interference, we consider beam control utilizing spatial multiplexing. Here, it is necessary to know where the beams of adjacent systems are pointing, but this is difficult because local 5G systems operate autonomously. This paper verifies a method that estimates the beam index of adjacent systems by performing spectrum sensing using the local terminal and base station and capturing beam directionality in three dimensions.

title: Photovoltaic Power Generation Prediction Based on PCA-XGBoost
authors: Wenbo Liu, Shilei Hu, Junhao Du
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: This study focuses on enhancing the accuracy of day-ahead photovoltaic (PV) power generation forecasting, a critical task for grid stability and renewable energy integration. Current prediction methods often face challenges due to the inherent variability of solar power, influenced by weather conditions and geographical factors. To address this, we propose a comprehensive framework combining theoretical modeling and data-driven approaches. For theoretical analysis, we develop a solar radiation model incorporating seasonal shading factors to estimate PV power under clear-sky conditions. For data-driven prediction, we employ SARIMA and LSTM models to capture temporal patterns from historical data, and further integrate Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP) data using PCA for dimensionality reduction, followed by XGBoost to improve forecasting precision. Additionally, spatial downscaling techniques are applied to enhance the resolution of NWP data. The results demonstrate that the SARIMA model outperforms LSTM in stability and accuracy for periodic data, while the XGBoost model with NWP integration achieves superior performance, with RMSE reduced by up to 20% compared to baseline methods. This research provides a robust solution for PV power forecasting, highlighting the effectiveness of combining physical models with advanced machine learning techniques.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Maritime Microgrid Operation
authors: Kanendra Naidu, Puspendu Ghosh, Subho Paul, Lilik Jamilatul Awalin
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: For the last few years, the maritime industry has been experiencing a paradigm shift to install renewable energy resources, especially battery-associated solar photovoltaic panels as an alternative energy source of the conventional diesel generator to reduce reliance on fossil fuels. This is done to fulfill the target of the International Maritime Organization (IMO) to reduce the green-house gas (GHG) emission by 20 % by 2030. Therefore, currently maritime vessels are evolving as all-electric or multi-energy microgrids where battery energy storage (BESS), wind turbines, solar photovoltaic panels, combined cooling, heat, and power (CCHP) units, and thermal storage are equipped along with diesel generators to cater the propulsion loads and on-board service loads. These evolving ships are often called as all-electric ships (AESs) and multi-energy ships (MESs). However, optimal management of such ships is quite challenging because of the interaction between several thermal, mechanical, and electrical resources. In this regard, this article aims to provide a critical review on the present development and ongoing research works on the architecture and operation of AESs and MESs. The article first elucidates the importance of AES and MES in terms of decarbonization of the maritime sector followed by different existing power management systems, voyage planning methodologies, and optimization algorithms developed in the literature for ensuring a smooth, safe, efficient, resilient, reliable, and economic sailing. In addition, power quality issues and their resolving methodologies are discussed. At last, the future research directions are identified and illustrated to accelerate innovation in developing sustainable maritime energy system.

title: Adaptive SoC-Dependent Fast Charging Optimisation with Degradation-Aware Scheduling in DC Microgrid Architecture for Electric Vehicles
authors: Vikram Kumar Saxena, Kundan Kumar, Benjamin A Shimray, Sanjeet Kr. Dwivedi
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: The rapid growth of electric vehicles (EVs) demands fast-charging systems that ensure both user convenience and battery health while minimizing grid stress. Conventional fast-charging methods often ignore the nonlinear state-of-charge (SoC)-dependent charging behaviour of lithiumion batteries, leading to accelerated degradation and grid instability. This paper proposes an adaptive fast-charging framework for DC microgrid-based EV charging stations that integrates SoC-dependent nonlinear charging power constraints with semi-empirical battery degradation models combining both calendar and cycling ageing effects. A real-time model predictive control (MPC)-based scheduling algorithm dynamically adjusts charging power and energy allocation from the grid, photovoltaic generation, and battery energy storage system (BESS) to balance charging speed, degradation cost, and grid load. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed framework reduces battery degradation by over 60%, lowers grid peak demand by 35%, and decreases total grid energy consumption by 19% compared to conventional fast charging. The proposed approach offers a scalable, health-preserving, and grid-friendly solution for next-generation EV fast charging infrastructure.

title: Frugal Edge AI: Robust TinyML for Predictive Maintenance in Sri Lankan MSMEs
authors: Athithya Arunakirinathan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: The narrative of Industry 4.0 is often defined by high-bandwidth, cloud-centric cyber-physical systems. However, this paradigm is economically and operationally disconnected from the reality of Sri Lanka's manufacturing sector, which is dominated by Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) operating legacy “Brownfield” machinery. This paper proposes a “Frugal Edge AI” framework as a viable alternative to the traditional “Lift and Shift” digital transformation model. By retrofitting legacy equipment—specifically Orthodox Tea Rollers—with ultra-low-power Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML) models running on commodity microcontrollers (ESP32), manufacturers can achieve predictive maintenance capabilities without prohibitive Capital Expenditure (CAPEX). We introduce a Spectral Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) feature extraction pipeline capable of isolating mechanical fault signatures even in high-noise industrial environments (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sigma=0.2$</tex>). Simulation results on the Case Western Reserve University (CWRU) dataset demonstrate that while naive time-domain analysis fails (33.15% accuracy) under industrial noise, our noise-augmented frequency-domain model achieves 98.31% generalized accuracy while consuming only 7.98 KB of flash memory. Crucially, Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) benchmarking on the target ESP32 silicon validates real-time deployment feasibility, confirming an inference latency of 337.56 ms and a peak dynamic RAM usage of just 4.10 KB. Furthermore, comparative benchmarking reveals that optimized Random Forest ensembles offer a superior power-efficiency-to-accuracy ratio compared to lightweight Neural Networks for this specific domain. This confirms that localized, offline processing offers a sustainable path to Industry 5.0 resilience for the Global South. To ensure reproducibility of the “Vaccination Strategy” and quantization results, the source code and CWRU data splits are available at: https://github.com/Conference2026/Frugal-edge-ai.

title: Deep Learning-Based Purechain-Backed Robust Intrusion Detection System for Industrial IoT
authors: Mahbuba Iasmin Sumona, Esmot Ara Tuli, Md Mehedi Hasan Somrat, Dong-Seong Kim, Jae-Min Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The proliferation of the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) across critical infrastructure demands real-time intrusion detection systems (IDS) that guarantee both high accuracy and data integrity against sophisticated cyber threats. This paper introduces the Deep Learning Integrated PureChain Model (DLIPCM), a novel, low-latency framework for robust anomaly detection and verifiable logging in IIoT edge environments. The system's core is an LSTM-based IDS that effectively captures complex temporal dependencies in high-dimensional time-series data, achieving superior detection performance with an Accuracy of 0.9960 and an F1-Score of 0.9596. Crucially, when an attack is detected by the LSTM core, the DLI-PCM immediately generates a real-time alert, which is then secured by a specialized PureChain Data Sharing Layer utilizing a gas-free PoA<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> consensus mechanism. This unique layer ensures all detection alerts and corresponding data hashes are immediately and immutably logged via a Smart Contract, providing a tamper-proof, auditable record. Performance metrics validate its operational efficiency, showing an low Average Latency of 0.114 s for transaction logging and highly cost effective due to its low transaction gas cost, thus establishing a comprehensive, trustworthy defense-in-depth strategy essential for secure Industry 4.0 environments.

title: Deep Neural Network to Enhance Natural Image Structure for an Inverse Problem
authors: Kumaresh Sheelavant, T G Manjunath, Abhinaya Prakash, Dhanya V Tejaswini, Chandana S, Deepthi S
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Inverse problems in image processing aim to reconstruct original images from degraded or incomplete measurements such as blurred, noisy, or under sampled data. While traditional approaches rely on handcrafted priors and iterative optimization, Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) have transformed this domain by learning natural image structures directly from data. By implicitly capturing complex statistical priors, DNN-based methods enable high-quality reconstructions that preserve textures, edges, and perceptual fidelity. The solution proposed in this work exhibits significantly high performance over existing techniques, achieving a Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR) of 58.0 dB, indicating minimal reconstruction error, a Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) of 0.998, demonstrating near-perfect structural similarity, and a pixel accuracy of 99.7%. These parameters clearly illustrates that our approach preserves visual quality far better than previous techniques. The solution proposed sets a new benchmark, while other models show significantly lower performance across all metrics. The proposed method gives a detailed overview of how deep learning promotes natural image structure in inverse problems, discussing key architectures, training strategies, applications, and challenges.

title: Lightweight Adaptation of BERT for Negation Sentiment Classification
authors: Tao Hongmo, Hiroyuki Kamata
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Pre-trained language models (PLMs) achieve high accuracy on standard benchmarks for sentiment analysis. However, this performance can hide systematic weaknesses in determining the sentiment of negated sentences, for example when the phrase “not good” is still classified as positive. In this study, we use sentiment classification of English movie reviews in the Stanford Sentiment Treebank 2 (SST-2) as a case study to specifically examine and improve how BERT handles negated sentences. We perform a brief additional fine-tuning of the existing BERT model on a small, automatically constructed set of lexicon-based counterfactual examples that target simple lexical negation. Experimental results on carefully paired original-negated sentences show that this procedure substantially reduces prediction errors on negated inputs while leaving overall performance on SST-2 almost unchanged.

title: RCS Imputation for Recognition Based on Networked Radar Systems
authors: Zhihao Wang, Kaiming Li, Dan Wang, Mengtian Fan, Fulong Xu, Qun Zhang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: With the continuous increase in human space activities, many man-made objects, such as ballistic missiles, decoys, fuel tanks, space debris, etc., have posed serious threats to space security and national security. The recognition of space targets has become increasingly crucial. In multi-target and multi-task observation scenarios, radar echoes from space micromotion targets (SMMTs) often become sparse. This is due to factors like radar mode switching, task scheduling, and electromagnetic interference. This sparsity greatly reduces the performance of downstream tasks, such as target recognition. Imputing the missing values can provide more complete information and improve recognition performance. Networked radar systems’ multi-band and multi-view observation capabilities can enhance both data imputation and recognition. To address this, we propose an integrated learning framework. It combines a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) with a spatiotemporal network. This framework jointly performs imputation and recognition for complex-valued Radar Cross-Section (RCS) sequences of SMMTs. Our method, built on a networked radar system, achieves high-precision reconstruction of RCS sequences. It also delivers robust target recognition performance.

title: Climate Chamber Control System
authors: Danil V. Spitsyn, Vadim N. Ivanov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: A hardware-software system for controlling a small-scale climate chamber based on a Peltier thermoelectric module and an Arduino Nano microcontroller has been developed. Process automation is implemented using an asynchronous Python server and the Telegram Bot API, providing precision temperature regulation with an accuracy of ±0.8 °C and data logging in an SQLite database. Test results confirm the efficiency of the selected two-position control algorithm with hysteresis for thermal state stabilization.

title: Research on Color Optimization in Intangible Cultural Heritage Digitalization Process Based on Deep Learning Algorithms
authors: Lei Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: With society's transformation from industrialization and informatization to intelligence, intangible cultural heritage (ICH) is also experiencing a trend of “living heritage.” This research takes movable-type printing as an empirical study object, addressing the issues of distorted rendering effects and limited technical compatibility that affect immersion during the construction of interactive scenarios. To this end, we propose a Deep Learning Super Sample (DLSS) algorithm applicable to the digitalization process of ICH. This algorithm, based on in-depth analysis of scene rendering distortion and super-resolution reconstruction, employs Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) for rendering super-resolution and reconstruction. Combined with a color-attribute-based post-processing optimization method, the improved DLSS algorithm reconstructs a custom rendering shader, optimizing the clarity, contrast, and saturation of real-time rendering scenes, thereby enhancing the application value of ICH in the digital age. The experimental results show that the reconstructed DLSS algorithm significantly optimizes relevant data such as rendering frame rate and rendering time in real-time interactive scenes, thus verifying the feasibility and effectiveness of real-time rendering technology based on the deep learning super-sampling algorithm in the “living heritage” and innovative design of ICH.

title: Model Free Adaptive Control Enabled Voltage Regulation for PV-Rich Active Distribution Grid
authors: Yuchen Ye
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: The growing share of distributed photovoltaic (PV) systems are very vulnerable to triggering consecutive voltage violations, particularly in long-distance distribution feeders. To this end, this paper proposes a model-free adaptive control enabled voltage regulation method for electrical distribution grids with high PV penetration. In the developed strategy, the nonlinear voltage regulation system is first converted into a compact form dynamic linearization data model and then proceeds to design a corresponding adaptive data-driven controller for voltage regulation. The bounded stability of the voltage regulation closed-loop system is rigorously proven theoretically, and the convergence of the tracking error is formally derived. Numerical simulations on the IEEE case 33 and case 136 test systems validate the superior performance of the MFAC-based voltage regulation method.

title: Enhancing Tie-Line Utilization and Cost Efficiency in Multi-Area Dispatch via EV Integration Under Renewable Energy Uncertainty
authors: Priyanka Hooda, Prem Prakash
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: The increasing integration of electric vehicles (EVs) and renewable energy sources (RESs) with diesel generators (DGs) in microgrids, especially in multi-area microgrids, poses major challenges for modern power systems. Predominantly, wind power has created new challenges and opportunities for power system optimisation in microgrids. This paper proposed a two-stage stochastic optimization model to enhance the coordination between tie-lines and operational efficiency in multi-area economic dispatch (MAED). Results show that the tie-line utilisation improved by 2 cases with EV integration and by 2 cases without it. Furthermore, simulation results for a three-area system demonstrate a 6.5% reduction in total operating cost and improved tie-line performance, highlighting the benefits of coordinated EV participation with vehicle-to-grid (V2G) capabilities. Moreover, the approach provides a scalable solution for future power systems looking to integrate flexible resources for economic and secure dispatch under uncertainty.

title: Development of a Low-Cost Passive and Active Leg Rehabilitation Device with an Interactive Interface and IoT Capabilities
authors: Johan Campos-Cerdas, Arys Carrasquilla-Batista, Gabriela Ortiz-León
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: This project presents the design and prototyping of a low-cost mechatronic device for passive and active lower limb rehabilitation. The project was carried out at the Mechatronics and Automation Research Laboratory (LIMA) of the Costa Rica Institute of Technology (TEC), aiming to promote the development and accessibility of rehabilitation technologies in the local context. The proposed solution allows leg exercises based on flexion and extension movements, offering adjustable intensity levels and selectable rehabilitation modes (active or passive). An interactive game was integrated to enhance user engagement, along with a data logging system for both local and cloud-based storage. The design was validated through proof-of-concept testing on a functional prototype, assessing mechanical performance, user experience, and compliance with cost targets.

title: A Dynamic Evaluation-Based Model for High-Quality Feed-back Filtering in Blockchain Transaction Systems
authors: Fu Gao, Wenlong Feng, Mengxing Huang, Siling Feng, Jiangtao Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: To address the limitations of current blockchain-based transaction systems—specifically the redundancy of smart contract feedback information and the inability to effectively filter valuable insights for system upgrades—this study proposes a feedback information screening model that integrates a decay function with a scoring mechanism. First, the overall framework of the model is constructed, incorporating a time-decay module and a clustering-based scoring strategy. To address the issue of feedback losing value over time, the model considers both the timeliness of user feedback and user activity levels. A decay mechanism combining the Sigmoid function, exponential decay function, and user activity metrics is designed to enhance the reliability and authenticity of feedback evaluation. Second, to identify frequent and common user demands reflected in the feedback, a hybrid scoring approach is proposed that integrates TF-IDF vectorization with a clustering algorithm. This approach enables the classification of feedback based on frequency and quantifies the value of each feedback entry accordingly. Finally, the model's performance was systematically evaluated and applied to a corpus of 300 user reviews. Through this process, the model effectively identified eight high-quality feedback entries, which provide valuable insights for system optimization and are expected to enhance the system’s adaptability and responsiveness to diverse business transaction scenarios.

title: Multi-Layer Coordinated Scheduling of Balanced Groups Based on an Aggregated Equivalent Model
authors: Na Li, Yuntao Sun, Yuan Qi, Wei Zhou
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: The large-scale, hierarchical, and multi-point integration of renewable energy sources (RES) like wind and solar poses significant challenges to centralized power grid dispatch. The Balanced Group (BG) is an important approach for integrating distribution network resources. To achieve the efficient solution of day-ahead hierarchical optimal dispatch within BGs aiming for self-balancing, this paper proposes a multi-layer coordinated dispatch method for BGs based on a “lower-layer aggregation equivalent - multi-layer coordination” framework from the perspective of model dimensionality reduction and equivalence. This method aggregates the characteristics of distributed resources and network constraints in the lower-level grid to construct an aggregated equivalent model reflecting its adjustable capability. Coordinated dispatch is then performed between the upper-level grid and this equivalent model, reducing computational scale and improving optimization efficiency. The effectiveness of the proposed method is finally validated through simulation analysis.

title: Deterministic Mixed-flow Scheduling with Quick-preemption over Ground Test and Launch Networks of Carrier Rockets
authors: Zongrong Cheng, Min Lu, Liqing Ouyang, Rongjun Chen
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Deterministic guarantee of flow transmission latency, jitter, and high reliability is essential to support real-time communication for ground test and launch networks (GTLN) of carrier rockets. In this paper, we investigate mixed-flow scheduling problem in GTLN aiming to improve schedulable flow number and worst end-to-end latency of measurement flows. To support no-wait transmission of time-unknown control-ack flows, we propose a quick-preemption enabled traffic shaping model. By designing a quick-preemption policy, the control-ack flows can be protected from interference by other flows. Considering the complexity of mixed-flow scheduling, the deep reinforcement learning based flexible preemptive scheduling (FlexPS) algorithm is further designed. FlexPS embeds with a flow features-andresource utilization matrix to efficiently learn dynamic features of sending control-ack flows co-transmitted with measurement flows. Experimental results show that FlexPS increases 38.1% and 2x schedulable flows than SMT and Naive algorithms, respectively. The worst end-to-end latency of measurement flows can also be efficiently decreased.
